2:1: Al-Faryabi and Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Mujahid, who said: Four verses from the beginning of Surat Al-Baqarah were revealed about the believers, two verses about the disbelievers, and thirteen verses about the hypocrites. K. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Ibn Ishaq, on the authority of Muhammad Ibn Abi Ikrimah, on the authority of Saeed Ibn Jubayr, on the authority of Ibn Abbas, regarding His statement: Those who disbelieved, the two verses, were revealed about the Jews of Medina. K. And he narrated on the authority of Al-Rabi’ Ibn Anas, who said: “Two verses were revealed about the battle of the confederates: ‘Those who disbelieved, it is all the same for them,’ until His statement: ‘And for them is a great punishment.’”
2:14: The Almighty said: And when they meet those who believe, Al-Wahdai and Al-Tha’labi narrated on the authority of Muhammad bin Marwan and Al-Saddī Al-Saghīr on the authority of Al-Kalbi on the authority of Abi Salih on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: This verse was revealed about Abdullah bin Ubayy and his companions. They went out one day and were met by a group of the companions of the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace. Abdullah bin Ubayy said: Look how I will repel these fools from you. So he went and took Abu Bakr’s hand and said: Welcome to the truthful one, the master of Banu Taym and the Sheikh of Islam and the second of the Messenger of God in the cave, who sacrificed himself and his wealth for the Messenger of God. Then he took Umar’s hand and said: Welcome to the master of Banu Adi bin Ka’b Al-Faruq, the strong one in the religion of God, who sacrificed himself and his wealth for the Messenger of God. Then he took Ali’s hand and said: Welcome to the cousin of the Messenger of God and his son-in-law, the master of Banu Hashim except for the Messenger of God. Then they parted. Abdullah said to his companions: How did you see me do it? If you see them, do as I did and praise him well. The Muslims returned to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and told him about that, so this verse was revealed. This chain of transmission is very weak, for al-Suddi al-Saghir is a liar, and so is al-Kalbi, and Abu Salih is weak.
2:19: The Almighty said, or like a downpour, the verse K Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Al-Suddi Al-Kabir on the authority of Abu Malik and Abu Salih on the authority of Ibn Abbas and on the authority of Marra on the authority of Ibn Masoud and some of the companions, they said, there were two hypocrites from the people of Medina who fled from the Messenger of God to the polytheists, and this rain in which God mentioned severe thunder, lightning and thunderbolts struck them, so whenever the thunderbolts struck them, they put their fingers in their ears out of fear that the thunderbolts would enter their ears and kill them. And when the lightning flashed, they walked towards its light, and if it did not flash, they could not see, so they came to their place and walked, and they began to say, If only we had become so that we could go to Muhammad and put our hands in his hand. So they went to him and became Muslim and put their hands in his hand, and their Islam was good. So God made the matter of these two outgoing hypocrites an example for the hypocrites in Medina. And whenever the hypocrites attended the gathering of the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, they put their fingers in their ears out of fear that the words of the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, would be revealed. There is something in them or they are mentioned with something and they are killed, just as those two hypocrites who came out used to put their fingers in their ears and when it was light for them they would walk in it. Then when their wealth and children increased and they obtained booty or a conquest they would walk in it and say that the religion of Muhammad at that time is true and they would adhere to it, just as those two hypocrites used to walk when the lightning was light for them and when it was dark for them they would stand. And when their wealth and children were destroyed and afflicted with calamity they would say this is for the sake of the religion of Muhammad and they would apostatize as those two hypocrites said when the lightning was dark for them.
2:26: God Almighty says: God is not ashamed to present an example. Verse K Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Al-Suddi with his chains of transmission when God presented these two examples for the hypocrites, His saying: Its example is like the example of one who kindled a fire, and His saying: Or like a rainstorm from the sky. The hypocrites said: God is higher. And he was too noble to make these parables, so God revealed: Indeed, God is not ashamed to make a parable, until His saying: They are the losers. Al-Wahidi narrated on the authority of Abdul-Ghani bin Saeed Al-Thaqafi, on the authority of Musa bin Abdul-Rahman, on the authority of Ibn Jurayj, on the authority of Ata’, on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: “God mentioned the gods of the polytheists, and said: ‘And if the fly should steal something from them,’ and he mentioned the plot of the gods, so he made it like a spider’s web. They said: ‘Do you see where God mentioned the fly and the spider in what He revealed of the Qur’an to Muhammad, what was done with this?’ So God revealed this verse.” Abdul-Ghani is very weak. Abdul-Razzaq said in his interpretation: “Muammar told us on the authority of Qatadah, when God mentioned the spider and the fly, the polytheists said: ‘Why are the spider and the fly mentioned?’ So God revealed this verse.” Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Al-Hasan, who said: “When the verse: ‘O people, a parable was given,’ the polytheists said: ‘This is not one of the parables to be given,’ or something similar to these parables.” So God revealed: Indeed, God is not ashamed to make a parable, the verse. I said: The first statement is more authentic in chain of transmission and more appropriate to what was mentioned at the beginning of the surah, and mentioning the polytheists does not fit with the verse being Madinan, and what we have mentioned about Qatada and Al-Hasan narrated it from Al-Wahidi without a chain of transmission, with the wording: The Jews said, which is more appropriate.
2:44: The Almighty said: Do you command people to do good? Al-Wahidi and Al-Tha’labi narrated on the authority of Al-Kalbi on the authority of Abu Salih on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: This verse was revealed about the Jews of Medina. A man among them would say to his in-laws, his relatives, and those Muslims who were related to him by breastfeeding: Stick to the religion that you are upon, and whatever this man commands you to do, for his command is true. And they would command people to do that but they would not do it.
2:62: The Almighty said: Indeed, those who believed and those who were Jews, Ibn Abi Hatim and Al-Adani narrated in his Musnad on the authority of Ibn Abi Nujayh on the authority of Mujahid, who said: Salman said: I asked the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, about people of a religion I was with, and I mentioned their prayers and worship, so the verse was revealed: Indeed, those who believed and those who were Jews, the verse. Al-Wahidi narrated on the authority of Abdullah bin Katheer on the authority of Mujahid, who said: When Salman told the Messenger of God the story of his companions, He said: They are in the Fire. Salman said: So the earth became dark for me, so the verse was revealed: Indeed, those who believe and those who are Jews will grieve. He said: It was as if a mountain had been removed from me. Ibn Jarir and Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of al-Suddi that he said: This verse was revealed about the companions of Salman al-Farsi.
2:76: The Almighty said: And when they meet the verse Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Mujahid who said: The Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, stood on the day of Qurayzah under their fortresses and said: O brothers of the apes and O brothers of the pigs and O worshippers of the tyrant. They said: Who informed Muhammad of this? This one came from none but you. You tell them what God has revealed to you so that they may have an argument against you. So the verse was revealed. And it was narrated on the authority of Ikrimah on the authority of Ibn Abbas who said: When they met those who believed, they said: We believe that your companion is the Messenger of God, but he is specifically for you. And when some of them were alone with one another, they said: Does he tell the Arabs this? You used to seek victory through him against them. So he was one of them. So God revealed: And when they meet the verse. And it was narrated on the authority of Al-Suddi who said: It was revealed about some of the Jews who believed then became hypocrites and they used to come to the believers from the Arabs with what they had told them. So some of them said to one another: Do you tell them what God has revealed to you of the punishment so that they may say: We are more beloved to God than you and more honorable to God than you.
2:79: The Almighty said: So woe to those who write the Scripture with their own hands. Al-Nasa'i narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: This verse was revealed about the People of the Scripture. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Ikrimah on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: It was revealed about the Jewish rabbis who found the description of the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, written in the Torah: Black-eyed, medium build, curly hair, handsome face. They erased it out of envy and malice and said: We find him tall, blue-eyed, with straight hair.
2:80: The Almighty said: And they said, The Fire will not touch us. The verse. Al-Tabarani in Al-Kabeer, Ibn Jarir, and Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Ibn Ishaq on the authority of Muhammad ibn Abi Muhammad on the authority of Ikrimah or Saeed ibn Jubayr on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: The Messenger of God arrived in Medina while the Jews were saying that the duration of the world is only seven thousand years, and that people are only tormented for every thousand years of the days of The world is one day in the fire of the days of the Hereafter, but it is only seven days, then the torment stops. So God revealed about that, and they said, The Fire will not touch us, until His saying, Therein we will abide eternally. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Ad-Dahhak on the authority of Ibn Abbas that the Jews said: We will not enter Hell except as a recompense for the days in which we worshipped the calf, forty nights. When they have passed, the torment will be cut off from us. So the verse was revealed. And he narrated on the authority of Ikrimah and others.
2:89: The Almighty said: And before that they used to ask for victory. Al-Hakim included in Al-Mustadrak and Al-Bayhaqi in Al-Dala’il with a weak chain of transmission on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: The Jews of Khaybar used to fight Ghatafan, and whenever they met, the Jews were defeated. So they sought refuge with this supplication: O God, we ask You by the right of Muhammad, the unlettered prophet, whom You promised us to bring forth for us at the end of time, to grant us victory over them. So whenever they met, they used to supplicate with this and Ghatafan would be defeated. Then when the Prophet, peace be upon him, was sent, they disbelieved in him, so God revealed: And before that they used to ask You, O Muhammad, for victory over the disbelievers. And Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Saeed or Ikrimah on the authority of Ibn Abbas that the Jews used to seek victory over the Aws and Khazraj through the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, before his mission. When God sent him from the Arabs, they disbelieved in him and denied what they used to say about him. So Muadh bin Jabal, Bishr bin Al-Bara’, and Dawud bin Salamah said to them: O group of Jews, fear God and submit, for you used to seek victory over us through Muhammad, while we were polytheists, and you used to tell us that he would be sent and describe him by his description. So Salam bin Mishkam, one of Banu Nadir, said: He did not bring us anything that we know of, and it is not what we used to mention to you. So God revealed: And when there came to them a Book from God, the verse:
2:94: God Almighty said: Say, If the home of the Hereafter is yours, then you will have it exclusively with God. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Abu al-Aaliyah who said: The Jews said, None will enter Paradise except he who is a Jew. So God revealed: Say, If the home of the Hereafter is yours, then you will have it exclusively with God.
2:97: The Almighty said: Say: Whoever is an enemy to Gabriel, the verse K Al-Bukhari narrated on the authority of Anas who said: Abdullah bin Salam heard the arrival of the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, while he was in a land collecting dung. He came to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and said: I will ask you about three things that only a prophet knows: What is the first sign of the Hour? What is the first food of the people of Paradise? And what causes a child to be drawn to his father or his mother? He said: Gabriel told me about them just now. Gabriel said: Yes. He said: That is the enemy of the Jews from among the angels. Then he recited this verse: Say: Whoever is an enemy to Gabriel, for he has brought it down upon your heart. Sheikh al-Islam Ibn Hajar said in Fath al-Bari: The apparent context is that the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, recited the verse in response to the Jews, and that does not necessarily mean that it was revealed at that time. He said: This is the reliable one, for there is a story other than the story of Abdullah bin Salam that has been authenticated about the reason for the revelation of the verse. Ahmad, al-Tirmidhi, and al-Nasa’i narrated on the authority of Bakr bin Shihab on the authority of Saeed bin Jubayr on the authority of Ibn Abbas who said: The Jews came to the Messenger of God and said: O Abu al-Qasim, we will ask you about five things. He informed us of them, and we knew that you are a prophet. So he mentioned the hadith, and in it they asked him about what Israel had forbidden himself, and about the sign of the prophet, and about thunder and its voice, and how a woman is mentioned in the masculine and feminine, and about the one who brings him report from heaven until they said, then tell us who is your companion? He said, Gabriel. They said, Gabriel, that one descends with war, fighting, and torment, our enemy. If you had said, Michael, who descends with mercy, plants, and rain, it would have been better. So it was revealed. And Ishaq bin Rahawayh narrated in his Musnad and Ibn Jarir on the authority of Al-Sha’bi that Omar used to come to the Jews and hear from the Torah and wonder how they could believe what was in the Qur’an. He said: The Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, passed by them and I said: I ask you by God, do you know that he is the Messenger of God? Their scholar said: Yes, we know that he is the Messenger of God. I said: Then why don’t you follow him? They said: We asked him who would bring him his prophethood. He said: Our enemy is Gabriel, because he descends with harshness, severity, war and destruction. I said: Who is your messenger from the angels? They said: Michael, who descends with rain and mercy. I said: What is their status with their Lord? They said: One of them is on His right and the other is on the other side. I said: It is not permissible for Gabriel to be hostile to Michael, and it is not permissible for Michael to be at peace with the enemy of Gabriel. I bear witness that they and their Lord are at peace with whoever… Then I came to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and I wanted to inform him. When I met him, he said, Shall I not inform you of the verses that were revealed to me? I said, Yes, O Messenger of God. So he recited, Whoever is an enemy to Gabriel until he reached the unbelievers. I said, O Messenger of God, by God, I did not leave the Jews except to come to you to inform you of what they said to me and what I said to them, but I found that God had preceded me. Its chain of transmission is sound up to Al-Sha’bi, but he did not meet Umar. It was narrated by Ibn Abi Shubayh and Ibn Abi Hatim through another chain of transmission on the authority of Al-Sha’bi. It was narrated by Ibn Jarir through Al-Suddi through Umar and through Qatadah through Umar, and they are also disconnected. K and Ibn Abi Hatim narrated from another chain of transmission on the authority of Abd al-Rahman ibn Abi Laila that a Jew met Umar ibn al-Khattab and said: Gabriel, who your companion mentions, is an enemy to us. Umar said: Whoever is an enemy of God, His angels, His messengers, Gabriel, and Michael, then God is his enemy. So it was revealed on the tongue of Umar. These chains of transmission strengthen each other. Ibn Jarir transmitted the consensus that the reason for the revelation of the verse was that.
2:99: The Almighty said: And We have certainly sent down to you the two verses. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Saeed or Ikrimah on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: Ibn Suriya said to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace: O Muhammad, you have not brought us anything that we know of, and God has not sent down to you a clear verse. So God revealed regarding that: And We have certainly sent down to you clear verses, the verse. And Malik bin Al-Saif said when the Messenger of God was sent and mentioned what he took from them of the covenant and what he entrusted to them regarding Muhammad, and by God he did not entrust us with Muhammad nor did he take a covenant from us, so God Almighty revealed: Or whenever they made a covenant, the verse.
2:102: God Almighty said: And follow what the devils recite, the verse. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Shahr bin Hawshab, who said: The Jews said: Look at Muhammad, he mixes truth with falsehood, he mentions Solomon with the prophets. Was he not a magician who rode the wind? Then God Almighty revealed: And follow what the devils recite, the verse. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Abu Al-Aaliyah that the Jews asked the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, for a period of time about matters from the Torah. They did not ask him about anything except that God revealed to him what they asked about. And when they saw that, they said, This one knows better than we do what was revealed to us. And they asked him about magic and argued with him about it, so God revealed, And they followed what the devils recited.
2:104: The Almighty said: O you who have believed, do not say, Ra'ina, but say, Unzurna, and listen. Ibn al-Mundhir narrated on the authority of al-Suddi that two Jewish men, Malik ibn al-Sayf and Rafa'ah ibn Zayd, when they met the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, said while speaking to him, Ra'ina, listen to us and hear us. The Muslims thought that this was the thing that the People of the Book used to glorify their prophets, so they said that to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and God Almighty revealed: O you who have believed, do not say, Ra'ina, but say, Unzurna and listen. Abu Naim narrated in Al-Dala’il, on the authority of Al-Suddi Al-Sagheer, on the authority of Al-Kalbi, on the authority of Abu Salih, on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: We were alarmed by the ugly insults in the language of the Jews. When they heard his companions saying it, they made it public to him. They used to say that and laugh among themselves, so it was revealed. Sa’d bin Mu’adh heard it from them and said to the Jews: O enemies of God, if I hear it from a man among you after this meeting, I will strike his neck. K and Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Ad-Dahhak, who said: A man used to say: Listen to me with your hearing, so the verse was revealed. K and he narrated on the authority of Atiyyah, who said: Some of the Jews used to say: Listen to us with your hearing, until some of the Muslims said it, so God disliked that for them, so the verse was revealed. K and he narrated on the authority of Qatada, who said: They used to say: Listen to us with your hearing, so the Jews used to come and say the same thing, so the verse was revealed. And he narrated on the authority of Ata’ who said: It was the language of the Ansar in the pre-Islamic era, so it was revealed. And he narrated on the authority of Abu Al-Aaliyah, who said: When the Arabs spoke to each other, one of them would say to his companion: Listen to me, so they were forbidden from doing that.
2:106: God Almighty said: We do not abrogate the verse. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Ikrimah on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: Sometimes revelation would come down to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, at night and he would forget it. During the day, God revealed what we abrogate.
2:108: God Almighty said: Or do you want the verse K Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Saeed or Ikrimah on the authority of Ibn Abbas who said: Rafi’ bin Huraymila and Wahb bin Zaid said to the Messenger of God: O Muhammad, bring us a book that you send down to us from the sky so that we can read it, or cause rivers to flow for us so that we may follow you and believe you. So God revealed in that: Or do you want to ask your Messenger to the straight path? Huyayy ibn Akhtab and Abu Yasser ibn Akhtab were among the Jews who were most envious of the Arabs because God had chosen them for His Messenger. They were striving to turn people away from Islam as much as they could, so God revealed about them the verse: Many of the People of the Book love you. K and Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Mujahid who said: The Quraysh asked Muhammad to make Safa gold for them, so he said: Yes, and it is for you like the table for the Children of Israel if you disbelieve. So they refused and returned, so God revealed: Or do you want the verse? And he narrated on the authority of Al-Suddi, who said: The Arabs asked Muhammad, may God bless him and grant him peace, to bring them God so that they could see Him openly, so this verse was revealed: K and he narrated on the authority of Abu Al-Aaliyah who said: A man said: O Messenger of God, if our expiations were like the expiations of the Children of Israel, then the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, said: What God has given you is better than what the Children of Israel, when one of them committed a sin, would find it written on his door and its expiation. If he expiated it, it would be a disgrace for him in this world, and if he did not expiate it, it would be a disgrace for him in the Hereafter. And God has given you better than that. God Almighty said: And whoever does wrong or wrongs himself - the verse: And the five prayers and Friday to Friday are expiations for what is between them. So God revealed: Or do you want to ask your Messenger the verse?
2:113: The Almighty said: And the Jews said, the verse. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Saeed or Ikrimah on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: When the Christians of Najran came to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, The rabbis of the Jews came to them and they disputed. Rafi’ ibn Khuzaymah said, You are on nothing, and he disbelieved in Jesus and the Gospel. Then a man from Najran said to the Jews, You are on nothing, and he denied the prophethood of Moses and disbelieved in the Torah. So God revealed about that, and the Jews said, The Christians are on nothing, the verse.
2:114: The Almighty said: And who is more unjust than he who prevents the mosques of God? The verse Ibn Abi Hatim narrated from the aforementioned path that the Quraysh prevented the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, from praying at the Kaaba in the Sacred Mosque, so God revealed: And who is more unjust than he who prevents the mosques of God? The verse Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Zaid who said: It was revealed about the polytheists when they prevented the Messenger of God from Mecca on the day of Al-Hudaybiyyah.
2:115: The Almighty said: To God belong the East and the West. Muslim, Al-Tirmidhi, and Al-Nasa’i narrated on the authority of Ibn Umar that the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, used to pray voluntarily on his mount, wherever it was facing. He came from Mecca to Medina. Then Ibn Umar recited: To God belong the East and the West. He said: This is why this verse was revealed. Al-Hakim narrated on his authority that he said: “It was revealed: ‘So wherever you turn, there is the face of God that you pray wherever your mount takes you’ in voluntary prayer.” He said: “It is authentic according to the conditions of Muslim. This is the most authentic of what was reported in the verse in terms of chain of transmission, and a group of people relied on it, but it does not contain an explicit mention of the reason. Rather, he said: It was revealed in such-and-such, and what was in it was mentioned previously. The reason for its revelation was explicitly reported.” Ibn Jarir and Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Ali bin Abi Talha on the authority of Ibn Abbas that when the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, migrated to Medina, God ordered him to face Jerusalem, so the Jews rejoiced, so he faced it for about ten months. He loved the direction of Abraham’s qiblah, and he used to supplicate to God and look at the sky. Then God revealed: Turn your faces toward Him. The Jews doubted that, and said: What has turned them away from the qiblah which they used to face? Then God revealed: “Say: To God belong the East and the West.” And He said: So wherever you turn, there is the face of God. Its chain of transmission Strong and the meaning also helps him, so let him rely on it There are other weak narrations regarding this verse. Al-Tirmidhi, Ibn Majah, and Al-Darqutni narrated on the authority of Ash’ath Al-Samman on the authority of Asim bin Abdullah on the authority of Abdullah bin Amir bin Rabi’ah on the authority of his father, who said: We were with the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, on a journey on a dark night, and we did not know where the qiblah was, so each of us prayed in his own quarter. When morning came, we mentioned that to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and so the verse was revealed: Wherever you turn, there is the face of God. Al-Tirmidhi said: It is strange, and Ash’ath considers the hadith weak. Al-Darqutni and Ibn Mardawayh narrated on the authority of Al-Arzami on the authority of Ata’ on the authority of Jabir, who said: The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, sent out a military expedition in which I was a member, and darkness fell upon us, so we did not know the direction of the qiblah. A group of us said: We know the dome is here towards the north, so they prayed and drew lines. Some of us said: The qiblah is here towards the south, so they prayed and drew lines. When morning came and the sun rose, those lines were not facing the qiblah. When we returned from our journey, we asked the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and he remained silent. Then God revealed: To God belongs the East and the West. K and Ibn Mardawayh narrated on the authority of Al-Kalbi on the authority of Abu Salih on the authority of Ibn Abbas that the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, sent out a detachment and they were caught in a state of confusion and they could not find their way to the qiblah, so they prayed. Then it became clear to them after the sun had risen that they had prayed in a direction other than the qiblah. So when they came to the Messenger of God, they told him, and God revealed this verse: To God belongs the East and the West. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Qatada that the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, said: A brother of yours has died, meaning the Negus, so pray over him. They said: We will pray over a man who is not a Muslim. Then the verse was revealed: And among the People of the Book is he who believes in God. They said: He did not pray toward the qiblah. Then God revealed: And to God belong the East and the West. The verse is very strange and is either mursal or mu’dal. K and Ibn Jarir also narrated on the authority of Mujahid who said: When the verse Call upon Me, I will respond to you was revealed, they said: Where to? So it was revealed: Wherever you turn, there is the face of God.
2:118: The Almighty said and those who do not know said the verse. Ibn Jarir and Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Saeed or Ikrimah on the authority of Ibn Abbas who said that Rafi’ bin Khuzaymah said to the Messenger of God, If you are a messenger from God as you say, then say to God and He will speak to us so that we may hear His speech. So God revealed about that, And those who do not know said the verse.
2:119: The Almighty said: Indeed, We have sent you, the verse. Abd al-Razzaq said: Al-Thawri informed us on the authority of Musa bin Ubaidah on the authority of Muhammad bin Ka’b al-Qurazi who said: The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, said: I wish I knew what happened to my parents. Then the verse was revealed: Indeed, We have sent you with the truth as a bringer of good tidings and a warner. And do not ask about the companions of Hellfire. He did not mention it until God took him. It was transmitted by a mursal. Ibn Jarir transmitted on the authority of Ibn Jurayj who said: Dawud bin Abi Asim informed me that the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, said one day: Where are my parents? It was also transmitted by a mursal.
2:120: The Almighty said: And you will never be pleased with the verse. Al-Tha’labi narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas that he said: The Jews of Medina and the Christians of Najran were hoping that the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, would pray towards their qiblah. When God changed the qiblah towards the Kaaba, this was difficult for them and they despaired that he would agree with them on their religion. So God revealed: And the Jews and the Christians will never be pleased with you, the verse.
2:125: The Almighty said: And take, from the station of Abraham, a place of prayer. Al-Bukhari and others narrated on the authority of Omar, who said: I agreed with my Lord in three matters. I said: O Messenger of God, if you take, from the station of Abraham, a place of prayer. So the verse: And take, from the station of Abraham, a place of prayer was revealed. And I said: O Messenger of God, your women are visited by the righteous and the wicked, so why don’t you command them to cover themselves? So the verse of the veil was revealed. And the wives of the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, gathered around him in jealousy, so I said to them: Perhaps his Lord, if he divorces you, will give him in exchange wives better than you. So the verse was revealed. It has many chains of transmission, including what Ibn Abi Hatim and Ibn Mardawayh narrated on the authority of Jabir, who said: When the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, performed the circumambulation, Omar said to him: This is the station of our father Abraham. He said: Yes. He said: Should we not take it as a place of prayer? So God revealed: And take, from the station of Abraham, a place of prayer. Ibn Mardawayh narrated on the authority of Amr bin Maymun on the authority of Omar bin Al-Khattab He passed by the Station of Abraham and said, O Messenger of God, shall we not stand in the place of the friend of our Lord? He said, Yes. He said, Should we not take it? It was not long before the verse was revealed, And take the Station of Abraham as a place of prayer. The apparent meaning of this and what preceded it is that the verse was revealed during the Farewell Pilgrimage.
2:130: The Almighty said: And whoever turns away from the religion of Abraham, the verse. Ibn Uyaynah said: It was narrated that Abdullah bin Salam called the two sons of his brother, Salamah and Muhajir, to Islam and said to them: You know that God Almighty said in the Torah: I am sending from the descendants of Ishmael a prophet whose name will be Ahmad. Whoever believes in him has been guided and is rightly guided, and whoever does not believe in him is cursed. So Salamah and Abu Muhajir converted to Islam, and the verse was revealed about him.
2:135: The Almighty said: And they said, "Be Jews", the verse. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Saeed or Ikrimah on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: Ibn Suriya said to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace: "There is no guidance except what we are upon, so follow us, O Muhammad, and you will be guided." And the Christians said the same, so God revealed about them: And they said, "Be Jews or Christians and you will be guided."
2:142: The Almighty said: The foolish among the people will say: The verses Ibn Ishaq said: Ismail bin Abi Khalid told me on the authority of Abu Ishaq on the authority of Al-Baraa’ who said: The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, used to pray towards the Holy House and would often look at the sky to see the command of God. Then God revealed: We have certainly seen the turning of your face towards the heaven. We will surely turn you to a qiblah with which you will be pleased. So turn your face towards Al-Masjid Al-Haram. A man from among the Muslims said: We wish we knew the knowledge of those who died among us before we were turned towards the qiblah, and how about our prayer towards the Holy House. Then God revealed: And God would never let your faith go to waste. And the foolish among the people said: What has turned them away from the qiblah which they used to face? Then God revealed: The foolish among the people will say: To the end of the verse. There are chains of transmission similar to it. In the two Sahihs on the authority of Al-Baraa: Men died facing the qiblah before the qiblah was turned and were killed, so we did not know what to say about them. Then God revealed: And God would never let your faith go to waste. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Al-Suddi with his chains of transmission that he said: When the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, turned towards the Kaaba after his prayer towards the Holy House, the polytheists of Mecca said: Muhammad is confused about his religion, so he turned his qiblah towards you, and he knew that you were more guided than him in the path, and that he was about to enter your religion. So God revealed the verse: So that the people will have no argument against you.
2:154: The Almighty said: And do not say to those who are killed, The verse. Ibn Mandah narrated in the Companions, on the authority of al-Suddi al-Saghir, on the authority of al-Kalbi, on the authority of Abu Salih, on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: Tamim ibn al-Hammam was killed at Badr, and in it and in other cases it was revealed: And do not say to those who are killed in the way of God, They are dead. The verse. Abu Naim said: They agreed that it was Umair ibn al-Hammam, and that al-Suddi corrupted it.
2:158: The Almighty said: Indeed, As-Safa and Al-Marwah, the verse. The two sheikhs and others narrated on the authority of Urwah on the authority of Aisha who said: I said: Have you seen the statement of God: Indeed, As-Safa and Al-Marwah are among the symbols of God. So whoever performs Hajj or Umrah, there is no blame upon him for walking between them? I do not see anything wrong with anyone not walking between them. Aisha said: What a bad thing you have said, O son of my sister. If it were as I interpreted it, then there would be no blame upon him for not walking between them. However, it was only revealed because the Ansar, before they converted to Islam, used to enter ihram for the tyrant Manat, and some of its people were reluctant to walk between As-Safa and Al-Marwah. So they asked the Messenger of God about that and said: O Messenger of God, we used to be reluctant to walk between As-Safa and Al-Marwah during the pre-Islamic era. So God revealed: Indeed, As-Safa and Al-Marwah are among the symbols of God until His saying: Then there is no blame upon him for walking between them. Al-Bukhari narrated on the authority of Asim bin Sulayman, who said: I asked Anas about Safa and Marwa. He said: We used to think that they were from the pre-Islamic era, but when Islam came, we refrained from them. Then God revealed: Safa and Marwa are among the symbols of God. Al-Hakim narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: In the pre-Islamic era, the devils used to circle around at night between Safa and Marwa, and there were idols between them. When Islam came, the Muslims said: O Messenger of God, we do not circle between Safa and Marwa, because it is something we used to do in the pre-Islamic era. Then God revealed this verse.
2:159: The Almighty said: Indeed, those who conceal the verse, Ibn Jarir and Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Saeed or Ikrimah on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: Muadh bin Jabal, Saad bin Muadh, and Kharijah bin Zaid asked a group of Jewish rabbis about some of what was in the Torah, but they concealed it from them and refused to tell them, so God revealed about them: Indeed, those who conceal what We have sent down of clear proofs and guidance, the verse.
2:164: The Almighty’s saying: Indeed, in the creation of the heavens and the earth, the verse. Saeed bin Mansour narrated in his Sunan, Al-Faryabi in his Tafsir, and Al-Bayhaqi in Shu’ab Al-Iman on the authority of Abu Al-Dhaha, who said: When the verse And your God is one God, there is no god but He, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful was revealed, the polytheists were amazed and said: One God, if he is truthful, then let him bring us a sign. So God revealed: Indeed, in the creation of the heavens and the earth, up to His saying, For a people who reason. I said: This is problematic, but it has a witness. Ibn Abi Hatim and Abu Al-Sheikh narrated in the Book of Al-Azmah on the authority of Ata’ who said: The Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, descended in Medina and said: And your God is one God, there is no god but He, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful. The infidels of Quraysh in Mecca said: How can one God be sufficient for people? So God revealed: Indeed, in the creation of the heavens and the earth until His saying: For a people who understand. And Ibn Abi Hatim and Ibn Mardawayh narrated through a good, connected chain of transmission on the authority of Ibn Abbas who said: The Quraysh said to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace: Pray to God to make for us As-Safa gold by which we may gain strength against our enemy. So God revealed to him: I will give them, but if they disbelieve after that, I will punish them with a punishment such as I have not punished anyone in the worlds. So he said: My Lord, leave me and my people so that I may call upon them day by day. So God revealed this verse: “Indeed, in the creation of the heavens and the earth and the alternation of the night and the day. And how do they ask You for As-Safa while they see signs that are greater?”
2:170: God Almighty said: And when it is said to them, Follow the verse. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Saeed or Ikrimah on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, called the Jews to... Islam and encouraged them to do so and warned them of God’s punishment and vengeance. Rafi’ bin Huraymila and Malik bin Auf said: Rather, O Muhammad, we will follow what we found our sons doing, for they were more knowledgeable and better than us. So God revealed about that: “And when it is said to them, ‘Follow what God has revealed,’ the verse:
2:174: The Almighty said: Indeed, those who conceal the verse Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Ikrimah in his saying: Indeed, those who conceal what God has revealed of the Book and that in Al Imran 3:Indeed, those who exchange the covenant of God, both of them were revealed about the Jews. Al Tha’labi narrated on the authority of Al Kalbi on the authority of Abu Salih on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: This verse was revealed about the leaders of the Jews and their scholars. They used to receive gifts and favors from their lower class, and they hoped that the prophet sent would be from among them. But when Muhammad, may God bless him and grant him peace, was sent from among others, they feared that their livelihood would be lost and their leadership would be removed, so they took the description of Muhammad, may God bless him and grant him peace, and changed it, then they brought it out to them and said: This is the description of the prophet who will emerge at the end of time, it does not resemble the description of this prophet. So God revealed: Indeed, those who conceal what God has revealed of the Book, the verse.
2:177: The Almighty said: It is not righteousness, the verse K said: Abd al-Razzaq informed us, on the authority of Muammar, on the authority of Qatada, who said: The Jews used to pray towards the west and the Christians towards the east, so it was revealed: It is not righteousness that you turn your faces towards the east and the west. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated something similar on the authority of Abu al-Aaliyah, and Ibn Jarir and Ibn al-Mundhir narrated on the authority of Qatada, who said: It was mentioned to us that a man asked the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, about righteousness, so God revealed this verse: It is not righteousness that you turn your faces towards the east. So he called the man and recited it to him. It was before the obligatory duties, if he testified that there is no god but God and that Muhammad is His servant and Messenger, then he died upon that, then he was hoped for and desired for good, so God revealed: It is not righteousness that you turn your faces towards the east and the west. The Jews turned towards the west and the Christians towards the east.
2:178: God Almighty said: O you who have believed, prescribed for you is retaliation, verse K. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Saeed bin Jubair, who said that two tribes of Arabs fought each other in the pre-Islamic era. A little while before Islam, there was killing and wounding among them, until they killed slaves and women, and they did not take revenge on each other until they converted to Islam. One of the two tribes would outnumber the other in numbers and money, so they swore that they would not be satisfied until a free man among them was killed for a slave, and a man among them for a woman. So it was revealed among them: A free man for a free man, a slave for a slave, and a female for a female.
2:184: God Almighty said: And for those who are able to do it, the ransom is feeding a poor person. So break your fast and feed a poor person for each day. Ibn Saad included in his book, Tabaqat, on the authority of Mujahid, who said: This verse was revealed regarding my client Qais bin Al-Sa’ib.
2:186: The Almighty said: And when My servants ask you concerning Me, the verse. Ibn Jarir, Ibn Abi Hatim, Ibn Mardawayh, Abu Al-Sheikh, and others narrated through various chains of transmission on the authority of Jarir Ibn Abdul Hamid, on the authority of Abdah Al-Sijistani, on the authority of Al-Salt Ibn Hakim Ibn Muawiyah Ibn Haidah, on the authority of his father, on the authority of his grandfather, who said: An Arab came to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and said: Is our Lord near so that we may confide in Him, or is He far so that we may call out to Him? He remained silent about him, then God revealed: And when My servants ask you concerning Me, indeed I am near, the verse. And Abd al-Razzaq narrated on the authority of al-Hasan that he said: The companions of the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, asked the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, where is our Lord? So God revealed: And if My servants ask you concerning Me, then indeed I am near. The verse is transmitted and has other chains of transmission. Ibn Asakir narrated on the authority of Ali, who said: The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, said: Do not be unable to supplicate, for God revealed to me: Call upon Me, and I will answer you. A man said: O Messenger of God, does our Lord hear supplication, or how? So God revealed: And when My servants ask you concerning Me, the verse: Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Ata’ bin Abi Rabah that he was informed that when the verse was revealed, And your Lord said, ‘Call upon Me; I will respond to you,’ they said, ‘We do not know at what hour we should call upon Me.’ Then the verse was revealed, “And when My servants ask you concerning Me, until His saying, they are guided.
2:187: The Almighty said: It is lawful for you on the night of the fast, the verse. Ahmad, Abu Dawud and Al-Hakim narrated on the authority of Abd Al-Rahman bin Abi Laila on the authority of Muadh bin Jabal, who said: They used to eat and drink and come to women as long as they did not sleep. When they slept, they refrained. Then a man from the Ansar called Qais bin Sarmah prayed the Isha prayer and then slept and did not eat or drink until morning. He became exhausted. Omar had intercourse with women after he slept, so he came to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and mentioned that to him. Then God revealed: It is lawful for you on the night of the fast to have sexual relations with your wives until the words, then complete the fast until nightfall. This hadith is well-known from Ibn Abi Laila, but he did not hear it from Muadh and it has corroborating evidence. Al-Bukhari narrated on the authority of Al-Baraa’, who said: When a man was fasting and the time for breaking the fast came and he slept before breaking the fast, he would not eat that night or that day until evening. Qais bin Sarmah Al-Ansari was fasting, and when the time for breaking the fast came, he came to his wife and said: Do you have food? She said: No, but I will go and look for it. And he was working that day, but his eyes overcame him, and his wife came to him. When she saw him, she said, You are disappointed. When it was midday, he fainted, and he mentioned that to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and this verse was revealed: Lawful to you on the night of the fast is the approach to your wives. They rejoiced over it with great joy, and it was revealed: And eat and drink until the white thread of dawn becomes distinct to you from the black thread. Al-Bukhari narrated on the authority of Al-Baraa’ who said: When the fasting of the month of Ramadan was revealed, they would not approach women during the entire month of Ramadan. There were men who would betray themselves, so God revealed: God knows that you were betraying yourselves, so He has turned to you in forgiveness and pardoned you. Ahmad, Ibn Jarir, Ibn Abi Hatim, narrated on the authority of Abdullah Ibn Ka’b Ibn Malik on the authority of his father, who said: “When a man fasted during Ramadan and went to sleep in the evening, he was forbidden from food, drink, and women until he broke his fast the next day. Then Umar returned from the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and he had spent the evening with him. He wanted to see his wife, but she said: ‘I slept.’ He said: ‘I did not sleep.’ He had intercourse with her. Ka’b did the same thing. Then Umar went to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and told him, so the verse was revealed.” God the Almighty said: From dawn. Al-Bukhari narrated on the authority of Sahl bin Saeed, who said: Eat and drink until the white thread becomes distinct from the black thread. It was not revealed from dawn. So when men wanted to fast, one of them would tie a white thread and a black thread to his feet and he would continue eating and drinking until he could clearly see them. Then God revealed after dawn, so they knew that he meant the night and the day. God Almighty said: And do not have sexual relations with them while you are in seclusion in the mosques. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Qatada who said: When a man was in seclusion and left the mosque, he could have sexual relations with them if he wished. Then the verse was revealed: And do not have sexual relations with them while you are in seclusion in the mosques.
2:188: The Almighty said: And do not consume one another’s wealth unjustly. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Saeed bin Jubair, who said: Imru’ al-Qais bin Abis and Abdun bin Ashwa’ al-Hadrami had a dispute over a piece of land, and Imru’ al-Qais wanted to swear an oath, so the following verse was revealed: And do not consume one another’s wealth unjustly.
2:189: God Almighty says: They ask you about the new moons. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Al-Awfi on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: The people asked the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, about the new moons, so this verse was revealed. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Abu Al-Aaliyah, who said: It has reached us that they said: O Messenger of God, why did you shave the crescent moon? So God revealed: They ask you about the crescent moon. Abu Naim and Ibn Asakir narrated in Tarikh Dimashq, on the authority of Al-Suddi Al-Sagheer, on the authority of Al-Kalbi, on the authority of Abu Salih, on the authority of Ibn Abbas, that Muadh bin Jabal and Tha’laba bin Ghanima said: O Messenger of God, what is wrong with the crescent moon that it appears or rises as thin as a thread, then increases until it becomes large, even, and round, then it continues to decrease and become thin until it returns to how it was, and it is not in one state, so the verse was revealed: They ask you about the crescent moons. God Almighty said: Righteousness is not that you enter houses from their backs. Al-Bukhari narrated on the authority of Al-Baraa’ that he said: When they entered ihram in the pre-Islamic period, they would come to the House from its back. Then God revealed: Righteousness is not that you enter houses from their backs. Ibn Abi Hatim and Al-Hakim narrated, and he authenticated it, on the authority of Jabir, who said: The Quraysh were called Al-Hams, and they would enter through the gates during the state of ihram. The Ansar and the rest of the Arabs did not enter through a gate during the state of ihram. Then while the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, was in a garden, he went out through its gate and Qutbah ibn Amir Al-Ansari went out with him. They said: O Messenger of God, Qutbah ibn Amir is a wicked man and he went out with you through the gate. He said to him: What made you do what you did? He said: I saw you do it, so I did as you did. He said: I am an Al-Hams man. He said to him: My religion is your religion. Then God revealed: And righteousness does not consist in entering houses from their backs. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Al-Awfi on the authority of Ibn Abbas something similar to it. Al-Tayalisi narrated in his Musnad on the authority of Al-Bara’ who said: When the Ansar returned from a journey, a man would not enter through his door, so this verse was revealed. And Abd bin Hamid narrated on the authority of Qais bin Habtar Al-Nahshali, who said: When they entered ihram, they did not enter a house from its door. The Hamis were different from that. The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, entered an enclosure and then exited from its door. A man called Rafa’ah bin Tabut followed him, and he was not from the Hamis. They said: O Messenger of God, Rafa’ah is a hypocrite. He said: What made you do what you did? He said: I followed you. He said: I am from the Hamis. He said: Our religion is one. Then the verse was revealed: And righteousness does not consist in entering houses from their backs.
2:190: The Almighty said: And fight in the way of God. Al-Wahidi narrated from Al-Kalbi on the authority of Abu Salih on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: This verse was revealed in the Treaty of Hudaybiyyah. That is because when the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, was prevented from the House, then the polytheists made peace with him on the condition that he would return the following year. When the following year came, he and his companions prepared for the Umrah of Compensation and feared that the Quraysh would not fulfill that and would prevent them from the Sacred Mosque and fight them. His companions hated fighting them in the sacred month, so God revealed that. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Qatada who said: The Prophet of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and his companions set out to perform the minor pilgrimage in Dhul-Qa’dah, with sacrificial animals, until they reached Al-Hudaybiyah. The polytheists stopped them and the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, made peace with them on the condition that he would return that year and then return the following year. When the following year came, he set out with his companions until they entered Mecca to perform the minor pilgrimage in Dhul-Qa’dah. He stayed there for three nights. The polytheists had boasted to him when they turned him back, so God distanced him from them and brought him into Mecca in the month in which they had turned him back. So God revealed the sacred month for the sacred month and the sacred things are retribution.
2:195: God Almighty said: And spend in the cause of God and do not throw yourselves with your own hands into destruction. Al-Bukhari narrated on the authority of Hudhayfah who said: This verse was revealed about spending. Abu Dawud, Al-Tirmidhi who authenticated it, Ibn Hayyan, Al-Hakim and others narrated on the authority of Abu Ayyub Al-Ansari who said: This verse was revealed about us, the Ansar, when God made Islam victorious and its supporters increased. Some of us said to one another secretly: Our wealth has been lost and God has made Islam victorious. If we stay with our wealth and repair what has been lost from it, then God revealed: Restore to us what we said: And spend in the way of God and do not throw yourselves with your own hands into destruction. So the destruction was staying with the wealth and repairing it, and we abandoned fighting. Al-Tabarani narrated with a sound chain of transmission on the authority of Abu Jubayrah bin Al-Dahhak, who said: The Ansar used to give in charity and give whatever God willed, then a famine struck them and they held back, so God revealed: And do not throw yourselves with your own hands into destruction verse. He also narrated with a sound chain of transmission on the authority of Al-Nu’man bin Bashir, who said: A man would commit a sin and say, I will not be forgiven. Then God revealed: And do not throw yourselves into destruction with your own hands. And it has a witness from Al-Bara’, narrated by Al-Hakim.
2:196: God Almighty said: And complete the Hajj and Umrah for God. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Safwan ibn Umayya that a man came to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, covered in saffron and wearing a cloak. He said: How do you command me, O Messenger of God, regarding my Umrah? So God revealed: And complete the Hajj and Umrah for God. He said: Where is the one who asked about the Umrah? He said: Here I am. So he, may God bless him and grant him peace, said to him: Take off your clothes, then wash yourself and sniff water as much as you can. Then whatever you were going to do in your Hajj, do in your Umrah. The Almighty said: And whoever among you is ill, the verse. Al-Bukhari narrated on the authority of Ka’b bin Ujrah that he was asked about His statement: Then a ransom of fasting. He said: “I went to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and lice were falling on my face.” He said: “I did not think that you had reached this level of hardship. Couldn’t you find a sheep?” I said: No. He said: Fast for three days, feed six poor people, giving each poor person half a sa’ of food, and shave your head. This was revealed for a specific reason, but it is for all people. Ahmad narrated on the authority of Ka`b who said: We were with the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, at Al-Hudaybiyah and we were deprived and the polytheists had besieged us. I had plenty of hair and insects were falling on my face. The Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, passed by me and said: Are the insects on your head bothering you? He ordered him to shave. He said: Then this verse was revealed: And whoever among you is ill or has an ailment in his head - then a ransom of fasting or charity or sacrifice. Al-Wahidi narrated on the authority of Ata’ on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: When we arrived at Al-Hudaybiyyah, Ka’b bin Ujrah came with lice on his face and said: O Messenger of God, these lice have eaten me. So God revealed in that situation: “So whoever among you is sick, the verse:
2:197: God Almighty said: And take provisions, the verse. Al-Bukhari and others narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: The people of Yemen used to perform Hajj without taking provisions, and they would say: We trust in God. Then God revealed: And take provisions, for the best provision is piety.
2:198: The Almighty said: There is no blame upon you for the verse. Al-Bukhari narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas that Ukaz, Majannah, and Dhu al-Majaz were markets in the pre-Islamic era, and they considered it a sin to trade during the season, so they asked the Messenger of God, may God’s prayers and peace be upon him, about that, and the verse was revealed: There is no blame upon you for seeking bounty from your Lord during the seasons of Hajj. Ahmad, Ibn Abi Hatim, Ibn Jarir, Al-Hakim and others narrated through various chains of transmission on the authority of Abu Umamah Al-Taimi who said: I said to Ibn Umar: We rent, so is there a Hajj for us? Ibn Umar said: A man came to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and asked him about what you asked me about, but he did not answer him until Gabriel revealed to him this verse: There is no blame upon you for seeking bounty from your Lord. Then the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, called him and said: You are performing Hajj.
2:199: God Almighty said: Then hasten. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas that he said: The Arabs used to stand at Arafat and the Quraysh used to stand further away at Muzdalifah. Then God revealed: Then hasten from where the people had hastened. Ibn al-Mundhir narrated on the authority of Asma bint Abi Bakr, who said: The Quraysh used to stand at Muzdalifah, and the people used to stand at Arafat, except for Shaibah ibn Rabi’ah. Then God revealed: Then proceed from where the people proceed.
2:200: God Almighty said: So when you have completed the verse. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas that he said: The people of ignorance used to stand during the season and a man among them would say: My father used to feed and carry the loads and carry the blood money. They have no remembrance except for the deeds of their fathers. Then God revealed: So when you have completed your rites, remember God, the verse. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Mujahid who said: When they completed their rituals, they would stand at the Jamrah and mention their fathers in the pre-Islamic era and the deeds of their fathers, so this verse was revealed. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas that he said: A group of Bedouins used to come to the place of standing and say: O God, make it a year of rain, a year of fertility, a year of prosperity and goodness. They did not mention anything about the Hereafter. So God revealed about them: Some of the people say: Our Lord, give us in this world, but they will have no share in the Hereafter. And others of the believers will come after them and say: Our Lord, give us in this world that which is good and in the Hereafter that which is good and protect us from the punishment of the Fire. Those will have a share for what they have earned, and God is swift in account.
2:204: The Almighty said: And among the people is he who pleases you, the verse. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Saeed or Ikrimah on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: When the expedition that included Asim and Murthad was killed, two men from the hypocrites said: Woe to these seduced ones who perished like this. They neither stayed with their families nor delivered the message of their master. Then God revealed: And among the people is he who pleases you, His statement, the verse. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Al-Suddi who said: It was revealed about Al-Akhns bin Shuraik. He came to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and showed him Islam, and that pleased him. Then he went out and passed by a crop belonging to some Muslims and a donkey, so he burned the crops and hamstrung the donkeys, and God revealed the verse.
2:207: The Almighty said: And among the people is he who sells himself, the verse. Al-Harith bin Abi Usamah narrated in his Musnad and Ibn Abi Hatim on the authority of Saeed bin Al-Musayyab, who said: Suhaib came as an immigrant to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and a group of Quraysh followed him. He got down from his mount and took what was in his quiver and then said: O people of Quraysh, you know that I am the best archer among you. By God, you will not reach me until I shoot every arrow I have in my quiver, then I will strike with my sword whatever is left in my hand of it. Then do as you wish, and if you wish, I will show you my wealth in Mecca and you will let me go. They said: Yes. So when he came to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, in Medina, he said: The sale is profitable, Abu Yahya, Abu Yahya, profited. And the verse was revealed: And among the people is he who sells himself, seeking the pleasure of God, and God is Kind to His servants. Al-Hakim included in Al-Mustadrak something similar to it, on the authority of Ibn Al-Musayyab, on the authority of Suhaib, in a connected chain of transmission. He also brought out something similar to it from the mursal of Ikrimah, and he also brought it out from the path of Jad bin Salamah from Thabit from Anas, and in it is an explicit statement of the revelation of the verse, and he said it is authentic according to the conditions of Muslim. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Ikrimah, who said: It was revealed about Suhaib, Abu Dharr, and Jundub ibn al-Sakn, one of the people of Abu Dharr.
2:208: The Almighty said: O you who have believed, enter into peace, the verse. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Ikrimah who said: Abdullah bin Salam, Tha’laba, Ibn Yamin, Asad, and Asid, the sons of Ka’b, Sa’d bin Amr, and Qais bin Zaid, all of them Jews, said: O Messenger of God, the Sabbath is a day that we honor, so leave us and let us recite it. And the Torah is the Book of God, let us leave us and let us stand in prayer by it at night. Then the verse was revealed: O you who have believed, enter into peace, the entire verse.
2:214: God Almighty said: Or do you think that you will enter Paradise? The verse. Abd al-Razzaq said: Ma`mar informed us on the authority of Qatada, who said: This verse was revealed on the Day of the Confederates. On that day, the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, was afflicted with affliction and confinement.
2:215: God Almighty says: They ask you, what they should spend. The verse. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Ibn Jurayj who said: The believers asked the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, where they should put their money, so the verse was revealed: They ask you, what they should spend. Say: Whatever you spend is good. The verse Ibn al-Mundhir narrated on the authority of Abu Hayyan that Amr ibn al-Jamuh asked the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, what we should spend from our money and where we should put it, so this verse was revealed:
2:217: The Almighty said: They ask you about the sacred month, the verse. Ibn Jarir, Ibn Abi Hatim, Al-Tabarani in Al-Kabeer, and Al-Bayhaqi in his Sunan narrated on the authority of Jundub bin Abdullah that the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, sent a group and sent Abdullah bin Jahsh over them. They encountered Ibn Al-Hadrami and killed him, and they did not know whether that day was from Rajab or Jumada. The polytheists said to the Muslims: You killed in the sacred month. Then God Almighty revealed: They ask you about the sacred month, fighting in it. Some of them said: If they have not committed a sin, then they will have no reward. Then God revealed: Indeed, those who have believed and those who have emigrated and striven in the cause of God - those are the ones who hope for the mercy of God, and God is Forgiving and Merciful. Ibn Mandah narrated it in Al-Sahaba on the authority of Uthman bin Ata’ on the authority of his father on the authority of Ibn Abbas.
2:219: The Almighty said: They ask you about wine. It is mentioned in Surat Al-Ma'idah. God the Almighty said: And they ask you what they should spend. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Saeed or Ikrimah on the authority of Ibn Abbas that a group of the Companions, when they were commanded to spend in the way of God, came to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and said: We do not know what this spending is that we have been commanded to do from our wealth, so what should we spend from it? So God revealed: And they ask you what they should spend. Say: The excess. And he also narrated on the authority of Yahya that he was informed that Muadh ibn Jabal and Tha’laba came to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and said: O Messenger of God, we have slaves and families, so what should we spend from our wealth? So this verse was revealed.
2:220: The Almighty said: And they ask you about orphans. Abu Dawud, Al-Nasa'i, Al-Hakim and others narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: When the verse was revealed: And do not approach the property of an orphan, except in a way that is best. And indeed, those who devour the property of orphans, the verse, the one who had an orphan went and separated his food from his food and his drink from his drink, and he would keep some of his food for him until he ate it or spoiled it. This was difficult for them, so they mentioned it to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and God revealed the verse: And they ask you about orphans, the verse.
2:221: The Almighty said: And do not marry polytheistic women until they believe. Ibn al-Mundhir, Ibn Abi Hatim, and al-Wahidi narrated on the authority of Muqatil that he said: This verse was revealed concerning Ibn Abi Marthad al-Ghanawi. He asked the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, for permission to marry Anaq, who was a polytheist and had good fortune and beauty, so it was revealed. God Almighty said: And for a believing slave girl verse. Al-Wahidi narrated on the authority of Al-Suddi on the authority of Abu Malik on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: This verse was revealed about Abdullah bin Rawahah, who had a black slave girl. He became angry with her and slapped her. Then he was frightened and came to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and told him. He said: I will embrace her and marry her. So he did. Some people criticized him and said: He marries a slave girl. So God revealed this verse. Ibn Jarir narrated it on the authority of Al-Suddi, with an interrupted chain of transmission.
2:222: God Almighty said: And they ask you about menstruation, the verse. Muslim and Al-Tirmidhi narrated on the authority of Anas that when a Jewish woman menstruated, they would not eat with her or have intercourse with her in their homes. So the companions of the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, asked, and the verse was revealed: And they ask you about menstruation, the verse: Do everything except intercourse. Al-Baroudi narrated in Al-Sahaba, on the authority of Ibn Ishaq, on the authority of Muhammad ibn Abi Muhammad, on the authority of Ikrimah or Saeed, on the authority of Ibn Abbas, that Thabit ibn Al-Dahdah asked the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and the verse And they ask you about menstruation was revealed. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Al-Suddi something similar.
2:223: The Almighty said: Your women are a place of cultivation for you, the verse. The two sheikhs, Abu Dawud, and Al-Tirmidhi narrated on the authority of Jabir that he said: The Jews used to say: If a man had intercourse with her from behind, the child would be born cross-eyed. So the verse was revealed: Your women are a place of cultivation for you, so go to your place of cultivation however you wish. Ahmad and Al-Tirmidhi narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas that he said: Umar came to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and said: O Messenger of God, I am ruined. He said: What ruined you? He said: I moved my saddle last night. But he did not answer him anything, so God revealed the verse: Your women are as a tilth unto you, so approach your tilth however you will, coming forward or going back. And beware of the anal intercourse and menstruation. Ibn Jarir, Abu Ya’la, and Ibn Mardawayh narrated on the authority of Zaid bin Aslam on the authority of Ata’ bin Yasar on the authority of Abu Sa’id Al-Khudri that a man had intercourse with his wife in her anus, and the people denounced him for that, so the verse was revealed: Your women are a tilth for you. Al-Bukhari narrated on the authority of Ibn Omar, who said: This verse was revealed regarding having intercourse with women in their backsides. Al-Tabarani narrated in Al-Awsat with a good chain of transmission on his authority, who said: It was revealed only... On the authority of the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, your women are a field for you, and you are permitted to have anal intercourse. He also narrated on his authority that a man had intercourse with a woman in her anus during the time of the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and the people denounced that, so God revealed: Your women are a place of cultivation for you. Abu Dawud and Al-Hakim narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas that he said: Ibn Omar, may God forgive him, was only the people of this tribe of the Ansar, who were idolaters, with this tribe of the Jews, who were People of the Book, who saw that they had superiority over them in knowledge, so they used to imitate many of their actions. It was the custom of the People of the Book that they would not approach women except on the verge, and that was the most modest way for a woman to be. This tribe of the Ansar had taken that, and this tribe of the Quraysh used to treat women with pleasure and enjoy them facing forward, facing backward, and lying down. When the Muhajireen came to Medina, a man from them married a woman from the Ansar, and he went to do that with her, but she disapproved of him and said: We were only approached on the verge. Their affair spread and reached the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, so God revealed: Your women are a tilth for you, so approach your tilth if you wish, meaning facing forward, facing backward, and lying down, meaning the place of the child. Al-Hafiz Ibn Hajar said in his explanation of Al-Bukhari: The reason mentioned by Ibn Omar for the revelation of the verse is well-known, and it is as if the hadith of Abu Saeed did not reach Ibn Abbas, but the hadith of Ibn Omar reached him, so he misunderstood it.
2:224: The Almighty’s saying: And do not make God an object of your oaths, the verse. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Ibn Jurayj, who said: I was told that His saying: And do not make God an object of your oaths, the verse, was revealed about Abu Bakr regarding Mastih.
2:228: The Almighty said: And divorced women shall wait, the verse. Abu Dawud and Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Asma bint Yazid bin Al-Sakan Al-Ansariyyah, who said: I was divorced during the time of the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and the divorced woman did not have a waiting period, so God revealed the waiting period for al-Talaq 65:And divorced women shall wait, concerning themselves, three menstrual periods. Al-Tha’labi and Hibat God bin Salamah mentioned in Al-Nasikh on the authority of Al-Kalbi and Muqatil that Ismail bin Abdullah Al-Afari divorced his wife who was killed during the time of the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and he did not know about her pregnancy, then he found out and took her back, and she gave birth and died, and her child died, so the verse: And divorced women shall wait, concerning themselves, three menstrual periods was revealed.
2:229: The Almighty said: Divorce is twice, the verse. Al-Tirmidhi, Al-Hakim and others narrated on the authority of Aisha that she said: A man would divorce his wife as much as he wanted and she was still his wife if he took her back while she was in her waiting period, even if he divorced her a hundred times or more, until a man said to his wife: By God, I will not divorce you, so separate from me and I will never take you in. She said: How is that? He said: I will divorce you, and whenever your waiting period was about to end, I would take you back. So the woman went and told the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and he remained silent until the Qur’an revealed: Divorce is twice, then either keep her in kindness or release her with kindness. God Almighty said: It is not lawful for you to take back anything of what you have given them. Abu Dawud narrated in Al-Nasikh wa Al-Mansukh on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: A man used to eat his wife’s money from the gifts he gave her and other things, and he did not think that there was any blame on him. So God revealed: It is not lawful for you to take back anything of what you have given them. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Ibn Jurayj who said: This verse was revealed about Thabit bin Qais and Habeeba. She had complained about him to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace. He said: Will you return his garden to him? She said: Yes. So he called him and mentioned that to him. He said: And did you please me with that? He said: Yes. He said: I have done so. So the verse was revealed: And it is not lawful for you to take back anything of what you have given them unless you fear the verse.
2:230: The Almighty said: If he divorces her, the verse. Ibn al-Mundhir narrated on the authority of Muqatil ibn Hibban who said: This verse was revealed about Aisha bint Abd al-Rahman ibn Atik. She was married to Rifa’ah ibn Wahb ibn Atik, who was her cousin, and he divorced her with a final divorce. After that, she married Abd al-Rahman ibn al-Zubayr al-Qurazi, who divorced her. She came to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and said: He divorced me before he touched me. Should I go back to the first time? He said: No, until he touches me. And he revealed in it: “If he divorces her, she is not lawful to him afterward until she marries another husband and he has intercourse with her. But if he divorces her after he has intercourse with her, there is no blame upon them if they return to each other.”
2:231: The Almighty said: And when you divorce women and they have fulfilled their term, then keep them according to acceptable terms. The verse Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Al-Awfi on the authority of Ibn Abbas who said: A man would divorce his wife and then take her back before the end of her waiting period and then divorce her. He would do that to harm her and prevent her, so God revealed this verse. And he narrated on the authority of Al-Suddi, who said: It was revealed about a man from the Ansar called Thabit bin Yasar who divorced his wife, and when her waiting period had ended except for two or three days, he took her back, then he divorced her in order to cause her harm. So God revealed: And do not keep them, in order to cause them harm, in order to aggravate their condition. God Almighty said: And do not take the verses of God in jest. Ibn Abi Omar included in his Musnad and Ibn Mardawayh on the authority of Abu al-Darda’ who said: “A man would divorce and then say, ‘I played,’ and he would free and then say, ‘I played.’ Then God revealed: ‘And do not take the verses of God in jest.’” Ibn Al-Mundhir narrated on the authority of Ubadah bin Al-Samit something similar to it. Ibn Mardawayh narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas something similar to it. Ibn Jarir narrated something similar to it from the mursal of Al-Hasan.
2:232: The Almighty said: And when you divorce women, the verse. Al-Bukhari, Abu Dawud, Al-Tirmidhi and others narrated on the authority of Maqal bin Yasar that he married his sister to a Muslim man and she was with him, then he divorced her once and did not take her back until the waiting period had ended. He desired her and she desired him, so he proposed to her with the suitor and said to him: O wretch, I honored you with her and married you to her, so he divorced her and by God she will never return to you. So God knew his need for her and her need for him, so God revealed: And when you divorce women, and they reached his saying while you do not know. So when Maqal heard it, he said: I hear and obey my Lord. Then he called him and said: I marry you to her and honor you. Ibn Mardawayh narrated it through many chains of narration. Then he narrated on the authority of Al-Suddi who said: It was revealed about Jabir bin Abdullah Al-Ansari, who had a female cousin. Her husband divorced her once, and her waiting period ended. Then he came back wanting to take her back, but Jabir refused. He said: You divorced our female cousin, and then you want to marry her a second time. The woman wanted her husband and she was satisfied with him, so this verse was revealed. The first is more correct and stronger.
2:238: The Almighty said: Guard strictly the prayers. Ahmad, Al-Bukhari in his history, Abu Dawud, Al-Bayhaqi, and Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Zaid bin Thabit that the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, used to pray the noon prayer at noon, and it was the heaviest prayer for his companions, so the verse was revealed: Guard strictly the prayers, and the middle prayer. Ahmad, Al-Nasa’i and Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Zaid bin Thabit that the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, used to pray the noon prayer in the heat of the day, and behind him there would be only one or two rows, and the people were in their afternoon snacks and doing business. Then God revealed: Guard the prayers, especially the middle prayer. The six imams and others narrated on the authority of Zaid bin Arqam who said: We used to talk during the time of the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, during prayer.
