One of us would talk to his companion who was next to him during prayer until the verse, Stand before God, devoutly obedient, was revealed. Then we were commanded to be silent and forbidden from talking. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Mujahid, who said: They used to talk during prayer, and a man would order his brother to do something, so God revealed: Stand before God, devoutly obedient.
2:240: The Almighty said: And those of you who die and leave wives behind them, the verse. Ishaq Ibn Rahawayh narrated in his interpretation on the authority of Muqatil Ibn Hibban that a man from the people of Taif came to Madinah and he had children, men and women, and with him were his parents and his wife. He died in Madinah, so this was brought to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, so he gave to his parents and his children according to what is reasonable, and he did not give his wife anything, except that they were ordered to spend on her the estate of her husband for a year, and in it was revealed: And those of you who die and leave wives behind them, the verse.
2:241: God Almighty said: And for divorced women is a provision according to what is acceptable. The verse Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Ibn Zayd who said: When the verse was revealed: And give them provision according to what is acceptable, the wealthy according to his ability and the poor according to his ability. A provision according to what is acceptable - a duty upon the doers of good. A man said: If I do good, I will do it, and if I do not want to do it, I will not do it. So God revealed: And for divorced women is a provision according to what is acceptable, a duty upon the righteous.
2:245: The Almighty said: Who is it that would loan God a goodly loan so He may multiply it for him many times over? Ibn Hibban narrated in his Sahih, Ibn Abi Hatim, and Ibn Mardawayh on the authority of Ibn Umar, who said: When the verse The parable of those who spend their wealth in the way of God is that of a grain of corn was revealed, the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, said: O Lord, increase my nation. Then the verse was revealed: Who is it that would loan God a goodly loan so He may multiply it for him many times over?
2:256: The Almighty said: There is no compulsion in religion. Abu Dawud, Al-Nasa’i, and Ibn Hibban narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: A woman would be a non-Muslim and would make a vow that if she had a child alive, she would convert to Judaism. When Banu Nadir were expelled, there were some of the sons of the Ansar among them, so they said: We will not abandon our sons. Then God revealed: There is no compulsion in religion. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Saeed or Ikrimah on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: There is no compulsion in religion was revealed concerning a man from the Ansar from Banu Salim ibn Awf called Al-Hussain. He had two Christian sons and he was a Muslim. He said to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, Should I not compel them, for they have refused to accept anything but Christianity? So God revealed the verse.
2:257: God Almighty said: God is the protector of those who believe. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Abdah bin Abi Lubabah regarding his statement: God is the protector of those who believe. He said: They are those who believed in Jesus, then when Muhammad, may God bless him and grant him peace, came to them, they believed in him and this verse was revealed about them. And he narrated on the authority of Mujahid, who said: There were people who believed in Jesus and people who disbelieved in him. Then when Muhammad, may God bless him and grant him peace, was sent, those who disbelieved in Jesus believed in him and those who believed in Jesus disbelieved in him. Then God revealed this verse.
2:267: The Almighty said: O you who have believed, spend from the good things which you have earned. The verse. Al-Hakim, Al-Tirmidhi, Ibn Majah and others narrated on the authority of Al-Baraa’ who said: This verse was revealed about us, the Ansar. We were owners of palm trees and a man would come from his palm trees according to their number or number. There were people who did not desire goodness and would come with a reed in which there were husks and chaff and with a reed that had broken and would hang it up. So God revealed: O you who have believed, spend from the good things which you have earned. The verse. Abu Dawud, al-Nasa’i and al-Hakim narrated on the authority of Sahl ibn Hunaif who said: “People used to use the worst of their fruits for tayammum and give them in charity, so this verse was revealed: ‘And do not use the bad ones of them, spending [otherwise]’.” Al-Hakim narrated on the authority of Jabir who said: “The Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, ordered that the zakat al-fitr be a sa’ of dates, and a man came with bad dates, so the Qur’an was revealed: ‘O you who have believed, spend from the good things which you have earned,’” al-Bukhari and Muslim. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas who said: “The companions of the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, used to buy cheap food and give it in charity, so God revealed this verse.”
2:272: The Almighty said: You are not responsible for their guidance. Al-Nasa'i, Al-Hakim, Al-Bazzar, Al-Tabarani and others narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas that they were reluctant to submit to their polytheist lineages, so they asked, and he permitted them, so this verse was revealed: You are not responsible for their guidance, until His saying: And you will not be wronged. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas that the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, used to command that charity should only be given to the people of Islam, so the verse: You are not responsible for their guidance, was revealed, so he commanded that charity be given to everyone who asked from every religion.
2:274: The Almighty said: Those who spend their wealth by night and by day, the verse. Al-Tabarani and Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Yazid bin Abdullah bin Gharib, on the authority of his father, on the authority of his grandfather, on the authority of the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, who said: This verse was revealed: Those who spend their wealth by night and by day, secretly and publicly, will have their reward, regarding the owners of horses. Yazid and his father are unknown. Abdul Razzaq, Ibn Jarir, Ibn Abi Hatim, and Al-Tabarani narrated with a weak chain of transmission on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: This verse was revealed about Ali ibn Abi Talib. He had four dirhams, and he spent one dirham at night, one dirham during the day, one dirham in secret, and one dirham openly. Ibn al-Mundhir narrated on the authority of Ibn al-Musayyab, who said: The verse was revealed regarding Abd al-Rahman ibn Awf and Uthman ibn Affan regarding their expenses in the Army of Hardship.
2:278: The Almighty said: O you who have believed, fear God and abandon the verse. Abu Ya`la in his Musnad and Ibn Mandah narrated on the authority of Al-Kalbi on the authority of Abu Salih on the authority of Ibn Abbas, he said: It has reached us that this verse was revealed regarding Banu Amr ibn Awf from Thaqeef and Banu Al-Mughirah. Banu Al-Mughirah used to pay interest for Thaqeef, but when God made His Messenger victorious over Mecca, He abolished all interest that day. Banu Amr and Banu Al-Mughirah came to `Atab ibn Asid, who was over Mecca, and Banu Al-Mughirah said: Didn’t He make us the most miserable people with interest and abolish it for the people other than us? Banu `Amr said: We have made peace so that we will have interest. `Atab wrote about that to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and this verse and the one after it were revealed. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Ikrimah who said: This verse was revealed about Thaqif, including Masoud, Habib, Rabi’ah, and Abd Yalil, Banu Amr and Banu Umair.
2:285: The Almighty said: The Messenger has believed, the verse. Ahmad, Muslim and others narrated on the authority of Abu Hurairah that when the verse was revealed: And whether you disclose what is in yourselves or conceal it, God will bring you to account for it, it was difficult for the Companions, so they came to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, then they knelt down and said: This verse has been revealed to you and we cannot bear it. He said: Do you want to say as the People of the Book before you said: We hear and disobey? Rather, say: We hear and obey. Your forgiveness, our Lord, and to You is the final destination. So when the people recited it and their tongues were made to listen to it, God revealed immediately after it: The Messenger has believed, the verse. So when they did that, God abrogated it and revealed: God does not charge a soul except [with that within] its capacity, to the end of it. Muslim and others narrated something similar on the authority of Ibn Abbas.
3:1: Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Al-Rabi’ that the Christians came to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and argued with him about Jesus, so God revealed: There is no god but He, the Ever-Living, the Self-Sustaining, to eighty-some verses. Ibn Ishaq said: Muhammad ibn Sahl ibn Abi Umamah told me that when the people of Najran came to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, asking him about Jesus, son of Mary, the opening of the Family of Imran was revealed about them to the top of the eightieth verse. Al-Bayhaqi narrated it in Al-Dala’il.
3:12: God Almighty said: Say to those who disbelieve, You will be overcome. Abu Dawud narrated in his Sunan and Al-Bayhaqi in Al-Dala'il on the authority of Ibn Ishaq on the authority of Muhammad ibn Abi Muhammad on the authority of Saeed or Ikrimah on the authority of Ibn Abbas that when the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, afflicted the people of Badr with what he afflicted and returned to Medina, he gathered the Jews in the market of Banu Qaynuqa and said: O group of Jews, submit to Islam before God afflicts you with what afflicted Quraysh. They said: O Muhammad, do not be deceived by yourself because you killed a group of Quraysh who were ignorant and did not know how to fight. By God, if you had fought us, you would have known that we are the people and that you have not encountered the likes of us. Then God revealed: Say to those who disbelieve, You will be overcome, up to His saying: For those with insight. Ibn al-Mundhir narrated on the authority of Ikrimah that Phinehas the Jew said on the day of Badr: Do not let Muhammad be deceived by killing and defeating Quraysh, for Quraysh are not good at fighting. So this verse was revealed.
3:23: The Almighty said: Have you not seen those who were given the verse Ibn Abi Hatim and Ibn Al-Mundhir narrated on the authority of Ikrimah on the authority of Ibn Abbas who said: The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, entered the school house upon a group of Jews and called them to God. Then Nuaim bin Amr and Al-Harith bin Zaid said to him: What religion are you on, O Muhammad? He said: On the religion and faith of Abraham. He said: Abraham was a Jew. Then the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, said to them: Come to the Torah, for it is between us and you. But they refused him, so God revealed: Have you not seen those who were given a portion of the Scripture, who are invited to His statement: They invent.
3:26: God Almighty said: Say, O God, Owner of the Kingdom, the verse. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Qatada who said: It was mentioned to us that the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, asked his Lord to make the kingdom of the Romans and Persians among his nation, so God revealed: Say, O God, Owner of the Kingdom, the verse.
3:28: The Almighty said: The verse Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Saeed or Ikrimah on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: Al-Hajjaj bin Amr, an ally of Ka’b bin Al-Ashraf, Ibn Abi Al-Haqiq, and Qais bin Zaid, had entangled a group of the Ansar in order to tempt them away from their religion. So Rafa’ah bin Al-Mundhir, Abdullah bin Jubayr, and Sa’d bin Hathmah said to those al-Nas 114:Avoid these Jews and beware of entanglement with them lest they tempt you away from your religion. But they refused, so God revealed about them: The believers should not take until His saying: And God is over all things competent.
3:31: God Almighty said: Say, If you love God. Ibn al-Mundhir narrated on the authority of al-Hasan that some people said during the time of our Prophet: By God, O Muhammad, we love our Lord. So God revealed: Say, If you love God, then follow me. The verse
3:58: The Almighty said: This We recite to you. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Al-Hasan that a monk from Najran came to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and one of them said: Who is Abu Isa? The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, did not hasten until he commanded his Lord. Then this was revealed to him: This We recite to you from the verses and the wise remembrance to those in doubt. And he brought out through the path of Al-Awfi on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: A group of people from Najran came to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and among them were Al-Sayyid and Al-Aqib, so they said: What is the matter with you that you mention our companion? He said: Who is he? They said: Jesus, you claim that he is the servant of God. He said: Yes. They said: Have you seen the likeness of Jesus or have you been informed of him? Then they left him, and Gabriel came and said: Tell them when they come to you: The likeness of Jesus before God is like the likeness of Adam, up to His saying: Of the doubters. Al-Bayhaqi narrated in Al-Dala’il on the authority of Salamah bin Abd Yashu’ on the authority of his father on the authority of his grandfather that the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, wrote to the people of Najran before… The Tas of Solomon descended upon him in the name of the God of Abraham, Isaac and Jacob from Muhammad the Prophet, the hadith, and in it, so they sent to Shurahbil bin Wada’ah al-Hamdani and Abdullah bin Shurahbil al-Asbahi and Jabbar al-Harithi, so they went and came to him, so he asked them and they asked him, and he kept asking them and them until they said, What do you say about Jesus? He said, I have nothing about him today, so stay until I tell you. So the next morning, God had revealed these verses: The likeness of Jesus is with God, until His saying, Then we will invoke the curse of God upon the liars. Ibn Saad narrated in Al-Tabaqat on the authority of Al-Azraq bin Qais who said: The bishop of Najran and Al-Aqib came to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and he offered them Islam. They said: We were Muslims before you. He said: You have both lied. Islam was forbidden to you on three counts: your saying that God has taken a son, your eating of pork, and your prostrating to idols. They said: Who is Abu Isa? The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, did not know what to say to them until God revealed: The likeness of Jesus is with God until His saying: And indeed, God is the Exalted in Might, the Wise. So he called them to curse each other, but they refused and acknowledged the jizyah and returned.
3:65: God Almighty said: O People of the Book, why do you argue? The verse was narrated by Ibn Ishaq with his repeated chain of transmission to Ibn Abbas who said: The Christians of Najran and the rabbis of the Jews gathered in the presence of the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and they argued in his presence. The rabbis said: Abraham was nothing but a Jew, and the Christians said: Abraham was nothing but a Christian. Then God revealed: O People of the Book, why do you argue? The verse was narrated by Al-Bayhaqi in Al-Dala’il.
3:72: God Almighty said: And a group said: The verse: Ibn Ishaq narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas who said: Abdullah bin Al-Saif, Adi bin Zaid, and Al-Harith bin Awf said to each other: Come, let us believe in what God revealed to Muhammad and his companions in the morning and disbelieve in it in the evening, so that we may confuse them about their religion, perhaps they will do as we do and turn back from their religion. So God revealed about them: O People of the Scripture, why do you mix truth with falsehood? Until His saying: Vast, Knowing. K and Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Al-Suddi on the authority of Abu Malik who said: The Jews used to say to their rabbis to those other than them: Do not believe except those who follow your religion. Then God revealed: Say: Indeed, the guidance is the guidance of God.
3:77: The Almighty said: Indeed, those who buy the verse. The two sheikhs and others narrated that Al-Ash'ath said: There was a land between me and a Jewish man, and he denied it, so I brought him to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and he said: Do you have evidence? I said: No. He said to the Jew: Swear. I said: O Messenger of God, then he will swear and my money will be lost. Then God revealed: Indeed, those who buy the covenant of God and their oaths for a small price, until the end of the verse. Al-Bukhari narrated on the authority of Abdullah bin Abi Awfa that a man displayed his merchandise in the market and swore by God that he had been given something that he had not been given in order to make a Muslim fall for it. Then this verse was revealed: Indeed, those who exchange the covenant of God and their oaths for a small price. Al-Hafiz Ibn Hajar said in his explanation of Al-Bukhari: There is no contradiction between the two hadiths, but rather it is understood that the revelation was for both reasons together. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Ikrimah that the verse was revealed about Huyayy ibn Akhtab, Ka’b ibn al-Ashraf, and other Jews who concealed what God revealed in the Torah and changed it and swore that it was from God. Al-Hafiz Ibn Hajar said: The verse is ambiguous, but the main evidence for that is what is proven in Sahih.
3:79: The Almighty said: It was not for a human being, the verse. Ibn Ishaq and Al-Bayhaqi narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas that Abu Rafi’ Al-Qurazi said: When the Jewish and Christian rabbis from Najran gathered at the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and he called them to Islam, do you want, O Muhammad, for us to worship you as the Christians worship Jesus? He said: God forbid. So God revealed regarding that: It was not for a human being until His saying: after you have become Muslims. And Abd al-Razzaq narrated in his interpretation on the authority of al-Hasan, who said: It reached me that a man said: O Messenger of God, we greet you as we greet one another. Should we not prostrate to you? He said: No, but honor your Prophet and recognize the right for its people, for it is not appropriate for anyone to prostrate to anyone other than God. So God revealed: It is not for a human being to prostrate until His saying: After you are Muslims.
3:86: The Almighty said: How can God guide a people who disbelieved? The verses were narrated by Al-Nasa’i, Ibn Hayyan, and Al-Hakim on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: There was a man from the Ansar who converted to Islam, then he regretted it, so he sent to his people, “Send to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, ‘Is there any repentance for me?’ So the verse was revealed: How can God guide a people who disbelieved, until His saying: Indeed, God is Forgiving and Merciful. So his people sent to him, and he converted to Islam.” Musaddad narrated in his Musnad and Abd al-Razzaq on the authority of Mujahid who said: Al-Harith ibn Suwaid came and became Muslim with the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, then he disbelieved and returned to his people. Then God revealed the Qur’an concerning him: How does God guide a people who disbelieved until His saying: Forgiving, Merciful. Then a man from his people brought it to him and recited it to him. Al-Harith said: By God, you are, as I know, truthful, and the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, is more truthful than you, and God is the most truthful of the three. So he returned and became Muslim, and his Islam was good.
3:97: The Almighty said: And whoever disbelieves - then indeed, God is Free of need of the worlds. Saeed bin Mansour narrated on the authority of Ikrimah who said: When the verse: And whoever desires other than Islam as religion was revealed, the Jews said: We are Muslims. So the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, said to them: God has imposed on the Muslims the pilgrimage to the House. They said: It has not been prescribed for us, and they refused to perform the pilgrimage. So God revealed: And whoever disbelieves - then indeed, God is Free of need of the worlds.
3:100: The Almighty said, O you who have believed, if you obey the verse, Al-Faryabi and Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: There was evil between the Aws and the Khazraj in the pre-Islamic period. While they were sitting, they mentioned what was between them until they became angry and some of them rose up against each other with weapons. Then the verse and the two verses after it were revealed: How do you disbelieve? Ibn Ishaq and Abu Al-Sheikh narrated on the authority of Zaid bin Aslam, who said: Shas bin Qais, who was a Jew, passed by a group of Aws and Khazraj who were talking. He was angered by what he saw of their reconciliation after the enmity, so he ordered a young Jew who was with him to sit among them and remind them of the Day of Buath. He did so, and they argued and boasted until two men, Aws bin Qayzi, jumped up. From Aws and Jabbar bin Sakhr from Khazraj, and they quarreled and the two groups became angry and jumped to fight. This reached the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, so he came and preached to them and reconciled between them, so they listened and obeyed. So God revealed about Aws and Jabbar and those who were with them: O you who have believed, if you obey a party of those who were given the Scripture, the verse, and about Shas bin Qais: O People of the Scripture, why do you turn away?
3:113: The Almighty said: They are not equal, the verse. Ibn Abi Hatim, Al-Tabarani, and Ibn Mandah narrated in the Companions on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: When Abdullah bin Salam, Tha’laba bin Sa’iah, Asid bin Sa’iah, Asad bin Abd, and those Jews who converted with them converted to Islam, and they believed and were truthful and desired Islam, the rabbis of the Jews and the unbelievers among them said: None believed in Muhammad and followed him except the worst of us. If they had been the best of us, they would not have abandoned the religion of their fathers and gone to another one. So God revealed about that: They are not equal among the People of the Book, the verse. Ahmad and others narrated on the authority of Ibn Masoud that he said: The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, delayed the Isha prayer, then he went out to the mosque and saw the people waiting for the prayer. He said: As for the fact that there is no one among the people of these religions who remembers God at this hour except you, and this verse was revealed: They are not all alike. Among the People of the Scripture is a community standing until it reached the point of God being Knowing of the righteous.
3:118: God Almighty said: O you who have believed, do not take as intimates those other than yourselves. Ibn Jarir and Ibn Ishaq narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas that he said: There were men from the Muslims who used to keep close relations with men from the Jews because of the neighborliness and alliance between them in the pre-Islamic era. So God revealed concerning them, forbidding them from keeping close relations with them, fearing sedition for them: O you who have believed, do not take as intimates those other than yourselves. The verse
3:121: The Almighty said: And when you go forth in the morning, Ibn Abi Hatim and Abu Ya`la narrated on the authority of Al-Miswar Ibn Makhramah who said: I said to Abd al-Rahman Ibn Awf, tell me about your story on the Day of Uhud. He said: Read after the one hundred and twenty of Al Imran and you will find our story. And when you go forth in the morning from your family, assigning the believers positions for battle, until His saying: When two parties among you were about to lose heart. He said: They are the ones who sought safety from the polytheists until His saying: And you were wishing for death before you met it, but now you have seen it. He said: It is the wish of the believers to meet the enemy until His saying: So if he died or was killed, would you turn back? He said: It is the shouting of Satan on the day of Uhud, the battle of Muhammad until His saying: Safety from drowsiness. He said: He cast sleep upon them. Al-Bukhari and Muslim narrated on the authority of Jabir bin Abdullah, who said: It was revealed about us, Banu Salamah and Banu Haritha, when two groups were about to lose heart. Ibn Abi Shabia narrated in Al-Musannaf and Ibn Abi Hatim on the authority of Al-Sha’bi that the Muslims were informed on the day of Badr that Karz bin Jaber Al-Muharibi was supplying the polytheists, so it was difficult for them, so God revealed: Is it not sufficient for you that your Lord should supply you with reinforcements? until His saying: marked. So Karz was defeated, so he did not supply the polytheists, and God did not supply the Muslims with the five.
3:128: The Almighty said: You have no part in the matter, the verse. Ahmad and Muslim narrated on the authority of Anas that the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, had his front tooth broken on the day of Uhud and his face was wounded until blood flowed down his face. He said: How can a people succeed who did this to their Prophet while he was calling them to their Lord? So God revealed: You have no part in the matter, the verse. Ahmad and Al-Bukhari narrated on the authority of Ibn Umar that I heard the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, say: O God, curse so-and-so, O God, curse Al-Harith ibn Hisham, O God, curse Suhayl ibn Amr, O God, curse Safwan ibn Umayya. So this verse was revealed: You have no part in the matter, to the end of it, so forgive them all. Al-Bukhari narrated on the authority of Abu Hurairah something similar. Al-Hafiz Ibn Hajar said: The way to reconcile the two hadiths is that he, may God bless him and grant him peace, prayed against those mentioned in his prayer after what happened to him of the aforementioned matter on the day of Uhud, so the verse was revealed regarding both matters together, regarding what happened to him and regarding what resulted from it of praying against them. He said: But what is found in Muslim from the hadith of Abu Hurairah is problematic regarding that, that he, may God bless him and grant him peace, used to say in the dawn prayer: O God, curse Ra’l, Dhakwan, and Asiyah until God revealed to him, You have no part in the matter. The point of difficulty is that the verse was revealed in the story of Uhud, and the story of Ra’l and Dhakwan after it. Then the reason for the report became clear to me, and that there is an insertion in it, for his saying, Until God revealed, is disconnected from the narration of Al-Zuhri from the one who conveyed it to him. Muslim explained that this narration is not correct in what I mentioned. He said, It is possible to say that their story was after that, and the revelation of the verse was delayed a little from its cause, then it was revealed in all of that. I said, “And what was also mentioned in the cause of its revelation is what Al-Bukhari included in his history and Ibn Ishaq on the authority of Salim bin Abdullah bin Omar, who said, “A man from Quraysh came to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and said, ‘You forbid cursing.’ Then he turned and turned his back to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and exposed his anus, so he cursed him and invoked against him. Then God revealed, “You have no part in the matter.” The verse. Then the man converted to Islam, and his conversion to Islam was good.”
3:130: The Almighty said: O you who have believed, Al-Faryabi narrated on the authority of Mujahid, who said: They used to trade on credit, but when the term came, they would increase it and increase the term, so the verse was revealed: O you who have believed, do not consume usury, doubled and multiplied. He also narrated on the authority of Ata’, who said: Thaqif used to lend to Banu Nadir in the pre-Islamic period, but when the term came, they would say: We give you interest and you delay from us. So the verse was revealed: Do not consume usury, doubled and multiplied.
3:140: The Almighty said: And He will take from among you witnesses. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Ikrimah who said: When the report was delayed for the women, they went out to inquire, and there were two men approaching on a camel. A woman said: What has the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, done? They said: He is alive. She said: I do not care. God will take from among His servants witnesses. The Qur’an was revealed in accordance with what she said: And He will take from among you witnesses.
3:143: The Almighty said: And you were the verse. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Al-Awfi on the authority of Ibn Abbas that men from the Companions used to say: I wish we were killed as the companions of Badr were killed, or I wish we had a day like the day of Badr in which we fight the polytheists and do good deeds, or seek martyrdom and Paradise, or life and provision. So God made them witness to one of them, and they did not remain except for whomever God willed of them. Then God revealed: And you were wishing for death, the verse.
3:144: God Almighty said: And Muhammad is only a messenger verse. Ibn al-Mundhir narrated on the authority of Umar, who said: We parted ways from the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, on the day of Uhud, so I climbed the mountain and heard the Jews saying: Muhammad has been killed. So I said: “I will not hear anyone say: ‘Muhammad has been killed’ except that I will strike his neck.” So I looked and saw the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and the people were retreating. So I revealed: And Muhammad is only a messenger verse. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Al-Rabi’ who said: When they were afflicted with the wounds that afflicted them on the day of Uhud and they called each other about the Prophet of God, they said: He has been killed. Some people said: If he had been a prophet, he would not have been killed. Others said: Fight for what your Prophet fought for until God grants you victory or you join him. Then God revealed: Muhammad is only a messenger, the verse. Al-Bayhaqi narrated in Al-Dala’il on the authority of Abu Nujayh that a man from the Muhajireen passed by a man from the Ansar who was wallowing in his blood and said: Do you know that Muhammad has been killed? He said: If Muhammad has been killed, then he has conveyed the message, so fight for your religion. Then this verse was revealed. Ibn Rahawayh narrated in his Musnad on the authority of Al-Zuhri that the devil cried out on the day of Uhud that Muhammad had been killed. Ka’b bin Malik said: “I was the first to recognize the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace. I saw his eyes from under the helmet, so I called out at the top of my voice: This is the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace.” Then God revealed: Muhammad is no more than a Messenger.
3:154: God Almighty said: Then He sent down upon you the verses. Ibn Rahawayh narrated on the authority of Al-Zubayr, who said: I saw myself on the day of Uhud when fear became intense upon us and sleep was sent upon us, and there was not one of us except that his chin was on his chest. By God, I heard, as if in a dream, the words of Mu'tab bin Qushair: If we had had any part in the matter, we would not have been killed here. So I memorized it, and God revealed in that regard: Then He sent down upon you, after the distress, security and drowsiness, until His saying: And God is Knowing of that within the breasts.
3:161: The Almighty said: It is not for a prophet to take spoils of war verse. Abu Dawud and Al-Tirmidhi narrated it and authenticated it on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: This verse was revealed regarding a red velvet that was lost on the day of Badr. Some people said: Perhaps the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, took it. Then God revealed: It is not for a prophet to take spoils of war verse. Al-Tabarani included in Al-Kabeer with a chain of transmission of trustworthy men on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: The Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, sent an army and its banner was returned. Then he sent it and it was returned. Then he sent it and it was returned with the spoils of a gazelle’s head made of gold. Then the following was revealed: It is not for a prophet to take spoils.
3:165: The Almighty said: Or when a disaster befell you, the verse. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Omar Ibn Al-Khattab, who said: They were punished on the day of Uhud for what they did on the day of Badr, when they took ransoms, so seventy of them were killed, and the companions of the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, fled, and his front tooth was broken, and the shell was smashed on his head, and blood ran down his face, so God revealed: Or when a disaster befell you, the verse.
3:169: The Almighty said: And do not think, the verse Ahmad, Abu Dawud and Al-Hakim narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas that the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, said: When your brothers were afflicted at Uhud, God placed their souls in the bodies of green birds that frequent the rivers of Paradise, eat of its fruits and take refuge in golden lanterns in the shade of the Throne. When they found the goodness of their food and drink and the beauty of their resting place, they said: Oh, if only our brothers knew what God has done for us so that they would not give up jihad or turn away from war. God said: I will convey it to them on your behalf. Then God revealed this verse: And do not think that those who were killed, the verse and what follows it. Al-Tirmidhi narrated something similar on the authority of Jabir.
3:172: The Almighty said: Those who responded, the verse: Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Al-Awfi on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: God cast terror into the heart of Abu Sufyan on the day of Uhud after what happened to him, so he returned to Mecca. The Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, said: Abu Sufyan has taken a part of you and has returned, and God cast terror into his heart. The Battle of Uhud was in Shawwal, and the merchants used to come to Medina in Dhu al-Qi'dah and camp at Badr. The younger one is that they came after the Battle of Uhud and the believers were afflicted with ulcers and complained of that, so the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, called upon the people to go with him. Then Satan came and frightened his followers, saying, The people have gathered against you. But the people refused to follow him, so he said, I will go even if no one follows me. So Abu Bakr, Umar, Uthman, Ali, al-Zubayr, Sa`d, Talhah, Abd al-Rahman ibn Awf, Abdullah ibn Mas`ud, Hudhayfah ibn al-Yaman, and Abu Ubaydah ibn al-Jarrah volunteered with him with seventy men. They set out in pursuit of Abu Sufyan and they pursued him until they reached al-Safra’. Then God revealed, Those who responded to God and the Messenger, the verse. And Al-Tabarani narrated with a sound chain of transmission on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: When the polytheists returned from Uhud, they said: You did not kill Muhammad, nor did you take the young maidens with you. What an evil thing you have done! Go back. The Messenger of God heard this and called upon the Muslims, so they volunteered until they reached Hamra’ Al-Asad or Bir Abu Utaybah. Then God revealed the verse: Those who responded to God and the Messenger. Abu Sufyan had said to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, Your meeting place is the season of Badr, where you killed our companions. As for the coward, he returned, and as for the brave, he prepared for battle and trade. They came to him but did not find anyone there, and they went back. Then God revealed: They returned with a blessing from God. Ibn Mardawayh narrated on the authority of Abu Rafi’ that the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, sent Ali with a group of people with him to pursue Abu Sufyan. They were met by a Bedouin from Khuza’ah who said, The people have gathered against you. They said, God is sufficient for us, and He is the best Disposer of affairs. So this verse was revealed about them.
3:181: God Almighty said: God has heard. Ibn Ishaq and Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas that Abu Bakr entered the school house and found the Jews gathered around a man from among them called Finhas. He said to him: By God, O Abu Bakr, we have no poverty towards God and He is indeed poor towards us. If He were rich, He would not have borrowed from us as your companion claims. Abu Bakr became angry and slapped him in the face. Finhas went to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and said: O Muhammad, look at what your companion did to me. He said: O Abu Bakr, what made you do what you did? He said: O Messenger of God, a terrible statement. He claims that God is poor and they are rich from Him. Finhas denied it, so God revealed: God has heard the statement of those who said the verse. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: The Jews came to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, when God revealed: Who is it that would loan God a goodly loan? They said: O Muhammad, your Lord is poor and asks His servants. So God revealed: “God has heard the statement of those who say: God is poor.”
3:186: The Almighty said: And you will surely hear the verse. Ibn Abi Hatim and Ibn Al-Mundhir narrated with a good chain of transmission on the authority of Ibn Abbas that it was revealed regarding what happened between Abu Bakr and Phinehas, regarding his saying that God is poor and we are rich. And Abd Al-Razzaq mentioned on the authority of Muammar on the authority of Al-Zuhri on the authority of Abd Al-Rahman Ibn Kaab Ibn Malik that it was revealed regarding Kaab Ibn Al-Ashraf regarding the poetry he used to satirize the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and his companions.
3:188: The Almighty said: Do not think that those who rejoice in the verse. The two sheikhs and others narrated on the authority of Hamid bin Abd al-Rahman bin Awf that Marwan said to his gatekeeper: Go, O Rafi’, to Ibn Abbas and say: If every man among us who rejoices in what he has done and loves to be praised for what he has not done will be punished, then we will all be punished. Ibn Abbas said: What is the matter with you? This verse was only revealed about the People of the Book. The Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, asked them about something, so they concealed it from him and told him something else. So they left, showing him that they had told him what he had asked them about and had praised him for that, and they were happy with what they had done by concealing what he had asked them about. Al-Bukhari and Muslim narrated on the authority of Ibn Sa`id al-Khudri that when the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, went out to fight, some of the hypocrites would stay behind and rejoice in their absence from the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace. But when he returned, they would apologize to him and swear and wish to be praised for what they had not done. So the verse was revealed: Do not think that those who rejoice in what they have done Abd narrated in his interpretation on the authority of Zaid bin Aslam that Rafi bin Khadij and Zaid bin Thabit were with Marwan, and Marwan said: O Rafi, in what was this verse revealed? Do not think that those who rejoice in what they have done. Rafi` said: It was revealed about some hypocrites who, when the Prophet (peace and blessings of God be upon him) went out, would apologize and say: “We were kept from you only because of preoccupation. We wish we had been with you.” So God revealed this verse about them. It seemed that Marwan denied that, so Rafi` was alarmed by that and said to Zaid bin Thabit: I ask you by God, do you know what I am saying? He said: Yes. Al-Hafiz Ibn Hajar said: This can be reconciled with what Ibn Abbas said, that it is possible that it was revealed about both groups together. Al-Farra` said: It was revealed about the Jews’ saying: We are the people of the first Book, prayer, and obedience, and despite that they did not acknowledge Muhammad. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated something similar from a group of the Tabi`un, and Ibn Jarir preferred it. There is no objection to it being revealed about all of that. End quote.
3:190: The Almighty said: Indeed, in the creation of the heavens and the earth, the verse. At-Tabarani and Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas that he said: The Quraysh came to the Jews and said: What signs did Moses bring to you? They said: His staff and a white hand for the beholders. And they came to the Christians and said: How was Jesus? They said: He used to heal the blind and the lepers and bring the dead to life. So they came to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and said: Call upon your Lord for us to make for us the rock of gold. So he called upon his Lord and this verse was revealed: Indeed, in the creation of the heavens and the earth and the alternation of the night and the day are signs for those of understanding, so let them reflect upon them.
3:195: The Almighty said: So He answered them, the verse. Abd al-Razzaq, Saeed bin Mansour, al-Tirmidhi, al-Hakim, and Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Umm Salamah that she said: O Messenger of God, I do not hear God mentioning women in the migration at all. So God revealed: So their Lord answered them: I will not waste the work of any worker among you, whether male or female, until the end of the verse. The Almighty said: And among the People of the Book is he who believes in God. Al-Nasa’i narrated on the authority of Anas that when the report of the death of the Negus came, the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, said: Pray for him. They said: O Messenger of God, should we pray for an Ethiopian slave? So God revealed: And among the People of the Book is he who believes in God. Ibn Jarir narrated something similar on the authority of Jabir, and in Al-Mustadrak on the authority of Abdullah bin Al-Zubayr, he said: “It was revealed about the Negus: And among the People of the Book is he who believes in God.”
4:4: God Almighty said: And give the women their dowries as a free gift. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Abu Salih that he said: When a man married his daughter, he would take her dowry without hers, so God forbade them from that and revealed: And give the women their dowries as a free gift.
4:7: The Almighty said: For men is a share. Abu Sheikh and Ibn Hibban narrated in the Book of Inheritance on the authority of Al-Kalbi on the authority of Abu Salih on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: The people of the Age of Ignorance did not give inheritance to daughters or young males until they reached maturity. Then a man from the Ansar called Aws bin Thabit died and left two daughters and a young son. Then his cousins Khalid and Artafa, who were his relatives, came and took all of his inheritance. Then his wife came to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and mentioned that to him. He said: I do not know what to say. Then the verse was revealed: For men is a share of what the parents leave.
4:11: God Almighty says: God instructs you. The six imams narrated on the authority of Jabir bin Abdullah, who said: The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and Abu Bakr visited me in Banu Salamah while we were walking. The Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, found me unable to understand anything. He called for water and performed ablution, then sprinkled it on me. I regained consciousness and said: What do you command me to do with my wealth? Then the verse was revealed: God instructs you concerning your children: for the male, what is equal to the share of two females. Ahmad, Abu Dawud, Al-Tirmidhi and Al-Hakim narrated on the authority of Jabir that he said: The wife of Sa`d ibn Al-Rabi` came to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and said: O Messenger of God, these are the two daughters of Sa`d ibn Al-Rabi`. Their father was killed with you as a martyr at Uhud, and their uncle took their wealth and left them no wealth, and they will not marry unless they have wealth. He said: God will decide on that, and the verse of inheritance was revealed. Al-Hafiz Ibn Hajar said: Those who said that the verse It was revealed in the story of Saad’s two daughters, but it was not revealed in the story of Jabir, especially since Jabir did not have a son at that time. He said: The answer is that it was revealed in both matters together, and it is possible that the beginning of it was revealed in the story of the two daughters, and the end of it is his saying: And if a man leaves as an heir as descendants in the story of Jabir, and what Jabir meant by his saying: “So it was revealed: God commands you concerning your children,” meaning the mention of as descendants connected to this verse. End. A third reason was mentioned. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of al-Suddi who said: The people of the Age of Ignorance did not give inheritance to slave girls or weak boys. A man did not inherit from his children except those who were able to fight. Then Abd al-Rahman, the brother of Hassan the poet, died and left behind a woman called Umm Kahah and five daughters. The heirs came to take his money. Umm Kahah complained about that to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, so God revealed this verse: If there are more than two women, then they shall have two-thirds of what he left. Then He said about Umm Kahah: “And they shall have a quarter of what you left, if you have no children. But if you have children, then they shall have an eighth.” K and there was another aspect in the story of Saad bin Al-Rabi’, so Judge Ismail included in Ahkam Al-Qur’an on the authority of Abdul Malik bin Muhammad bin Hazm that Amra bint Hizam was married to Saad bin Al-Rabi’ and he was killed on her behalf at Uhud and he had a daughter from her, so she came to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, asking for her daughter’s inheritance, so in it was revealed: They ask you about women, the verse.
4:19: God Almighty said: O you who have believed, it is not lawful for you to inherit women against their will. Al-Bukhari, Abu Dawud, and Al-Nasa’i narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas that he said: When a man died, his guardians had more right to his wife. If one of them wished, he could marry her, and if they wished, they could marry her off. They had more right to her than her family. Then this verse was revealed. Ibn Jarir and Ibn Abi Hatim narrated with a good chain of transmission on the authority of Abu Umamah ibn Sahl ibn Hanif, who said: When Abu Qais ibn al-Aslat died, his son wanted to marry his wife, and they had that in the pre-Islamic era. So God revealed: It is not lawful for you to inherit women against their will. And there is a corroborating verse on the authority of Ikrimah on the authority of Ibn Jarir. Ibn Abi Hatim, Al-Faryabi and Al-Tabarani narrated on the authority of Adi bin Thabit on the authority of a man from the Ansar who said: Abu Qais bin Al-Aslat died. He was one of the righteous men of the Ansar, so his son Qais proposed to his wife, but she said: I only consider you a son and you are one of the righteous men of your people. So she came to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and told him. He said: Return to your home. Then this verse was revealed: And do not marry those women whom your fathers married, except what has already occurred. Ibn Saad narrated on the authority of Muhammad ibn Ka’b al-Qurazi who said: If a man died leaving behind a wife, his son had more right to marry her if he wished if she was not his mother, or he could marry her to whomever he wished. When Abu Qais ibn al-Aslat died, his son Mahsan arose and inherited the marriage to his wife but did not bequeath her any of the money. She came to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and mentioned that to him. He said: Go back, perhaps God will reveal something about you. Then this verse was revealed: And do not marry those women whom your fathers married. And this verse was revealed: It is not lawful for you to inherit women against their will. He also narrated on the authority of Al-Zuhri, who said: This verse was revealed about some people from the Ansar. When a man died, the most powerful person in control of his wife was his guardian, who would keep her until she died. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Ibn Jurayj who said: I said to Ata’, And the wives of your sons who are from your loins. He said: We used to say that it was revealed about Muhammad, may God bless him and grant him peace, when he married the wife of Zayd ibn Haritha. The polytheists said about that: And the wives of your sons who are from your loins was revealed, and And He did not make your adopted sons your sons was revealed, and Muhammad was not the father of any of your men was revealed.
4:24: The Almighty said: And the chaste women, the verse. Muslim, Abu Dawud, Al-Tirmidhi and Al-Nasa’i narrated on the authority of Abu Saeed Al-Khudri, who said: We captured female captives from the captives of Awtas who had husbands, and we disliked having intercourse with them while they had husbands. So we asked the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and the verse was revealed: And the chaste women, except those whom your right hands possess. He said: Except what God has given you as booty, so we made their private parts lawful for them. Al-Tabarani narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: It was revealed on the day of Hunayn when God conquered Hunayn. The Muslims had intercourse with women from among the People of the Book who had husbands. When a man wanted to have intercourse with a woman, she would say, I have a husband. So the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, was asked about that, and God revealed the verse: And the chaste women among the women. God Almighty said: And there is no blame upon you for what you mutually agree upon after the obligation. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Muammar bin Sulayman on the authority of his father who said: A Hadrami claimed that some men used to impose a dowry, then perhaps hardship would overtake one of them, so this was revealed: And there is no blame upon you for what you mutually agree upon after the obligation.
4:32: God Almighty said: And do not wish. Al-Hakim narrated on the authority of Umm Salamah that she said: Men fight and women do not, and we only have half the inheritance. Then God revealed: And do not wish for that by which God has made some of you excel others. And He revealed in it: Indeed, the Muslim men and Muslim women. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: A woman came to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and said: O Prophet of God, a man has the share of two females, and the testimony of two women is equal to that of a man. Is this how we deal with deeds? If a woman does a good deed, half a good deed will be written for her. Then God revealed: And do not wish for the verse.
4:33: The Almighty said: And those with whom you have made a covenant, the verse. Abu Dawud included in his Sunan, on the authority of Ibn Ishaq, on the authority of Dawud ibn al-Husayn, who said: I was reading to Umm Sa`d bint al-Rabi`, who was staying in the care of Abu Bakr, so I read: And those with whom you have made a covenant, and she said: No, but those with whom you have made a covenant, and it was only revealed about Abu Bakr and his son when he refused Islam, so Abu Bakr swore that he would not inherit from him, but when he converted to Islam, he ordered him to give him his share.
4:34: God Almighty said: Men are in charge of women. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Al-Hasan that a woman came to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, to seek redress from her husband for slapping her. The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, said: Retaliation. Then God revealed: Men are in charge of women. The verse: So she returned without retribution. Ibn Jarir narrated from several chains of transmission on the authority of Al-Hasan, and in some of them, that a man from the Ansar slapped his wife, so she came seeking retaliation, so the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, made retaliation between them, so the following was revealed: And do not hasten with the Qur’an before its revelation is completed to you, and the following was revealed: Men are the protectors and maintainers of women. Something similar was narrated on the authority of Ibn Jurayj and Al-Suddi. Ibn Mardawayh narrated on the authority of Ali, who said: A man from the Ansar came to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, with his wife, and she said to him: O Messenger of God, he hit me and left a mark on my face. The Messenger of God said: He has no right to do that. Then God revealed: Men are the protectors and maintainers of women, the verse. These are pieces of evidence that strengthen each other.
4:37: The Almighty said: Those who are stingy, the verse. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Saeed bin Jubair, who said: The scholars of the Children of Israel were stingy with what they had of knowledge, so God revealed: Those who are stingy and command people to be stingy, the verse. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Ibn Ishaq on the authority of Muhammad ibn Abi Muhammad on the authority of Ikrimah or Saeed on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: Kurdum ibn Zaid was an ally of Ka’b ibn al-Ashraf, Usama ibn Habib, Nafi’ ibn Abi Nafi’, Bahri ibn Amr, Huyayy ibn Akhtab, and Rafa’ah ibn Zaid ibn al-Tabut. They used to come to some men from the Ansar and advise them, saying: Do not spend your money, for we fear that it will lead to poverty if it is lost. Do not rush to spend, for you do not know what will happen. So God revealed about them: Those who are miserly and enjoin miserliness on people, until His saying: And He is Knowing of them.
4:43: The Almighty said, O you who have believed, do not approach the verse. Abu Dawud, Al-Tirmidhi, Al-Nasa'i and Al-Hakim narrated on the authority of Ali, who said, Abd Al-Rahman bin Awf made us food, invited us and gave us wine to drink. The wine took its toll on us and the time for prayer came, so they brought me forward. I recited, Say, O you disbelievers, I do not worship what you worship, and we worship what you worship. Then God revealed, O you who have believed, do not approach prayer while you are intoxicated until you know what you are saying. K and Al-Faryani, Ibn Abi Hatim and Ibn Al-Mundhir narrated on the authority of Ali who said: This verse was revealed, nor one in a state of major ritual impurity regarding a traveler who is afflicted with major ritual impurity and performs tayammum and prays. Ibn Mardawayh narrated on the authority of Al-Asla’ bin Sharik, who said: I was tending the camel of the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and I became impure on a cold night. I was afraid that if I washed myself with cold water I would die or become ill. So I mentioned that to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and God revealed: Do not approach prayer while you are intoxicated, the entire verse. Al-Tabarani narrated on the authority of Al-Asla’ who said: I used to serve the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and I used to travel for him. One day he said to me: O Asla’, get up and travel. I said: O Messenger of God, I have become ritually impure. The Messenger of God remained silent, and Gabriel came to him with the verse of dust. The Messenger of God said: Get up, Asla’, and perform tayammum. He showed me the tayammum: a stroke for the face and a stroke for the hands up to the elbows. So I got up and performed tayammum, then I traveled for him. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Yazid bin Abi Habib that some men from the Ansar had doors in the mosque, and they would become ritually impure and not have water with them, so they would seek water but would not find a way out except in the mosque. So God revealed His words: And do not enter upon one who is ritually impure except when passing through. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Mujahid, who said: This verse was revealed about a man from the Ansar who was sick and could not stand up to perform ablution, and he did not have a servant to carry it to him. So he mentioned that to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and God revealed: And if you are sick verse. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Ibrahim al-Nakha’i, who said: The companions of the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, were wounded and it spread among them, then they were afflicted with major ritual impurity, so they complained about that to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and the entire verse was revealed: And if you are ill.
4:44: God Almighty said: Have you not seen? Ibn Ishaq narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas that he said: Rafa’ah ibn Zayd ibn al-Tabut was one of the great Jews, and when he spoke to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, he would twist his tongue and say: Listen to us, O Muhammad, so that we may understand you. Then he made a joke about Islam, so God revealed about it: Have you not seen those who were given a portion of the Scripture, who purchase error?
4:47: God Almighty said: O you who have been given the Scripture, the verse. Ibn Ishaq narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas that the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, spoke to the chief rabbis of the Jews, among them Abdullah bin Suriya and Kaab bin Asid, and said to them: O group of Jews, fear God and submit, for by God you know that what I have brought to you is the truth. They said: We do not know that, O Muhammad. So God revealed about them: O you who have been given the Scripture, believe in what We have revealed, the verse.
4:48: God Almighty says: Indeed, God does not forgive association with Him. Ibn Abi Hatim and Al-Tabarani narrated on the authority of Abu Ayyub Al-Ansari that he said: A man came to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and said: I have a nephew who does not refrain from what is forbidden. He said: What is his religion? He said: He prays and declares the oneness of God. He said: Ask him for his religion, and if he refuses, then follow it from him. So the man asked him for that, but he refused. So he came to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and told him. He said: I found him stingy with his religion. So this verse was revealed: Indeed, God does not forgive association with Him, but He forgives what is less than that for whomever He wills.
4:49: God Almighty said: Have you not seen those who purify themselves? The verse: Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: The Jews used to bring their children forward to pray with them and offer their sacrifices, claiming that they had no sins or faults. Then God revealed: Have you not seen those who purify themselves? Ibn Jarir narrated something similar on the authority of Ikrimah, Mujahid, Abu Malik, and others.
4:51: The Almighty said: Have you not seen those who were given the verse K Ahmad and Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas who said: When Ka’b bin Al-Ashraf came to Mecca, the Quraysh said: Do you not see this one who has been cut off from his people? He claims that he is better than us, and we are the people of the pilgrimage, the people of the custodianship, and the people of the water supply. He said: You are better. So it was revealed about them: Indeed, your enemy is the one cut off. And it was revealed: Have you not seen those who were given a portion of the Scripture? To a supporter. Ibn Ishaq narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: Those who formed the confederates from Quraysh, Ghatafan, and Banu Qurayzah were Huyayy ibn Akhtab, Salam ibn Abi al-Haqiq, Abu Rafi’, al-Rabi’ ibn Abi al-Haqiq, Abu ‘Amara, and Hudha ibn Qays. The rest of them were from Banu al-Nadir. When they came to Quraysh, they said: These are Jewish rabbis and people of knowledge of the first scriptures, so ask them: Is your religion better or the religion of Muhammad? So they asked them, and they said: Your religion is better than his religion, and you are better guided than him and those who follow him. Then God revealed: Have you not seen those who were given a portion of the Scripture until His saying: A great kingdom. K and Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Al-Awfi on the authority of Ibn Abbas who said: The People of the Book said: Muhammad claimed that he was given what he was given in humility and he has nine wives and his only concern is marriage. So what king is better than this? So God revealed: Or do they envy people? The verse. Ibn Saad narrated on the authority of Umar, the freed slave of Afra, something similar but more simple than it.
4:58: God Almighty says: God commands you. Ibn Mardawayh narrated on the authority of Al-Kalbi on the authority of Abu Salih on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: When the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, conquered Mecca, he called Uthman bin Talha. When he came to him, he said: Show me the key. So he brought it to him. When he stretched out his hand to it, Al-Abbas stood up and said: O Messenger of God, may my father and mother be sacrificed for you, collect it for me with the water-giving vessel. But Uthman withdrew his hand. The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, said: Give me the key, O Uthman. He said: Here is God’s trust. So he stood up and opened the Kaaba, then he went out and circumambulated the House. Then Gabriel came down to him with the key. So he called Uthman bin Talha and gave him the key. Then he said: God commands you to return trusts to their owners until he finished the verse. And Shu`bah narrated in his interpretation on the authority of Hajjaj on the authority of Ibn Jurayj who said: This verse was revealed about Uthman ibn Talhah. The Messenger of God took the key to the Ka`bah from him and entered the House with it on the day of the conquest. He came out reciting this verse. Then he called Uthman and gave him the key. And Umar ibn al-Khattab said: When the Messenger of God came out of the Ka`bah reciting this verse, may my father and mother be sacrificed for him, I had never heard him recite it before that. I said: The apparent meaning of this is that it was revealed in the interior of the Ka`bah.
4:59: The Almighty said: O you who believe, obey God, the verse. Al-Bukhari and others narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: This verse was revealed about Abdullah bin Hudhafah bin Qais when the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, sent him on a military expedition. Thus, he narrated it in an abbreviated form. Al-Dawudi said: This is an illusion, meaning a slander, on the authority of Ibn Abbas, for Abdullah bin Hudhafah went out to an army and became angry, so he lit a fire and said: Crush in. Some people refused, while others thought that he would do so. He said: If the verse was revealed before, then how can it single out Abdullah bin Hudhafah for obedience without others? And if it was revealed after him, then it was said to them: Obedience is only in what is good, and what was said to them was that they did not obey him. Al-Hafiz Ibn Hajar responded that what was meant in his story was: If you dispute about something, then they disputed about complying with the command to obey and refraining in order to escape from the Fire, so it was appropriate to reveal in that what guides them to what they should do when they dispute, which is to refer to God and the Messenger. Ibn Jarir narrated that it was revealed in a story that happened to Ammar bin Yasir with Khalid bin Al-Walid, and Khalid was Amir, so Ammar gave refuge to a man without his permission, and they quarreled, so this verse was revealed.
4:60: God Almighty said: Have you not seen those who claim that they believe in God, except goodness and reconciliation? Ibn Abi Hatim and Al-Tabarani narrated with a sound chain of transmission on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: Abu Bazrah Al-Aslami was a soothsayer who used to judge between the Jews regarding matters over which they disputed. Some Muslims disputed with him, so God revealed: Have you not seen those who claim that they believe in God, except goodness and reconciliation. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Ikrimah or Saeed on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: Al-Jalas bin Al-Samit, Mu’tab bin Qushair, Rafi’ bin Zaid, and Bishr were calling to Islam. Then some men from their people, the Muslims, called them to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, in a dispute that had occurred between them. So they called them to the soothsayers, the rulers of the Age of Ignorance. So God revealed about them: Have you not seen those who claim the verse? Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Al-Sha’bi who said: There was a dispute between a Jewish man and a hypocrite. The Jew said: I will bring you to the people of your religion, or the Prophet said: Because he knew that he would not take bribes in judgment. They disagreed and agreed to go to a soothsayer in Juhayna. Then this verse was revealed.
4:65: The Almighty said: No, by your Lord! The six Imams narrated on the authority of Abdullah bin Al-Zubayr, who said: Al-Zubayr argued with a man from the Ansar about the streams of Al-Harrah. The Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, said: Irrigate, O Zubayr, then let the water flow to your neighbor. The Ansari said: O Messenger of God, if he is your cousin? His face changed color and he said: Irrigate, O Zubayr, then hold back the water until it flows back to the wall, then let the water flow to your neighbor. He gave Al-Zubayr his due. He had advised them to do something that was broad for them. Al-Zubayr said: “I do not think that these verses were revealed except in that regard: ‘No, by your Lord, they will not [truly] believe until they make you, [O Muhammad], judge concerning that over which they dispute.’” Al-Tabarani included in Al-Kabeer and Al-Hamidi in his Musnad on the authority of Umm Salamah who said: Al-Zubayr disputed with a man before the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and he ruled in favor of Al-Zubayr. The man said: He only ruled in his favor because he was his cousin. Then the verse was revealed: No, by your Lord, they will not [truly] believe until they make you their judge. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Saeed bin Al-Musayyab regarding his statement, No, by your Lord, the verse. He said, “It was revealed about Al-Zubayr bin Al-Awwam and Hatib bin Abi Bal’atah. They had a dispute over water, so the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, ruled that the upper one should be given water, then the lower one.” And Ibn Abi Hatim and Ibn Mardawayh narrated on the authority of Abu Al-Aswad, who said: Two men disputed before the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and he ruled between them. The one against whom he ruled said: Refer us to Umar ibn Al-Khattab. So they came to him and the man said: “The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, ruled in my favor.” He said: Refer us to Umar. He said: Is that so? He said: Yes. Umar said: Stay where you are until I come out to you and I will judge between you. So he went out to them, his sword drawn, and he struck the one who said: Refer us to Umar, and killed him. Then God revealed the verse: No, by your Lord, they will not believe. It is a strange mursal in its chain of transmission, Ibn Lahi’ah, and it has a corroborating narration by Rahim in his interpretation, on the authority of Utbah ibn Damrah, on the authority of his father. And Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Al-Suddi who said: When the verse was revealed: And if We had prescribed for them, ‘Kill yourselves’ or ‘Leave your homes,’ they would not have done it, except for a few of them. Thabit bin Qais bin Shammas and a Jewish man boasted, so the Jew said: By God, God prescribed for us, ‘Kill yourselves,’ so we killed ourselves. Thabit said: By God, if God had prescribed for us, ‘Kill yourselves,’ we would have killed ourselves. Then God revealed: And if they had done what they were advised, it would have been better for them and a firmer position. And the Almighty’s saying: And whoever obeys God Al-Tabarani and Ibn Mardawayh narrated with an acceptable chain of transmission on the authority of Aisha who said: A man came to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and said: O Messenger of God, you are dearer to me than my own self, and you are dearer to me than my children. When I am in the house and remember you, I cannot wait until I come and look at you. When I remember my death and your death, I know that when you enter Paradise, you will be raised with the prophets, and when I enter Paradise, I fear that I will not see you. So the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, did not respond to anything until Gabriel revealed to him this verse: And whoever obeys God and the Messenger Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Masruq who said: The companions of Muhammad, may God bless him and grant him peace, said: O Messenger of God, we should not leave you, for if you had come, you would have been raised above us and we would not have seen you. Then God revealed: And whoever obeys God and the Messenger, the verse: And he narrated on the authority of Ikrimah, who said: He came to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and said: O Prophet of God, we have a glimpse of you in this world and on the Day of Resurrection we will not see you, for you are in Paradise in the highest ranks. So God revealed this verse, and the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, said to him: You are with me in Paradise, God willing. And Ibn Jarir narrated something similar from the mursal of Saeed bin Jubayr, Masruq, Al-Rabi’, Qatadah, and Al-Suddi.
4:77: The Almighty said: Have you not seen those who were told, Restrain your hands? Al-Nasa'i and Al-Hakim narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas that Abd al-Rahman ibn Awf and his companions came to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and said: O Prophet of God, we were in glory while we were polytheists, but when we believed we became humiliated. He said: I have been commanded to pardon, so do not fight the people. When God moved him to Medina, He commanded him to fight, so they refrained. Then God revealed: Have you not seen those who were told, Restrain your hands? The verse
4:83: The Almighty said: And when there comes to them a verse Muslim narrated on the authority of Omar bin Al-Khattab who said: When the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, had withdrawn from his wives, I entered the mosque and found the people poking with pebbles and saying: The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, has divorced his wives. So I stood at the door of the mosque and called out at the top of my voice: He has not divorced his wives. So this verse was revealed: And when there comes to them a matter of security or fear, they broadcast it. But if they had referred it back to the Messenger or to those in authority among them, those among them who are able to investigate it would have known about it. So I was the one who investigated that matter.
4:88: The Almighty said: What is the matter with you regarding the hypocrites? The two sheikhs and others narrated on the authority of Zaid bin Thabit that the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, went out to Uhud and some people who had gone out with him returned. The companions of the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, were divided into two groups: one group said, We will kill them and the other group said, No. So God revealed: What is the matter with you regarding the hypocrites? That you are two groups. And Saeed bin Mansour and Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Saad bin Muadh who said: The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, addressed the people and said: Who will harm me and gather in his house those who will harm me? Saad bin Muadh said: If he is from the Aws, we will kill him, and if he is from our brothers from the Khazraj, I will command you and we will obey you. Saad bin Ubadah stood up and said: What is wrong with you, O son of Muadh? Obedience to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, is not acceptable, and you know what is wrong with you. Asid bin Hudhair stood up and said: O son of Ubadah, you are a hypocrite and you love hypocrites. Muhammad bin Maslama stood up and said: Be quiet, O people, for among us is the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and he commands us and we carry out his command. Then God revealed: What is wrong with you that you are divided into two groups regarding the hypocrites? Ahmad narrated on the authority of Abd al-Rahman ibn Awf that some Arabs came to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, in Medina and converted to Islam. Then the epidemic and fever of Medina struck them, so they retreated. They left Medina and were met by a group of the Companions who said to them, Why have you returned? They said, The epidemic of Medina struck us. They said, Do you not have a good example in the Messenger of God? Some of them said, They are hypocrites, and some of them said, They are not hypocrites. Then God revealed, Why are you divided into two groups regarding the hypocrites? The verse contains a forgery and an interruption in its chain of transmission.
4:90: The Almighty said: Except those who pray, the verse. Ibn Abi Hatim and Ibn Mardawayh narrated on the authority of Al-Hasan that Saraqa bin Malik Al-Mudliji told them that when the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, defeated the people of Badr and Uhud and those around them converted to Islam, Saraqa said: It has reached me that he wants to send Khalid bin Al-Walid to my people, Banu Mudlij, so I went to him and said: I ask you for favor, it has reached me that you want to send to my people and I want you to make peace with them. If your people convert to Islam, they will convert to Islam and enter Islam, and if they do not convert to Islam, it is not good for your people to prevail over them. So the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, took Khalid by the hand and said: Go with him and do what he wants. So Khalid made peace with them on the condition that they would not help against the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace. If Quraysh converted to Islam, they would convert to Islam with them. And God revealed: Except those who pray to a people between you and them is a covenant. So whoever connected to them was with them according to their covenant. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: Except for those who pray to a people between whom and you there is a treaty, was revealed about Hilal bin Uwaimir al-Aslami, Saraqa bin Malik al-Mudlaji, and Banu Juthayma bin Amir bin Abd Manaf. He also narrated on the authority of Mujahid that it was revealed about Hilal bin Uwaimir Al-Aslami, who had a treaty with the Muslims, and some of his people came to him, so he did not want to fight the Muslims and he did not want to fight his people.
4:92: The Almighty said: And it is not for a believer to kill a believer except by mistake, the verse Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Ikrimah, who said: Al-Harith bin Yazid from Banu Amir bin Luay was torturing Ayyash bin Abi Rabi’ah with Abu Jahl. Then Al-Harith went out as an immigrant to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and Ayyash met him in Al-Harrah and struck him with the sword, thinking that he was an unbeliever. Then he came to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and informed him. Then the verse was revealed: And it is not for a believer to kill a believer except by mistake. And something similar was narrated on the authority of Mujahid and Al-Suddi. Ibn Ishaq, Abu Ya’la, Al-Harith bin Abi Usama, and Abu Muslim Al-Kaji narrated on the authority of Al-Qasim bin Muhammad something similar to it. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Saeed bin Jubair on the authority of Ibn Abbas something similar to it.
4:93: The Almighty said: And whoever kills a believer intentionally, the verse Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Ibn Jurayj on the authority of Ikrimah that a man from the Ansar killed the brother of Muqays ibn Subaba, so the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, gave him the blood money and he accepted it. Then he attacked his brother’s killer and killed him. The Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, said: I will not give him safety in a state of ihram or sanctuary. So he was killed on the day of the conquest. Ibn Jurayj said: And in it this verse was revealed: And whoever kills a believer intentionally, the verse.
4:94: The Almighty said: O you who have believed, when you strike the verse. Al-Bukhari, Al-Tirmidhi, Al-Hakim and others narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: A man from Banu Salim passed by a group of the companions of the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, while he was herding his sheep, so he greeted them. They said: He only greeted us to seek refuge from us. So they went to him and killed him and brought his sheep to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace. Then the verse was revealed: O you who have believed, when you strike the verse. Al-Bazzar narrated from another chain of transmission on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, sent a detachment that included Al-Miqdad. When they reached the people, they found that they had dispersed, and a man with a lot of money remained. He said: I bear witness that there is no god but God. Al-Miqdad killed him, so the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, said to him: How will you be able to say: There is no god but God tomorrow? And God revealed this verse.
