Ahmad, Al-Tabarani, and others narrated on the authority of Abdullah bin Abi Hadrad Al-Aslami, who said: The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, sent us with a group of Muslims, among them Abu Qatada and Muhallim bin Jaththama. Amir bin Al-Adbat Al-Ashja’i passed by us and greeted us, so Muhallim attacked him and killed him. When we returned to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and told him the report, the Qur’an was revealed concerning us: O you who have believed, when you go forth in the cause of God…” Ibn Jarir narrated something similar from the hadith of Ibn Umar. Al-Tha’labi narrated on the authority of Al-Kalbi on the authority of Abu Salih on the authority of Ibn Abbas that the name The murdered man was Mardas bin Nahik from the people of Fadak, and the name of the murderer was Usama bin Zaid, and the name of the commander of the expedition was Ghalib bin Fadala al-Laithi, and when the people of Mardas were defeated, he remained alone, and he had taken refuge with his sheep on a mountain, and when they caught up with him, he said, “There is no god but God, Muhammad is the Messenger of God, peace be upon you,” and Usama bin Zaid killed him, and when they returned, the verse was revealed. Ibn Jarir narrated from the path of Al-Suddi and Abd from the path of Qatada something similar to it. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated from the path of Ibn Lahi’ah from Abu Al-Zubayr from Jabir who said: This verse was revealed: “And do not say to one who offers you peace in Mirdas” while he is witnessing a good witness. Ibn Mandah narrated on the authority of Juz’ bin Al-Hadrijan, who said: My brother Muqdad came to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, from Yemen. The Prophet’s company, may God bless him and grant him peace, met him and he said to them: I am a believer. They did not accept it from him and killed him. This reached me, so I went out to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and the verse: O you who have believed, when you fight in the cause of God, investigate was revealed. So the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, gave me my brother’s blood money.
4:95: The Almighty said: Not equal are those who sit at home The verse Al-Bukhari narrated on the authority of Al-Baraa’ who said: When the verse was revealed: Not equal are those of the believers who sit at home the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, said: Call so-and-so. So he came with an inkwell, a tablet and a shoulder blade and said: Write: Not equal are those of the believers who sit at home and those who strive in the cause of God. The Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, was behind Ibn Umm Maktum who said: O Messenger of God, I am blind. So it was revealed in its place: Not equal are those of the believers who sit at home except those who are disabled. Al-Bukhari and others narrated from the hadith of Zaid bin Thabit and Al-Tabarani from the hadith of Zaid bin Arqam and Ibn Hibban from the hadith of Al-Faltan bin Asim something similar. Al-Tirmidhi narrated something similar from the hadith of Ibn Abbas and in it Abdullah bin Jahsh and Ibn Umm Maktum said: We are blind. I have cited their hadiths in Tarjuman Al-Qur’an and Ibn Jarir has many chains of transmission that are not connected to that.
4:97: The Almighty said: Indeed, those whom the angels take while they are wronging themselves. Al-Bukhari narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas that some Muslims were with the polytheists, increasing the number of polytheists against the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and an arrow would come and be shot and would hit one of them and kill him, or he would strike and kill him. So God revealed: Indeed, those whom the angels take while they are wronging themselves. Ibn Mardawayh narrated it and named among them in his narration Qais bin Al-Walid bin Al-Mughira, Abu Qais bin Al-Fakih bin Al-Mughira, Al-Walid bin Utbah bin Rabi’a, Amr bin Umayyah bin Sufyan, and Ali bin Umayyah bin Khalaf. He mentioned about them that they went out to Badr, and when they saw the small number of Muslims, doubt entered them and they said, These people have been deceived by their religion, so they were killed at Badr. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated it and added among them Al-Harith bin Zama’ah bin Al-Aswad and Al-Aas bin Munabbih bin Al-Hajjaj. Al-Tabarani narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: There were people in Mecca who had converted to Islam. When the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, migrated, they were reluctant to migrate and were afraid. So God revealed: Indeed, those whom the angels take in death while they are wronging themselves until His saying: Except the oppressed. Ibn al-Mundhir and Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas that he said: There were some people from Mecca who had converted to Islam but were hiding their Islam, so the polytheists expelled them with them on the day of Badr and some of them were injured. The Muslims said: These were Muslims and were forced, so ask forgiveness for them. Then the verse was revealed: Indeed, those whom the angels take in death 2:10. They wrote about it to those who remained in Mecca and that they had no excuse. So they went out and the polytheists caught up with them and tried to tempt them, so they returned. Then the verse was revealed: And among the people is he who says, ‘We believe in God,’ but when he is harmed for the sake of God, He regards the trial of the people as like the punishment of God. So the Muslims wrote about that to them and they were saddened. Then the verse was revealed: Then indeed, your Lord is for those who emigrated after they were tried. So they wrote about that to them, so they went out and caught up with them. Some of them were saved and some of them were killed. Ibn Jarir narrated something similar from many chains of narration.
4:100: The Almighty said: And whoever leaves his home, the verse. Ibn Abi Hatim and Abu Ya`la narrated with a good chain of transmission on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: Damrah bin Jundub left his home as an emigrant and said to his family: Carry me and take me out of the land of the polytheists to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace. He died on the way before he reached the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace. Then the revelation was revealed: And whoever leaves his home, an emigrant, the verse. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Saeed bin Jubair on the authority of Abu Damrah Al-Zarqi, who was in Mecca. When the verse Except for the weak among men, women and children who are unable to devise a plan was revealed, he said, Indeed, I am rich and indeed, I am able to devise a plan. So he prepared himself, meaning the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, but death overtook him in At-Tan’eem, so this verse was revealed: And whoever emigrates from his home to God and His Messenger... Ibn Jarir narrated something similar to this through the chains of transmission of Sa`id ibn Jubayr, `Ikrimah, Qatadah, al-Suddi, al-Dahhak, and others. In some of them, he named Damrah ibn al-`Ays or al-`Ays ibn Damrah, in some of them Jundub ibn Damrah al-Junda`i, in some of them al-Damri, in some of them a man from Banu Damrah, in some of them a man from Banu Khuza`ah, in some of them a man from Banu Layth, in some of them from Banu Bakr Kinanah, and in some of them from Banu Bakr. Ibn Saad narrated in Al-Tabaqat on the authority of Yazid bin Abdullah bin Qast that Junda’ bin Damrah Al-Damri was in Mecca and fell ill. He said to his sons: Take me out of Mecca, for its grief is killing me. They said: Where to? He pointed with his hand toward Medina, intending to emigrate. So they took him out. When they reached the valley of Banu Ghaffar, he died. So God revealed about him: And whoever emigrates from his home, the verse. K and Ibn Abi Hatim, Ibn Mannah and Al-Baroudi narrated in the Companions on the authority of Hisham Ibn Urwah on the authority of his father that Al-Zubayr Ibn Al-Awwam said: Khalid Ibn Haram emigrated to the land of Abyssinia and a snake bit him on the road and he died, so the verse was revealed about him: And whoever emigrates from his home. Al-Umawi narrated in his book Maghaziyyah on the authority of Abdul-Malik bin Umair, who said: When Aktham bin Saifi heard of the departure of the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, he wanted to come to him, but his people refused to let him. He said: Let someone come to him who will convey to him from me and convey to me from him. So two men volunteered for him and came to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and said: We are messengers of Aktham bin Saifi, and he is asking you who you are, what are you, and why have you come? He said: I am Muhammad bin Abdullah, and I am the servant and messenger of God. Then he recited to them: God commands justice and doing good. So they came to Aktham and said that to him. He said: O people, he commands good morals and forbids bad ones, so be leaders in this matter and do not be followers in it. So he rode his camel heading to Medina and died on the way, so the verse was revealed concerning him: And whoever emigrates from his home is a weak mursal. Hatim narrated in the Book of the Long-Lived, through two chains of transmission, on the authority of Ibn Abbas, that he was asked about this verse, and he said: It was revealed about Aktham bin Saifi. It was said: Where is Al-Laythi? He said: This was before Al-Laythi by a time, and it is specific and general.
4:101: The Almighty said: And when you travel the land, Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Ali, who said: A group of Banu An-Najjar asked the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and said: O Messenger of God, we travel the land, so how do we pray? So God revealed: And when you travel the land, there is no blame upon you if you shorten the prayer. Then the revelation was cut off. Then, after a year, the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, went on an expedition and prayed the noon prayer. The polytheists said: Muhammad and his companions have given you power over their backs. Why did you not make things difficult for them? One of them said: They have another like it in its wake. So God revealed between the two prayers: If you fear that those who disbelieve will tempt you to a humiliating punishment. So the prayer of fear was revealed. Ahmad and Al-Hakim narrated, and Al-Bayhaqi authenticated it in Al-Dala’il, on the authority of Ibn Ayyash Al-Zazqi, who said: We were with the Messenger of God in Usfan, and the polytheists, led by Khalid ibn Al-Walid, faced us, and they were between us and the qiblah. The Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, led us in the noon prayer. They said: They were in a state, if only we could catch them by surprise. Then they said: Now a prayer will come upon them that is dearer to them than their children and themselves. So Gabriel came down with these verses between the noon and afternoon prayers: And if you are among them, then lead them in prayer. The hadith. Al-Tirmidhi narrated something similar on the authority of Abu Hurayrah and Ibn Jarir, and something similar on the authority of Jabir ibn Abdullah and Ibn Abbas.
4:102: As for the Almighty’s saying: There is no blame upon you. Al-Bukhari narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: If you are harmed by rain or you are ill, it was revealed about Abd al-Rahman ibn Awf, who was wounded.
4:105: The Almighty said: Indeed, We have sent down the verse. Al-Tirmidhi, Al-Hakim and others narrated on the authority of Qatada Ibn Al-Nu'man, who said: There were a family of ours called Banu Ubayraq, Bishr, Bashir and Mubashir. Bashir was a hypocrite who used to recite poetry satirizing the companions of the Messenger of God, then some Arabs would attribute it to him, saying: So-and-so said such-and-such. They were a family of need and poverty in the pre-Islamic period and Islam. The people's only food in Medina was dates and barley. My uncle Rifa'ah Ibn Zayd bought a load of dirhams and put it in his drinking place, in which he had weapons, armor and a sword. Then he was attacked from underneath, so the drinking place was dug up and the food and weapons were taken. When morning came, my uncle Rifa'ah came to me and said: O son of my brother, we were attacked last night. So our drinking place was dug up and our food and weapons were taken. We spied in the house and asked, and we were told: We saw Banu Ubayraq lighting a fire this night, and we did not see from what we saw except some of your food. Banu Ubayraq said: We were asking in the house, and by God, we did not see your companion. Except for Labid bin Sahl, a man from among us who is pious and Muslim. When Labid heard this, he drew his sword and said, “I steal. By God, this sword will mix with you, or you will expose this theft.” They said, Leave us alone, man, for you are not the one who does it. So we asked in the house until we had no doubt that they were our companions. Then my uncle said to me, “O son of my brother, if you go to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and mention that to him.” So I went to him and said, “A family from among us are rude people who went to my uncle and dug into his drinking place and took his weapons and food. Let them return our weapons to us, but as for the food, we have no need for it.” The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, said, I will look into that. When Banu Ubayraq heard this, they went to a man from among them called Aseer bin Urwah and spoke to him about that. Some people from the house gathered and said, O Messenger of God, Qatada bin An-Nu’man and his uncle went to a family from among us who are pious and Muslim, accusing them of theft without proof or evidence. Qatada said, “So I went to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and he said, “You went to a family from among whom Islam and righteousness are mentioned, and you accuse them of theft without proof or evidence.” So I went back and told my uncle, and he said: God is the one to be sought for help. And it was not long before the Qur’an was revealed: Indeed, We have sent down to you the Book. In truth, judge between people by what God has shown you, and do not be an advocate for the traitors, O Banu Abiraq. And ask forgiveness of God, that is, of what you said to Qatada until His saying, great. So when the Qur’an was revealed, the Messenger of God, peace and blessings be upon him, came with the weapon and returned it to Rifa’ah, and Bashir joined the polytheists, so he descended upon Salafah bint Sa’d, so God revealed, And whoever contradicts the Messenger after it has become clear to him - He will guide him far astray. Al-Hakim said, It is authentic according to the conditions of Muslim. Ibn Saad narrated in Al-Tabaqat on the authority of Mahmoud bin Labid who said: Bashir bin Al-Harith attacked the upper room of Rafa’ah bin Zaid, the uncle of Qatada bin Al-Nu’man, and dug into its back and took food for himself and two armors with their tools. Qatada went to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and informed him of that. He called Bashir and asked him, but he denied it and accused Labid bin Sahl, a man of noble lineage and lineage, of that. The Qur’an was revealed denying Bashir and disowning Labid, Indeed, We have sent down to you the Book in truth so that you may judge between the people. verses. When the Qur’an was revealed about Bashir and he was found out, he fled to Mecca as an apostate. He went to Salamah bint Saad and began to speak ill of the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and the Muslims. The verse And whoever contradicts the Messenger was revealed about him and Hassan bin Thabit satirized him until he returned. That was in the month of Rabi’ in the year four of the Hijra.
4:123: God Almighty said: It is not according to your desires, the verse. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: The Jews and Christians said: No one will enter Paradise except us, and the Quraysh said: We will not be resurrected. So God revealed: It is not according to your desires nor the desires of the People of the Book. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Masruq who said: The Christians and the Muslims boasted. These said: We are better than you. And these said: We are better than you. So God revealed: It is not according to your desires nor the desires of the People of the Book. And he narrated something similar to it on the authority of Qatada, Al-Dahhak, Al-Suddi, and Abu Salih, and their wording is: We boast to the people of religions. And in another wording: Some Jews, some Christians, and some Muslims sat together, and these said: We are better. So it was revealed. He also narrated on the authority of Masruq, who said: When the verse Not according to your desires nor the desires of the People of the Book was revealed, the People of the Book said: We and you are equal. So this verse was revealed: And whoever does righteous deeds, whether male or female, while he is a believer.
4:127: The Almighty said: And they ask you concerning women, the verse. Al-Bukhari narrated on the authority of Aisha regarding this verse. She said: It is the man who has an orphan girl with him and he is her guardian and heir, and he has shared her wealth, even in the amount of time, so he desires to marry her and he hates to marry her to a man who shares her wealth with him and he oppresses her. So it was revealed. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Al-Suddi that Jabir had an ugly cousin who had money that she had inherited from her father. Jabir was reluctant to marry her and would not marry her for fear that the husband would take her money. So he asked the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, about that, and this verse was revealed.
4:128: The Almighty said: And if a woman fears desertion from her husband, the verse. Abu Dawud and Al-Hakim narrated on the authority of Aisha that Sawdah feared that the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, would leave her when she grew old, so she said to Aisha: My day is upon you. Then God revealed: And if a woman fears desertion from her husband, the verse. Al-Tirmidhi narrated something similar on the authority of Ibn Abbas. K and Saeed bin Mansour narrated on the authority of Saeed bin Al-Musayyab that the daughter of Muhammad bin Maslama was with Rafi bin Khadij and he disliked something from her, either because of her arrogance or something else, so he wanted to divorce her, but she said, Do not divorce me and swear to me whatever that may be. So God revealed, And if a woman fears the verse, and it has a connected witness that Al-Hakim narrated on the authority of Ibn Al-Musayyab on the authority of Rafi bin Khadij. Al-Hakim narrated on the authority of Aisha that he said: This verse was revealed, And reconciliation is better, concerning a man who had a wife who had given birth to children for him, and he wanted to exchange his dowry for her, on the condition that she would remain with him and he would not divide it for her. K and Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Saeed bin Jubair who said: A woman came when this verse was revealed: And a woman feared from her husband disobedience or disdain. She said: I want you to share for me from your expenses, and she was satisfied that he would leave her and not divorce her or have intercourse with her. So God revealed: And the souls are afflicted with greed.
4:135: The Almighty said: O you who have believed, be persistently standing firm in justice. The verse: Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Al-Suddi who said: When this verse was revealed about the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, two men, a rich man and a poor man, disputed before him. The Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, was with the poor man, thinking that the poor man would not wrong the rich man. But God refused except that he should be just in the case of the rich and the poor.
4:148: God Almighty says: God does not like the public mention of evil. Hanad bin Al-Sarri narrated in the Book of Asceticism on the authority of Mujahid who said: God does not like the public mention of evil except by someone who has been wronged. A man hosted a man in Madinah and treated him badly. He turned away from him and began to praise him for what he had done. So he permitted him to praise him for what he had done.
4:153: The Almighty said: The People of the Book ask you, verse Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Muhammad ibn Ka'b al-Qurazi who said: Some Jews came to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and said: Moses came to us with the tablets from God, so bring us the tablets so that we may believe you. So God revealed: The People of the Book ask you, until His saying: a great slander. Then a man from the Jews knelt down and said: God did not reveal anything to you, nor to Moses, nor to Jesus, nor to anyone. So God revealed: And they did not value God with His true value, verse.
4:163: As for the Almighty’s saying, Indeed, We have revealed to you. Ibn Ishaq narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said, Adi bin Zaid said, ‘We do not know that God has revealed anything to any human being after Moses.’ So God revealed the verse…
4:166: The Almighty said: But God bears witness to the verse. Ibn Ishaq narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas that a group of Jews entered upon the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and he said to them: By God, I know that you know that I am the Messenger of God. They said: We do not know that. So God revealed: But God bears witness to the verse.
4:176: The Almighty said: They ask you for a legal opinion. Say: God gives you a legal opinion concerning those who are not related to the verse. An-Nasa’i narrated on the authority of Abu al-Zubayr on the authority of Jabir who said: I fell ill and the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, came to me. I said: O Messenger of God, should I leave a third for my sisters? He said: That is good. I said: Half. He said: That is good. Then he left and came to me. He said: I do not see you dying in this pain of yours. God has revealed or clarified what is for your sisters, which is two-thirds. So Jabir used to say: This verse was revealed: They ask you for a legal opinion. Say: God gives you a legal opinion concerning those who are not related to the verse. Al-Hafiz Ibn Hajar said: This is another story of Jabir other than the one mentioned at the beginning of the surah. K and Ibn Mardawayh narrated on the authority of Omar that he asked the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, how the descendants of the kalalah inherit, so God revealed: They ask you for a legal opinion. Say: God gives you a legal opinion concerning the kalalah, to the end of it. Warning: If you reflect on what we have mentioned about the reasons for the revelation of the verses of this Surah, you will know the response to those who say that it is Meccan.
5:2: God Almighty said: Do not violate the symbols of God. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Ikrimah, who said: Al-Hatim bin Hind Al-Bakri came to Medina with a caravan of his carrying food, so he sold it. Then he entered upon the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and pledged allegiance to him and converted to Islam. When he turned away, he looked at him and said to those with him: He has entered upon me with an immoral face and has turned his back treacherously. When he arrived at Yamamah, he apostatized from Islam and went out with a caravan of his carrying food in Dhul-Qa'dah, heading to Mecca. When the companions of the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, heard of him, a group of the Muhajireen and Ansar prepared to go out to him to cut off his caravan. Then God revealed: O you who believe, do not violate the symbols of God, the verse. The people stopped. And something similar was narrated on the authority of Al-Suddi. God Almighty said: And let not the verse prevent you from being guided. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Zaid bin Aslam, who said: The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, was at Al-Hudaibiyah with his companions when they were repelled. The polytheists were prevented from entering the House, and this was difficult for them. Then some polytheists from the East passed by them wanting to perform Umrah. The companions of the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, said: We will turn these people away as they turned our companions away. Then God revealed: And let not the verse be forbidden to you.
5:3: God Almighty has forbidden you dead meat, the verse. Ibn Mandah included in the Book of the Companions, on the authority of Abdullah bin Jabalah bin Habban bin Hajar, on the authority of his father, on the authority of his grandfather Habban, who said: We were with the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and I was lighting a fire under a pot in which there was the meat of a dead animal, and the prohibition of dead meat was revealed, so I turned the pot over.
5:4: The Almighty said: They ask you what is lawful for them, the verse. Al-Tabarani, Al-Hakim, Al-Bayhaqi and others narrated on the authority of Abu Rafi’ that Gabriel came to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and asked permission to enter, so he gave him permission, but he was slow, so he took his cloak and went out to him while he was standing at the door and said: You have been given permission. He said: Yes, but we do not enter a house in which there is a picture or a dog. They looked and saw a puppy in one of their houses. Abu Rafi’ ordered that no dog be left in the city without killing it. Some people came to him and said: O Messenger of God, what is lawful for us from this nation that you were ordered to kill? So they revealed: They ask you what is lawful for them. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Ikrimah that the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, sent Abu Rafi’ to kill dogs until he reached Al-Awali. Then Asim bin Adi, Saad bin Hathmah and Uwaimir bin Sa’idah entered and said: What is lawful for us, O Messenger of God? So they revealed: They ask you what is lawful for them, the verse. And Muhammad bin Ka’b Al-Qurazi narrated that when the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, ordered the killing of dogs, they said: O Messenger of God, what is lawful for us from these? The nation came down And he narrated through the path of Al-Sha’bi that Adi bin Hatim Al-Ta’i said: A man came to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, asking him about hunting with dogs, and he did not know what to say to him until this verse was revealed: You teach them from what God has made for you. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Saeed bin Jubair that Adi bin Hatim and Zaid bin Muhalhil al-Ta’i asked the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and said: O Messenger of God, we are a people who hunt with dogs and falcons, and the dogs of the family of Dharih hunt cows, donkeys, and gazelles, and God has forbidden dead animals, so what is permissible for us from them? Then the verse was revealed: They ask you, what is permissible for them? Say: Lawful for you are good things.
5:6: The Almighty said: O you who believe, when you rise to prayer, the verse. Al-Bukhari narrated on the authority of Amr bin Al-Harith on the authority of Abd Al-Rahman bin Al-Qasim on the authority of his father on the authority of Aisha, she said: My necklace fell in Al-Baida while we were entering Madinah, so the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, dismounted and bent his head in my lap, sleeping, and Abu Bakr came and gave me a hard shove and said: You have detained the people with a necklace. Then the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, woke up and the dawn came, so he looked for water but could not find it, so the verse was revealed: O you who believe, when you rise to prayer until His saying that you may be grateful. Then Asid bin Hudayr said: God has certainly blessed the people in you, O family of Abu Bakr. Al-Tabarani narrated on the authority of Ibad bin Abdullah bin Al-Zubayr on the authority of Aisha, she said: When my necklace happened as it was and the people of slander said what they said, I went out with the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, on another expedition, and my necklace also fell off until the people were detained in their search for it. Abu Bakr said to me: My daughter, in every journey you are a burden and a trial for the people. So God revealed the permission for dry ablution, and Abu Bakr you are blessed Two notes: The first is that Al-Bukhari narrated this hadith from the narration of Amr ibn Al-Harith, and it clearly states that the verse of tayammum mentioned in another narration is the verse of Al-Ma’idah, and most of the narrators said that the verse of tayammum was revealed, but they did not clarify it. Ibn Abd Al-Barr said: This is a dilemma for which I have not found a cure, because we do not know which of the two verses Aisha meant. Ibn Battaal said: It is the verse of women, and his reasoning is that the verse of Al-Ma’idah is called the verse of ablution, and the verse of women does not mention ablution in it, so it is appropriate to specify it with the verse of tayammum. Al-Wahidi included this hadith in the reasons for revelation when mentioning the verse of women as well. There is no doubt that what Al-Bukhari leaned towards, that it is the verse of Al-Ma’idah, is correct, because it is clearly stated in the aforementioned chain of transmission. The second: The hadith indicates that ablution was obligatory for them before the revelation of the verse, and for this reason they considered it great that they had come without water, and what happened from Abu Bakr regarding Aisha happened. Ibn Abd al-Barr said: It is known to all the people of the battles that the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, did not pray since prayer was made obligatory for him except with ablution, and no one can deny that except a denier or an obstinate person. He said: The wisdom in the revelation of the verse on ablution is what was previously acted upon, so that its obligation would be followed by the revelation. And another said: It is possible that the beginning of the verse was revealed in advance with the obligation of ablution, then the rest of it was revealed, which is the mention of tayammum in this story. I said: The first is more correct, because the obligation of ablution was with the obligation of prayer in Mecca, and the verse is Medinan.
5:8: The Almighty said: O you who believe, remember the favor of God upon you. The verse Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Ikrimah and Yazid bin Abi Ziyad, and the wording is his, that the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, went out with Abu Bakr, Umar, Uthman, Ali, Talhah, and Abd al-Rahman bin Awf until they entered upon Ka’b bin al-Ashraf and the Jews of Banu al-Nadir, seeking their help in a blood money he had lost. They said: Yes, sit down until we feed you and give you what you ask of us. So he sat down, and Huyayy bin Akhtab said to his companions: You do not see him closer than he is now. Throw stones at him and kill him, and you will never see any evil. So they came to a huge millstone to throw them at him, but God held back their hands from it until Gabriel came to him and made him stand up from there. Then God revealed: O you who believe, remember the favor of God upon you when they were a people. The verse was narrated in a similar manner on the authority of Abdullah bin Abi Bakr, Asim bin Umair bin Qatadah, Mujahid, Abdullah bin Kathir, and Abu Malik. And he narrated on the authority of Qatada who said: It was mentioned to us that this verse was revealed to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, while he was in Batn Nakhl during the seventh battle. Then the Banu Tha’laba and the Banu Muharrib wanted to kill the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, so they sent to him the Bedouin, meaning the one who came to him while he was sleeping in one of the houses, and he took his weapon and said: Who will stand between me and you? God said: So he took the sword and did not punish him. Abu Naim narrated in Dala’il al-Nubuwwah on the authority of al-Hasan on the authority of Jabir ibn Abdullah that a man from Muharib called Ghawrath ibn al-Harith said to his al-Nas 114:Shall I kill Muhammad for you? So he went to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, while he was sitting with his sword in his lap and said: O Muhammad, look at this sword of yours. He said: Yes. So he took it and drew it and began to shake it and intend to do so, but God Almighty suppressed him. So he said: O Muhammad, do you not fear me? He said: No. He said: Do you not fear me while the sword is in my hand? He said: God will not protect me from you. Then he sheathed the sword and returned it to the Messenger of God, and God revealed the verse.
5:11: God Almighty said: O People of the Book, Our Messenger has come to you, the verse. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Ikrimah who said: The Jews came to the Prophet of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, asking him about stoning. He said: Which of you is more knowledgeable? They pointed to Ibn Suriya. He implored him by the One who sent down the Torah to Moses, and the One who raised the mountain, and the covenants that were taken from them until Afkal took hold of him. He said: When it increased among us, we flogged a hundred and shaved our heads. So he ruled that they should be stoned. Then God revealed: O People of the Book, until His saying: A straight path.
5:18: God Almighty said: And the Jews said, The verses. Ibn Ishaq narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas that Nu’man ibn Qusayy, Bahr ibn Umar, and Shash ibn Adi came to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and they spoke to him. He spoke to them and called them to God and warned them of His vengeance. They said: What do you fear, O Muhammad? By God, we are the sons of God and His beloved, as the Christians say. So God revealed about them: And the Jews and Christians said, the verse. It was narrated on his authority that the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, called the Jews to Islam and encouraged them to do so, but they refused. So Muadh ibn Jabal and Sa`d ibn `Ubadah said to them, “O people of the Scripture, fear God, for by God you know that he is the Messenger of God. You used to mention him to us before his mission and describe him to us in his description.” Then Rafi` ibn Huraymila and Wahb ibn Yahudah said, We did not tell you this, and God did not send down a book after Moses, nor did He send a bearer of good tidings or a warner after him. So God revealed, O people of the Scripture, Our Messenger has come to you explaining to you the verse.
5:33: The Almighty said: The only reward of those who wage war against God and His Messenger is that they will be killed or their camels destroyed. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Yazid ibn Abi Habib that Abd al-Malik ibn Marwan wrote to Anas asking him about this verse: The only reward of those who wage war against God and His Messenger. Anas wrote to him informing him that this verse was revealed about the two Arabs who apostatized from Islam, killed the shepherd, and drove away the camels. Then he narrated something similar on the authority of Jarir, and Abd al-Razzaq narrated something similar on the authority of Abu Hurayrah.
5:38: The Almighty said: And the male thief, male thief, verse K. Ahmad and others narrated on the authority of Abdullah bin Amr that a woman stole during the time of the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and her right hand was cut off. She said: Is there any repentance for me, O Messenger of God? So God revealed in Surat al-Ma’idah 5:But whoever repents after his wrongdoing and reforms the verse.
5:41: The Almighty said: O Messenger, the verse K. Ahmad and Abu Dawud narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: God revealed it regarding two groups of Jews, one of which had subjugated the other in the pre-Islamic era until they agreed and agreed that for every person killed by a powerful woman from a humiliated woman, the ransom would be fifty wasqs, and for every person killed by a humiliated woman from a powerful woman, the ransom would be one hundred wasqs. They remained like that until the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, came. The humiliated woman from the powerful woman killed someone, so the powerful woman sent word that they should send us one hundred wasqs. The humiliated woman said: Has that ever happened in two tribes whose religion was the same, whose lineage was the same, and whose country was the same? The blood money of one of them was half the blood money of the other. We gave you this out of oppression from you, fear, and fear. But now that Muhammad has come, we will not give you anything. So war was about to break out between them. Then they agreed to make the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, between them. They sent to him some of the hypocrites to test his opinion, so God revealed: O Messenger, let not those who grieve you. They hasten into disbelief Ahmad, Muslim and others narrated on the authority of Al-Bara’ bin Azib who said: The Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, passed by a Jew who had been flogged and had been bathed. He called them and said: This is how you will find the punishment for adultery in your book. They said: Yes. So he called one of their scholars and said: I ask you by God. He who sent down the Torah to Moses, thus you will find the punishment for adultery in your book. He said, No, by God, if you had not asked me about this, I would not have told you. We find the punishment for adultery in our book as stoning, but it has become widespread among our nobles. So if a noble committed adultery, we would leave him, and if a weak person committed adultery, we would carry out the punishment on him. So we said, Come, let us make something that we can carry out on the noble and the lowly. So we agreed on stoning and flogging. So the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, said, O God, I am the first to revive Your command when they killed him. So he ordered him to be stoned. Then God revealed, O Messenger, let not those who hasten into disbelief grieve you until His saying, If you are given this, then take it. They say, Go to Muhammad, and if he gives you a fatwa regarding stoning and flogging, then take it. But if he gives you a fatwa regarding stoning, then beware until His saying, And whoever does not judge by what God has revealed - then it is those who are the wrongdoers. K and Al-Hamidi narrated in his Musnad on the authority of Jabir bin Abdullah who said: A man from the people of Fadak committed adultery. So the people of Fadak wrote to the people of Medina to ask Muhammad about that. If he orders flogging, then take it from him. But if he orders stoning, then do not take it from him. So they asked him about that and he mentioned something similar to what was mentioned above. So he ordered him to be stoned. Then the verse was revealed: So if they come to you, judge between them. Al-Bayhaqi narrated in Al-Dala’il something similar from the hadith of Abu Hurayrah.
5:49: God Almighty said: And judge between them by what God has revealed. Ibn Ishaq narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas that Ka`b ibn Asid, Abdullah ibn Suriya, and Shas ibn Qays said: Let us go to Muhammad so that we may tempt him away from his religion. So they came and said: O Muhammad, you know that we are the rabbis of the Jews, their nobles and their leaders, and that if we follow you, the Jews will follow us and they will not oppose us, and that there is a dispute between us and our people, so we will bring them to you for judgment, so that you may judge for us against them and we will believe in you. But he refused that, and God revealed about them: And judge between them by what God has revealed, until His saying: For a people who are certain.
5:51: The Almighty said: O you who believe, do not take the verse. Ibn Ishaq, Ibn Jarir, Ibn Abi Hatim, and Al-Bayhaqi narrated on the authority of Ubadah bin Al-Samit, who said: When Banu Qaynuqa fought, Abdullah bin Ubayy bin Salul held on to their matter and stood up for them. Ubadah bin Al-Samit walked to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and disavowed to God and His Messenger their alliance. He was one of Banu Awf bin Al-Khazraj and had the same alliance as Abdullah bin Ubayy, so he allied with them to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and disavowed the alliance of the infidels and their allegiance. He said: It was about him and Abdullah bin Ubayy that the story was revealed in al-Ma’idah 5:O you who believe, do not take the Jews and the Christians as allies. The verse
5:55: The Almighty’s saying: Your guardian is only God verse. Al-Tabarani included in Al-Awsat with a chain of transmission that includes unknown narrators on the authority of Ammar ibn Yasir who said: A beggar stopped Ali ibn Abi Talib while he was bowing in a voluntary prayer, so he took off his ring and gave it to the beggar, so the verse: Your guardian is only God and His Messenger verse was revealed. It has a corroborating evidence. Abd al-Razzaq said: Abd al-Wahhab ibn Mujahid told us on the authority of his father on the authority of Ibn Abbas: Your guardian is only God and His Messenger verse. He said: It was revealed about Ali ibn Abi Talib. Ibn Mardawayh narrated from another source on the authority of Ibn Abbas something similar, and he also included something similar on the authority of Ali. Ibn Jarir included something similar on the authority of Mujahid and Ibn Abi Hatim on the authority of Salamah ibn Kuhayl. These are corroborating evidence that strengthen each other.
5:57: The Almighty said: O you who believe, do not take those who take your religion as your companions, the verse. Abu Sheikh and Ibn Hibban narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: Rafa’ah bin Zaid bin Al-Tabut and Suwaid bin Al-Harith had outwardly declared Islam and were hypocrites, and there was a man from the Muslims who loved them, so God revealed: O you who believe, do not take those who take your religion as your companions, until His saying: What they used to conceal. And he said: A group of Jews came to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, among them Abu Yasir bin Akhtab, Nafi’ bin Abi Nafi’, and Ghazi bin Umar, and they asked him about the messengers who believe in him. He said: I believe in God and in what was revealed to Abraham, Ishmael, Isaac, Jacob, and the Tribes, and in what was given to Moses and Jesus, and in what was given to the prophets from their Lord. We make no distinction between any of them, and we are Muslims [in submission] to Him. The verse. So when Jesus was mentioned, they denied his prophethood and said: We do not believe in Jesus or in those who believe in him, so God revealed about them: Say: O People of the Scripture, do you blame us? The verse.
5:64: The Almighty said: And the Jews said, the verse. Al-Tabarani narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: A Jewish man said to him: Al-Nabash bin Qais: Your Lord is stingy and does not spend. So God revealed: And the Jews said: The hand of God is chained. Abu Al-Sheikh narrated from another source on his authority: It was revealed: And the Jews said: The hand of God is chained. In Phinehas, the chief of the Jews of Qaynuqa.
5:67: God Almighty said, O Messenger, announce the verse. Abu Al-Sheikh narrated on the authority of Al-Hasan that the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, said, God sent me with a message and I was fed up with it and I knew that people would deny me, so He promised me that I would announce it or He would punish me, so He revealed, O Messenger, announce what has been revealed to you from your Lord. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Mujahid who said: When the verse, O Messenger, announce that which has been revealed to you from your Lord, was revealed, he said, O Lord, what can I do when I am alone and they will gather against me? So the verse was revealed, If you do not do it, then you have not conveyed His message. Al-Hakim and Al-Tirmidhi narrated on the authority of Aisha that she said: The Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, was on guard until this verse was revealed: And God will protect you from the people. Then he stuck his head out of the dome and said: O people, go away, for God has protected me. This hadith is evidence that it, meaning the verse, was revealed at night, while the Messenger was in his bed. Al-Tabarani narrated on the authority of Abu Saeed Al-Khudri, who said: Al-Abbas, the uncle of the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, was among those who guarded him. When the verse And God will protect you from the people was revealed, he left the guards. K also narrated on the authority of Asma bin Malik Al-Khatmi, who said: We were guarding the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, at night until the verse And God will protect you from the people was revealed, so he left the guards. K And Ibn Hibban included in his Sahih on the authority of Abu Hurairah, who said: Whenever we woke up and the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, was on a journey, we would leave for him the largest and most shady tree, and he would alight. Under it, one day he went down under the tree and hung his sword in it. A man came and took it and said, O Muhammad, who can protect you from me? The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, said, “God protects me from you. Put down the sword.” So he put it down and it came down. God will protect you from the people. And Ibn Abi Hatim and Ibn Mardawayh narrated on the authority of Jabir ibn Abdullah who said: When the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, invaded Banu Anmar, he alighted at Dhat al-Raqee’ at the top of a palm grove. While he was sitting at the head of a well with his feet hanging down, al-Warith from Banu al-Najjar said: I will kill Muhammad. His companions said to him: How will you kill him? He said: I will tell him: Give me your sword. When he gives it to me, I will kill him. So he came to him and said to him: O Muhammad, give me your sword. I smell it. So he gave it to him, but his hand trembled. The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, said: God has come between you and what you want. So God revealed: O Messenger, deliver the verse. Among the strange things that were reported about the reason for its revelation is what Ibn Mardawayh and Al-Tabarani reported on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: The Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, was guarded, and Abu Talib used to send with him every day men from Banu Hashim to guard him until this verse was revealed: And God will protect you from the people. So he wanted to send with him someone to guard him, so he said: O uncle, God has protected me from the jinn and mankind. Ibn Mardawayh reported something similar on the authority of Jabir ibn Abdullah, and this requires that the verse is Meccan, but the apparent opposite is true.
5:68: As for the Almighty’s saying, Say, O People of the Scripture, the verse. Ibn Jarir and Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas that Rafi’, Salam bin Mishkam, and Malik bin Al-Saif came and said, O Muhammad, do you not claim that you are on the religion and faith of Abraham and believe in what we have? He said, Yes, but you have innovated and denied what is in it and concealed what you were commanded to make clear to the people. They said, Then we take what is in our hands, for we are on guidance and truth. Then God revealed, Say, O People of the Scripture, you are on nothing, the verse.
5:82: The Almighty said: And you will find the nearest of them in affection to the verse. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Saeed bin Al-Musayyab, Abu Bakr bin Abdul Rahman, and Urwah bin Al-Zubayr, who said: He sent... The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, sent Amr ibn Umayya al-Damri and wrote a letter with him to the Negus. He went to the Negus and read the letter of the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace. Then he called Ja`far ibn Abi Talib and the immigrants with him and sent to the monks and priests. Then he ordered Ja`far ibn Abi Talib to read to them Surat Maryam. They believed in the Qur’an and their eyes overflowed with tears. They are those about whom God revealed the Qur’an. We will find the closest of them in love to His saying: So write us down with the witnesses. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Saeed bin Jubair, who said: The Negus sent thirty of his best companions to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and he recited to them Surat Yasin, so they cried, and the verse was revealed about them. Al-Nasa’i narrated on the authority of Abdullah bin Al-Zubayr, who said: This verse was revealed about the Negus and his companions. When they heard what was revealed to the Messenger, you would see their eyes overflowing with tears. Al-Tabarani narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas something similar, but more simply.
5:87: The Almighty said: O you who believe, do not forbid the verse. Al-Tirmidhi and others narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas that a man came to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and said: O Messenger of God, when I eat meat, I become attracted to women and my desire takes hold of me, so you forbade me meat. Then God revealed: O you who believe, do not forbid the good things which God has made lawful for you, the verse. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Al-Awfi that Abbas ibn men from the Companions, including Uthman ibn Mazun, forbade themselves from women and meat and took razors to cut their genitals so that desire would cease from them and they could devote themselves to worship, so this verse was revealed. And he brought out something similar to it from the mursal of Ikrimah, Abu Qilabah, Mujahid, Abu Malik, Al-Nakha’i, Al-Suddi and others. In Al-Suddi’s narration, they were ten, including Ibn Maz’un and Ali Ibn Abi Talib. In Ikrimah’s narration, they included Ibn Maz’un. And Ali, Ibn Masoud, Al-Miqdad bin Al-Aswad, and Salim, the freed slave of Abu Hudhayfah, and in Mujahid’s narration, among them are Ibn Maz’un and Abdullah bin Omar. Ibn Asakir narrated in his history, on the authority of Al-Suddi Al-Sagheer, on the authority of Al-Kalbi, that Abu Salih, on the authority of Ibn Abbas, said: This verse was revealed about a group of the Companions, including Bakr, Umar, Ali, Ibn Masoud, Uthman bin Mazun, Al-Miqdad bin Al-Aswad, and Salim, the freed slave of Abu Hudhayfah. They agreed to be generous to themselves, to stay away from women, not to eat meat or fat, to wear hairy clothes, not to eat food except what was sufficient, and to wander the earth like monks. So it was revealed. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Zaid bin Aslam that Abdullah bin Rawahah was hosted by a guest from his family while he was with the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace. Then he returned to his family and found that they had not fed his guest, waiting for him. So he said to his wife, “You have detained my guest for my sake. He is forbidden to me.” His wife said, He is forbidden to me. The guest said, He is forbidden to me. When he saw that, he put his hand on it and said, Eat in the name of God. Then he went to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and mentioned what had happened to them. Then God revealed, O you who have believed, do not prohibit the good things which God has made lawful to you.
5:90: The Almighty said: O you who believe, indeed, intoxicants and gambling are only verses. Ahmad narrated on the authority of Abu Hurairah who said: The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, came to Medina while they were drinking intoxicants and gambling. They asked the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, about them, so God revealed: They ask you about intoxicants and gambling, the verse. The people said: What has been forbidden to us? He only said: A great sin. They used to drink intoxicants until one day a man from the immigrants led his companions in the Maghrib prayer and mixed up his recitation. So God revealed a verse more severe than that: O you who believe, do not approach prayer while you are intoxicated until you know what you are saying. Then a verse more severe than that was revealed: O you who believe, indeed, intoxicants and gambling are only verses until His saying: Will you then desist? They said: We have desisted, our Lord. The people said: O Messenger of God, they were killed in the way of God and died in their beds. They used to drink intoxicants and gamble, and God has made it an abomination from the work of Satan. So God revealed: There is no blame upon those who believe. And they do righteous deeds, there is no blame on them for what they have eaten, until the end of the verse. Al-Nasa’i and Al-Bayhaqi narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: The prohibition of alcohol was revealed regarding two tribes of the Ansar who drank. When the people became drunk, they played with each other. When they sobered up, a man would see the effect on his face, head and beard, and he would say: My brother so-and-so did this to me. They were brothers with no grudge in their hearts. He would say: By God, if he had been kind and merciful to me, he would not have done this to me. Until grudges fell into their hearts. Then God revealed this verse: O you who have believed, indeed, wine and gambling are but [only] the verse. Some of those who were responsible said: It is an abomination, and it is in the stomach of so-and-so, who was killed on the Day of Uhud. Then God revealed: There is no blame on those who believe and do righteous deeds, except the verse.
5:100: The Almighty said: Say: Not equal are the verse. Al-Wahidi and Al-Asbahani narrated in Al-Targhīb on the authority of Jabir that the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, mentioned the prohibition of alcohol, so an Arab stood up and said: I was a man whose trade was this, and I made money from it. Will that money benefit me by obeying God Almighty? The Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, said: God does not accept anything but that which is good. So God Almighty revealed, confirming what His Messenger, may God bless him and grant him peace, said: Say: Not equal are the bad and the good. The verse
5:101: The Almighty said: O you who have believed, do not ask about the verse K Al-Bukhari narrated on the authority of Anas bin Malik, the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, gave a sermon and a man from Abi said: So-and-so, so this verse was revealed: Do not ask about things, the verse. It was also narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: There were people who asked the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, mockingly, so a man would say: Who is Abi? And a man would say: His she-camel has gone astray, where is my she-camel? So God revealed this verse about them: O you who have believed, do not ask about things until he finished the entire verse. Ibn Jarir narrated something similar from the hadith of Abu Hurairah. Ahmad, Al-Tirmidhi and Al-Hakim narrated on the authority of Ali, who said: When the verse And by God, the people have been commanded to perform Hajj to the House was revealed, they said: O Messenger of God, every year. He remained silent. They said: O Messenger of God, every year. He said: No. If I had said yes, it would have been obligatory. Then God revealed: Do not ask about things that, if they are made clear to you, will distress you. Ibn Jarir narrated something similar from the hadith of Abu Hurairah, Abu Umamah, and Ibn Abbas. Al-Hafiz Ibn Hajar said: There is nothing preventing it from being revealed regarding both matters, and the hadith of Ibn Abbas regarding that has a more authentic chain of transmission.
5:106: The Almighty said: O you who believe! A testimony between you, the verse. Al-Tirmidhi narrated, and he declared it weak, and others on the authority of Ibn Abbas on the authority of Tamim al-Dari regarding this verse: O you who believe! A testimony between you, when death approaches one of you. He said: People are innocent of it except me and Adi bin Bada. They were Christians who used to go to Ash-Sham before Islam. They came to Ash-Sham for their trade, and a client of Banu Sahm called Badil bin Abi Maryam came to them for trade, and with him was a silver cup. He fell ill, so he made a will to them and ordered them to deliver what his family had left. Tamim said: When he died, we took that cup and sold it for a thousand dirhams, then Adi bin Bada and I divided it up. When we returned to his family, we gave them what we had with us, and they lost the cup. They asked us about it, and we said: He did not leave anything but this, and he did not give us anything else. When I converted to Islam, I felt guilty about that, so I went to his family and told them the report, and I gave them five hundred dirhams and told them that my companion had the same amount. They brought it to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and he asked them. The evidence was not found, so he ordered them to swear him an oath, so he swore. Then God revealed: O you who believe! Testimony between you until His saying: That oaths be returned after their oaths. Then Amr ibn al-Aas and another man stood up and swore an oath, so the five hundred dirhams were taken from Adi ibn Bada’. Alert: Al-Dhahabi asserted that the Tamim who revealed it is not Tamim al-Dari, and he attributed it to Muqatil Ibn Hibban. Al-Hafiz Ibn Hajar said: It is not good, because it is stated explicitly in this hadith that he is al-Dari.
5:120: The Almighty said: Say, What thing is greater in testimony? Ibn Ishaq and Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Saeed or Ikrimah on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: An-Nahham bin Zaid, Qurum bin Kaab, and Bahri bin Amr came and said: O Muhammad, we do not know of any god with God other than Him. He said: There is no god but God. I was sent with this and to this I call. Then God revealed regarding their saying: Say, What thing is greater in testimony? The verse. The Almighty said: And they are humiliated by it and keep away from it. The verse. Al-Hakim and others narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas that this verse was revealed about Abu Talib. He used to forbid the polytheists from harming the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and he kept away from what he brought. K and Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Saeed bin Abi Hilal who said: It was revealed about the paternal uncles of the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and they were ten. They were the most severe people with him in public and the most severe people with him in private. God Almighty said: We know that it grieves you, the verse. Al-Tirmidhi and Al-Hakim narrated on the authority of Ali that Abu Jahl said to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace: We do not belie you, but we belie what you have brought. So God revealed: They do not belie you, but the wrongdoers deny the verses of God. The Almighty said: And do not drive away those who call upon their Lord until His saying: Is not God most knowing of those who are grateful? Ahmad, Al-Tabarani and Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Ibn Masoud who said: The leaders of Quraysh passed by the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and with him were Khabbab bin Al-Arat, Suhaib, Bilal and Ammar. They said: O Muhammad, are you satisfied with these people and those whom God has favored from among us? If you had expelled these people, we would have followed you. So God revealed the Qur’an about them and warned with it those who fear that they will be gathered together until His saying: The way of the criminals. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Ikrimah who said: Utaybah ibn Rabi’ah, Shaiba ibn Rabi’ah, Mut’am ibn ‘Adi, and Al-Harith ibn Nawfal came with the nobles of Banu Abd Manaf from the people of disbelief to Abu Talib and said: If your nephew were to expel these slaves from him, he would be greater in our hearts and more obedient to us and more likely to follow him. So Abu Talib spoke to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and ‘Umar ibn Al-Khattab said: If you did that, then we will see what they want. So God revealed: And warn with it those who fear, until His saying: Is not God most knowing of the grateful? And they were Bilal, ‘Ammar ibn Yasir, Salim, the freed slave of Abu Hudhayfah, Salih, the freed slave of Asid, Ibn Mas’ud, Al-Miqdad ibn ‘Abdullah, Waqid ibn ‘Abdullah Al-Hanthali, and others like them. So ‘Umar came and apologized for his statement, so the verse: And when those who believe in Our verses come to you, the verse was revealed. Ibn Jarir, Ibn Abi Hatim and others narrated on the authority of Khabbab who said: Al-Aqra’ ibn Habis and Uyaynah ibn Hisn came and found the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, with Suhaib, Bilal, Ammar and Khabbab sitting with some weak believers. When they saw them around the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, they looked down on them, so they came to him and were alone with him and said: We want you to make a place for us where you can make known to the Arabs our superiority. The delegations of the Arabs come to you and we feel ashamed that the Arabs see us with these slaves. So when we come to you, make them leave us, and when we are done, sit with them if you wish. He said: Yes. So the verse was revealed: And do not drive away those who call upon their Lord, the verse. Then he mentioned Al-Aqra’ and his companion and said: And thus We test some of them by others. The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, used to sit with us, and when he wanted to get up, he would get up and leave us. Then the verse was revealed: And be patient with those who call upon their Lord, the verse. Ibn Kathir said: This is a strange hadith, for the verse is Meccan and Al-Aqra’ and Uyaynah only converted to Islam after the migration for a long time. Al-Faryabi and Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Mahan who said: People came to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and said: We have committed great sins. He did not respond to them with anything. Then God revealed: And when those who believe in Our verses come to you, the verse: As for the Almighty’s saying, Say, ‘He is the Able to send upon you a punishment from above you,’ Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Zayd ibn Aslam who said: When the verse, Say, ‘He is the Able to send upon you a punishment from above you,’ was revealed, the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, said, Do not return to disbelief after me, striking one another’s necks with swords. They said, And we bear witness that there is no god but God and that you are the Messenger of God. Some of the people said, This will never happen, that we should kill one another while we are Muslims. Then the verse, Look how We explain the verses, that they may understand, was revealed. But your people denied it, while it is the truth. Say, “I am not a guardian over you. For every matter there is a fixed term, and you will come to know.” As for the Almighty’s saying: Those who believed, the verse: Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Ubayd God bin Zahr on the authority of Bakr bin Sawada, who said: A man from the enemy charged at the Muslims and killed a man, then he charged and killed another, then he charged and killed another, then he said: Will Islam benefit me after this? The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, said: Yes. So he struck his horse and entered among them, then he charged at his companions and killed a man, then another, then another, then he was killed. He said: They think that this verse was revealed about those who believed and did not mix their faith with injustice, the verse. God Almighty said: And they did not value God, the verse. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Saeed bin Jubair who said: A Jewish man called Malik bin Al-Saif came and argued with the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace. The Prophet said to him: I ask you by the One who sent down the Torah to Moses, do you find in the Torah that God hates the fat rabbi? And he was a fat rabbi. So he became angry and said: God did not send down anything to any human being. His companions said: Woe to you, not even to Moses. So God revealed: And they did not value God with His true value, the verse. Sent. Ibn Jarir narrated something similar on the authority of Ikrimah, and another hadith was presented in Surat An-Nisa. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Ibn Abi Talha on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: The Jews said: By God, God did not send down a book from the sky, so I sent down... The Almighty said: And who is more unjust than he who invents a lie against God or says: It has been revealed to me, while nothing has been revealed to him. He said: Musaylimah was revealed: And whoever says: I will reveal the like of what God has revealed. He said: It was revealed about Abdullah bin Saad bin Abi Sarh. He used to write for the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and he would dictate to him: Mighty and Wise. He would write: Forgiving and Merciful. Then he would read to him and he would say: Yes, all the same. So he turned away from Islam and joined the Quraysh. And Al-Suddi narrated something similar to it and added: He said: If Muhammad was being revealed to, then it was revealed to me. And if God was revealing it, then I have revealed the like of what God revealed. He said: Muhammad is All-Hearing, All-Knowing. So I said: I am All-Knowing, All-Wise. The Almighty said: And you have certainly come to Us alone, the verse. Ibn Jarir and others narrated on the authority of Ikrimah who said: Al-Nadr bin Al-Harith said: You will intercede with Al-Lat and Al-Uzza, so this verse was revealed: And you have certainly come to Us alone, until His saying: partners. God Almighty said: And do not insult the verse. Abd al-Razzaq said: Ma'mar informed us on the authority of Qatada who said: The Muslims used to insult the idols of the infidels, so the infidels would insult God. Then God revealed: And do not insult those they invoke other than God, the verse. The Almighty said: And they swore the verse. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Muhammad bin Kaab Al-Qurazi who said: The Messenger of God spoke to the Quraysh and they said: O Muhammad, you tell us that Moses had a staff with which he struck the stone and that Jesus brought the dead back to life and that Thamud had the she-camel, so bring us some signs so that we may believe you. The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, said: What do you want me to bring you? They said: Will you turn Safa into gold for us? He said: If I do, will you believe me? They said: Yes, by God. So the Messenger of God stood up to pray and Gabriel came to him and said to him: If you wish, it will become gold, but if they do not believe, then we will punish them, and if you wish, then leave them until their repentant repents. So God revealed: And they swore by God their most solemn oaths until His saying: They are ignorant. God Almighty said: So eat the verse. Abu Dawud and Al-Tirmidhi narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas that some people came to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and said: O Messenger of God, should we eat what we kill and not eat what God kills? So God revealed: So eat of that upon which the name of God has been mentioned, if you are believers in His verses, until His saying: And if you obey them, then indeed you are polytheists. Abu Dawood, Al-Hakim and others narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas regarding his statement: The devils inspire their allies to argue with you. He said: “They said: ‘What God has slaughtered, you do not eat, and what you have slaughtered, you eat.’ So God revealed the verse: Al-Tabarani and others narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: When the verse “And do not eat of that on which the name of God has not been mentioned” was revealed, the Persians sent to the Quraysh to argue with Muhammad and say to him: Whatever you slaughter with your own hand with a knife is permissible, but whatever God slaughters with a golden saw, meaning dead animals, is forbidden. Then this verse was revealed: And the devils inspire their allies to argue with you. The devils said: The Persians and their allies are the Quraysh. God Almighty said: Or was he dead? The verse Abu Sheikh narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas regarding his statement: Or was he dead and We gave him life. He said: It was revealed about Umar and Abu Jahl. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Ad-Dahhak something similar.
6:141: The Almighty said: And give its due on the day of its harvest, and do not be extravagant. The verse. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Abu Al-Aaliyah who said: They used to give something other than zakat, then they were extravagant, and this verse was revealed. And he narrated on the authority of Ibn Jurayj that it was revealed about Thabit bin Qais bin Shammas, the grandfather of Nakhalah, who ate until evening and had no fruit.
7:31: God Almighty said: Take your adornment at every mosque, the verse Muslim narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: A woman would circumambulate the House in the pre-Islamic period while naked and wearing a rag over her private parts, and she would say: Today some or all of it will be visible, and whatever of it is visible, I do not permit it. So the verse was revealed: Take your adornment at every mosque, and the verse was revealed: Who has forbidden the adornment of God, the two verses.
7:184: As for the Almighty’s saying, Or have they not considered the verse? Ibn Abi Hatim and Abu al-Sheikh narrated on the authority of Qatada, who said: It was mentioned to us that the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, stood on al-Safa and called the Quraysh, calling them clan by clan, O sons of so-and-so, O sons of so-and-so, warning them of God’s wrath and its events. Then one of them said, Indeed, this companion of yours is a madman who spent the night in a state of delusion until morning. Then God revealed, “Or have they not considered? There is no madness in their companion. He is only a clear warner.”
7:187: God Almighty said: They ask you about the Hour, etc. Ibn Jarir and others narrated, and Ibn Abbas said: Haml bin Abi Shira and Samuul bin Zaid said to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace: Tell us when the Hour will be, if you are a prophet as you say, then we know what it is. So God revealed: They ask you about the Hour: when will it arrive? The verse. And he also narrated on the authority of Qatada that he said: Quraysh said, and he mentioned something similar.
7:204: The Almighty said: And when the Qur’an is recited, the verse. Ibn Abi Hatim and others narrated on the authority of Abu Hurairah, who said: It was revealed: And when the Qur’an is recited, then listen to it and be silent in raising voices in prayer behind the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace. It was also narrated on his authority that he said: They were talking in prayer, so it was revealed: And when the Qur’an is recited, the verse. It was narrated on the authority of Abdullah bin Mughaffal, something similar to it, and Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Ibn Masoud something similar. And he narrated on the authority of Al-Zuhri, who said: This verse was revealed about a young man from the Ansar. Whenever the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, would recite something, he would recite it. And Saeed bin Mansour said: In his Sunnah, Abu Ma`shar told us on the authority of Muhammad ibn Ka`b who said: They used to listen to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, when he recited something and they would recite with him until this verse was revealed in Al-A`raf: And when the Qur’an is recited, listen to it and be silent. I said: It appears from this that the verse is Medinan.
8:1: Abu Dawud, al-Nasa'i, Ibn Hibban and al-Hakim narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas that the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, said: Whoever kills someone will be entitled to such and such, and whoever takes a prisoner captive will be entitled to such and such. As for the elders, they stood firm under the banners, and as for the young men, they hastened to kill and take the spoils. The elders said to the young men: Include us with you, for we were a support for you, and if there had been anything from you, you would have taken refuge with us. So they argued before the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and the verse was revealed: They ask you about the spoils of war. Say: The spoils belong to God and the Messenger. Ahmad narrated on the authority of Sa`d ibn Abi Waqqas who said: When the day of Badr came, my brother Umair was killed, so I killed Sa`id ibn al-`As with him and took his sword. I brought it to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and he said: Go and throw it in the handcuffs. I returned with what only God knows about the killing of my brother and the taking of my spoils. I had not gone far when Surat al-Anfal was revealed, and the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, said to me: Go and take your sword. Abu Dawud, Al-Tirmidhi and Al-Nasa’i narrated on the authority of Sa’d who said: “When the day of Badr came, I came with a sword and said: ‘O Messenger of God, my chest has been healed by the polytheists. Give me this sword.’ He said: ‘This is neither for me nor for you.’ I said: ‘Perhaps this will be given to someone who will not suffer as I have.’ Then the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, came to me and said: ‘You asked me something that was not mine, and it has now become mine and is yours.’ He said: ‘Then the verse was revealed: They ask you about the spoils of war.’” K and Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Mujahid that they asked the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, about the fifth after the four fifths, so the verse was revealed: They ask you about the spoils of war.
8:5: As the Almighty said, just as He brought you out, the verse. Ibn Abi Hatim and Ibn Mardawayh narrated on the authority of Abu Ayyub al-Ansari, who said: The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, said to us while we were in Medina, and he was informed that the caravan of Abu Sufyan had arrived. What do you think of them? Perhaps God will take them as booty and keep us safe. So we went out and traveled for a day or two. He said: What do you think of them? We said: O Messenger of God, we do not have the strength to fight the people. We only went out for the sake of the caravan. Al-Miqdad said: Do not say as the people of Moses said: Go, you and your Lord, and fight. We are sitting here. Then God revealed: As your Lord brought you out from your home with truth, and a party of the believers were averse. Ibn Jarir narrated something similar on the authority of Ibn Abbas.
8:9: As for the Almighty’s saying, When they call for help, the verse narrated by Al-Tirmidhi on the authority of Omar bin Al-Khattab, who said: The Prophet of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, looked at the polytheists, who were a thousand, and his companions were three hundred and a few men, so he faced the qiblah, then he stretched out his hands and began to call out to his Lord, “O God, fulfill for me what You promised me. O God, if You destroy this group of the people of Islam, You will not be worshipped on earth.” He continued to call out to his Lord, stretching out his hands and facing the qiblah, until his cloak fell off. Abu Bakr came to him, took his cloak, threw it over his shoulders, then embraced him from behind and said, O Prophet of God, it is enough for you to implore your Lord, for He will fulfill for you what He promised you. Then God revealed, When you call for help from your Lord, and He answered you, I will reinforce you with a thousand angels, one after the other. So God reinforced them with the angels.
8:17: The Almighty said: And you did not throw. Al-Hakim narrated on the authority of Saeed bin Al-Musayyab on the authority of his father, who said: Abi bin Khalaf came to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, on the day of Uhud, so they let him go. Mus`ab bin `Umair met him, and the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, saw Abi climbing over him from a gap between the hem of the armor and the shield, so he stabbed him with his spear, so he fell from his horse, and no blood came out of his stab wound, so he broke one of his ribs, and his companions came to him while he was mooing. The bull, so they said: What makes you unable to do so? It is only a scratch. So the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, mentioned to them: Rather, I will kill my father. Then he said: By the One in Whose Hand is my soul, if this had happened to the people of Dhu al-Majaz, they would all have died. So my father died before he reached Mecca, so God revealed: And you did not throw when you threw, but God threw. The verse is authentic in chain of transmission, but it is strange. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Abd al-Rahman ibn Jubayr that the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, on the day of Khaybar called for a bow and shot at the fortress, and the arrow came crashing down until it killed Ibn Abi al-Haqiq while he was in his bed. Then God revealed: And you did not throw when you threw verse, a mursal with a good chain of transmission, but it is strange. What is well-known is that it was revealed about throwing a handful of pebbles on the day of Badr. Ibn Jarir, Ibn Abi Hatim, and Al-Tabarani narrated on the authority of Hakim bin Hizam who said: When the day of Badr came, we heard a sound falling from the sky to the earth as if it were the sound of a pebble falling into a basin. The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, threw those pebbles and we fled. This is what he said: And you did not throw when you threw verse. Abu Al-Sheikh narrated something similar on the authority of Jabir and Ibn Abbas, and Ibn Jarir narrated from another source, with a mursal, something similar to it.
8:19: The Almighty said: That you seek victory. Al-Hakim narrated on the authority of Abdullah bin Tha’labah bin Sagheer that the one who sought victory was Abu Jahl, for when the people met he said: O God, whichever of us has severed ties of kinship and brought what is unknown, then test him this morning. And that was a request for victory, so God revealed: If you seek victory, then victory has come to you. Until His saying: And indeed, God is with the believers. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Atiyah that Abu Jahl said: O God, support the most honorable of the two parties and the most generous of the two divisions. So it was revealed.
