8:27: The Almighty said: O you who believe, do not betray God. Saeed bin Mansour and others narrated on the authority of Abdullah bin Abi Qatada who said: This verse was revealed: Do not betray God and the Messenger. The Banu Qurayzah asked him on the day of Qurayzah: What is this matter? He pointed to a circle and said: Slaughtering. Then it was revealed. Abu Lubabah said: My feet did not move until I knew that I had betrayed God and His Messenger. And Ibn Jarir and others narrated on the authority of Jabir ibn Abdullah that Abu Sufyan left Mecca and Gabriel came to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and said: Abu Sufyan is in such and such a place. So the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, said: Abu Sufyan is in such and such a place, so go out to him and keep it secret. Then a man from the hypocrites wrote to Abu Sufyan: Muhammad wants you, so be careful. So God revealed: Do not betray God and the Messenger. The verse is very strange in its chain of transmission and its context. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of al-Suddi, who said: They used to hear hadith from the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and they would spread it until it reached the polytheists, so it was revealed.
8:30: As for the Almighty’s saying, And when he plotted, the verse, Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas that a group of Quraysh and nobles from each tribe gathered to enter the House of Assembly, and Satan intercepted them in the form of a venerable old man. When they saw him, they said, Who are you? He said, “An old man from Najd. I heard about what you gathered for, so I wanted to bring you together. You will not be deprived of my advice and counsel.” They said, Yes, go in. So he went in with them and said, Look into the matter of this man. Someone said, “Imprison him in chains, then await death for him until he perishes as those poets before him perished, such as Zuhair and al-Nabigha. He is like one of them.” The enemy of God, the Najdi old man, said, “No, by God, this is not your opinion. By God, a leader will come out from his prison to his companions, and they will soon pounce on him until they seize him from your hands and then prevent him from you. I do not feel safe that they will expel you from your country. So look for something other than this opinion.” Someone said, Expel him from among you and be relieved of him, for if he comes out, what he has done will not harm you. The old man said, Al-Najdi: By God, this is not your opinion. Have you not seen the sweetness of his speech, the fluency of his tongue, and his taking hold of hearts with what he hears of his talk? By God, if you do this, then he reviewed the Arabs so that they may gather around him and then march towards you until he expels you from your country and kills your nobles. They said: He is truthful, by God, so look for an opinion other than this. Abu Jahl said: By God, I will advise you with an opinion that I do not think you have seen after he saw something else. They said: What is this? He said: Take from each tribe a young, strong middleman, then give each of their boys a sharp sword, then strike him with the blow of a man or one. If you kill him, his blood will be distributed among all the tribes. I do not think that this tribe of Banu Hashim will be able to fight all of Quraysh. If they see that, they will accept reason and we will be at ease and his harm will be cut off from us. The Najdi said: This, by God, is the right opinion. What the young man said, I do not see anything else. So they dispersed on that basis, and they were united for it. Then Gabriel came to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and ordered him not to spend the night in the bed he used to sleep in, and he informed him of the plot of the people. But the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, did not spend the night in his house that night. At that time, God gave him permission to go out and revealed to him after his arrival in Medina, reminding him of His favor upon him: And when those who disbelieved plotted against you, the verse. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Ubayd ibn Umair on the authority of al-Muttalib ibn Abi Wada’ah that Abu Talib said to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, What are your people plotting against you? He said, They want to imprison me or kill me or expel me. He said, Who told you this? He said, My Lord. He said, Yes, your Lord is your Lord, so treat Him well. He said, “Treat Him well. Rather, he treats me well.” Then the verse was revealed, And when those who disbelieved plotted against you. Ibn Kathir said, The mention of Abu Talib in it is strange, rather strange, because the story is on the night of the Hijrah, and that was three years after the death of Abu Talib.
8:31: As for the Almighty’s saying, And when the verse is recited, Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Saeed bin Jubair, who said that the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, killed Uqbah bin Abi Mu’ayt, Tu’aymah bin Adi, and Al-Nadr bin Al-Harith patiently on the day of Badr. Al-Miqdad had taken Al-Nadr prisoner, and when he ordered him to be killed, Al-Miqdad said, O Messenger of God, my prisoner. The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, said, He used to say about the Book of God what he said. He said, And in it this verse was revealed, ‘And when Our verses are recited to them, they say, ‘We have heard the verse.’
8:32: God Almighty said: And when they said, O God, if this is the truth, the verse. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Saeed bin Jubair regarding His statement: And when they said, O God, if this is the truth, the verse. He said: It was revealed. In Al-Nadr bin Al-Harith, and Al-Bukhari narrated on the authority of Anas that he said: Abu Jahl bin Hisham said: O God, if this is the truth from You, then rain down upon us stones from the sky or bring us a painful punishment. So the verse was revealed: And God would not punish them while you are among them. K and Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: The polytheists used to circumambulate the House and say: Your forgiveness, your forgiveness. Then God revealed: And God would not punish them. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Yazid bin Ruman and Muhammad bin Qais, who said: The Quraysh said to one another: “Muhammad, may God honor him, is among us. O God, if this is the truth from You, then rain down upon us stones from the sky.” The verse. When evening came, they regretted what they had said and said: Your forgiveness, O God. So God revealed: And God would not punish them while they seek forgiveness. Up to His saying: They do not know. And Ibn Jarir also narrated on the authority of Ibn Abi Zayd, who said: The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, was in Mecca, and God revealed: And God would not punish them while you are among them. So he went out to Medina, and God revealed: And God would not punish them while they seek forgiveness. And those remaining Muslims who remained there sought forgiveness. So when they went out, God revealed: And why should God not punish them? The verse was given. So he permitted the conquest of Mecca, and it was the punishment that He had promised them.
8:35: The Almighty said: And their prayer was not the same. Al-Wahidi narrated on the authority of Ibn Omar that he said: They used to circumambulate the House, clapping and whistling, then this verse was revealed. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Saeed, who said: The Quraysh used to oppose the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, during the circumambulation, mocking him, whistling and clapping, so this verse was revealed.
8:36: God Almighty said: Those who disbelieved, the verse. Ibn Ishaq said: Al-Zuhri, Muhammad ibn Yahya ibn Hibban, Asim ibn Umair ibn Qatada, and Al-Hussain ibn Abd al-Rahman told me that when the Quraysh were defeated on the day of Badr and returned to Mecca, Abdullah ibn Abi Rabi’ah walkedGod And Ikrimah bin Abi Jahl and Sufyan bin Abi Umayya were among men from Quraysh whose fathers and sons had been killed. They spoke to Abu Sufyan and whoever had business with that caravan from Quraysh and said, “O people of Quraysh, Muhammad has killed you and your best people. Help us with this money to fight him, perhaps we can take revenge on him.” So they did. As Ibn Abbas mentioned, God revealed about them: Those who disbelieve spend their wealth and their children until His saying, ‘They will be gathered.’ Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Al-Hakam bin Utaybah, who said: It was revealed about Abu Sufyan, who spent forty ounces of gold on the polytheists. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Ibn Abzi and Saeed bin Jubair, who said: It was revealed about Abu Sufyan who hired two thousand Abyssinians on the day of Uhud to fight with the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace.
8:47: As God Almighty said, And do not be like those who left their homes insolently. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Muhammad ibn Ka’b al-Qurazi, who said, When the Quraysh left Mecca for Badr, they went out with singing girls and drums, so God revealed, “And do not be like those who left their homes insolently.
8:49: The Almighty said: When the hypocrites say, the verse. At-Tabarani narrated in Al-Awsat with a weak chain of transmission on the authority of Abu Hurairah, who said: When God revealed to His Prophet in Mecca, The host will be defeated and they will turn their backs, Omar bin Al-Khattab, may God be pleased with him, said: O Messenger of God, which host? And that was before Badr. When the day of Badr came and the Quraysh were defeated, I looked at the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, following them with his sword drawn, saying: The host will be defeated and they will turn their backs. So it was the day of Badr. Then God revealed about them: Until when We seized their affluent ones with punishment, the verse: And revealed: Have you not seen those who exchanged the favor of God for disbelief? The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, shot at them, and the shot suffocated them and filled their eyes and mouths, until a man was killed while he was still bleeding from his eyes and mouth. Then God revealed: And you did not shoot when you shot, but God shot. And it was revealed about Satan, And when the two companies saw each other, he turned back on his heels. And Utbah bin Rabi’ah and some polytheists with him said on the day of Badr, These people were deceived by their religion. So God revealed: When the hypocrites say, and those who In their hearts is a disease that has deceived them. Their religion has deceived them.
8:55: As for the Almighty’s saying, Indeed, the worst of living creatures in the sight of God are those who disbelieve, so they will not believe. Abu Al-Sheikh narrated on the authority of Saeed bin Jubair, who said, Indeed, the worst of living creatures in the sight of God are those who disbelieve, so they will not believe. It was revealed about six men from the Jews, among them the son of the Ark.
8:58: The Almighty said: But if you fear treachery from a people, the verse. Abu Sheikh narrated on the authority of Ibn Shihab that Gabriel entered upon the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and said: I have laid down my weapons and have continued to pursue the people, so go out, for God has given you permission to deal with Qurayza and revealed about them: But if you fear treachery from a people, the verse.
8:64: The Almighty said: O Prophet, sufficient for you is God, the verse K. Al-Bazzar narrated with a weak chain of transmission on the authority of Ikrimah on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: When Umar converted to Islam, the polytheists said: The people have taken their revenge on us today. And God revealed: O Prophet, sufficient for you is God and those who follow you among the believers. And it has supporting evidence. K and Al-Tabarani and others narrated on the authority of Saeed bin Jubair on the authority of Ibn Abbas who said: When thirty-nine men and women converted to Islam with the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and then Umar converted to Islam and they were forty, the verse was revealed: O Prophet, sufficient for you is God and those who follow you among the believers. K and Ibn Abi Hatim narrated with a sound chain of transmission on the authority of Saeed bin Jubair who said: When thirty-three men and six women converted to Islam with the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and then Umar converted to Islam, the verse O Prophet, God is sufficient for you was revealed. Abu Al-Sheikh narrated on the authority of Saeed bin Al-Musayyab who said: When Umar converted to Islam, God revealed about his conversion to Islam, O Prophet, God is sufficient for you the verse.
8:65: The Almighty said: If there are twenty among you, patient, the verse. Ishaq bin Rahawayh included in his Musnad on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: When God made it obligatory for them that one person should fight ten, that was difficult and burdensome for them, so God relieved them until one person fought two men, so God revealed: If there are twenty among you, patient, they will overcome two hundred, to the end of the verse.
8:67: The Almighty said: It was not for a prophet of the verse. Ahmad and others narrated on the authority of Anas that he said: The Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, consulted the people about the prisoners on the day of Badr and said: God has given you power over them. Then Umar ibn al-Khattab stood up and said: O Messenger of God, strike their necks. He turned away from him. Then Abu Bakr stood up and said: We think that you should pardon them and accept their ransom. So he pardoned them and accepted their ransom. Then God revealed: Had it not been for a previous Book from God, the verse would have been revealed. Ahmad, Al-Tirmidhi, Al-Hakim and Ibn Masoud narrated that when the day of Badr came and the prisoners were brought, the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, said: What do you say about these prisoners? The hadith says: Then the Qur’an was revealed with the words of Umar: It is not for a prophet to have prisoners, until the end of the verses. Al-Tirmidhi narrated on the authority of Abu Hurairah on the authority of the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, who said: Spoils of war were not permissible. They were not permissible for any of the black-headed people before you. Fire used to come down from the sky and consume them. Then on the day of Badr, they took possession of the spoils before they were permissible for them. Then God revealed: Had it not been for a previous decree from God, a great punishment would have afflicted you for what you took.
8:70: The Almighty said, O Prophet, say to those in your hands, the verse. Al-Tabarani narrated in Al-Awsat on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said, Al-Abbas said, “By God, it was revealed when I informed the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, of my conversion to Islam and asked him to account for me for the twenty ounces that were found with me, so he gave me twenty slaves in return, all of whom were traders with my money in his hands, along with what I hope for from God’s forgiveness.”
8:73: As for the Almighty’s saying: And those who disbelieved, the verse. Ibn Jarir and Abu al-Sheikh narrated on the authority of al-Suddi on the authority of Abu Malik, who said: A man said: We inherit from our polytheist relatives, so the verse was revealed: And those who disbelieved are allies of one another.
8:75: The Almighty said: And those of kinship, the verse Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Ibn al-Zubayr, who said: A man would make a contract with another man: You inherit from me and I inherit from you. So this verse was revealed: And those of kinship are more worthy of one another in the Book of God. And Ibn Saad narrated on the authority of Hashim Ibn Urwah on the authority of his father, who said: The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, made a brotherhood between al-Zubayr Ibn al-Awwam and Ka`b Ibn Malik. Al-Zubayr said: I saw Ka`b wounded at Uhud, so I said: If he died and was cut off from the world and its people, I would inherit from him. So this verse was revealed: And those of kinship are more worthy of one another in the Book of God. So the inheritances were then for the wombs and relatives, and those inheritances were cut off in brotherhood.
9:14: K His Almighty saying: Fight them; God will punish them. The verse. Abu Sheikh narrated on the authority of Qatada who said: It was mentioned to us that this verse was revealed about Khuza'ah when they began to kill Banu Bakr in Mecca. He narrated on the authority of Ikrimah who said: This verse was revealed about Khuza'ah. Al-Suddi narrated: And He heals the hearts of a believing people. He said: They are Khuza'ah, the allies of the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace. He heals their hearts from Banu Bakr.
9:17: The Almighty said: It was not for the polytheists to have the verses. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Ali bin Abi Talha on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: Al-Abbas said when he was taken prisoner on the day of Badr: If you had preceded us in Islam, migration, and jihad, we used to maintain the Sacred Mosque, provide water to the pilgrims, and free the captives. Then God revealed: Have you made the provision of water to the pilgrims? Muslim, Ibn Hibban and Abu Dawud narrated on the authority of Al-Nu’man bin Bashir who said: I was at the pulpit of the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, with a group of his companions. One of them said: I do not care if I do not do any work for God after Islam except providing water to the pilgrims. Another said: Rather, the maintenance of the Sacred Mosque. And another said: Rather, jihad in the way of God is better than what you have said. So Umar rebuked them and said: Do not raise your voices at the pulpit of the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace. Peace be upon you, and that was on Friday, but when I prayed the Friday prayer, I went to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and asked him about what you differed about, so God revealed: Have you made the provision of water for the pilgrims to His saying: He does not guide the wrongdoing people. Al-Faryabi narrated on the authority of Ibn Sirin who said: Ali bin Abi Talib came to Mecca and said to Al-Abbas: O uncle, why don’t you emigrate? Why don’t you join the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace? He said: I will build the mosque and cover the house. Then God revealed: Have you made the provision of water for the pilgrims the verse. And he said to a people whom he named: Why don’t you emigrate? Why don’t you join the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace? They said: We will stay with our brothers, our tribes, and our dwellings. Then God revealed: Say: If your fathers were… the entire verse. And Abd Al-Razzaq narrated on the authority of Al-Sha’bi something similar to it. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Muhammad bin Kaab Al-Qurazi, who said: Talha bin Shaiba, Al-Abbas, and Ali bin Abi Talib boasted. Talha said: I am the owner of the house, and I have its key. Al-Abbas said: I am the owner of the water-giving vessel and the one who takes care of it. Ali said: I prayed toward the qiblah before the people, and I am the owner of the jihad. Then God revealed: Have you made the watering of the pilgrims the responsibility of the entire verse?
9:25: The Almighty said: And on the day of Hunayn, the verse. Al-Bayhaqi narrated in Al-Dala’il on the authority of Al-Rabi’ Ibn Anas that a man said on the day of Hunayn, We will not be defeated because of our small number, and they were twelve thousand. This upset the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, so God revealed: And on the day of Hunayn, when your great number pleased you, the verse.
9:28: As for the Almighty’s saying: And if you fear poverty, the verse: Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: The polytheists used to come to the House and bring food with them to trade in it. When they were forbidden from coming to the House, the Muslims said: From where will we get food? So God revealed: And if you fear poverty, then God will enrich you from His bounty. Ibn Jarir and Abu Al-Sheikh narrated on the authority of Saeed bin Jubair who said: When the verse, The polytheists are unclean, so let them not approach the Sacred Mosque after this year of theirs, was revealed, it was difficult for the Muslims and they said, Who will bring us food and provisions? Then God revealed, But if you fear poverty, God will enrich you from His bounty. And he narrated something similar on the authority of Ikrimah, Atiyah Al-Awfi, Ad-Dahhak, Qatadah, and others.
9:30: As for the Almighty’s saying, “And the Jews said, ‘The verse.’ Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said, ‘Salam bin Mishkam, Nu’man bin Awfa, Muhammad bin Dihya, Shas bin Qais, and Malik bin Al-Saif came to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and said, ‘How can we follow you when you have abandoned our qiblah and you do not claim that Ezra is the son of God?’ So God revealed the verse about that, ‘And the Jews said,’
9:37: As for the Almighty’s saying: The postponement is only an increase in disbelief. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Abu Malik who said: They used to make the year thirteen months, and they would make Muharram Safar, and they would record the forbidden things in it. Then God revealed: The postponement is only an increase in disbelief.
9:38: God Almighty said, O you who have believed, what is [the matter] with you when you are told, 'I am not able to do so,' verse. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Mujahid regarding this verse, saying, "This is when they were ordered to go to Tabuk after the conquest, and when they were ordered to go out in the summer when the fruits were good and they desired shade and the way out was difficult for them, so God revealed, 'Go forth, whether light or heavy.'"
9:39: Ka God’s saying: If you do not go forth, the verse. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Najdah bin Nafi’ who said: “I asked Ibn Abbas about this verse, and he said: The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, called some tribes of the Arabs to mobilize, but they were reluctant to come to him. So God revealed: “If you do not go forth, He will punish you with a painful punishment.” So He withheld the rain from them, and their punishment was…
9:41: Go forth, whether you are light or heavy. The verse: Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Hadrami that he mentioned to him people who were, perhaps one of them would be ill or old and say, I am a sinner. So God revealed, Go forth, whether you are light or heavy.
9:43: God Almighty said: God has pardoned you, the verse. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Amr bin Maymun Al-Azdi, who said: The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, did two things in which he was not commanded to do anything: his permission for the hypocrites and his taking ransom from the prisoners. Then God revealed: God has pardoned you, why did you give them permission?
9:49: The Almighty said: And among them is he who says: Give me permission, the verse. At-Tabarani, Abu Nu'aym, and Ibn Mardawayh narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas that when the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, wanted to go out to the Battle of Tabuk, he said to Al-Jadd bin Qais: O Jadd bin Qais, what do you say about fighting the Banu Al-Asfar? He said: O Messenger of God, I am a man who has women, and when I see the women of the Banu Al-Asfar, I will be tempted, so give me permission and do not tempt me. So God revealed: And among them is he who says: Give me permission and do not tempt me. The verse. Ibn Abi Hatim and Ibn Mardawayh narrated from the hadith of Jabir bin Abdullah similarly. At-Tabarani narrated from another chain of transmission on the authority of Ibn Abbas that the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, said: Go out to war and take the daughters of the Banu Al-Asfar as booty. Some of the hypocrites said: He will tempt you with women. So God revealed: And among them is he who says: Give me permission and do not tempt me.
9:50: As for the Almighty’s saying: If good befalls you, it will distress them. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Jabir Ibn Abdullah, who said: The hypocrites who stayed behind in Medina began to tell bad report about the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, saying that Muhammad and his companions had exhausted themselves on their journey and perished. Then they heard that their hadith was denied and that the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and his companions were well, so that upset them. So God revealed: If good befalls you, it will distress them. The verse.
9:53: The Almighty said: Say, Spend. The verse. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas who said: Al-Jadd bin Qais said: When I see women, I cannot be patient until I am tempted, but I will help you with my money. He said: So, regarding it, the verse was revealed: Spend willingly or unwillingly, it will not be accepted from you. He said: Regarding his saying, I will help you with my money.
9:58: The Almighty said: And among them are those who oblige you in charity, the verse. Al-Bukhari narrated on the authority of Abu Saeed Al-Khudri, who said: While the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, was distributing, Dhu Al-Khuwaysira came to him and said: Be just. He said: Woe to you, who will be just if I am not just? So the verse was revealed: And among them are those who oblige you in charity, the verse. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated something similar on the authority of Jabir.
9:61: The Almighty said: And among them are those who harm the Prophet, the verse. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: Nabtal bin Al-Harith used to come to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and sit with him, listening to him and conveying his speech to the hypocrites. Then God revealed: And among them are those who harm the Prophet, the verse.
9:65: God Almighty said: And if you ask them for the signs, Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Ibn Omar, he said: A man said in the Battle of Tabuk in a gathering one day, We have not seen the likes of the Qur’an of these people, nor do they have more greedy bellies, nor more lying tongues, nor more cowardly in combat than them. A man said to him, “You have lied, but you are a hypocrite. I will inform the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace.” That reached the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and the Qur’an was revealed. Ibn Omar said: “I saw him clinging to the camel’s saddlebag, may God bless him and grant him peace, and the stones were hitting him, and he was saying, ‘O Messenger of God, we were only joking and playing,’ and the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, was saying, ‘O Father of God, His signs, and His Messenger, you were mocking.’ Then he narrated from another chain of transmission on the authority of Ibn Omar something similar, and he named this man Abdullah bin Ubayy. He narrated on the authority of Ka’b bin Malik, Makhshi bin Himyar said: “I would like to sue on the condition that each man among you be struck one hundred times, so that you may be saved from having the Qur’an revealed about us.” That reached the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and they came apologizing, so God revealed, Do not apologize, the verse, and that was what God pardoned. On his authority, Makhshi Ibn Himyar called himself Abd al-Rahman and asked God to kill him as a martyr, so that his killing would not be known. So he was killed on the day of Yamamah, and his killing was not known, nor who killed him. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Qatada that some of the hypocrites said during the Battle of Tabuk, “This man hopes to conquer the palaces and fortresses of Syria. No way.” So God informed His Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, of that, so he came to them and said, You said such and such. They said, We were only joking and playing. So this was revealed.
9:74: The Almighty said: They swear by God that they did not say the verse K Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: Al-Jalas bin Suwaid bin Al-Samit was one of those who stayed behind from the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, in the Battle of Tabuk, and he said: If this man is truthful, then we are worse than donkeys. Umair bin Saeed reported that to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and he swore by God that I did not say. Then God revealed: They swear by God that they did not say the verse. They claimed that he repented and his repentance was good. K Then he narrated on the authority of Ka’b bin Malik and something similar. Ibn Saad narrated something similar in Al-Tabaqat on the authority of Urwah. K and Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Anas bin Malik who said: Zaid bin Arqam heard a man from the hypocrites saying while the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, was giving a sermon: If this man is truthful, then we are worse than donkeys. So this was reported to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and he denied the speaker, so God revealed: They swear by God that they did not say the verse. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, was sitting in the shade of a tree and said: A man will come to you who will look with the eyes of a devil. Then a blue-eyed man came out and the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, called him and said: Why are you and your companions cursing me? The man went and brought his companions and they swore by God that they had not said anything until he forgave them. Then God Almighty revealed: They swear by God that they have not said anything. And he narrated on the authority of Qatada who said that two men fought, one of them from Juhayna and the other from Ghifar. Juhayna were allies of the Ansar, and the Ghifarite prevailed over the Juhayni. So Abdullah bin Abi Al-Aws said: Support your brother, for by God, our example and the example of Muhammad are as the one who said: “Fatten your dog and it will eat you. If we return to Medina, the more honorable will expel therefrom the more humble.” So a man from the Muslims went to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and he sent for him and asked him, and he began to swear by God that he had not said anything, so God Almighty revealed: They swear by God that they did not say anything. Al-Tabarani narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: A man called Al-Aswad intended to kill the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, so the verse was revealed: And they intended to do something they did not achieve. Ibn Jarir and Abu Al-Sheikh narrated on the authority of Ikrimah that the client of Banu Adi bin Kaab killed a man from the Ansar, so the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, ruled that the blood money should be twelve thousand, and this was revealed regarding it: They only resented it because God and His Messenger enriched them from His bounty.
9:75: The Almighty said: And among them are those who made a covenant with God. The verse: At-Tabarani, Ibn Mardawayh, Ibn Abi Hatim, and Al-Bayhaqi in Ad-Dala'il narrated with a weak chain of transmission on the authority of Abu Umamah that Tha'laba ibn Hattab said: O Messenger of God, pray to God to grant me wealth. He said: Woe to you, O Tha'laba! A little that you can thank for is better than a lot that you cannot bear. He said: By God, if God gives me wealth, I will give every person who has a right his right. So he prayed for him and took sheep. They grew until the alleys of Madinah became too narrow for him, so he moved away with them. He used to attend the Friday prayer and then go out to it. Then they grew until the pastures of Madinah became too narrow for him, so he moved away with them. He used to attend the Friday prayer and then go out to it. Then they grew and he moved away with them, so he abandoned the Friday prayer and congregational prayers. Then God revealed to His Messenger: Take from their wealth a charity that will purify them and sanctify them thereby. So he appointed two men over the charity and wrote a letter for them. They came to Tha'laba and the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, read it. He said: Go to the people, and when you are done, pass by me. So they did. He said: This is nothing but a sister of the jizyah. So they set out, and God revealed: And among them are those who made a covenant with God, ‘If He should give us from His bounty,’ until His saying, ‘They deny the hadith.’ Ibn Jarir and Ibn Mardawayh narrated on the authority of Al-Awfi on the authority of Ibn Abbas something similar to it.
9:79: The Almighty said: Those who adhere to the volunteers, the verse. The two sheikhs narrated on the authority of Abu Masoud, who said: When the verse of charity was revealed, we were carrying loads on our backs, then a man came and gave a large amount in charity, so they said: Showing off. And a man came and gave a sa` in charity, so they said: God is independent of this charity. So those who criticize the volunteers, the verse was revealed. Something similar to this was reported in the hadith of Abu Hurairah, Abu Uqayl, Abu Sa`id al-Khudri, Ibn Abbas, and Umaira bint Suhayl ibn Rafi`, all of which were included by Ibn Mardawayh.
9:81: As for the Almighty’s saying: Those who stayed behind rejoiced. The verse: Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, ordered the people to go out with him, and that was in the summer. Then some men said: O Messenger of God, the heat is intense and we cannot go out, so do not go out in the heat. Then God revealed: “Say: The fire of Hell is more intense in heat.” The verse: And he narrated on the authority of Muhammad bin Ka’b Al-Qurazi, who said: The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, went out in intense heat to Tabuk, and a man from Banu Salamah said: Do not go out in the heat. So God revealed: Say: The fire of Hell is more intense in heat. Al-Bayhaqi narrated in Al-Dala’il, on the authority of Ibn Ishaq, on the authority of Asim bin Amr bin Qatada and Abdullah bin Abi Bakr bin Hazm, who said: A man from the hypocrites said: Do not go out in the heat, so this was revealed.
9:84: The Almighty said: And do not pray for any of them, the verse. The two sheikhs narrated on the authority of Ibn Omar, who said: When Abdullah Ibn Ubayy died, his son came to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and asked him to give him his shirt to shroud his father in, so he gave it to him. Then he asked him to pray for him, so he stood up to pray for him. Omar Ibn Al-Khattab stood up and took hold of his garment and said: O Messenger of God, will you pray for him when your Lord has forbidden you to pray for the hypocrites? He said: God has given me a choice and said: Ask forgiveness for them or do not ask forgiveness for them. If you ask forgiveness for them seventy times, I will add to the seventy. He said: He is a hypocrite, so he prayed for him. Then God revealed: And do not pray for any of them who dies ever, and do not stand at his grave. So he stopped praying for them. This was reported in the hadith of Omar, Anas, Jabir, and others.
9:91: As for the Almighty’s saying: There is no blame upon the weak, the verse. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Zaid bin Thabit, who said: “I was writing for the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and I was writing Bara’ah, and I was placing the pen on my ear when we were ordered to fight. The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, was looking at what was revealed to him when a blind man came to him and said: ‘How can I be, O Messenger of God, when I am blind?’ Then the verse: “There is no blame upon the weak, the verse” was revealed. And he narrated through the path of Al-Awfi on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, ordered the people to go out to fight with him. Then a group of his companions came, among them Abdullah bin Muqil Al-Muzani, and he said: O Messenger of God, give us a load. He said: By God, there is no reward for what I give you to carry. So they turned away crying and it was difficult for them to be prevented from fighting and not to find money or a mount. So God Almighty revealed: And there is no blame upon those who, when they come to you to be carried, the verse. Their names are mentioned in the ambiguous verses.
9:98: God Almighty said: And among the bedouins are those who believe in God, the verse. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Mujahid that it was revealed about Banu Muqrin, about whom it was revealed. Nor upon those who, when they come to you, that you may mount them. And Abd al-Rahman bin Muqal al-Muzani said: We were ten sons of Muqrin, and this verse was revealed about us.
9:102: The Almighty said: And others have confessed the verse. Ibn Mardawayh and Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Al-Awfi on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, went on a military expedition, and Abu Lubabah and five others with him stayed behind. Then Abu Lubabah and two men with him thought and regretted and were certain of their destruction and said: We are in the shade and tranquility with the women, and the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and the believers are with him in jihad. By God, we will tie ourselves to the pillars and not release them until the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, is the one who releases them. So they did and three remained who did not tie themselves. Then the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, returned from his expedition and said: Who are these who are tied to the pillars? A man said: This is Abu Lubabah and his companions who stayed behind and made a covenant with God that they would not release themselves until you are the one who releases them. He said: I will not release them until I am ordered to release them. So God revealed the verse: And others confessed their sins. When it was revealed, He released them and excused them. The three who did not tie themselves were not mentioned in anything. They are the ones about whom God said: And others are awaiting the command of God. The verse: Then some people said, They are lost because their excuse has not been revealed, and others said, Perhaps God will forgive them, until it was revealed about the three who were left behind. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Ali bin Abi Talha on the authority of Ibn Abbas something similar. And Abu Lubaba came with their money when they were released and they said, O Messenger of God, our money, so give it in charity on our behalf and seek refuge for us. He said, I was not commanded to take anything from your money. So God revealed, Take from their money charity verse, and this amount alone was reported by Saeed Ibn Jubayr, Ad-Dahhak, Zaid Ibn Aslam, and others. And Abd narrated on the authority of Qatada that it was revealed about seven people, four of whom tied themselves to the pillars, and they were Abu Abu Lubabah, Mirdas, Aws bin Khidham, and Tha’laba bin Wadi’ah. Abu al-Sheikh and Ibn Mandah narrated in the Companions on the authority of al-Thawri on the authority of al-A’mash on the authority of Abu Sufyan on the authority of Jabir, who said: Among those who stayed behind from the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, in Tabuk were six: Abu Lubabah, Aws bin Khidham, Tha’laba bin Wadi’ah, Ka’b bin Malik, Murarah bin al-Rabi’, and Hilal bin Umayya. Then Abu Lubabah, Aws, and Tha’laba came and tied themselves to the pillars and brought their money and said: O Messenger of God, take this that has kept us from you. He said: I will not release them until there is fighting. Then the Qur’an was revealed, and others confessed their sins. The verse’s chain of transmission is strong. Ibn Mardawayh narrated with a chain of transmission that includes Al-Waqidi on the authority of Umm Salamah who said: The repentance of Abu Lubabah was revealed in my house. I heard the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, laughing at dawn, so I said: What makes you laugh, O Messenger of God? He said: Abu Lubabah has repented to me. I said: Tell him about that. He said: Whatever you wish. So I stood at the door of the room, and that was before the curtain was drawn up, and I said: O Abu Lubabah, be happy, for God has repented to you. The people rose up to release him, but he said: Until the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, comes and he is the one who releases me. So when he went out to dawn, he released him. So it was revealed, and others confessed their sins.
9:107: The Almighty said: And those who have taken a mosque for the purpose of causing harm, the verse Ibn Mardawayh narrated on the authority of Ibn Ishaq who said: Ibn Shihab Al-Zuhri mentioned on the authority of Ibn Akima Al-Laythi on the authority of the nephew of Abu Ruhm Al-Ghifari that he heard Abu Ruhm who was among those who pledged allegiance under the tree saying: The one who built the mosque of harm came to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, while he was preparing for Tabuk and they said: O Messenger of God, we have built a mosque for those who are sick and in need, and for the night of winter and the night of rain, and we would like you to come to us and pray for us in it. He said: I am on the verge of a journey, and if we were to come, God willing, we would come to you and pray for you in it. So when he returned, he descended at Dhi Awan, an hour from Medina, and God revealed about the mosque: And those who have taken a mosque for the purpose of causing harm and disbelief, to the end of the story. So he called Malik bin Al-Dakhshin and Ma’n bin Adi or his brother Asim bin Adi and said: Go to this mosque whose people are unjust, and demolish it and burn it. So they did. Ibn Abi Hatim and Ibn Mardawayh narrated on the authority of Al-Awfi on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: When the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, built the Quba Mosque, some men from the Ansar came out, some of whom were Khadjj, and they built the Mosque of Hypocrisy. The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, said: Woe to you, Khadjj! What did you want to see? He said: O Messenger of God, I wanted nothing but goodness. Then God revealed the verse. Ibn Mardawayh narrated on the authority of Ali bin Talha on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: Some people from the Ansar built a mosque, so Abu Amir said to them: Build your mosque and obtain whatever strength and weapons you can, for I am going to Caesar, the king of the Romans. So he brought an army of the Romans and brought out Muhammad and his companions. When they finished their mosque, they came to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and said to him: We have finished building our mosque, and we would like you to pray in it. So God revealed: Do not perform prayer in it ever. Al-Wahidi narrated on the authority of Sa`d ibn Abi Waqqas, who said: The hypocrites proposed to build a mosque that they would imitate the Quba Mosque for Abu `Amir the monk, when he came, so that he would be their imam in it. When they finished building it, they came to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and said: We have built a mosque, so pray in it. Then the verse was revealed: You shall never stand in it. Al-Tirmidhi narrated on the authority of Abu Hurairah who said: This verse was revealed about the people of Quba. Among them were men who loved to purify themselves, and God loves those who purify themselves. He said: They used to clean themselves with water, so it was revealed about them. K and Omar bin Shabah narrated in the report of the city on the authority of Al-Walid bin Abi Sandar Al-Aslami on the authority of Yahya bin Sahl Al-Ansari on the authority of his father that this verse was revealed about the people of Quba who used to wash their backsides from excrement. There are men in it who love to purify themselves. The verse. K and Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Ata’ who said: A group of people from Quba performed ablution with water, so the verse was revealed about them: Men who love to purify themselves, and God loves those who purify themselves.
9:111: The Almighty said: Indeed, God has purchased the verse. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Muhammad bin Kaab Al-Qurazi who said: Abdullah bin Rawahah said to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, Set a condition for your Lord and for yourself as you wish. He said: Set a condition for my Lord that you worship Him and do not associate anything with Him, and set a condition for myself that you protect me from what you protect yourselves and your wealth from. They said: If we do that, what will we have? He said: Paradise. They said: “Profit in the sale. We do not take a break or ask for a break.” Then the verse was revealed: Indeed, God has purchased the believers’ souls.
9:113: The Almighty said: It was not for the Prophet, the verse. The two sheikhs narrated on the authority of Saeed bin Al-Musayyab, on the authority of his father, who said: When the Prophet of Abu Talib died, the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, entered upon him and Abu Jahl and Abdullah bin Abi Umayya were with him. He said: O uncle, say there is no god but God, and I will argue for you with it before God. Abu Jahl and Abdullah said: O Abu Talib, do you desire the religion of Abdul Muttalib? They kept talking to him until the last thing he said to them was that he was following the religion of Abdul Muttalib. The Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, said: I will seek forgiveness for you as long as I am not forbidden from doing so. Then the verse was revealed: It is not for the Prophet and those who have believed to seek forgiveness for the polytheists, the verse. And it was revealed about Abu Talib: You do not guide whom you love, the verse. It appears that the verse was revealed in Mecca. K and Al-Tirmidhi and Al-Hakim’s Hasanah narrated on the authority of Ali, who said: I heard a man asking forgiveness for his parents, and they were polytheists, so I said to him: Do you ask forgiveness for your parents, and they are polytheists? He said: Ibrahim asked forgiveness for his father, and he was a polytheist, so I mentioned that to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace. It was revealed: It is not for the Prophet and those who believe to ask forgiveness for the polytheists. Al-Hakim and Al-Bayhaqi narrated in Al-Dala’il and others on the authority of Ibn Mas’ud who said: The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, went out one day to the graveyards and sat by one of them and confided in it for a long time. Then he cried, so I cried because of his crying. Then he said: The grave by which I sat is the grave of my mother, and I asked my Lord for permission to pray for her, but He did not give me permission. Then God revealed: It is not for the Prophet and those who believe to ask forgiveness for the polytheists. Ahmad and Ibn Mardawayh narrated, and the wording is his, from the hadith of Buraidah, who said: I was with the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, when he stopped at Usfan and saw his mother’s grave. He performed ablution, prayed, and wept. Then he said: I asked my Lord for permission to ask forgiveness for her, but I was forbidden. Then God revealed: It is not for the Prophet and those who believe to ask forgiveness for the polytheists. Al-Tabarani and Ibn Mardawayh narrated something similar from the hadith of Ibn Abbas, and that this was after he returned from Tabuk and traveled to Mecca to perform Umrah, so he stopped at Thaniyat Usfan. Al-Hafiz Ibn Hajar said: It is possible that the revelation of the verse had earlier causes, which is the matter of Abu Talib, and later causes, which is the matter of Amina and the story of Ali, and the collection of others with multiple revelations.
9:117: As for the Almighty’s saying: God has indeed accepted the repentance of the Prophet. Verses narrated by Al-Bukhari and others on the authority of Ka’b bin Malik, who said: “I did not stay behind from the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, in any battle he fought except Badr, until the Battle of Tabuk, which was the last battle he fought, and he gave the people the call to depart.” He mentioned the hadith in its entirety, and in it: “So God revealed our repentance: God has indeed accepted the repentance of the Prophet and the emigrants,” until His saying: Indeed, God is the Accepter of Repentance, the Most Merciful. He said: “And about us it was also revealed: ‘Fear God and be with the truthful.’”
9:122: God Almighty said: And it is not for the believers to go forth all together. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Ikrimah who said: When the verse was revealed: If you do not go forth, He will punish you with a painful punishment, and some people had stayed behind in the deserts who were teaching their people, then the hypocrites said: There are still people in the deserts, the people of the deserts have perished. So it was revealed: And it is not for the believers to go forth all together. And he narrated on the authority of Abdullah bin Ubaid bin Umair, who said: The believers, due to their eagerness to fight, when the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, sent out a raiding party, would go out with it and leave the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, in Medina among the people’s weakness, so this verse was revealed:
10:2: The Almighty said: Was it a wonder to people? Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Ad-Dahhak on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: When God sent Muhammad as a Messenger, the Arabs denied that, or those of them denied that, and they said: God is greater than that His Messenger should be a human being. So God revealed: Was it a wonder to people? The verse: And He revealed: And We did not send before you except men. The verse: So when God repeated the arguments to them, they said: And if he was a human being, then other than Muhammad would have been more deserving of the message. Why was this Qur’an not sent down to a great man from the two cities? They say: More noble than Muhammad. They meant Al-Walid bin Al-Mughirah from Mecca and Masoud bin Amr Al-Thaqafi from At-Taif. So He revealed in response to them: Do they apportion the mercy of your Lord? The verse:
11:5: Al-Bukhari narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas regarding his statement: Indeed, they fold their breasts. He said: There were people who were ashamed to relieve themselves and expose their private parts to the sky, and to have intercourse with their wives and expose themselves to the sky, so this was revealed about them. Ibn Jarir and others narrated on the authority of Abdullah bin Shaddad, who said: When one of them passed by the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, he would turn his chest so that he would not see him, so this verse was revealed.
11:8: Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Qatada who said: When the verse, The reckoning of the people has drawn near, was revealed, some people said, The Hour has drawn near. So they desisted, and the people desisted for a little while, then they returned to their evil plotting. Then God revealed, And if We delay the punishment from them for a limited term, the verse. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Ibn Jurayj something similar.
11:114: The two sheikhs narrated on the authority of Ibn Masoud that a man kissed a woman, so he came to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and told him. Then God revealed: “And establish prayer at the two ends of the day and at the approach of the night. Indeed, good deeds do away with misdeeds.” The man said: Is this for me? He said: For all of my nation, all of them. Al-Tirmidhi and others narrated on the authority of Abu Al-Yusr, who said: A woman came to me to buy dates, so I said: There are better ones in the house than these. So she entered the house with me, and I was drawn to her and kissed her. Then I came to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and mentioned that to him. He said: Did you leave behind a warrior in the way of God with his family for something like this? And he lowered his head for a long time until God revealed to him: And establish prayer at the two ends of the day until His saying: For those who remember God. And something similar to it was narrated in the hadith of Abu Umamah, Muadh ibn Jabal, Ibn Abbas, Buraydah, and others. I have completed their hadiths in the translation of the Qur’an.
12:3: Al-Hakim and others narrated on the authority of Sa`d ibn Abi Waqqas that the Qur’an was revealed to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and he recited it to them for a period of time. They said, O Messenger of God, if you would tell us something. So God revealed the best of stories, the verse. Ibn Abi Hatim added, They said, O Messenger of God, if you would remind us. So God revealed, Is it not time for those who have believed that their hearts should become humble? The verse. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: They said: O Messenger of God, if you told us a story, then we would tell you the best of stories. Ibn Mardawayh narrated something similar on the authority of Ibn Masoud.
13:8: At-Tabarani and others narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas that Arbad bin Qais and Amir bin At-Tufayl came to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, in Medina. Amir said, O Muhammad, what will you give me if I become Muslim? He said, You have what the Muslims have and you are responsible for what they are responsible for. He said, Will you give me the authority after you? He said, That is not for you or your people. So they went out. Amir said to Arbad, I am distracting Muhammad from you by talking, so strike him with the sword. So they returned. Amir said, O Muhammad, stand with me so that I may speak to you. So he stood with him and spoke to him. Arbad drew the sword, but when he placed his hand on the hilt of the sword, it dried up. The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, turned and saw him, so he turned away from them. They went out until they were at Arqam. God sent a thunderbolt upon Arbad and killed him. Then God revealed, God knows what every female carries, until His saying, Severe in penalty. Al-Nasa’i and Al-Bazzar narrated on the authority of Anas that he said: The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, sent a man from his companions to a man from the leaders of the pre-Islamic era to call him to God. He said: Who is your Lord to whom you are calling me? Is it iron, copper, silver, or gold? So he came to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and told him. He repeated the question a second and third time, and God sent a thunderbolt upon him that burned him. Then this verse was revealed: And He sends thunderbolts and strikes with them whomever He wills. to the end of it.
13:31: Al-Tabarani and others narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas who said: They said to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace: If it is as you say, then show us our old men so that we may speak to them from the dead, and make spacious for us these mountains, the mountains of Mecca that have embraced us. So the verse was revealed: And if there were a Qur’an by which the mountains could be moved, the verse. K and Ibn Abi Hatim and Ibn Mardawayh narrated on the authority of Atiyah Al-Awfi who said: They said to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, if the mountains of Mecca were moved for us until they became wide so that we could plough them, or if the land were cut up for us as Solomon used to cut down for his people by the wind, or if the dead were brought back to life for us. As Jesus used to revive the dead, God revealed the verse: And if only we had a Qur’an.
13:39: And Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Mujahid who said: Quraysh said when it was revealed: “It is not for a messenger to bring a sign except by the permission of God. We do not see you, O Muhammad, in control of anything. The matter has been completed, so God revealed: God erases whatever He wills and confirms.”
14:28: Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Ata bin Yasar, who said: This verse was revealed about those who were killed on the day of Badr: Have you not seen those who exchanged the favor of God for disbelief?
15:24: The Almighty said: And We have certainly taught the verse. Al-Tirmidhi, Al-Nasa'i, Al-Hakim and others narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: There was a woman praying behind the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, who was beautiful, one of the most beautiful people. Some of the people would move forward until they were in the first row so that they would not see her, and some of them would move back until they were in the last row. When he bowed, he would look from under his armpits. Then God revealed: And We have certainly taught those among you who go forward, and We have certainly taught those who lag behind. K and Ibn Mardawayh narrated on the authority of Dawud bin Salih that he asked Sahl bin Hanif Al-Ansari, And We have certainly known those among you who have gone before, and We have certainly known those who have come after. Was it revealed in the way of God? He said, No, but it is in the rows of prayer.
15:45: The Almighty’s saying: Indeed, the righteous The verse Al-Tha’labi narrated on the authority of Salman Al-Farsi that when he heard the Almighty’s saying: Indeed, Hell is their promised place all together, he fled for three days, escaping from fear, unable to reason. Then the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, was brought to him and he asked him, saying: “O Messenger of God, was this verse revealed: ‘Indeed, Hell is their promised place all together?’ By the One Who sent you with the truth, it has broken my heart.” Then God revealed: Indeed, the righteous will be in gardens and springs.
15:47: The Almighty said: And We have removed from their breasts any sense of resentment, the verse. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Ali bin Al-Hussein that this verse was revealed about Abu Bakr and Umar: And We have removed from their breasts any sense of resentment. It was said: What resentment? He said: The resentment of the Age of Ignorance. There was enmity between Banu Tamim, Banu Adi, and Banu Hashim during the Age of Ignorance. When these people converted to Islam, they loved each other. I took Abu Bakr by the flank, and Ali began to heat his hand and massage Abu Bakr’s flank with it, so this verse was revealed.
15:49: The Almighty said: Inform My servants, verse K. Al-Tabarani narrated on the authority of Abdullah Ibn Al-Zubayr, who said: The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, passed by a group of his companions who were laughing, so he said: Are you laughing while Paradise and Hell are mentioned before you? Then this verse was revealed: Inform My servants that I am the Forgiving, the Most Merciful, and that My punishment is the painful punishment. Ibn Mardawayh narrated from another source on the authority of a man from the companions of the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, who said: The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, looked at us from the door from which the Banu Shaybah entered and said: I do not see you laughing. Then he turned away and retreated and said: I went out until I was at the Black Stone, and Gabriel came and said: O Muhammad, God says to you: Why do you make My servants despair? Inform My servants that I am the Forgiving, the Most Merciful, and that My punishment is the most painful punishment.
15:95: The Almighty said: Indeed, We will suffice you against the mockers. The verse: K Al-Bazzar and Al-Tabarani narrated on the authority of Anas bin Malik that the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, passed by some people in Mecca and they began to nudge him on the back of his head and say: This is the one who claims to be a prophet and Gabriel is with him. So Gabriel nagged his finger and it fell like a fingernail on their bodies and they became sores until they stunk and no one could come near them. Then God revealed: Indeed, We will suffice you against the mockers.
16:1: Ibn Mardawayh narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas that he said: When the command of God came and the companions of the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, were deceived until the verse was revealed: Do not hasten it. So they remained silent. Abdullah bin Al-Imam Ahmad narrated in Zawa’id Al-Zuhd, Ibn Jarir and Ibn Abi Hatim on the authority of Abu Bakr bin Abi Hafs, who said: When the verse The command of God has come, they will stand up was revealed, then it was revealed, so do not hasten it.
16:38: God Almighty said: And they swore the verse. Ibn Jarir and Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Abu Al-Aaliyah, who said: A Muslim man owed a polytheist a debt, so he came to him to collect it. Among what he said and what I hope for after death was such and such. The polytheist said to him: You claim that you will be resurrected after death. So he swore by God with his strongest oath that God will not resurrect anyone who dies. Then the verse was revealed.
16:41: God Almighty said: And those who emigrated, the verse. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Dawud bin Abi Hind, who said: It was revealed: And those who emigrated for the sake of God after they had been wronged, until His saying: And upon their Lord they rely, in Abu Jandal bin Suhayl. God Almighty said: God sets forth an example, the verse. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas regarding his statement: God sets forth an example: a slave owned by a man. He said: It was revealed about a man from Quraysh and his slave. Regarding his statement: Two men, one of whom was mute, he said: It was revealed about Uthman and a freedman of his who hated Islam and refused it and forbade him from charity and good deeds, so it was revealed about them both.
16:83: The Almighty said: They recognize the favor of God, the verse. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Mujahid that a Bedouin came to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and asked him, so he recited to him: And God has made for you, from your houses, a place of rest. The Bedouin said: Yes. Then he recited to him: And He has made for you, from the hides of cattle, houses which you find light on the day of your stabbing and the day of your staying. He said: Yes. Then he recited to him all of that, saying: Yes, until he reached: Thus He perfects His favor upon you that you may submit. So the Bedouin turned away, so God revealed: They recognize the favor of God, then they deny it, and most of them are disbelievers.
16:91: God Almighty said: And fulfill the verse. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Buraidah who said: This verse was revealed regarding the pledge of allegiance to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace.
16:92: God Almighty said: And do not be like the verse like Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Abu Bakr bin Abi Hafs, who said: Sa’idah al-Asadiyyah was crazy and used to collect hair and fibres, so this verse was revealed: And do not be like the one who undoes her spinning.
16:103: God Almighty said: And We certainly know that they say: It is only a human being who teaches him. The verse: Ibn Jarir narrated with a weak chain of transmission on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, taught a blacksmith in Mecca whose name was Balaam, and he was a foreigner. The polytheists used to see the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, entering and leaving him, so they said: It is only Balaam who teaches him. So God revealed: And We certainly know that they say: It is only a human being who teaches him. The verse: Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Hisin on the authority of Abdullah bin Muslim Al-Hadrami, who said: We had two slaves, one of whom was called Yasar and the other Jabr, and they were Sicilians. They used to recite their book and teach their knowledge, and the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, would pass by them and listen to their recitation. They said: He only teaches from them, so this was revealed.
16:106: The Almighty said: Except for one who is forced. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated the verse on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: When the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, wanted to migrate to Medina, the polytheists took Bilal, Khabab, and Ammar ibn Yasir. As for Ammar, he said to them a word that pleased them out of caution. When he returned to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, he told him and said: How was your heart when you said it? Was it pleased with what you said? He said: No. Then God revealed: Except for one who is forced and whose heart is at rest with faith. And he narrated on the authority of Mujahid, who said: This verse was revealed about some people from Mecca who believed, so some of the Companions in Medina wrote to them to emigrate, so they left, intending to go to Medina, but the Quraysh caught up with them on the road and tempted them, so they disbelieved under duress, so this verse was revealed about them.
16:110: And Ibn Saad narrated in Al-Tabaqat on the authority of Omar bin Al-Hakam, who said: Ammar bin Yasir was tortured until he did not know what he was saying, and Suhaib was tortured until he did not know what he was saying, and Abu Fakeeh was tortured until he did not know what he was saying, and Bilal and Amer bin Fuhaira and a group of Muslims, and this verse was revealed about them: Then indeed, your Lord is for those who emigrated after they were persecuted.
16:126: The Almighty said: And if you punish, Al-Hakim, Al-Bayhaqi in Al-Dala’il and Al-Bazzar narrated on the authority of Abu Hurairah that the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, stood over Hamza when he was martyred and he had mutilated him and said: I will mutilate seventy of them in your place. Then Gabriel came down and the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, was standing with the last verses of Surat al-Nahl 16:And if you punish, then punish with the like of that with which you were punished until the end of the surah. Then the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, refrained and held back from what he wanted. Al-Tirmidhi narrated, and he authenticated it, and Al-Hakim narrated on the authority of Abi bin Kaab, who said: When the Day of Uhud occurred, sixty-four of the Ansar were killed, and six of the Muhajireen, including Hamza, were mutilated. The Ansar said: If we kill them on a day like this, we will annihilate them. When the Day of the Conquest of Mecca occurred, God revealed: And if you punish, then punish. The apparent meaning of this is that its revelation was delayed until the conquest. In the hadith before it, it was revealed at Uhud. Ibn Al-Hisar gathered that it was revealed first in Mecca, then secondly at Uhud, then thirdly on the Day of the Conquest, as a reminder from God to His servants.
17:15: God Almighty said: And no bearer of burdens shall bear the burden of another, the verse. Ibn Abd al-Barr narrated with a weak chain of transmission on the authority of Aisha, who said: Khadija asked the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, about the children of the polytheists, and he said: They are from their fathers. Then she asked him after that, and he said: God knows best what they used to do. Then she asked him after Islam had become firmly established, and the verse: And no bearer of burdens shall bear the burden of another was revealed. He said: They are upon the fitrah, or he said: in Paradise.
17:26: The Almighty said: And give to the near of kin the verse. Al-Tabarani and others narrated on the authority of Abu Saeed Al-Khudri, who said: When the verse: And give to the near of kin his right was revealed, the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, called Fatima and gave her Fadak. Ibn Kathir said: This is problematic, as it indicates that the verse is Medinan, but the well-known opinion is the opposite. Mardawayh narrated something similar on the authority of Ibn Abbas. God Almighty said: And when you recite the Qur’an, the verse. Ibn al-Mundhir narrated on the authority of Ibn Shihab that when the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, recited the Qur’an to the polytheists of Quraysh and called them to the Book, they said: They mock at it. Our hearts are covered from that to which you call us to God, and in our ears is deafness, and between us and you is a veil. So God revealed concerning that of their saying: And when you recite the Qur’an, the verses.
17:28: The Almighty said: But if you turn away from them, seeking mercy verse. Saeed bin Mansour narrated on the authority of Ata’ al-Khurasani, who said: Some people from Muzaynah came to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, asking for a mount to carry them. He said: I do not find anything to carry you on. So they turned away, their eyes overflowing with tears of grief. They thought that was because the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, was angry. So God revealed: But if you turn away from them, seeking mercy verse. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of al-Dahhak, who said: It was revealed about those who used to ask the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, from among the poor.
17:29: God Almighty said: And do not make your hand to be chained to your neck nor stretch it out, the verse. Saeed bin Mansour narrated on the authority of Sayyar Abu al-Hakam who said: The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, was given wheat, and he was a generous giver, so he distributed it among the people. Then some people came to him and found that he had finished with it, so God revealed: And do not make your hand to be chained to your neck nor stretch it out, the verse. Ibn Mardawayh and others narrated on the authority of Ibn Masoud who said: A young boy came to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and said: My mother asks you for such and such. He said: We have nothing today. He said: So she says to you: Put on your shirt. So he took off his shirt and gave it to him, and he sat in the house bareheaded. Then God revealed: And do not make your hand chained to your neck nor stretch it forth completely lest you sit down blamed and destitute. K and he also narrated on the authority of Abu Umamah that the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, said to Aisha: Spend what is on the back of my hand. She said: Then nothing will remain. So God revealed: And do not make your hand chained to your neck, the verse. It appears from this that it is Medinan.
17:56: As God Almighty said, Say, Call upon those you claim besides Him, the verse. Al-Bukhari and others narrated on the authority of Ibn Masoud, who said, “There were people from among mankind who worshipped people from among the jinn, so the jinn converted to Islam and the others held fast to their worship, so God revealed, Say, Call upon those you claim besides Him, the verse.”
17:59: The Almighty said: And what prevented Us from sending the signs except that the former ones denied them, the verse. Al-Hakim, Al-Tabarani, and others narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas that he said: The people of Mecca asked the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, to turn Safa into gold for them and to remove the mountains from them so that they could farm. It was said to him: If you wish, you can take your time with them, or you can give them what they asked for. But if they disbelieve, they will be destroyed as those before them were destroyed. He said: Rather, I will take my time with them. So God revealed: And nothing prevented Us from sending the signs except that the former ones denied them, the verse. Al-Tabarani and Ibn Mardawayh narrated from Al-Zubayr something similar, but more simply.
17:60: The Almighty said: And We did not make the vision which We showed you except a trial for the people. Abu Ya`la narrated on the authority of Umm Hani` that when the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, was taken on the Night Journey, he began to speak to a group of Quraysh who mocked him. They asked him for a sign, so he described to them the Holy House and mentioned to them the story of the caravan. Al-Walid ibn al-Mughira said: This is a magician. Then God revealed: And We did not make the vision which We showed you except as a trial for the people. Ibn al-Mundhir narrated something similar on the authority of al-Hasan. Ibn Mardawayh narrated on the authority of Al-Husayn ibn Ali that the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, woke up one day worried, so it was said to him: What is the matter with you, O Messenger of God? Do not worry, for your vision is a trial for them. So God revealed: And We did not make the vision which We showed you except a trial for the people. Ibn Jarir narrated something similar from the hadith of Sahl ibn Sa`d, and Ibn Abi Hatim narrated something similar from the hadith of `Amr ibn al-`As, from the hadith of Ya`la ibn Murrah, and from the mursal of Sa`id ibn al-Musayyab, and their chains of transmission are weak. God Almighty said: And the accursed tree in the Qur’an verse. Ibn Abi Hatim and Al-Bayhaqi narrated in Al-Ba’th on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: God mentioned Zaqqum to frighten this tribe of Quraysh with it. Abu Jahl said: Do you know what this Zaqqum is that Muhammad frightens you with? They said: No. He said: Thread with butter, “But if we have power over it, We will surely make it flow abundantly.” So God revealed: And the accursed tree in the Qur’an. And We frighten them, but it only increases them in great transgression. And He revealed: Indeed, the tree of Zaqqum is food for the sinful.
17:73: The Almighty said: And they almost tempted you, the verse. Ibn Mardawayh and Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Ishaq on the authority of Muhammad on the authority of Ikrimah on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: Umayyah ibn Khalaf, Abu Jahl ibn Hisham, and some men from Quraysh went out and came to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and said: O Muhammad, come and touch our gods and we will enter your religion with you. He loved the Islam of his people and felt sorry for them, so God revealed: And they almost tempted you away from that which We have revealed to you to a helper. I said: This is the most authentic of what has been reported about the reason for its revelation, and it is a good chain of transmission and it has a corroborating evidence. Abu al-Sheikh narrated on the authority of Saeed ibn Jubayr, who said: The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, was touching the Black Stone, so they said: We will not let you touch it until you touch our gods. The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, said: What is wrong with me if I did, and God knows that I am different? So it was revealed. He narrated something similar on the authority of Ibn Shihab, and he narrated on the authority of Jubayr ibn Nufayr that Quraysh came to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and said: If you have sent to us, then expel those who followed you from the scum of the people and their clients, and we will be your companions. So he leaned towards them and I went down And he narrated on the authority of Muhammad bin Ka’b Al-Qurazi that he, may God bless him and grant him peace, recited By the star until Have you considered Al-Lat and Al-’Uzza? Then Satan threw at him Those are the exalted cranes, and indeed their intercession is to be hoped for. So it was revealed. He remained worried until God revealed: “And We did not send before you any messenger or prophet except that when he made a wish, Satan threw into his wish. So God abrogates what Satan throws in, then God decides.” The verse. And in this is evidence that these verses are Meccan. And whoever considers them Medinan relied on what Ibn Mardawayh narrated on the authority of Al-Awfi on the authority of Ibn Abbas that Shu’b said to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, “Give us a year until he guides to our gods. If we take away the one who guides to the gods, we will protect him. Then we will submit.” So he intended to give them a year, so it was revealed. Its chain of transmission is weak.
17:76: The Almighty said: And indeed they almost provoked you from the land to expel you from it, and He ordered him to return to Medina, and Gabriel said to him: Ask your Lord, for every prophet has a question. He said: What do you command me to ask? He said: Say: My Lord, let me enter with a truthful entrance and exit with a truthful exit, and grant me from Yourself a supporting authority. These were revealed about his return from Tabuk. This is a weak mursal hadith, and it has a corroborating narration from Saeed bin Jubayr with Ibn Abi Hatim, and its wording is: The polytheists said to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, that the prophets used to live in the Levant, so what is the matter with you? And the city understood that he was going to be diagnosed, so it was revealed and he has another way sent by Ibn Jarir that some of the Jews told him
17:80: The Almighty said: And say, "My Lord, let me enter the verse." Al-Tirmidhi narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: The Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, was in Mecca, then he was commanded to emigrate, so the verse was revealed to him: And say, "My Lord, let me enter a truthful entrance and a truthful exit, and grant me from Yourself a supporting authority." This is explicit that the verse is Meccan, and Ibn Mardawayh narrated it with a more explicit wording than it.
17:85: The Almighty said: They ask you about the spirit, the verse. Al-Bukhari narrated on the authority of Ibn Masoud, who said: I was walking with the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, in Medina, and he was leaning on a palm branch. He passed by a group of Quraysh, and some of them said: Why don’t you ask him? They said: Tell us about the spirit. He stood for a while and raised his head, and I knew that revelation was being sent to him until the revelation ascended, then he said: The spirit is from the command of my Lord, and you have been given only a little knowledge. Al-Tirmidhi narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas who said: Quraysh said to the Jews: Teach us something so we can ask this man. They said: Ask him about the soul.
