So they asked him, and God revealed: And they ask you about the soul. Say: The soul is from the command of my Lord. Ibn Kathir reconciles the two hadiths by the multiple revelations, and so does Al-Hafiz Ibn Hajar. Or his silence when the Jews asked him is to be interpreted as an expectation of further clarification on that, otherwise what is in the Sahih is more authentic. I said: What is in the Sahih is preferred because the narrator of the story was present, unlike Ibn Abbas.
17:88: The Almighty said: Say: If mankind and the jinn gathered to produce the like of this Qur’an, they could not produce the like of it. So God revealed: Say: If mankind and the jinn gathered to produce the like of this Qur’an, they could not produce the like of it.
17:90: The Almighty said: And they said: We will never believe you, the verse. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Ibn Ishaq on the authority of a sheikh from the people of Egypt on the authority of Ikrimah on the authority of Ibn Abbas that Utbah and Shaibah, the sons of Rabiah, Abu Sufyan ibn Harb, a man from Banu Abd al-Dar, Abu al-Buhturi, al-Aswad ibn al-Muttalib, Rabiah ibn al-Aswad, al-Walid ibn al-Mughira, Abu Jahl, Abdullah ibn Umayyah, Umayyah ibn Khalaf, al-Aasi ibn Wa’il, Nabiha, and Munabbiha, the sons of al-Hajjaj, gathered and said: O Muhammad, we do not know of a man from the Arabs who has brought to his people what you have brought to your people. You have cursed the fathers, reviled the religion, belittled dreams, reviled the gods, and divided the community. There is no ugly thing that I have not done in what we have said between us and you. If you have only come with this hadith seeking money, we will collect for you from our money so that you will have more money. If you are only seeking honor among us, we will make you our leader. If this one who brings you what he brings to you is a vision that you see as overwhelming, we will spend our money in seeking knowledge until we exonerate you from him. The Messenger of God said: God's Messenger (may peace be upon him) said: I do not have what you say, but God has sent me to you as a Messenger and has revealed to me a Book and has commanded me to be a bringer of good tidings and a warner to you. They said: "If you do not accept what we have offered you, then you know that there is no one among the people who is more constricted in his land, less in wealth, or more difficult in his life than us. So ask your Lord who sent you for us to remove these mountains that have become constricted for us, and to expand our land for us, and to make rivers flow in it like the rivers of Syria and Iraq, and to send to us those of our fathers who have passed away. If you do not do that, then ask your Lord for a king who will confirm what you say and to make for us gardens, treasures, and palaces of gold and silver, with which we will help you in what we see you seeking. You stand in the markets and seek a living. If you do not do that, then let the sky fall as you claim that your Lord, if He wills, will do, for we will not believe you unless you do that." The Messenger of God (may peace be upon him) stood up from them, and Abdullah ibn Abi Umayya stood up with him and said: "O Muhammad, your people have offered you what they have offered you, and you did not accept it. Then they asked you for themselves matters so that they might know your status with God, but you did not do that. Then they asked you to hasten." What you are threatening them with of punishment, by God I will never believe in you until you take a ladder to the sky and ascend it while I watch until you reach it and bring with you a published copy and with you four angels who will testify for you that you are as you say. So the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, turned away sad, and what Abdullah bin Abi Umayya had said was revealed to him. They said, We will never believe you until he says, A human being, a messenger. Saeed bin Mansour included in his Sunan on the authority of Saeed bin Jubair regarding his statement, And they said, ‘We will never believe you.’ He said, “It was revealed about the brother of Umm Salamah, Abdullah bin Abi Umayya. It is a sound mursal hadith that supports what came before it and makes up for the ambiguity in its chain of transmission.”
17:110: God Almighty said: Say: Call upon God, the verse. Ibn Mardawayh and others narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas that the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, was in Mecca one day and supplicated, saying in his supplication: O God, O Most Merciful. The polytheists said: Look at this Sabean who forbids us from calling upon the easy-going, while he is calling upon the easy-going. Then God revealed: Say: Call upon God or call upon the Most Merciful. Whichever you call upon, He has the best names. The Almighty said: And do not recite your prayer aloud, nor in a low voice. Al-Bukhari and others narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas regarding his statement: And do not recite your prayer aloud, nor in a low voice. He said: “It was revealed while the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, was hiding in Mecca. When he prayed with his companions, he would raise his voice with the Qur’an. When the polytheists heard the Qur’an, they would curse it, the one who revealed it, and the one who brought it.” So the verse was revealed. Al-Bukhari also narrated on the authority of Aisha that it was revealed in supplication. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas something similar, then he preferred the first because it has a more authentic chain of transmission. Al-Nawawi and others also preferred it. Al-Hafiz Ibn Hajar said, but it is possible to combine them because it was revealed in supplication within the prayer. Ibn Mardawayh narrated on the authority of Abu Hurayrah that he said, “When the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, prayed at the House, he would raise his voice in supplication, so we revealed...” Ibn Jarir and Al-Hakim narrated on the authority of Aisha that she said: This verse was revealed in the testimony of faith, and its meaning is explained in the previous narration. And Ibn Mani’ in his Musnad narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas that they used to recite the supplication aloud: O God, have mercy on me, so it was revealed, and they were commanded not to recite it in a low voice nor to recite it aloud.
17:111: God Almighty said: And say, Praise be to God, the verse. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Muhammad bin Ka'b al-Qurazi, who said: The Jews and Christians said: God has taken a son, and the Arabs said: "Here I am, you have no partner except a partner who is yours. You own him and what he owns." And the Sabians and Magians said: Were it not for the allies of God, he would have been humiliated. So God revealed: And say, Praise be to God, who has not taken a son and has no partner in dominion.
18:1: Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Ibn Ishaq on the authority of a sheikh from the people of Egypt on the authority of Ikrimah on the authority of Ibn Abbas who said: The Quraysh sent Al-Nadr bin Al-Harith and Uqbah bin Abi Mu'ayt to the Jewish rabbis in Medina and said to them: Ask them about Muhammad and describe his characteristics to them and inform them of what he said, for they are the people of the first book and they have what we do not have of the knowledge of the prophets. So they went out until they reached Medina and asked the Jewish rabbis about the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and described to them his affair and some of his sayings. They said to them: Ask him about three things. If he tells you about them, then he is a prophet sent, and if he does not, then the man is a liar. Ask him about the youths who passed away in the first era, what was their affair, for they had an amazing affair. Ask him about a wandering man who reached the easts and wests of the earth, what was his report, and ask him about the spirit, what is it? So they set out until they reached the Quraysh and said: We have come to you with a decisive statement between you and Muhammad. So they came to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and asked him. He said: I will tell you tomorrow what you asked about, and he did not ask for more. So they departed and the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, remained for fifteen nights without God revealing to him any revelation about that and Gabriel not coming to him until the people of Mecca were alarmed and until the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, was saddened by the delay in revelation from him and what the people of Mecca were saying was difficult for him. Then Gabriel came to him from God with the Surah of the People of the Cave in which was His reproach to him for his sadness over them and the report of what they asked him about regarding the matter of the young men and the man performing the circumambulation and the statement of God, And they ask you about the spirit. Ibn Mardawayh narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas that he said: Utbah ibn Rabi’ah, Shaiba ibn Rabi’ah, Abu Jahl ibn Hisham, Al-Nadr ibn Al-Harith, Umayyah ibn Khalaf, Al-Aasi ibn Wa’il, Al-Aswad ibn Al-Muttalib, and Abu Al-Buhturi met with a group of Quraysh. The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, was greatly grieved by what he saw of his people’s opposition to him and their denial of the advice he had brought, so it saddened him greatly. Then God revealed: Perhaps you would kill yourself with grief over their traces verse. Ibn Mardawayh also narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: And they remained in their cave for three hundred years. It was said: O Messenger of God, years or months. So God revealed: Years, and they increased by nine.
18:23: And Ibn Jarir narrated it on the authority of Al-Dahhak, and Ibn Mardawayh also narrated it on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: The Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, swore an oath, and forty nights passed, then God revealed: And never say of anything, I will do that tomorrow, except if God wills.
18:28: God Almighty said: And be patient with yourself. The verse was presented as a reason for revelation in Surat Al-An’am in the hadith of Khabbab. God Almighty said: And do not obey the verse. Ibn Mardawayh narrated on the authority of Juwaybir on the authority of Ad-Dahhak on the authority of Ibn Abbas regarding His statement: And do not obey one whose heart We have made heedless of Our remembrance. He said: It was revealed regarding Umayyah ibn Khalaf Al-Jumahi, and that was because he called the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, to do something that God disliked, which was to expel the poor from him and bring close the leaders of the people of Mecca. So it was revealed. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Al-Rabi’ who said: He told us that the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, confronted Umayyah ibn Khalaf while he was absent-minded and unaware of what was being said to him, so this verse was revealed. He narrated on the authority of Abu Hurayrah who said: Uyaynah ibn Hisn entered upon the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and Salman was with him. Uyaynah said: When we come to you, bring this one out and bring us in. So this verse was revealed.
18:109: The Almighty said: Say, If the sea were ink for the words of my Lord, the verse. Al-Hakim and others narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas that he said: Quraysh said to the Jews: Give us something that we can ask this man about. They said: Ask him about the spirit. So they asked him, and the verse was revealed: And they ask you about the spirit. Say: The spirit is from the command of my Lord. And you have not been given of knowledge except a little. And the Jews said: We have been given much knowledge. We have been given the Torah, and whoever has been given the Torah has certainly been given much good. So the verse was revealed: Say, If the sea were ink for the words of my Lord, the verse.
18:110: The Almighty said: So whoever hopes to meet his Lord, the verse. Ibn Abi Hatim and Ibn Abi Al-Dunya narrated in the Book of Sincerity on the authority of Tawus, who said: A man said: O Messenger of God, I stand seeking the face of God and I would like to see my homeland. But he did not respond to him at all until this verse was revealed: So whoever hopes to meet his Lord, let him do righteous work and not associate anyone in the worship of his Lord. Al-Hakim narrated it in Al-Mustadrak in a connected chain of transmission on the authority of Tawus on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who authenticated it according to the conditions of the two sheikhs. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Mujahid that a man from the Muslims was fighting and he wanted to see his place, so God revealed: Whoever hopes to meet his Lord verse. Abu Nu’aym and Ibn Asakir narrated in his history on the authority of al-Suddi al-Saghir on the authority of al-Kalbi on the authority of Abu Salih on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: Jundub bin Zuhair said: If a man prays or fasts or gives charity and is mentioned with good, he will be comforted by it, so he increased in that because of what people said to him, so God revealed concerning that: Whoever hopes to meet his Lord verse.
19:64: The Almighty said: And we do not descend except by the command of your Lord, the verse. Al-Bukhari narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas that the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, said to Gabriel: What prevents you from visiting us more often than you visit us? So the verse was revealed: And we do not descend except by the command of your Lord. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Ikrimah, who said: Gabriel was slow in descending for forty days, and he mentioned something similar. Ibn Mardawayh narrated on the authority of Anas, who said: The Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, asked Gabriel: Which of the places is most beloved to God and most hated to God? He said: I do not know until I ask. Then Gabriel descended, and he had been slow to come to him. He said: You were so slow to come to me that I thought you would see some resentment on me. He said: We do not descend except by the command of your Lord. Ibn Ishaq narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas that when the Quraysh asked about the People of the Cave, God remained for fifteen nights without revealing anything to him about that. Then when Gabriel came down, he said to him, You have been slow. So he mentioned it.
19:77: The Almighty said: Have you seen he who disbelieved in Our verses? The two Sheikhs and others narrated on the authority of Khabbab bin Al-Arat who said: I came to Al-Aasi bin Wa’il Al-Sahmi to collect a right that I had from him. He said: I will not give it to you until you disbelieve in Muhammad. I said: No, not until you die and not until you are resurrected. He said: Then I will die and then be resurrected. I said: Yes. He said: I have wealth and children there, so I will pay you. Then the verse was revealed: Have you seen he who disbelieved in Our verses and said: I will surely be given wealth and children?
19:96: God Almighty said: Indeed, those who believed - Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Abd al-Rahman ibn Awf when he migrated to Medina, he found himself separated from his companions in Mecca, including Shaiba and Utba, the sons of Rabi’ah, and Umayya ibn Khalaf, so God revealed: Indeed, those who believed and did righteous deeds - the Most Merciful will appoint for them love. He said: Love in the hearts of the believers.
20:1: Ibn Mardawayh narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas that the first thing that God revealed to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, was to stand on the soles of his feet when he prayed. Then God revealed Ta-Ha: We have not sent down the Qur’an to you to make you miserable. Abdullah bin Hamid narrated in his interpretation on the authority of Al-Rabi’ bin Anas, who said: The Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, would alternate between his feet to stand on each foot until the verse was revealed: We have not sent down the Qur’an to you to make you miserable. Ibn Mardawayh narrated on the authority of Al-Awfi on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: They said: This man has been made miserable by his Lord. So God revealed Ta-Ha: We have not sent down the Qur’an to you to make you miserable.
20:2: Ibn Mardawayh narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas that the first thing that God revealed to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, was to stand on the soles of his feet when he prayed. Then God revealed Ta-Ha: We have not sent down the Qur’an to you to make you miserable. Abdullah bin Hamid narrated in his interpretation on the authority of Al-Rabi’ bin Anas, who said: The Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, would alternate between his feet to stand on each foot until the verse was revealed: We have not sent down the Qur’an to you to make you miserable. Ibn Mardawayh narrated on the authority of Al-Awfi on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: They said: This man has been made miserable by his Lord. So God revealed Ta-Ha: We have not sent down the Qur’an to you to make you miserable.
20:105: God Almighty said: And they ask you about the mountains, the verse. Ibn al-Mundhir narrated on the authority of Ibn Jurayj that the Quraysh said: O Muhammad, how will your Lord deal with these mountains on the Day of Resurrection? So the verse was revealed: And they ask you about the mountains, the verse.
20:114: The Almighty said: And do not hasten with the Qur’an before the verse. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Al-Suddi who said: When Gabriel came down to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, with the Qur’an, he would tire himself out memorizing it until it became difficult for him and he would fear that Gabriel would ascend without him having memorized it. So God revealed: And do not hasten with the Qur’an before the verse. Another reason was mentioned in Surat An-Nisa’, and this is more correct.
20:131: The Almighty said: And do not extend your eyes toward what We have given to [some] categories of them. Ibn Abi Shaybah, Ibn Mardawayh, Al-Bazzar, and Abu Ya`la narrated on the authority of Abu Rafi` who said: The Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, hosted a guest and sent me to a Jewish man to lend me flour until the crescent of Rajab. He said: No, not unless I prove it. So I came to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and informed him. He said: By God, I am trustworthy in heaven and trustworthy on earth. So I did not leave him until this verse was revealed: And do not extend your eyes toward what We have given to [some] categories of them.
21:6: Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Qatada who said: The people of Mecca said to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace: If what you say is true and it pleases you that we believe, then turn Safa into gold for us. Gabriel, peace be upon him, came to him and said: If you wish, then what your people asked of you will happen, but if it happens and they do not believe, then they will not be given respite. And if you wish, then you can ask your people for help. Then God revealed: “No town believed before them which We destroyed. Will they believe?”
21:34: Ibn al-Mundhir narrated on the authority of Ibn Jurayj who said: The death of the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, was announced, so he said: O Lord, who will be for my nation? Then the verse was revealed: And We have not granted to any human being before you immortality.
21:36: Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Al-Suddi who said: The Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, passed by Abu Jahl and Abu Sufyan while they were talking. When Abu Jahl saw him, he laughed and said to Abu Sufyan: This is the prophet of Banu Abd Manaf. Abu Sufyan became angry and said: Do you deny that Banu Abd Manaf has a prophet? The Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, heard them and returned to Abu Jahl, attacked him and frightened him. He said: I do not see you ending until what happened to you happens to you, other than his covenant. Then the verse was revealed: And when those who disbelieve see you, they take you only in ridicule.
21:101: Al-Hakim narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: When the verse was revealed: You and what you worship besides God are fuel for Hell; you will surely enter it, Ibn Al-Zubayri said: Abd Al-Shams, the moon, the angels, and Aziz, so all of these will be in the Fire with our gods. Then the verse was revealed: Those for whom good has preceded from Us - those will be removed from it. Then the verse was revealed: And when the son of Mary is cited as an example, to the opponents.
22:3: The Almighty said: And among the people is he who disputes about God. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Abu Malik regarding his saying: And among the people is he who disputes about God. He said: It was revealed about Al-Nadr bin Al-Harith.
22:11: The Almighty said: And among the people is he who worships God according to the letter of the verse. Al-Bukhari narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas that he said: A man would come to Medina and convert to Islam. If his wife gave birth to a boy and his horses bore fruit, he would say: This is a good religion. But if his wife did not give birth to a boy and his horses did not bear fruit, he would say: This is a bad religion. Then God revealed: And among the people is he who worships God according to the letter of the verse. Ibn Mardawayh narrated on the authority of Atiyah Ibn Masoud, who said: A Jewish man converted to Islam and lost his sight, wealth, and children. He took Islam as a bad omen and said: I have not gained any good from this religion of mine. My sight, wealth, and children have died. So the verse was revealed: And among the people is he who worships God according to the letter of the verse.
22:19: The Almighty said: These two are opponents. The verse: The two sheikhs and others narrated on the authority of Abu Dharr, who said: This verse was revealed: These two are opponents who disputed about their Lord, about Hamza and Ubaidah and Ali ibn Abi Talib and Utbah and Shaibah and Al-Walid ibn Utbah. Al-Hakim narrated on the authority of Ali, who said: This verse was revealed about us in our duel on the day of Badr: These two are opponents who disputed about their Lord, until His saying: The burning. And he narrated from another source on his authority that he said: It was revealed about those who fought on the day of Badr: Hamza, Ali, Ubaidah ibn al-Harith, Utbah ibn Rabi’ah, Shaiba ibn Rabi’ah, and al-Walid ibn Utbah. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Al-Awfi on the authority of Ibn Abbas that it was revealed about the People of the Book. They said to the believers, We are more worthy of God than you, and our Book is older, and our Prophet is before your Prophet. The believers said, “We are more worthy of God. We believe in Muhammad and your Prophet and in what God has revealed of the Book.” Ibn Abi Hatim narrated something similar on the authority of Qatada.
22:25: The Almighty said: And whoever intends therein to commit an act of injustice, the verse Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: The Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, sent Abdullah bin Unays with two men, one of whom was an immigrant and the other from the Ansar, and they boasted about their lineage. Abdullah bin Unays became angry and killed the Ansar, then he apostatized from Islam and fled to Mecca, so the verse was revealed about him: And whoever intends therein to commit an act of injustice, the verse.
22:27: God Almighty said: And on every lean camel verse. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Mujahid who said: They did not ride, then God revealed: They will come to you on foot and on every lean camel. So He commanded them to take provisions and permitted them to ride and trade.
22:37: The Almighty said: Their meat will not reach God, the verse. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Ibn Jurayj who said: The people of the Age of Ignorance used to smear the Kaaba with camel meat and blood. The companions of the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, said: We are more deserving of being smeared. So God revealed: Their meat will not reach God, the verse.
22:39: God Almighty said: Permission is given to those who are being fought, because they have been wronged, and God is indeed able to give them victory. Ahmad, Al-Tirmidhi, Hasanah, and Al-Hakim narrated it and authenticated it on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: The Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, left Mecca, and Abu Bakr said: They have expelled their Prophet so that he may perish. So God revealed: Permission is given to those who are being fought, because they have been wronged, and God is indeed able to give them victory.
22:52: The Almighty said: And We did not send before you any messenger or prophet, the verse. Ibn Abi Hatim, Ibn Jarir and Ibn Al-Mundhir narrated from a chain of transmission with a sound chain of transmission on the authority of Saeed bin Jubair who said: The Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, recited in Mecca: By the star. Then when he reached: Have you considered Al-Lat and Al-Uzza and Manat, the third, the other? Satan cast upon his tongue: These are the exalted cranes. And indeed, their intercession is to be hoped for. The polytheists said: He did not mention our gods with any good before today. So he prostrated and they prostrated, and then the verse was revealed: And We did not send before you any messenger or prophet, the verse. Al-Bazzar and Ibn Mardawayh narrated it from another chain of transmission on the authority of Saeed bin Jubair on the authority of Ibn Abbas, as I think, and he said: It is not narrated connected except with this chain of transmission, and Umayyah bin Khalid was the only one who connected it, and he is a trustworthy and famous person. Al-Bukhari narrated it from Ibn Abbas with a chain of transmission that includes Al-Waqidi and Ibn Mardawayh on the authority of Al-Kalbi on the authority of Abi Salih on the authority of Ibn Abbas and Ibn Jarir on the authority of Al-Awfi on the authority of Ibn Abbas. Ibn Ishaq included it in Al-Seerah on the authority of Muhammad bin Kaab and Musa bin Uqbah on the authority of Ibn Shihab and Ibn Jarir on the authority of Muhammad bin Qais and Ibn Abi Hatim. On the authority of al-Suddi, they all have the same meaning and they are all either weak or disconnected except for the first path of Sa`id ibn Jubayr. Al-Hafiz Ibn Hajar said: But the large number of paths indicates that the story has a basis, even though it has two authentic, mursal paths that Ibn Jarir included, one of them on the authority of al-Zuhri on the authority of Abu Bakr ibn `Abd al-Rahman ibn al-Harith ibn Hisham and the other on the authority of Dawud ibn Hind on the authority of Abu al-`Aliyah, and it is not to be taken seriously. Ibn al-`Arabi and `Iyad say that these narrations are invalid and have no basis. End quote.
22:60: The Almighty said: And whoever punishes with the like of that with which he was punished, the verse Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Muqatil that it was revealed regarding a secret mission that the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, sent out. They encountered the polytheists two nights before the end of Muharram. The polytheists said to each other: Fight the companions of Muhammad, for they forbid fighting in the sacred month. The companions appealed to them and reminded them by God not to expose themselves to fighting them, for they do not consider fighting permissible in the sacred month. The polytheists refused that and fought them and rebelled against them. The Muslims fought them and were victorious over them, so this verse was revealed.
23:2: Al-Hakim narrated on the authority of Abu Hurairah that when the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, prayed, he would raise his gaze to the sky, so the verse, Those who are humble in their prayer, was revealed. So he lowered his head. Ibn Mardawayh narrated it with the wording, He used to turn around in prayer. Saeed bin Mansour narrated it on the authority of Ibn Sirin, without a chain of transmission, with the wording, He used to turn his gaze, so the verse was revealed.
23:14: Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Ibn Sirin, without a chain of transmission, that the Companions would raise their eyes to the sky in prayer, so it was revealed. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Umar, who said: I agreed with my Lord in four things, and it was revealed: And We created man from an extract of clay, the verse. So when it was revealed, I said: So blessed be God, the best of creators.
23:67: Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Saeed bin Jubair, who said: The Quraysh used to spend the night around the House, but they did not circumambulate it, and they were proud of it. So God revealed, arrogantly, spending the night around it, ignoring it.
23:76: Al-Nasa'i and Al-Hakim narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas that he said: Abu Sufyan came to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and said: O Muhammad, I ask you by God and the womb, we have eaten the hair and blood. So God revealed: We seized them with punishment, but they did not submit to their Lord, nor did they supplicate. Al-Bayhaqi included in Al-Dala’il with the wording that when Ibn Ayaz Al-Hanafi was brought to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, as a prisoner, he released him and converted to Islam. He then went to Mecca and returned, and he prevented the people of Mecca from getting provisions from Al-Yamamah until the Quraysh ate the ‘al-‘alhaz. Abu Sufyan came to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and said: Do you not claim that you were sent as a mercy to the worlds? He said: Yes. He said: You killed fathers with the sword and sons with hunger. Then this verse was revealed.
24:3: God Almighty said: The adulterer shall not marry except an adulteress. An-Nasa’i narrated on the authority of Abdullah ibn Amr, who said: There was a woman called Umm Mahzul who used to commit adultery. A man from among the companions of the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, wanted to marry her. So God revealed: And the adulteress shall not marry except an adulterer or a polytheist, and that this is forbidden to the believers. Abu Dawud, At-Tirmidhi, An-Nasa’i, and Al-Hakim narrated on the authority of Amr ibn Shu’ayb, on the authority of his father, on the authority of his grandfather, who said: A man called Mazid used to travel from Anbar to Mecca until he came to them. There was a woman in Mecca who was a friend of his called Anaq, so he asked the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, for permission to marry her, but he did not answer him at all until the verse: The adulterer shall not marry except an adulteress or a polytheist was revealed. So the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, said: O Mazid, the adulterer shall not marry except an adulteress or a polytheist, so do not marry her. Saeed bin Mansour narrated on the authority of Mujahid who said: When God forbade adultery, there were some women who had camels, so the people said: Let them go and get married. So this verse was revealed.
24:6: The Almighty said: And those who accuse their wives, the verse. Al-Bukhari narrated on the authority of Ikrimah on the authority of Ibn Abbas that Hilal bin Umayyah slandered his wife in the presence of the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, so the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, said to him: Proof or a lash on your back. He said: O Messenger of God, if one of us sees a man with his wife, he goes out seeking proof. The Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, kept saying: Proof or a lash on your back. Hilal said: By Him who sent you with the truth, I am telling the truth, and God will send down that which will clear my back of the punishment. Gabriel came down and God revealed to him: And those who accuse their wives. He recited until he reached: If he is of the truthful. Ahmad narrated it with the wording: “When it was revealed: And those who accuse chaste women and do not produce four witnesses, lash them with eighty lashes and do not accept their testimony ever after.” Sa`d bin `Ubadah, the chief of the Ansar, said: Is this how it was revealed, O Messenger of God? The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, said: O group of Ansar, do you not hear what your chief is saying? They said: O Messenger of God, do not blame him, for he is a jealous man, and by God, he has never married a woman. One of us dared to marry her out of intense jealousy. Sa`d said, “By God, O Messenger of God, I know that it is true and that it is from God, but I am amazed that if I found a woman possessed by a man, I would not be able to move her or remove her until I bring four witnesses. By God, I will not bring them until he has satisfied his desire.” They did not stay long until Hilal ibn Umayyah, one of the three whom God had repented for, came from his land in the evening and found a man with his wife. He saw with his eyes and heard with his ears, but he did not criticize him until morning. He went to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and said to him, “I came to my wife in the evening and found a man with her. I saw with my eyes and heard with my ears.” The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, disliked what he had brought and was angry with him. The Ansar gathered and said, “We have been tested by what Sa`d ibn `Ubadah has said. Now he is striking the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and his testimony is invalidated among the people.” Hilal said, “By God, I hope that God will make a way out for me from it. By God, the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, wants to order that he be struck.” God revealed: The revelation came to him, so they held back from him until he finished the revelation. Then the verse, And those who accuse their wives, was revealed. Abu Ya’la narrated something similar from the hadith of Anas. Al-Bukhari and Muslim and others narrated on the authority of Sahl ibn Sa`d who said: Uwaimir came to Asim ibn `Adi and said: Ask the Messenger of God (peace and blessings of God be upon him) for me, What do you think of a man who finds a man with his wife and kills him? Should he be killed for that or what should be done? So `Asim asked the Messenger of God (peace and blessings of God be upon him), but the Messenger of God (peace and blessings of God be upon him) criticized the questioner. Uwaimir met him and said: What did you do? He said: “What did you do? You did not bring me anything good. I asked the Messenger of God (peace and blessings of God be upon him), and he criticized the questioner. Uwaimir said: “By God, I will go to the Messenger of God (peace and blessings of God be upon him) and ask him.” So he asked him, and he said: The hadith was revealed about you and your companion. Al-Hafiz Ibn Hajar said: The imams differed concerning these passages. Some of them preferred that it was revealed about Uwaimir, and some preferred that it was revealed about Hilal, and some combined them by saying that the first to whom that happened was Hilal and it coincided with the arrival of Uwaimir as well, so it was revealed about them both. An-Nawawi leaned towards this, and al-Khatib followed him, saying: Perhaps that happened to them at the same time. Al-Hafiz Ibn Hajar said: It is possible that the revelation was prompted by Hilal, so when he came… Uwaimir did not know what had happened to Hilal, but the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, informed him of the ruling. For this reason, he said in the story of Hilal, Gabriel came down, and in the story of Uwaimir, God has revealed concerning you. His statement, God has revealed concerning you, means that he who has suffered the same thing as you has suffered. Ibn al-Sabbagh answered this in al-Shamel, and al-Qurtubi leaned toward permitting the verse to be revealed twice. Al-Bazzar narrated on the authority of Zaid bin Mut’i’ on the authority of Hudhayfah who said: The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, said to Abu Bakr: If you saw a man with Umm Ruman, you would not do anything to him. He said: I would do something bad to him. He said: And you, Umar? He said: I used to say: May God curse the weak, for he is evil. Then this was revealed. Al-Hafiz Ibn Hajar said: There is no objection to multiple reasons.
24:11: The Almighty said: Indeed, those who brought falsehood - the two Sheikhs and others narrated on the authority of Aisha, who said: Whenever the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, wanted to travel, he would cast lots among his wives. Whichever of them won the lot, he would go with her. So he cast lots among us during a campaign he went on, and my lot won, so I went out. That was after the veil was revealed, so I would be carried in my litter and dismount in it. We set out until, when the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, had finished a campaign and returned and we were close to Medina, he gave the call to depart one night. So I got up and walked until I passed the army. When I had finished my business, I went to the saddlebags and touched my chest. I saw a necklace of Dhofar agate that had broken. So I went back and looked for my necklace, but I was held back by searching for it. The group who were transporting me came and loaded my litter onto my camel that I was riding, thinking that I was on it. She said: At that time, women were light, not fat, and the flesh did not cover them. They only ate a small amount of food, so the people did not find the weight of the litter strange. They removed him and raised him up, so they sent the camel and set out. I found my necklace when the army set out, so I came to their homes and there was no one calling or answering, so I headed towards the house I was in, and I thought that the people would miss me and return to me. While I was sitting in my house, my eyes overcame me and I fell asleep. Safwan ibn al-Mu’attal had camped behind the army, so he set out early in the morning and came to my house. He saw the blackness of a sleeping person and recognized me when he saw me, and he used to see me before the veil was drawn over me. I woke up with his appeal when he recognized me, so I covered my face with my cloak. By God, he did not say a word to me nor did I hear a word from him except his appeal when he dismounted his mount and stepped on its hand, so I mounted it. He set out leading me in the mount until we reached the army after they had dismounted, raging at noon. Those who perished were killed because of my affair, and the one who took responsibility for it was Abdullah ibn Ubayy ibn Salul. I went to Medina and I was ill when we arrived for a month, and the people were spreading the words of the slanderers, and I did not feel anything of that until I went out after I had recovered and went out with Umm Mastih before al-Manasi’ while we were defecating. Umm Mastah stumbled in her cloak and said, May Mastah be cursed! I said to her, What a bad thing you have said! You curse a man who witnessed Badr! She said, What did he say? Didn’t you hear what he said? I said, And what did he say? She told me what the slanderers had said, and my illness increased. When the Messenger of God (peace and blessings of God be upon him) entered upon me, I said, Do you permit me to go to my parents? I want to ascertain the report from them. So he gave me permission. I went to my parents and said to my mother, O mother, what are the people talking about? She said, “O my daughter, take it easy. By God, there has rarely been a beautiful woman with a man who loves her and she has co-wives, except that they talk about her a lot.” I said, Glory be to God! Are the people talking about this? I wept that night until morning, and my tears could not stop and I could not sleep. Then I woke up weeping again. The Messenger of God (peace and blessings of God be upon him) called Ali ibn Abi Talib and Usama ibn Zayd when the revelation was delayed, to consult them about separating from his family. As for Usama, he advised him of what he knew about the innocence of his family. He said, O Messenger of God, they are your family, and we know nothing but good about them. As for Ali, he said, “God will not make things difficult for you, and there are many other women besides her. If you ask a slave girl, she will tell you the truth.” So he called Barrah and said, O Barrah, have you seen anything suspicious from Aisha? She said, “By Him Who sent you with the truth, I have never seen anything about her that I would conceal from her more than that she was a young slave girl who would sleep while her family’s dough was being made, and the goats would come and eat it.” So the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, stood on the pulpit and apologized to Abdullah bin Ubayy, saying, O group of Muslims, who will excuse me from a man who has harmed my family? By God, I know nothing but good about my family. She said, “And I wept that day, my tears not stopping. Then I wept that night, my tears not stopping, and I could not sleep. My parents thought that weeping would split my liver. While they were sitting with me and I was weeping, a woman from the Ansar asked permission to enter, and I gave her permission. She sat and wept with me. Then the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, entered and greeted him, then sat down. He remained for a month without receiving any revelation concerning me, so he bore witness and said, “As for what follows?” O Aisha, I have heard such and such about you. If you are innocent, God will acquit you, and if you have committed a sin, then ask God for forgiveness and then repent to Him, for if a servant confesses a sin and then repents, God accepts his repentance. When he had finished his statement, I said to my father, “Answer the Messenger of God (peace and blessings of God be upon him) on my behalf.” He said, By God, I do not know what to say. I said to my mother, “Answer the Messenger of God (peace and blessings of God be upon him).” She said, By God, I do not know what to say. I said, while I was a young girl, “By God, I know that you have heard this until it has settled in your hearts and you have believed it. If I tell you that I am innocent, and God knows that I am innocent, you will not believe me.” In another narration, “And if I confess to you something, and God knows that I am innocent of it, you will believe me. By God, I do not find an example for me and you except as Abu Yusuf said. So patience is beautiful, and God is the sought for help against what you describe.” Then I turned and lay down on my bed. By God, the Messenger of God (peace and blessings of God be upon him) did not seek his place nor did anyone from the people of the house leave until God revealed to His Prophet, and he was seized by what seized him from the affliction. When he recovered, the first word he spoke was, “Be of good cheer, O Aisha: God has acquitted you. My mother said to me: Go to him. I said: By God, I will not go to him and I will not praise anyone but God. He is the One who revealed my innocence. God revealed: Those who brought forth the slander are a group among you, ten verses. Abu Bakr said: He used to spend on Mistah because of his kinship to him and his poverty. By God, I will not spend anything on him after what he said to Aisha. Then God revealed: And let not those among you who are endowed with virtue and wealth swear not to love that you should not love that God should forgive you. Abu Bakr said: By God, I love that God should forgive me. So he returned to Mistah what he used to spend on him. There is a hadith on this topic from Ibn Abbas and Ibn Umar according to al-Tabarani, Abu Hurayrah according to al-Bazzar, and Abu al-Yusr according to Ibn Mardawayh.
24:12: The Almighty said: Indeed, those who brought falsehood - the two Sheikhs and others narrated on the authority of Aisha, who said: Whenever the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, wanted to travel, he would cast lots among his wives. Whichever of them won the lot, he would go with her. So he cast lots among us during a campaign he went on, and my lot won, so I went out. That was after the veil was revealed, so I would be carried in my litter and dismount in it. We set out until, when the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, had finished a campaign and returned and we were close to Medina, he gave the call to depart one night. So I got up and walked until I passed the army. When I had finished my business, I went to the saddlebags and touched my chest. I saw a necklace of Dhofar agate that had broken. So I went back and looked for my necklace, but I was held back by searching for it. The group who were transporting me came and loaded my litter onto my camel that I was riding, thinking that I was on it. She said: At that time, women were light, not fat, and the flesh did not cover them. They only ate a small amount of food, so the people did not find the weight of the litter strange. They removed him and raised him up, so they sent the camel and set out. I found my necklace when the army set out, so I came to their homes and there was no one calling or answering, so I headed towards the house I was in, and I thought that the people would miss me and return to me. While I was sitting in my house, my eyes overcame me and I fell asleep. Safwan ibn al-Mu’attal had camped behind the army, so he set out early in the morning and came to my house. He saw the blackness of a sleeping person and recognized me when he saw me, and he used to see me before the veil was drawn over me. I woke up with his appeal when he recognized me, so I covered my face with my cloak. By God, he did not say a word to me nor did I hear a word from him except his appeal when he dismounted his mount and stepped on its hand, so I mounted it. He set out leading me in the mount until we reached the army after they had dismounted, raging at noon. Those who perished were killed because of my affair, and the one who took responsibility for it was Abdullah ibn Ubayy ibn Salul. I went to Medina and I was ill when we arrived for a month, and the people were spreading the words of the slanderers, and I did not feel anything of that until I went out after I had recovered and went out with Umm Mastih before al-Manasi’ while we were defecating. Umm Mastah stumbled in her cloak and said, May Mastah be cursed! I said to her, What a bad thing you have said! You curse a man who witnessed Badr! She said, What did he say? Didn’t you hear what he said? I said, And what did he say? She told me what the slanderers had said, and my illness increased. When the Messenger of God (peace and blessings of God be upon him) entered upon me, I said, Do you permit me to go to my parents? I want to ascertain the report from them. So he gave me permission. I went to my parents and said to my mother, O mother, what are the people talking about? She said, “O my daughter, take it easy. By God, there has rarely been a beautiful woman with a man who loves her and she has co-wives, except that they talk about her a lot.” I said, Glory be to God! Are the people talking about this? I wept that night until morning, and my tears could not stop and I could not sleep. Then I woke up weeping again. The Messenger of God (peace and blessings of God be upon him) called Ali ibn Abi Talib and Usama ibn Zayd when the revelation was delayed, to consult them about separating from his family. As for Usama, he advised him of what he knew about the innocence of his family. He said, O Messenger of God, they are your family, and we know nothing but good about them. As for Ali, he said, “God will not make things difficult for you, and there are many other women besides her. If you ask a slave girl, she will tell you the truth.” So he called Barrah and said, O Barrah, have you seen anything suspicious from Aisha? She said, “By Him Who sent you with the truth, I have never seen anything about her that I would conceal from her more than that she was a young slave girl who would sleep while her family’s dough was being made, and the goats would come and eat it.” So the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, stood on the pulpit and apologized to Abdullah bin Ubayy, saying, O group of Muslims, who will excuse me from a man who has harmed my family? By God, I know nothing but good about my family. She said, “And I wept that day, my tears not stopping. Then I wept that night, my tears not stopping, and I could not sleep. My parents thought that weeping would split my liver. While they were sitting with me and I was weeping, a woman from the Ansar asked permission to enter, and I gave her permission. She sat and wept with me. Then the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, entered and greeted him, then sat down. He remained for a month without receiving any revelation concerning me, so he bore witness and said, “As for what follows?” O Aisha, I have heard such and such about you. If you are innocent, God will acquit you, and if you have committed a sin, then ask God for forgiveness and then repent to Him, for if a servant confesses a sin and then repents, God accepts his repentance. When he had finished his statement, I said to my father, “Answer the Messenger of God (peace and blessings of God be upon him) on my behalf.” He said, By God, I do not know what to say. I said to my mother, “Answer the Messenger of God (peace and blessings of God be upon him).” She said, By God, I do not know what to say. I said, while I was a young girl, “By God, I know that you have heard this until it has settled in your hearts and you have believed it. If I tell you that I am innocent, and God knows that I am innocent, you will not believe me.” In another narration, “And if I confess to you something, and God knows that I am innocent of it, you will believe me. By God, I do not find an example for me and you except as Abu Yusuf said. So patience is beautiful, and God is the sought for help against what you describe.” Then I turned and lay down on my bed. By God, the Messenger of God (peace and blessings of God be upon him) did not seek his place nor did anyone from the people of the house leave until God revealed to His Prophet, and he was seized by what seized him from the affliction. When he recovered, the first word he spoke was, “Be of good cheer, O Aisha: God has acquitted you. My mother said to me: Go to him. I said: By God, I will not go to him and I will not praise anyone but God. He is the One who revealed my innocence. God revealed: Those who brought forth the slander are a group among you, ten verses. Abu Bakr said: He used to spend on Mistah because of his kinship to him and his poverty. By God, I will not spend anything on him after what he said to Aisha. Then God revealed: And let not those among you who are endowed with virtue and wealth swear not to love that you should not love that God should forgive you. Abu Bakr said: By God, I love that God should forgive me. So he returned to Mistah what he used to spend on him. There is a hadith on this topic from Ibn Abbas and Ibn Umar according to al-Tabarani, Abu Hurayrah according to al-Bazzar, and Abu al-Yusr according to Ibn Mardawayh.
24:22: Refer to the reasons for the revelation of verses 11 and 12 of this surah Abu Bakr said: He used to spend on Mistah because of his kinship to him and his poverty. By God, I will not spend anything on him after what he said to Aisha. Then God revealed: And let not those among you who are endowed with virtue and wealth swear not to love that God should forgive you. Abu Bakr said: By God, I love that God should forgive me. So he returned to Mistah what he used to spend on him. There is a hadith on this topic from Ibn Abbas and Ibn Umar according to al-Tabarani, Abu Hurayrah according to al-Bazzar, and Abu al-Yusr according to Ibn Mardawayh.
24:23: And Al-Tabarani narrated on the authority of Khasif that I said to Saeed bin Jubair, Which is more serious, adultery or slander? He said, Adultery. I said, God says, ‘Those who accuse chaste, unaware, believing women,’ He said, “This was only revealed in relation to Aisha specifically. In its chain of transmission Yahya Al-Hammani is weak.” K and he also narrated on the authority of Al-Dahhak bin Muzahim who said: This verse was revealed about the wives of the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace: Those who accuse chaste women who are unaware… K and Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Saeed Ibn Jubayr on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: This verse was revealed specifically about Aisha. K and Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Aisha, who said: I was accused of what I was accused of while I was unaware, and it reached me after that. While the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, was with me, revelation came to him. Then he sat up straight and wiped his face and said: O Aisha, be of good tidings. I said: Praise be to God, not to you. Then he recited: Indeed, those who accuse chaste, unaware, believing women, until he reached: Those are innocent of what they say.
24:26: And Al-Tabarani narrated with a chain of transmission whose men are trustworthy on the authority of Abd al-Rahman bin Zaid bin Aslam regarding his statement, Evil women are for evil men, the verse. He said, It was revealed about Aisha when the hypocrite accused her of slander and falsehood, but God cleared her of that. K and Al-Tabarani narrated with two chains of transmission in which there is weakness on the authority of Ibn Abbas who said: The verse was revealed for the wicked men about those who said what they said about the wife of the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, of slander. Al-Tabarani narrated on the authority of Al-Hakam bin Utaybah who said: When the people were discussing the matter of Aisha, the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, sent to Aisha and said: O Aisha, what are the people saying? She said: I will not apologize for anything until my excuse comes down from the sky. So God sent down fifteen verses from Surat An-Nur about it. Then he recited until he reached: The evil ones are for the evil ones. The verse is mursal with a sound chain of transmission.
24:27: The Almighty said, O you who have believed, do not enter houses other than your own until you have asked permission. Al-Faryabi and Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Adi bin Thabit that a woman from the Ansar came and said, O Messenger of God, I am in my house in a state that I do not like anyone to see me in, and a man from my family keeps entering upon me while I am in that state, so what should I do? Then the verse was revealed, O you who have believed, do not enter houses other than your own houses until you have asked permission. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Muqatil ibn Hibban who said: When the verse about asking permission to enter houses was revealed, Abu Bakr said: O Messenger of God, what about the merchants of Quraysh who go back and forth between Mecca, Medina and Syria, and they have known houses on the road? How can they ask permission to greet each other when there are no residents in them? So the verse was revealed: There is no blame upon you for entering uninhabited houses.
24:31: God Almighty said: And say to the believing women, the verse. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Muqatil, who said: It has reached us that Jabir ibn Abdullah narrated that Asma’ bint Marthad was in a palm grove of hers, and women would enter upon her without wearing belts, so what was on their feet, meaning the anklets, and their chests and hair were visible. So Asma’ said: How ugly this is. So God revealed regarding that: And say to the believing women, the verse. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Hadrami that a woman made two silver bracelets and agate. She passed by some people and struck them with her foot. The anklet fell on the agate and made a sound. Then God revealed: And let them not strike their feet.
24:33: God Almighty said: And those who seek the Book, the verse. Ibn al-Sakn narrated in Ma'rifat al-Sahaba on the authority of Abdullah bin Subaih on the authority of his father who said: I was a slave of Huwaytib bin Abd al-Uzza and I asked him to write, so the verse was revealed: And those who seek the Book, the verse. God Almighty said: And do not compel your girls to prostitution. Muslim narrated on the authority of Abu Sufyan on the authority of Jabir bin Abdullah, who said: “Abdullah bin Ubayy used to say to a female slave of his: ‘Go and seek something for us.’ Then God revealed: “And do not compel your girls to prostitution.” Al-Hakim narrated on the authority of Abu al-Zubayr on the authority of Jabir, who said: There was a woman who belonged to one of the Ansar, and she said: My master forces me to prostitution. So the verse was revealed: And do not compel your girls to prostitution. Al-Bazzar and Al-Tabarani narrated with a sound chain of transmission on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: Abdullah Ibn Abi had a slave girl who used to commit adultery during the pre-Islamic period. When adultery was forbidden, she said: No, by God, I will never commit adultery. Then the verse And do not compel your girls into prostitution was revealed. Al-Bazzar narrated with a weak chain of transmission on the authority of Anas something similar, and he named the slave girl Mu’adhah. Saeed bin Mansour narrated on the authority of Shaaban on the authority of Amr bin Dinar on the authority of Ikrimah that Abdullah bin Ubayy had two female slaves, Masikah and Mu’adhah, and he used to force them to commit adultery. One of them said, If it is good, then I have done a lot of it, but if it is not, then I should leave it. Then God revealed, And do not force your girls into prostitution, the verse.
24:48: God Almighty said: And when they are called upon to the verse. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated from the mursal of al-Hasan that if a man had a dispute with another man and was called to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and he was in the right, he would submit and know that the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, would rule in his favor with justice. And if he wanted to be wronged and was called to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, he would turn away and say: Go to so-and-so. So God revealed: And when they are called upon to God and His Messenger, the verse.
24:55: The Almighty said: God has promised those who believe, the verse. Al-Hakim narrated and authenticated it, and Al-Tabarani narrated on the authority of Abi bin Kaab, who said: When the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and his companions arrived in Medina and the Ansar gave them shelter, the Arabs shot them with a single bow, and they would not sleep except with weapons and would not wake up except with them. They said: Do you think we will live until we sleep in safety and security, fearing none but God? So the verse was revealed: God has promised those among you who believe, the verse. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Al-Baraa, who said: This verse was revealed about us while we were in intense fear.
24:61: God Almighty said: There is no blame upon the blind, the verse. Abd al-Razzaq said: Muammar told us on the authority of Ibn Abi Nujayh on the authority of Mujahid, who said: A man would take the blind, the lame, or the sick to his father’s house, or his brother’s house, or his sister’s house, or his aunt’s house, or his maternal aunt’s house. The lame would be embarrassed by that, saying: They are only taking us to the houses of others. So this verse was revealed as a concession to them: There is no blame upon the blind, the verse. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: When God revealed the verse, O you who have believed, do not consume one another’s wealth unjustly, the Muslims were reluctant and said, Food is the best of wealth, so it is not permissible for any of us to eat at someone else’s house. So the people refrained from that, and the verse was revealed, There is no blame upon the blind man until His saying, or his keys. And he narrated on the authority of Al-Dahhak, who said: Before the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, was sent, the people of Medina would not let the blind, the sick, or the lame mix with them in their food, because the blind cannot see the goodness of the food, and the sick cannot enjoy the food as well as the healthy. The lame man could not compete for food, so permission was granted to eat with them. And he narrated on the authority of Muqsim, who said: They were afraid to eat with the blind and the lame, so this was revealed. And Al-Tha’labi narrated in his interpretation on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: Al-Harith went out to fight with the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and he left Khalid bin Zaid in charge of his family, so he was embarrassed to eat from his food and he was exhausted, so this was revealed. God the Almighty said: There is no blame upon you for the verse. Al-Bazzar narrated with a sound chain of transmission on the authority of Aisha, who said: The Muslims used to desire to travel with the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, so they would give their keys to their people and say to them: We have made it lawful for you to eat whatever you like. They used to say: It is not lawful for us because they gave permission against their will. So God revealed: There is no blame upon you for the verse: or for what you possess the keys to. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Al-Zuhri that he was asked about his statement, There is no blame upon the blind, why are the blind, the lame, and the sick mentioned here? He said, “Abdullah bin Abdullah informed me that when the Muslims went out to war, they would stay behind and give them the keys to their doors and say, ‘We have made it permissible for you to eat from what is in our houses.’ They would be embarrassed by that and say, ‘We will not enter them while they are absent.’ So this verse was revealed as a concession to them.” And he narrated on the authority of Qatada, who said: There is no blame upon you if you eat together or separately in a tribe of Arabs. A man among them would not eat his food alone, but would carry it for part of a day until he found someone to eat it with him. And he narrated on the authority of Ikrimah and Abu Salih, who said: When a guest came to the Ansar, they would not eat until the guest ate with them, so a concession was revealed for them.
24:62: The Almighty said: The believers are only the believers, the verse Ibn Ishaq and Al-Bayhaqi narrated in Al-Dala’il on the authority of Urwah and Muhammad bin Ka’b Al-Qurazi and others, they said: When the Quraysh came in the year of the confederates, they camped at Majma’ Al-Asyal from Ruma, a well in Medina, its leader was Abu Sufyan, and the Ghatafan came until they camped at Na’i next to Uhud, and the report reached the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, so he dug the trench around Medina and worked on it, and the Muslims worked on it, and some of the hypocrites were slow and would bring the weak from the work, sneaking back to their families without the knowledge of the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, or permission, and when a Muslim man was afflicted with a calamity or an unavoidable need, he would mention that to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and ask his permission to go to his need, and he would give him permission, and when he had completed his need, he would return. So God revealed about those believers: The believers are only those who have believed in God and His Messenger, and when they are with him on a matter of common concern, until His saying: And God is Knowing of all things.
24:63: God Almighty said: Do not make the verse. Abu Naim included in Al-Dala’il on the authority of Al-Dahhak on the authority of Ibn Abbas who said: They used to say: O Muhammad, O Abu Al-Qasim. Then God revealed: Do not make the calling of the Messenger among you like the calling of some of you to others. So they said: O Prophet of God, O Messenger of God.
25:1: Ibn Abi Shaybah narrated in Al-Musannaf, and Ibn Jarir and Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Khaithamah who said: It was said to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace: If you wish, we will give you the keys to the earth and its treasuries, and that will not decrease you in the least with Us in the Hereafter. And if you wish, I will gather them both for you in the Hereafter. He said: Rather, gather them both for me in the Hereafter. So the verse was revealed: Blessed is He who wills, He has made for you something better than that.
25:20: Al-Wahidi narrated on the authority of Juwaybir on the authority of Al-Dahhak on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: When the polytheists taunted the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, with poverty and said: What is the matter with this Messenger that he eats food and walks in the markets? The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, became sad, so the verse was revealed: And We did not send before you any messengers except that they ate food and walked in the markets.
25:27: And Ibn Jarir narrated something similar to it on the authority of Saeed and Ikrimah on the authority of Ibn Abbas. And Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: Abi bin Khalaf used to attend the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and Uqba bin Abi Mu’ayt would rebuke him, so the following was revealed: And the Day the wrongdoer will bite his hands until his saying: Abandoner. And he narrated something similar on the authority of Al-Sha’bi and Muqsim. K and Ibn Abi Hatim and Al-Hakim narrated - and he authenticated it - and Al-Dhiya in Al-Mukhtara on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: The polytheists said: If Muhammad was, as he claims, a prophet, then why would his Lord not punish him? Why did He not send down the Qur’an to him all at once? So He sent down one or two verses to him. So God sent down the Qur’an and those who disbelieved said: Why was the Qur’an not sent down to him all at once?
25:68: And the two sheikhs narrated on the authority of Ibn Mas`ud, who said: I asked the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, which sin is the greatest. He said: That you make a rival to God when He created you. I said: Then what? He said: That you kill your son for fear that he will eat with you. I said: Then what? He said: That you commit adultery with your neighbor’s wife, and God revealed the confirmation of her. And those who do not invoke with God another deity or kill the soul which God has forbidden, except by right, and do not commit unlawful sexual intercourse. Al-Bukhari and Muslim narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas that some people from the polytheists killed a lot and committed adultery a lot. Then they came to Muhammad, may God bless him and grant him peace, and said: What you say and call to is good. If only you would tell us that what we have done is expiation. Then the verse was revealed: And those who do not invoke with God another deity until His saying: Forgiving, Merciful. Then the verse was revealed: Say, ‘O My servants who have transgressed against the law,’
25:70: Al-Bukhari and others narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: When the verse And those who do not invoke with God another deity or kill the soul which We have revealed in the Criterion was revealed, the polytheists of Mecca said, We have killed the soul unjustly and invoked with God another deity and committed immoralities. Then the verse was revealed, Except for those who repent.
26:201: 201 - They will not believe in it until they see the painful punishment.
26:205: Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Abu Jahdam who said: The Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, was seen as if he was perplexed, so they asked him about that and he said: Why? And I saw that my enemy would be from among my nation after me. So the verse was revealed: Have you seen if We grant them enjoyment for years and then what they were promised comes to them? What will what they were granted enjoyment do them good? So his soul was at ease.
26:214: Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Ibn Jurayj who said: When the verse And warn your nearest relatives was revealed, he began with his family and clan, and that was difficult for the Muslims. Then God revealed the verse And lower your wing to those who follow you among the believers.
26:215: Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Ibn Jurayj who said: When the verse And warn your nearest relatives was revealed, he began with his family and clan, and that was difficult for the Muslims. Then God revealed the verse And lower your wing to those who follow you among the believers.
26:224: Ibn Jarir and Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Al-Awfi on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: Two men exchanged insults during the time of the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace. One of them was from the Ansar and the other from another people. With each of them were some of the fools from his people, and they were the fools. So God revealed: And the poets - those who go astray follow them [verses]. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Ikrimah something similar, and he narrated on the authority of Urwah who said: When the verse And the poets was revealed until His saying what they do not do, Abdullah bin Rawahah said: God knows that I am one of them, so God revealed: Except for those who believe until the end of the surah. Ibn Jarir and Al-Hakim narrated on the authority of Abu Hassan Al-Barad, who said: When the verse And the poets was revealed, Abdullah bin Rawahah, Ka’b bin Malik, and Hassan bin Thabit came and said: O Messenger of God, by God, God revealed this verse and He knew that we poets have perished. Then God revealed the verse Except for those who believe. So the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, called them and recited it to them.
28:27: 27 - (He said, "Indeed, I want to marry you to one of these two daughters of mine on condition that you hire me for eight years. But if you complete ten years, it will be from you. And I do not want to make it difficult for you. You will find me, God willing, to seek blessings from God, among the righteous.")
28:51: Ibn Jarir and Al-Tabarani narrated on the authority of Rifa’a Al-Qurazi, who said: The statement was revealed to us concerning ten people, one of whom was I. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Ali bin Rifa’a, who said: “Ten men from the People of the Book, including Rifa’a, meaning his father, went out to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and they believed, but they were harmed, so the verse was revealed: ‘Those to whom We gave the Scripture.’” And he narrated on the authority of Qatada, who said: We were told that it was revealed about people from the People of the Book who were on the truth until God sent Muhammad, may God bless him and grant him peace, and they believed, including Uthman and Abdullah bin Salam.
28:52: God Almighty says: And those to whom We gave the Scripture, the verse. The reason for its revelation will come in Surat Al-Hadid.
28:56: God Almighty said: Indeed, you do not guide whom you love. The verse: Muslim and others narrated on the authority of Abu Hurairah who said: The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, said to his uncle: Say: There is no god but God, and I will bear witness for you on the Day of Resurrection. He said: Were it not that the women of Quraysh would reproach me and say: It was his impatience that made him do that, I would have made you happy with it. Then God revealed: Indeed, you do not guide whom you love, but God guides whom He wills. Al-Nasa’i and Ibn Asakir narrated in Tarikh Dimashq with a good chain of transmission on the authority of Abu Sa’id Ibn Rafi’ who said: I asked Ibn Umar about this verse: You cannot guide whom you love. Is it about Abu Jahl and Abu Talib? He said: Yes.
28:57: God Almighty said: And they said: If we follow the guidance with you, the verse. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Al-Awfi on the authority of Ibn Abbas that some people from Quraysh said to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace: If we follow you, people will snatch us away. So it was revealed. Al-Nasa’i narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas that Al-Harith bin Amir bin Nawfal was the one who said that.
28:61: God Almighty said: Is he whom We have promised the verse? Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Mujahid regarding His saying: Is he whom We have promised the verse? He said: It was revealed about the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and about Abu Jahl ibn Hisham. And it was narrated from another chain of transmission on his authority that it was revealed about Hamza and Abu Jahl.
28:85: God Almighty said: Indeed, He who has enjoined upon you the Qur’an, the verse. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Ad-Dahhak who said: When the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, left Mecca and reached Al-Juhfah, he longed for Mecca, so God revealed: Indeed, He who has enjoined upon you the Qur’an will surely bring you back to a place of return.
29:1: Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Al-Sha’bi regarding his statement, Do people think that they will be abandoned? The verse said, “It was revealed about people who were in Mecca and had acknowledged Islam. Then the companions of the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, wrote to them from Medina that it would not be accepted from you until you emigrated. So they went out intending to go to Medina, but the polytheists pursued them and turned them back. Then this verse was revealed. Then they wrote to them that such and such had been revealed about you. They said, “We will go out, and if anyone follows us, we will fight him.” So they went out, and the polytheists pursued them and fought them. Some of them were killed and some of them escaped. Then God revealed about them, Then your Lord is for those who emigrated after they were persecuted. The verse K and he narrated on the authority of Qatada who said: “The verse ‘Alif, Laam, Meem’ was revealed about some people from Mecca who went out seeking the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, but the polytheists attacked them and they returned. Then their brothers wrote to them about what was revealed about them, so they went out. Some were killed and some were saved. Then the Qur’an was revealed: ‘And those who strive for Us - We will surely guide them to Our ways’” verse. Ibn Saad narrated on the authority of Abdullah bin Ubaid on the authority of Ibn Umair, who said: This verse was revealed about Ammar bin Yasir when he was being tortured for the sake of God. Do people think that the verse...
29:2: Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Al-Sha’bi regarding his statement, Do people think that they will be abandoned? The verse said, “It was revealed about people who were in Mecca and had acknowledged Islam. Then the companions of the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, wrote to them from Medina that it would not be accepted from you until you emigrated. So they went out intending to go to Medina, but the polytheists pursued them and turned them back. Then this verse was revealed. Then they wrote to them that such and such had been revealed about you. They said, “We will go out, and if anyone follows us, we will fight him.” So they went out, and the polytheists pursued them and fought them. Some of them were killed and some of them escaped. Then God revealed about them, Then your Lord is for those who emigrated after they were persecuted. The verse K and he narrated on the authority of Qatada who said: “The verse ‘Alif, Laam, Meem’ was revealed about some people from Mecca who went out seeking the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, but the polytheists attacked them and they returned. Then their brothers wrote to them about what was revealed about them, so they went out. Some were killed and some were saved. Then the Qur’an was revealed: ‘And those who strive for Us - We will surely guide them to Our ways’” verse. Ibn Saad narrated on the authority of Abdullah bin Ubaid on the authority of Ibn Umair, who said: This verse was revealed about Ammar bin Yasir when he was being tortured for the sake of God. Do people think that the verse...
29:8: The Almighty said: But if they endeavor to make you associate others with Me, the verse. Muslim, Al-Tirmidhi, and others narrated on the authority of Sa`d ibn Abi Waqqas, who said: Umm Sa`d said: Has not God commanded righteousness? By God, I will not eat food or drink a drink until I die or you disbelieve. Then the verse was revealed: And We have enjoined upon man, to be dutiful to his parents, but if they endeavor to make you associate others with Me, the verse.
29:10: God Almighty said: And among the people is he who says, We believe in God. The reason for its revelation was presented in Surat An-Nisa.
29:27: 27 - And We bestowed upon him after Ishmael Isaac and Jacob after Isaac and We placed among his descendants prophethood so all the prophets after Abraham are from his descendants and the Scripture meaning the books, that is, the Torah, the Gospel, the Psalms, and the Criterion and We gave him his reward in this world which is good praise among all people of religions and indeed, in the Hereafter he will be among the righteous who have the highest ranks.
29:51: God Almighty said: Was it not sufficient for them that We sent down to you the Book which is recited to them? Ibn Jarir, Ibn Abi Hatim, and Al-Darimi in his Musnad narrated on the authority of Amr ibn Dinar on the authority of Yahya ibn Ja’dah, who said: Some Muslims came with books in which they had written some of what they had heard from the Jews. The Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, said: It is sufficient for a people astray that they should turn away from what their Prophet brought to them and turn to what others brought to others. So the verse was revealed: Was it not sufficient for them that We sent down to you the Book which is recited to them?
29:60: The Almighty said: And how many a creature of the heavens and the earth - Abd bin Hamid, Ibn Abi Hatim, Al-Bayhaqi and Ibn Asakir narrated with a weak chain of transmission on the authority of Ibn Umair who said: I went out with the Messenger of God (peace and blessings of God be upon him) until he entered some of the walls of Madinah and he began to pick up dates and eat. He said to me: O Ibn Umar, why don’t you eat? I said: I don’t desire it. He said: But I desire it. This is the fourth morning since I have tasted food and I have not found it. If I wanted, I could have called upon my Lord and He would have given me the likes of the kingdom of Chosroes and Caesar. So what will you do, O Ibn Umar, when you meet people who hide their provision for the year and whose faith is weak?
