He said: By God, we did not leave or rest until the verse: And how many a creature carries not its provision? God provides for it and for you. And He is the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing. The Messenger of God (peace and blessings of God be upon him) said: God did not command me to hoard the world nor to follow desires. Beware, I do not have more dinars or dirhams, nor do I hide provision for tomorrow.
29:67: God Almighty said: Have they not seen the verse: Juwaybir narrated on the authority of Ad-Dahhak on the authority of Ibn Abbas that they said: O Muhammad, what prevents us from entering your religion except the fear that people will snatch us away to kill us, and the Bedouins are more numerous than us, so whenever they hear that we have entered your religion, they will snatch us away and we will be eaten by a head. Then God revealed: Have they not seen that We have made a secure sanctuary?
30:1: Al-Tirmidhi narrated on the authority of Abu Saeed, who said: When the day of Badr came, the Romans were victorious over the Persians, and that pleased the believers. So the verse, Alif, Laam, Meem, The Romans have been defeated was revealed, until His saying, By the victory of God, meaning with the letter fatha in the word ghayn. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Ibn Masoud, and something similar. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Ibn Shihab, who said: It has reached us that the polytheists were arguing with the Muslims while they were in Mecca before the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, left. They said: The Romans testify that they are People of the Book and the Magians defeated them, and you claim that you will defeat us with the Book that was revealed to your Prophet. So how did the Magians defeat the Romans, who are People of the Book, and we will defeat you as the Persians defeated the Romans? Then God revealed: Alif, Laam, Meem. The Romans have been defeated. Ibn Jarir narrated something similar on the authority of Ikrimah, Yahya bin Yaghmur, and Qatadah. The first narration is based on the reading of ghalaba with the fat-ha because it was revealed on the day of their defeat at Badr. The second is based on the reading of the damma, so its meaning is that after their defeat by the Persians, the Muslims will defeat them, so that the meaning of the statement is correct. Otherwise, it does not have much meaning.
30:2: Al-Tirmidhi narrated on the authority of Abu Saeed, who said: When the day of Badr came, the Romans were victorious over the Persians, and that pleased the believers. So the verse, Alif, Laam, Meem, The Romans have been defeated was revealed, until His saying, By the victory of God, meaning with the letter fatha in the word ghayn. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Ibn Masoud, and something similar. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Ibn Shihab, who said: It has reached us that the polytheists were arguing with the Muslims while they were in Mecca before the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, left. They said: The Romans testify that they are People of the Book and the Magians defeated them, and you claim that you will defeat us with the Book that was revealed to your Prophet. So how did the Magians defeat the Romans, who are People of the Book, and we will defeat you as the Persians defeated the Romans? Then God revealed: Alif, Laam, Meem. The Romans have been defeated. Ibn Jarir narrated something similar on the authority of Ikrimah, Yahya bin Yaghmur, and Qatadah. The first narration is based on the reading of ghalaba with the fat-ha because it was revealed on the day of their defeat at Badr. The second is based on the reading of the damma, so its meaning is that after their defeat by the Persians, the Muslims will defeat them, so that the meaning of the statement is correct. Otherwise, it does not have much meaning.
30:27: And Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Ikrimah, who said: The infidels were amazed at God’s revival of the dead, so the verse was revealed: He is the One who begins creation and then repeats it, and that is easier for Him.
30:28: And Al-Tabarani narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: The polytheists used to say: Here I am, O God, here I am, here I am. You have no partner except a partner who is Yours. You own him and what he owns. Then God revealed: Do you have among those whom your right hands possess partners in what We have provided for you? The verse. And Juwaybir narrated something similar: Dawud bin Abi Hind on the authority of Abu Ja`far Muhammad bin Ali on the authority of his father.
31:6: Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Al-Awfi on the authority of Ibn Abbas regarding his statement, And among the people is he who buys the amusement of speech, he said, It was revealed about a man from Quraysh who bought a singing slave girl. And Juwaybir narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: It was revealed about Al-Nasr bin Al-Harith, who bought a slave girl. He would not hear of anyone who wanted to become a Muslim except that he would go with him to his slave girl and say: Feed him, give him drink, and enrich him. This is better than what Muhammad calls you to, of prayer and fasting, and that you fight in his presence. So it was revealed.
31:27: Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Ikrimah who said: The People of the Book asked the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, about the spirit, so God revealed: “They ask you about the spirit. Say: The spirit is of the command of my Lord. And you have not been given of knowledge except a little.” They said: “You claim that we have not been given of knowledge except a little, and we have been given the Torah, which is wisdom. And whoever has been given wisdom has certainly been given much good.” So the verse was revealed: And if whatever trees are on the earth were pens. Ibn Ishaq narrated on the authority of Ata’ bin Yasar who said: “It was revealed in Mecca: ‘And you have been given of knowledge only a little.’ When he migrated to Medina, the Jewish rabbis came to him and said: ‘Have we not heard that you say: ‘And you have been given of knowledge only a little.’ Do you mean us or your people?’ He said: ‘No, I mean you.’ They said: ‘Then you recite: We have been given the Torah, and in it is clarification of everything.’ The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, said: ‘It is little in the knowledge of God.’ Then God revealed: ‘And if whatever trees are on the earth were pens.’” Ibn Abi Hatim narrated it with this wording on the authority of Sa’id or Ikrimah on the authority of Ibn Abbas. Abu Al-Sheikh included in the Book of Al-Azmah and Ibn Jarir on the authority of Qatada who said: The polytheists said: This is only a statement that is about to be implemented. So the verse was revealed: And if what is on the earth were…
31:34: Ibn Jarir and Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Mujahid, who said: A man from the desert came and said: My wife is pregnant, so tell me what she will give birth to. Our land is barren, so tell me when the rain will fall. I know when I gave birth, so tell me when I will die. Then God revealed: God has knowledge of the Hour.
32:16: Al-Bazzar narrated on the authority of Bilal who said: We were sitting in the mosque and some of the companions of the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, were praying from sunset to night prayer, so this verse was revealed: Their sides forsake their beds. In its chain of transmission is Abdullah Ibn Shabib, who is weak. Al-Tirmidhi narrated, and Anas authenticated it, that this verse, Their sides forsake their beds, was revealed regarding waiting for the prayer called Isha.
32:18: Al-Wahidi and Ibn Asakir narrated on the authority of Saeed bin Jubair on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: Al-Walid bin Uqbah bin Abi Mu’ayt said to Ali bin Abi Talib: I am sharper than you in spear, more eloquent than you in tongue, and more able to deal with the scribes than you. Ali said to him: Be quiet, for you are a sinner. Then the verse was revealed: Is he who is a believer like he who is a sinner? They are not equal. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Ata’ bin Yasar something similar. Ibn Adi and Al-Khatib narrated in his history on the authority of Al-Kalbi on the authority of Abu Salih on the authority of Ibn Abbas something similar. Al-Khatib and Ibn Asakir narrated on the authority of Ibn Lahi’ah on the authority of Amr ibn Dinar on the authority of Ibn Abbas that it was revealed about Ali ibn Abi Talib and Uqbah ibn Abi Mu’ayt, and that was due to an insult that occurred between them. Thus in this narration it is stated that it was revealed about Uqbah ibn al-Walid, not al-Walid.
32:28: Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Qatada that the Companions said: We have a day that is about to come when we will rest and enjoy ourselves. The polytheists said: When will this conquest be, if you are truthful? Then this verse was revealed.
33:1: Ibn Juwaybir narrated on the authority of Al-Dahhak on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said that the people of Mecca, including Al-Walid bin Al-Mughira and Shaibah bin Rabi’ah, called upon the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, to retract his statement on the condition that they would give him half of their wealth, and the hypocrites and the Jews in Medina threatened him that if he did not retract they would kill him. So God revealed: O Prophet, fear God and do not obey the disbelievers and the hypocrites.
33:4: The Almighty said: What God has made for any man... The verse: Al-Tirmidhi narrated and authenticated it on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: The Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, stood up one day to pray and a thought occurred to him. The hypocrites who were praying with him said: Do you not see that he has two hearts, one with you and one with him? Then God revealed: What God has made for any man of two hearts in his chest. K and Ibn Abi Hatim narrated through a weak chain of transmission on the authority of Saeed bin Jubair, Mujahid and Ikrimah, who said: There was a man called Dhu al-Qalbayn, so this verse was revealed. K and Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Qatada on the authority of Al-Hasan something similar, and he added: He used to say to me, A soul commands me and a soul forbids me. And he narrated on the authority of Ibn Abi Nujayh on the authority of Mujahid, who said: It was revealed about a man from Banu Fahm who said: There are two hearts in my chest, and I have a better understanding of each one of them than the understanding of Muhammad. And Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of al-Suddi that it was revealed about a man from Quraysh from Banu Jumah called Jamil ibn Muammar.
33:5: God Almighty says: Call them by the names of their fathers. Al-Bukhari narrated on the authority of Ibn Umar, who said: We did not call Zayd ibn Haritha anything but Zayd ibn Muhammad until the verse was revealed in the Qur’an: Call them by the names of their fathers. It is more just in the sight of God.
33:9: The Almighty said, O you who have believed, remember the blessing of God upon you. Al-Bayhaqi narrated in Al-Dala'il on the authority of Hudhayfah, who said, "I saw us on the night of the Confederates, sitting in rows, and Abu Sufyan and those with him from the Confederates were above us, and Qurayzah was below us, and we feared them for our children. There has never been a night more dark or more windy than this one. So the hypocrites began to ask permission from the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, saying, 'Our houses are exposed, but they are not exposed.' None of them asked permission except that he gave him permission. They slipped away, one by one, when the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, met us, until he came to Ali, who said, 'Bring me report of the people.' So I came, and the wind was in their camp, it had not gone beyond their camp by a handspan. By God, I could hear the sound of stones in their tents and their mattresses, the wind hitting them with them, and they were saying, 'Depart, depart.' So I came and told him the report of the people, and God revealed, 'O you who have believed, remember the favor of God upon you when the armies came to you.'"
33:12: And Ibn Abi Hatim and Al-Bayhaqi narrated in Al-Dala’il, on the authority of Katheer bin Abdullah bin Amr Al-Muzani, on the authority of his father, on the authority of his grandfather, who said: The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, drew the trench in the Year of the Confederates, and God brought forth from the bottom of the trench a round white rock. The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, took the pickaxe and struck it with a blow that split it and lightning flashed from it that lit up the area between the two lava plains of Medina. He said, God is most great and the Muslims said, God is most great. Then he struck it a second time and cracked it and lightning flashed from it that lit up the area between the two lava plains of Medina. He said, God is most great and the Muslims said, God is most great. Then he struck it a third time and broke it and lightning flashed from it that lit up the area between the two lava plains of Medina. He said, God is most great and the Muslims said, God is most great. He was asked about that and he said, I struck the first one and the palaces of Al-Hirah and the cities of Kisra were lit up for me, and Gabriel informed me that my nation would be victorious over them. Then I struck it a second time and the palaces of Al-Himra in the land of the Romans were lit up for me, and Gabriel informed me that my nation would be victorious over them. Then I struck it a third time and the palaces of Sana’a were lit up for me, and Gabriel informed me that my nation would be victorious over them. The hypocrites said, Are you not amazed that he is telling you… And he gives you false hope and promises you falsehood and tells you that he sees from Yathrib the palaces of Al-Hirah and the cities of Kisra and that they will be opened for you, and you are only digging the trench to avoid being able to emerge. Then the Qur’an was revealed. And the hypocrites and those in whose hearts is disease say, And God has not promised us. And His Messenger, except for delusion. Ibn Juwaybir narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: This verse was revealed about Mutaib bin Qushair Al-Ansari, who is the author of this article. Ibn Ishaq and Al-Bayhaqi also narrated on the authority of Urwah bin Al-Zubayr, Muhammad bin Kaab Al-Qurazi and others, who said: Mutab bin Qushair said: Muhammad thought he would eat from the treasures of Chosroes and Caesar, and none of us would feel safe going to the toilet. And Aws bin Qayzi said to a group of his al-Nas 114:Our houses are exposed and they are outside the city. Give us permission to return to our women and children. So God revealed to His Messenger when he was afraid of what they were in of affliction, reminding them of His favor upon them and His sufficiency for them after their bad opinion and the statement of the hypocrites who said: O you who have believed, remember the favor of God upon you when the armies came to you…
33:23: The Almighty said, Among the believers are men. Muslim, Al-Tirmidhi, and others narrated on the authority of Anas, who said, “My uncle Anas bin Al-Nadr was absent from Badr, so he glorified him and said, ‘I was absent from the first battle that the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, witnessed. If God shows me a battle with the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, God will show them what I will do.’ So he witnessed the Battle of Uhud and fought until he was killed. Eighty-odd wounds were found on his body, between blows, stab wounds, and arrow wounds. Then this verse was revealed, ‘Men who have been true to what they pledged to God,’ until the end of it.
33:28: The Almighty said, O Prophet, tell your wives verse. Muslim, Ahmad, and al-Nasa’i narrated on the authority of Abu al-Zubayr on the authority of Jabir that he said, “Abu Bakr came seeking permission to see the Messenger of God (peace and blessings of God be upon him), but he was not given permission. Then Umar came and sought permission, but he was not given permission. Then they were given permission, and they entered while the Prophet (peace and blessings of God be upon him) was sitting with his wives around him and he was silent. Umar said, “I will speak to the Prophet (peace and blessings of God be upon him), perhaps he will laugh.” Umar said, O Messenger of God, if you had seen the daughter of Zayd, the wife of Umar, who asked me for maintenance just now, and I struck her neck. The Prophet (peace and blessings of God be upon him) laughed until his molar teeth showed and said, They are around me asking me for maintenance. Abu Bakr went to Aisha to hit her, and Umar went to Hafsa, both of them saying, “You are asking the Prophet (peace and blessings of God be upon him) for something he does not have.” God revealed the option, and he began with Aisha and said, I am going to mention to you a matter which I do not like you to hasten until you consult your parents. She said, What is it? Then he recited to her, O Prophet, tell your wives verse. Aisha said, Is it not for you to consult my parents? Rather, I choose God. and his messenger
33:35: The Almighty said: Indeed, the Muslims, verse K. Al-Tirmidhi narrated and authenticated it on the authority of Ikrimah on the authority of Umm Amara Al-Ansari that she came to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and said: I do not see everything except for men, and I do not see women mentioned in anything. So the verse was revealed: Indeed, the Muslim men and Muslim women, verse K. K and Al-Tabarani narrated with an acceptable chain of transmission on the authority of Ibn Abbas who said: The women said: O Messenger of God, why does he mention the believing men and not the believing women? So the verse was revealed: Indeed, the Muslim men and Muslim women. The hadith of Umm Salamah was presented at the end of Surat Al Imran. Ibn Saad narrated on the authority of Qatada who said: When the wives of the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, were mentioned, the women said: If there was any good in us, we would have been mentioned. So God revealed: The Muslim men and Muslim women…
33:36: The Almighty said: And it is not for a believing man or a believing woman to have any choice. Al-Tabarani narrated with a sound chain of transmission on the authority of Qatada, who said: The Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, proposed to Zaynab, intending her for Zayd. She thought that he wanted her for himself, but when she learned that he wanted her for Zayd, she refused. Then God revealed: And it is not for a believing man or a believing woman to have any choice. So she agreed and submitted. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Ikrimah on the authority of Ibn Abbas that the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, proposed to Zaynab bint Jahsh for Zayd ibn Haritha, but she refused him and said, I am better than him in lineage. So God revealed, And it is not for a believer to have a right to disbelieve, the entire verse. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Al-Awfi on the authority of Ibn Abbas something similar. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Ibn Zayd who said: It was revealed about Umm Kulthum bint Uqbah ibn Abi Mu’ayt, who was the first woman to emigrate. She offered herself to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and he married her to Zayd ibn Haritha. She and her brother were displeased and said: We only wanted the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and we married him to his slave. So it was revealed.
33:37: God Almighty said: And when you say the verses. Al-Bukhari narrated on the authority of Anas that this verse: And you conceal within yourself what God is about to reveal. It was revealed about the daughter of Jahsh and Zaid bin Haritha. Al-Hakim narrated on the authority of Anas that he said: Zaid bin Haritha came to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, complaining about Zainab bint Jahsh. The Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, said: Keep your wife. So the following was revealed: And you conceal in yourself what God will reveal. Muslim, Ahmad and al-Nasa’i narrated that he said: When Zaynab’s waiting period had ended, the Messenger of God (peace and blessings of God be upon him) said to Zayd, Go and mention her to me. So he went and informed her. She said, I will not do anything until I consult my Lord. So she got up to go to her place of prayer and the Qur’an was revealed. The Messenger of God (peace and blessings of God be upon him) came and entered upon her without permission. I saw us when she entered upon the Messenger of God (peace and blessings of God be upon him), we fed her bread and meat. Then the people went out and some men remained talking in the house after the meal. Then the Messenger of God (peace and blessings of God be upon him) went out and I followed him. He kept following his wives’ rooms. Then he was informed that the people had gone out, so he went until he entered the house. I went to enter with him and threw a curtain between me and him. Then the veil was revealed and the people were admonished with what they were admonished with: Do not enter the houses of the Prophet unless you are given permission. At-Tirmidhi
33:40: Al-Tirmidhi narrated on the authority of Aisha that she said: When the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, married Zaynab, they said: He married his son’s wife. Then God revealed: Muhammad is not the father of any of your men.
33:43: God Almighty says: He is the One who sends blessings upon you, the verse. Abd bin Hamid narrated on the authority of Mujahid that he said: When the verse was revealed: God and His angels send blessings upon the Prophet, Abu Bakr said: O Messenger of God, God has not sent down any good upon you without making us share in it. So the verse was revealed: He is the One who sends blessings upon you, and His angels.
33:47: God Almighty said: And give good tidings to the believers. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Ikrimah and Al-Hasan Al-Basri that when the verse was revealed: So that God may forgive you your sins, past and future, some men from among the believers said: “Congratulations to you, O Messenger of God. We know what will be done to you, so what will be done to us?” Then God revealed: So that the believing men and women may enter the Gardens of Paradise. And in Surat Al-Ahzab it was revealed: And give good tidings to the believers that they will have from God great bounty. Al-Bayhaqi narrated in Dala’il al-Nubuwwah on the authority of al-Rabi’ ibn Anas, who said: When the verse And I do not know what will be done with me or with you was revealed, it was revealed after it, So that God may forgive you your past and future sins. They said: O Messenger of God, we know what will be done with you, so what will be done with us? Then it was revealed, And give good tidings to the believers that they will have from God a great bounty. He said: The great bounty is Paradise.
33:50: The Almighty said, O Prophet, indeed We have made lawful for you [women], until the end of the verse. Al-Tirmidhi included it and declared it good, and Al-Hakim authenticated it on the authority of Al-Suddi on the authority of Abu Salih on the authority of Ibn Abbas on the authority of Umm Hani’ bint Abi Talib, who said, “The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, proposed to me, so I apologized to him, and he excused me, so God revealed, “Indeed, We have made lawful for you [women] those who emigrated with you,” until His saying, But I was not lawful for him because I had not emigrated. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Ismail bin Abi Khalid on the authority of Abu Saleh on the authority of Umm Hani’ who said: This verse was revealed regarding: And the daughters of your paternal uncles and the daughters of your paternal aunts and the daughters of your maternal uncles and the daughters of your maternal aunts who migrated with you. The Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, wanted to marry me, but he was forbidden from me because I did not migrate. God Almighty said: And a believing woman (verse). Ibn Saad narrated on the authority of Ikrimah regarding His statement: And a believing woman verse, he said: It was revealed about Umm Sharik al-Dawsiyyah. Ibn Saad narrated on the authority of Munir bin Abdullah Al-Du’ali that Umm Sharik Ghaziyya bint Jabir bin Hakim Al-Dawsiyya offered herself to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and she was beautiful, so he accepted her. Aisha said, There is no woman who gives herself to a good man. Umm Sharik said, I am that one. So God called her a believer, and He said, And a believing woman is if she gives herself to the Prophet. So when the verse was revealed, Aisha said, God hastens your desire for you.
33:51: God Almighty says: You may have intercourse with whomever you wish. The two sheikhs narrated on the authority of Aisha that she used to say: Isn’t a woman ashamed to give herself away? Then God revealed: You may have intercourse with whomever you wish. Aisha said: I see your Lord hastening to fulfill your desires. Ibn Saad narrated on the authority of Abu Razin who said: The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, wanted to divorce some of his wives, but when they saw that, they absolved him of his responsibility and allowed him to prefer whomever he wanted over whomever he wanted. Then God revealed: Indeed, We have made lawful to you your wives until His saying: You may defer to any of them you wish.
33:52: God Almighty said: It is not lawful for you to have other wives after that. Ibn Saad narrated on the authority of Ikrimah who said: The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, gave his wives the choice, so they chose God and His Messenger. Then God revealed: It is not lawful for you to have other wives after that, nor to exchange them for other wives.
33:53: God Almighty said, O you who have believed, do not enter the verse. The hadith of Omar was presented in Surat Al-Baqarah. Al-Bukhari and Muslim narrated on the authority of Anas that he said: When the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, married Zaynab bint Jahsh, he invited the people and they ate, then sat down to talk. He looked as if he was preparing to get up, but they did not get up. When he saw that, he got up, and some of the people got up, and three sat down, then they left. I came and told the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, that they had left. He came until he entered, and I went to enter, but he threw down the veil between me and him. God revealed: O you who have believed, do not enter the houses of the Prophet until His saying: Indeed, that is in the sight of God an enormity. Al-Tirmidhi narrated and authenticated it on the authority of Anas, who said: I was with the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and he came to the door of a woman he had married. There were people with her, so he left and returned and they had left. He entered and lowered a curtain between me and him. I mentioned it to Abu Talha, and he said: If it is as you say, something will be revealed about this. Then the verse of the veil was revealed. Al-Tabarani narrated with a sound chain of transmission on the authority of Aisha, who said: “I was eating with the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, in a bowl. Umar passed by and invited him to eat. His finger touched my finger, and he said: ‘Oh, if he had obeyed you, no eye would have seen you.’ Then the verse of hijab was revealed.” And Ibn Mardawayh narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: A man entered upon the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and he sat for a long time. So the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, went out three times to go out, but he did not. Then Umar entered and saw the dislike on his face, so he said to the man: Perhaps you have offended the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace. The Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, said: I stood up three times so that he would follow me, but he did not. So Umar said to him: O Messenger of God, if you were to wear a veil, then your women are not like other women, and that is purer for their hearts. So the verse of the veil was revealed. Al-Hafiz Ibn Hajar said: It is possible to reconcile that by saying that this happened before the story of Zaynab, because of his closeness to her, the verse of the veil was revealed for this reason, and there is no objection to multiple reasons. Ibn Saad narrated on the authority of Muhammad bin Kaab, who said: Whenever the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, would go to his house, they would rush to him and take up their seats. But that was not evident on the face of the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, nor would he extend his hand to the food out of shyness from them. So they were rebuked for that, so God revealed: O you who have believed, do not enter the houses of the Prophet, the verse. God Almighty said: And it is not for you to harm the Messenger of God - Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Ibn Zayd who said: The Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, was informed that a man was saying: “If the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, had died, I would marry so-and-so after him.” So the verse was revealed: And it is not for you to harm the Messenger of God - the verse. And he narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: It was revealed about a man who intended to marry one of the wives of the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, after him. Sufyan said: They mentioned that it was Aisha. K and he narrated on the authority of Al-Suddi who said: It has reached us that Talha bin Ubaid God said: Will Muhammad keep us away from our paternal cousins and marry our women? If something happens to him, we will marry his women after him. So this verse was revealed. K and Ibn Saad narrated on the authority of Abu Bakr bin Muhammad bin Amr bin Hazm who said: It was revealed about Talha bin Ubaid God because he said: When the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, dies, I will marry Aisha. And Juwaybir narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas that a man came to one of the wives of the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and spoke to her, and he was her cousin. The Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, said: Do not stand in this position after this day of yours. He said: O Messenger of God, she is my cousin, and by God I did not say anything reprehensible to her, and she did not say anything to me. The Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, said: I know that, for there is no one more jealous than God, and there is no one more jealous than me. Then he went on and said: He prevents me from speaking to my cousin so that I may marry her after him. Then God revealed this verse. Ibn Abbas said: So that man freed a slave, carried ten camels in the way of God, and performed Hajj on foot, in repentance for his words.
33:57: God Almighty says: Those who harm the verse Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Al-Awfi on the authority of Ibn Abbas in his saying: Those who harm God and His Messenger the verse, he said: It was revealed about those who slandered the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, when he took Safiyyah bint Huyayy as his wife. Juwaybir said on the authority of Al-Dahhak on the authority of Ibn Abbas: It was revealed about Abdullah bin Ubayy and some people with him who slandered Aisha, so the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, delivered a sermon and said: Who will excuse me from a man who harms me and gathers in his house those who harm me? So it was revealed.
33:59: God Almighty said, O Prophet, tell your wives and your daughters, verse K. Al-Bukhari narrated on the authority of Aisha, who said, Sawda went out after the veil was imposed for her needs. She was a large woman who was not hidden from those who knew her. Umar saw her and said, O Sawda, by God, you are not hidden from us, so look how you go out. She said, I turned back and the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, was in my house, and he was eating dinner and in his hand was a vessel. I entered and said, O Messenger of God, I went out for some of my needs, and Umar said to me such and such. She said, God revealed to him and then lifted it from him, and the vessel was in his hand, but he did not put it down. He said, He has given you permission to go out for your needs. Ibn Saad narrated in Al-Tabaqat on the authority of Abu Malik, who said: The wives of the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, would go out at night to relieve themselves, and some of the hypocrites would approach them and cause them harm. They complained about that, and it was said to the hypocrites, and they said: We only do that to the female slaves. Then this verse was revealed: O Prophet, tell your wives and your daughters and the women of the believers to bring down over themselves [part] of their outer garments. That is more suitable that they will be known and not be harmed. Then he narrated something similar on the authority of Al-Hasan and Muhammad ibn Ka’b Al-Qurazi.
34:15: Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Ali bin Rabah who said: So-and-so told me that Furwah bin Masik Al-Ghatafani came to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and said: O Prophet of God, the Sheba are a people who had glory in the pre-Islamic era, and I fear that they will turn away from Islam, so should I fight them? He said: I have not been commanded to do anything about them yet. Then this verse was revealed: There were signs for the Sheba in their dwelling place.
34:34: And Ibn al-Mundhir and Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Sufyan on the authority of Asim on the authority of Ibn Razin that there were two men who were partners, one of them went to the Levant and the other remained. When the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, was sent, he wrote to his companion asking him what he should do. He wrote to him that no one from Quraysh followed him except the dregs and poor of the people, so he left his business and then came to his companion and said: Show me who he was. He was reading some books, so he came to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and said: What do you call to? He said: To such and such. He said: I bear witness that you are the Messenger of God. He said: How do you know that? He said: No prophet was sent except that the dregs and poor of the people followed him. Then this verse was revealed: And We did not send to a city a warner but its affluent said: Indeed, we, in that with which you have been sent, are disbelievers. So the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, sent to him: God has revealed confirmation of what you have said.
35:8: Juwaybir narrated on the authority of Al-Dahhak on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: This verse was revealed: Is he to whom the evil of his deeds has been made attractive? The verse is where the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, said: O God, strengthen your religion with Umar ibn Al-Khattab or with Jahl ibn Hisham. So God guided Umar and led Abu Jahl astray. It was about them that it was revealed.
35:29: And Abdul Ghani bin Saeed Al Thaqafi narrated in his interpretation on the authority of Ibn Abbas that Husayn bin Al Harith bin Abdul Muttalib bin Abdul Manaf Al Qurashi was the subject of the verse: Indeed, those who recite the Book of God and establish prayer
35:35: Al-Bayhaqi in Al-Ba'th and Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Nafi` bin Al-Harith on the authority of Abdullah bin Abi Awfa who said: A man said to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace: O Messenger of God, sleep is one of the things with which God comforts our eyes in this world. Is there sleep in Paradise? He said: No, sleep is a partner of death, and there is no death in Paradise. He said: What is their rest? The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, was most distressed by that. He said: There is no weariness in it. All their affair is rest. Then the verse was revealed: No portion therein shall touch us, nor shall weariness therein touch us.
35:42: Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Ibn Abi Hilal that he had heard that the Quraysh used to say, If God had sent a prophet from among us, no nation would have been more obedient to its Creator, more obedient to its Book, or more steadfast in adhering to its Book than us. So God revealed, They used to say, ‘If only we had had a reminder from among the former peoples, and if the Book had been sent down to us, we would have been better guided than they.’ And they swore. By God, their oaths were so strong that if a warner came to them, they would be better guided than any of the nations. And the Jews used to seek victory through him over the Christians, saying, Indeed, we find a prophet coming out.
36:1: Abu Naim narrated in Al-Dala’il on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, would recite in prostration, and he would recite loudly until some of the Quraysh were bothered by him, so they stood up to take him, and their hands were tied to their necks, and they were blind and could not see. They came to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and said: We ask you by God and the womb, O Muhammad. So he prayed until that went away from them, and then Ya-Sin was revealed, and by the Wise Qur’an, up to His saying: Or do you not warn them that they will not believe? He said: Not one of those people believed.
36:8: And Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Ikrimah who said: Abu Jahl said: If I see Muhammad, I will do this and I will do that. So God revealed: Indeed, We have placed shackles on their necks until His saying: They do not see. So they would say: This is Muhammad. So he would say: Where is he? Where is he? And he would not see.
36:12: Al-Tirmidhi narrated and authenticated it, and Al-Hakim narrated and authenticated it, on the authority of Abu Saeed Al-Khudri, who said: The Banu Salamah were in the outskirts of Medina, and they wanted to move closer to the mosque, so this verse was revealed: Indeed, it is We who bring the dead to life, and We record what they have put forth and what they left behind. The Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, said: Your left behind are being recorded, so do not move. Al-Tabarani narrated something similar on the authority of Ibn Abbas.
36:77: And Al-Hakim narrated and authenticated it on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: Al-Aasi bin Wa’il came to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, with a decayed bone, so he crumbled it and said: O Muhammad, will this be resurrected after it has decayed? He said: Yes, God will resurrect this, then cause you to die, then bring you back to life, then cause you to enter the fire of Hell. Then the verses were revealed: Does man not see that We created him from a sperm-drop? until the end of the surah. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated, through various chains of transmission on the authority of Mujahid, Ikrimah, Urwah bin Al-Zubayr, and Al-Suddi, something similar to it, and they named the man Abi bin Khalaf.
37:64: Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Qatada who said: Abu Jahl said: Your companion claimed that there is a tree in Hell and Hell consumes trees. By God, we do not know Zaqqum to be anything but dates and foam. So God revealed when they were amazed that there is a tree in Hell: It is a tree that springs forth from the bottom of Hellfire. And he narrated something similar on the authority of Al-Suddi.
37:158: And Juwaybir narrated on the authority of Al-Dahhak on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: This verse was revealed about three tribes of Quraysh 106:Salim, Khuza’ah, and Juhaynah, and they made a relationship between him and Paradise. The verse. Al-Bayhaqi narrated in Shu’ab al-Iman on the authority of Mujahid who said: The elders of Quraysh said: The angels are the daughters of God. Abu Bakr al-Siddiq said to them: Who are their mothers? They said: The daughters of the leaders of the jinn. Then God revealed: And Paradise knows that they will be brought to justice.
37:165: Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Yazid bin Abi Malik, who said: The people were praying in rows, then God revealed: And indeed, it is We who line up verse, and He commanded them to line up. Ibn al-Mundhir narrated on the authority of Ibn Jurayj, who said: I was told, and he mentioned something similar to it. Juwaybir narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: They said: O Muhammad, show us the punishment with which you have threatened us, and hasten it for us. So the verse was revealed: Do they then seek to hasten Our punishment? This verse is authentic according to the conditions of the two sheikhs.
38:5: Ahmad, Al-Tirmidhi, Al-Nasa'i and Al-Hakim narrated - and authenticated - on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: Abu Talib fell ill and the Quraysh came to him and the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, came to him and complained about him to Abu Talib. He said: O son of my brother, what do you want from your people? He said: I want from them one word by which the Arabs will submit to them and the non-Arabs will pay them the jizyah - one word. He said: What is it? He said: There is no god but God. They said: One God. This is indeed an amazing thing. Then the following verse was revealed about them, and the Qur’an, up to His saying: Rather, when they taste the punishment of the verse.
39:3: God Almighty said: And those who took the verse. Juwaybir narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas regarding this verse. He said: It was revealed about three tribes: Amir, Kinanah, and Banu Salamah. They used to worship idols and say: The angels are his daughters. They said: We only worship them so that they may bring us closer to God.
39:9: The Almighty’s saying: Or is he who is devoutly obedient during the hours of the night? The verse. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Ibn Umar regarding the Almighty’s saying: Or is he who is devoutly obedient the verse. He said: It was revealed about Uthman ibn Affan. Ibn Saad narrated on the authority of Al-Kalbi on the authority of Abu Salih on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: It was revealed about Ammar ibn Yasir. Juwaybir narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: It was revealed about Ibn Masoud, Ammar ibn Yasir, and Salim, the freed slave of Abu Hudhayfah. Juwaybir narrated on the authority of Ikrimah, who said: It was revealed about Ammar ibn Yasir.
39:17: God Almighty said: And those who avoided Taghut, the verse. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Zaid bin Aslam that this verse was revealed about three men who used to say, There is no god but God, during the pre-Islamic period: Zaid bin Amr bin Nufail, Abu Dharr al-Ghifari, and Salman al-Farsi. God Almighty said: So give good tidings to My servants, the verse. Juwaybir narrated on the authority of Jabir ibn Abdullah that he said: When the verse Seven chapters was revealed, a man from the Ansar came to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and said: O Messenger of God, I have seven slaves, and I have freed a slave for each chapter of them. So this verse was revealed about him: So give good tidings to My servants who listen to the word and follow the best of it.
39:23: God Almighty said: God revealed the verse. The reason for it was presented in Surat Yusuf.
39:36: And they frighten you with those besides Him, the verse. Abd al-Razzaq narrated on the authority of Muammar that a man said to me: They said to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace: You must refrain from cursing our gods, or we will command them to drive you crazy. So the verse was revealed: And they frighten you with those besides Him, the verse.
39:45: God Almighty said: And when God is mentioned, the verse. Ibn al-Mundhir narrated on the authority of Mujahid that it was revealed regarding the Prophet’s, may God bless him and grant him peace, recitation of Surat al-Najm at the Kaaba and their joy at the mention of the gods.
39:53: God Almighty said: Say, O My servants who have transgressed. The verse was presented in the hadith of the two sheikhs in Surat Al-Furqan. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated with a sound chain of transmission on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: This verse was revealed about the polytheists of Mecca. Al-Hakim and Al-Tabarani narrated on the authority of Ibn Omar, who said: We used to say: There is no repentance for a tempted person if he abandons his religion after his conversion to Islam and his knowledge. Then when the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, came to Medina, the verse was revealed about them: O My servants who have transgressed. Al-Tabarani narrated with a weak chain of transmission on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, sent to Wahshi, the killer of Hamza, to invite him to Islam. He sent him a message: How do you invite me while you claim that whoever kills, commits adultery, or associates partners with God will receive a double punishment on the Day of Resurrection and will abide therein in disgrace? I did that. Do you find any dispensation for me? Then God revealed: Except for those who repent, believe, and do righteous work. The verse. Wahshi said: This is a strict condition. Except for those who repent, believe, and do righteous work. Perhaps I will not be able to do this. Then God revealed: God does not forgive association with Him, but He forgives what is less than that for whomever He wills. Wahshi said: I see a will after this, but I do not know whether I will be forgiven or not. Is there anything else? Then God revealed: O My servants who have transgressed against themselves, do not despair of the mercy of God. The verse. Wahshi said: Yes, and he became Muslim.
39:64: As God Almighty said: Say: Then do you order me to worship other than God? The reason for its revelation will come in Surat Al-Kafirun. Al-Bayhaqi included in Al-Dala’il on the authority of Al-Hasan Al-Basri who said: The polytheists said to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace: Are your fathers and grandfathers led astray, O Muhammad? So God revealed: Say: Then do you order me to worship other than God? Up to His saying: Of the grateful.
39:67: And Al-Tirmidhi narrated and authenticated it on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: A Jew passed by the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and said: What do you say, O Abu Al-Qasim, when God placed the heavens on this and the earths on this and the water on this and the mountains on this and God sent down - and they did not appraise God with His true appraisal - the verse. And the hadith is in Sahih with the wording: he recited without - then he sent down. K and Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Al-Hasan who said: The Jews went out and looked at the creation of the heavens, the earth, and the angels. When they finished, they began to estimate it. Then God revealed: They did not estimate God as He should be estimated. K and he narrated on the authority of Saeed bin Jubair who said: The Jews spoke about the description of the Lord and said what they did not know and did not see, so God revealed this verse. K and Ibn al-Mundhir narrated on the authority of al-Rabi’ ibn Anas who said: When the verse His Throne encompasses the heavens and the earth was revealed, they said: O Messenger of God, this Throne is like this, so how is the Throne?” So God revealed: And they did not estimate God - the verse.
40:4: Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Al-Suddi on the authority of Abu Malik regarding his statement, None disputes concerning the verses of God except those who disbelieve, he said, It was revealed concerning Al-Harith bin Qais Al-Sahmi.
40:56: And he narrated on the authority of Abu al-Aaliyah, who said: The Jews came to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and mentioned the Antichrist, and they said: He will be one of us at the end of time. So they magnified his matter and said: He will do such and such. So God revealed: Indeed, those who dispute concerning the verses of God without any authority that has come to them - there is in their hearts nothing but pride. They will never attain it. So seek refuge in God, and He commanded His Prophet to seek refuge from the trial of the Antichrist.
40:57: The Almighty’s saying: The creation of the heavens and the earth is greater than the creation of mankind. He said: Who created the Antichrist? And he narrated on the authority of Ka’b al-Ahbar regarding his saying: Those who dispute concerning the signs of God without authority. He said: They are the Jews. It was revealed regarding what they are awaiting regarding the matter of the Antichrist.
40:66: And Juwaybir narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas that Al-Walid bin Al-Mughira and Shaiba bin Rabi’a said: O Muhammad, go back on what you are saying and stick to the religion of your fathers and grandfathers. So God revealed: Say: I have been forbidden to worship those you invoke besides God.
41:22: Al-Bukhari and Muslim, Al-Tirmidhi, Ahmad and others narrated on the authority of Ibn Mas`ud who said: Three men, two Quraysh and one Thaqafi, or two Thaqafis and one Quraysh, argued at the Ka`bah. One of them said: Do you think God hears what we say? The other said: He hears if we speak loudly but does not hear if we speak quietly. The third said: If He hears when we speak loudly, then He hears if we speak quietly. Then God revealed: And you were not concealing the verse.
41:40: Ibn al-Mundhir narrated on the authority of Bashir ibn Fath who said: This verse was revealed about Abu Jahl and Ammar ibn Yasir. Is he who is thrown into the Fire better or he who comes secure on the Day of Resurrection?
41:44: Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Saeed bin Jubair who said: Quraysh said: Why was this Qur’an not revealed in a foreign language and in Arabic? Then God revealed the verse, and they would have said: Why were its verses not explained in detail? And God revealed after this verse in it in every language. Ibn Jarir said: The recitation according to this is in a foreign language without question.
42:16: Ibn al-Mundhir narrated on the authority of Ikrimah who said: When the verse, When there comes the help of God and the conquest, was revealed, the polytheists in Mecca said to the believers among them, “People have entered the religion of God in multitudes, so leave us. So why do you remain among us?” Then the verse was revealed, And those who dispute about God after He has been answered. And Abd al-Razzaq narrated on the authority of Qatada regarding his statement, And those who argue with the verse, he said, “They are the Jews and Christians. They said, ‘Our book came before your book and our prophet came before your prophet, and we are better than you.’”
42:23: At-Tabarani narrated with a weak chain of transmission on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: The Ansar said: If we had gathered wealth for the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, then God revealed: “Say: I do not ask of you for it any reward except love for my relatives.” Some of them said: He only said this to fight for his family and support them. Then God revealed: Or do they say he has fabricated a lie against God? until His saying: And He is the One who accepts repentance from His servants. Then He offered them repentance until His saying: And He increases them from His bounty.
42:27: Al-Hakim narrated and authenticated it on the authority of Ali, who said: This verse was revealed about the people of the Suffa, and if God were to expand the provision for His servants, they would transgress on the earth. This is because they said: If only we had it, then they would desire the world. Al-Tabarani narrated something similar on the authority of Amr bin Harith.
43:19: Ibn al-Mundhir narrated on the authority of Qatada who said: Some of the hypocrites said that God married the jinn, so the angels came out from among them and He descended among them and made the angels, who are the servants of the Most Merciful, females.
43:31: And it was presented in Surah Yunus the reason for his saying, And they said, ‘Why was it not sent down?’ and the two verses.
43:36: And Ibn al-Mundhir narrated on the authority of Qatada who said: Al-Walid ibn al-Mughira said: If what Muhammad says is true, this Qur’an would have been revealed to me or to Masoud al-Thaqafi, and it would have been revealed. And Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Muhammad bin Uthman Al-Makhzumi that Quraysh said: Assign a man for each of the companions of Muhammad to take him. So they assigned Talha to Abu Bakr. He came to him while he was among the people, and Abu Bakr said: What do you call me to? He said: I call you to the worship of Al-Lat and Al-Uzza. Abu Bakr said: And what is Al-Lat? He said: Our Lord. He said: And what is Al-Uzza? He said: The daughters of God. Abu Bakr said: Who led them? Talha was silent and did not answer him. So Talha said to his companions: Answer the man. The people were silent. Then Talha said: “Rise, O Abu Bakr. I bear witness that there is no god but God and that Muhammad is the Messenger of God.” Then God revealed: And whoever turns away from the remembrance of the Most Merciful - We will appoint for him a devil.
43:57: Ahmad narrated with a sound chain of transmission and al-Tabarani on the authority of Ibn Abbas that the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, said to the Quraysh that there is no one who is worshipped other than God in whom there is good. They said, Do you not claim that Jesus was a prophet and a righteous servant and was worshipped other than God? Then God revealed, And when the son of Mary was cited as an example, the verse:
43:80: Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Muhammad ibn Ka`b al-Qurazi, who said: While there were three men between the Ka`bah and its curtains, two of them from Quraysh and one from Thaqafi, or two from Thaqafi and one from Quraysh, one of them said: Do you think that God hears our speech? Another said: If you speak aloud, He hears, and if you speak in secret, He does not hear. Then the verse was revealed: Or do they think that We do not hear their secrets and their private conversations?
43:83: 84 - And He is the One who is God in the heaven by realizing the two hamzas and dropping the first and facilitating it like the ya’, meaning worshipped and God on the earth and each of the two adverbials is related to what comes after it and He is the Wise in managing His creation the Knowing of their interests
44:10: Al-Bukhari narrated on the authority of Ibn Mas`ud, who said: When the Quraysh resisted the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, he invoked against them years like the years of Joseph, and they were afflicted with a drought until they ate their bones. A man would look at the sky and see what was between him and it was like smoke from the effort. Then God revealed: Wait for the day when the sky will bring forth a visible smoke. Then the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, came and it was said: O Messenger of God, ask God to send rain for Mudar, for they have perished. So he asked for rain and they were given rain. Then this was revealed.
44:15: You will return. But when they were granted prosperity, they returned to their state. Then God revealed, On the Day We will strike with the greatest strike, We will take revenge. That is, on the Day of Badr.
44:16: You will return. But when they were granted prosperity, they returned to their state. Then God revealed, On the Day We will strike with the greatest strike, We will take revenge. That is, on the Day of Badr.
44:43: And Saeed bin Mansour narrated on the authority of Abu Malik, who said: Abu Jahl used to bring dates and butter and say: Eat them, for this is the tree of Zaqqum that Muhammad promises you. So the verse was revealed: The tree of Zaqqum is food for the sinful.
44:49: Al-Umawi narrated in his book Al-Maghazi on the authority of Ikrimah who said: The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, met Abu Jahl and said: God has commanded me to say to you: ‘It is better for you, then better, then better for you, then better.’ He said: So he took his garment from his hand and said: ‘You and your companion cannot do anything to me. You know that I can protect the people of Batha’, and I am the mighty, the generous.’ So God killed him on the day of Badr and humiliated him and disgraced him with his word, and the verse ‘Taste, for you are the mighty, the generous’ was revealed about him. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Qatada something similar to it.
45:23: Ibn al-Mundhir and Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Saeed bin Jubair, who said: The Quraysh used to worship stones for a period of time, and when they found something better than it, they would throw away the first and worship the other. Then God revealed: Have you seen he who takes his desire as his god?
45:24: And he narrated on the authority of Abu Hurairah, who said: The people of the Age of Ignorance used to say: It is only the night and the day that destroy us. So God revealed: “And they said: ‘There is nothing but our worldly life; we die and live, and nothing destroys us except time.’”
46:10: Al-Tabarani narrated with a sound chain of transmission on the authority of Awf bin Malik Al-Ashja’i, who said: The Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and I went with him until we entered the church of the Jews on their feast day. They disliked our entering upon them, so the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, said to them: O group of Jews, show me twelve men from among you who bear witness that there is no god but God and that Muhammad is the Messenger of God. God will remove the wrath that is upon every Jew under the sky. They remained silent, but none of them answered him. Then he turned away, and there was a man behind him. He said: As you are, O Muhammad. So he came forward and said: Which man do you teach me from among you, O group of Jews? They said: By God, we do not know among us a man who was more knowledgeable of the Book of God or more understanding than you, nor than your father before you, nor than your grandfather. Say: Your father. He said: Then I bear witness that he is the prophet you find in the Torah. They said: You have lied. Then they responded to him and said evil about him. Then God revealed: Say: Have you considered, if it is from God and you have disbelieved in it? The verse. Al-Bukhari and Muslim narrated on the authority of Sa`d ibn Abi Waqqas, who said: It was revealed about Abdullah ibn Salam, and a witness from the Children of Israel testified to something similar. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Abdullah ibn Salam, who said: It was revealed about...
46:11: And he also narrated on the authority of Qatada who said: Some of the polytheists said: We are more honorable, we, we, and if it had been good, so-and-so and so-and-so would not have preceded us to it. So it was revealed: And those who disbelieved said: K and Ibn al-Mundhir narrated on the authority of Awn bin Abi Shaddad who said: Umar bin al-Khattab had a female slave who had converted to Islam before him and she was called Zanin. Umar used to beat her for her conversion to Islam until she became slack. The people of Quraysh used to say: If it had been good, Zanin would not have preceded us in it. So God revealed concerning her: “And those who disbelieved said to those who believed: ‘If it had been good,’” the verse. Ibn Saad narrated something similar on the authority of al-Dahhak and al-Hasan.
46:17: And Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Al-Suddi who said: This verse was revealed: And he who said to his parents, ‘Fie upon you both,’ concerning Abd al-Rahman ibn Abi Bakr. He said to his parents, and they had become Muslims, but he refused to become Muslims. They used to order him to become Muslims, but he would respond to them and call them liars, saying: Where is so-and-so and so-and-so? meaning the elders of Quraysh who had died and then converted to Islam afterward, and his conversion to Islam was good. So his repentance was revealed in this verse: And for each is degree according to what they have done. And Ibn Jarir narrated, on the authority of Al-Awfi, on the authority of Ibn Abbas, something similar. But Al-Bukhari narrated on the authority of Yusuf bin Mahan who said: Marwan said about Abd al-Rahman bin Abi Bakr: This is the one about whom God revealed, and he who said to his parents: Fiye to you both. Aisha said from behind the veil: God did not reveal anything about us in the Qur’an except that God revealed my excuse. And Abd al-Razzaq narrated on the authority of Makki that he heard Aisha denying that the verse was revealed about Abd al-Rahman bin Abi Bakr and she said: It was only revealed about so-and-so, she named a man. Al-Hafiz Ibn Hajar said: Aisha’s denial is more authentic in chain of transmission and more worthy of acceptance.
46:29: Ibn Abi Shaybah narrated on the authority of Ibn Mas`ud, who said that the jinn descended upon the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, while he was reciting the Qur’an in Batn Nakhl. When they heard him, they said, Listen! They were nine, one of them being Zub`ah. Then God revealed, And when We turned to you a company of the jinn, until His saying, manifest error.
47:1: Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas regarding his statement, Those who disbelieved and averted from the way of God, He will render worthless their deeds, he said, They are the people of Mecca, it was revealed about them, and those who believed and did righteous deeds, he said, They are the Ansar.
47:4: And he brought out on the authority of Qatada regarding his statement, And those who were killed in the way of God, he said, “It was mentioned to us that this verse was revealed on the day of Uhud, and the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, was in the valley, and wounds and killing had broken out among them. The polytheists cried out on that day, “Hubal is exalted,” and the Muslims cried out, God is Most High and Most Great. The polytheists said, We have Al-Uzza, and you have no Uzza. So the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, said, Say, ‘God is our protector, and you have no protector.’
47:13: Abu Ya`la narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: When the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, went out towards the cave, he looked at Mecca and said: You are the most beloved of God’s lands to me, and had your people not expelled me from you, I would not have left you. Then God revealed: How many a city was stronger in power than your city which expelled you? The verse.
47:16: Ibn al-Mundhir narrated on the authority of Ibn Jurayj, who said: The believers and the hypocrites would gather around the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace. The believers would hear what he said and understand it, and the hypocrites would hear it but not understand it. They were afraid and asked the believers what he had just said, so the verse was revealed: And among them are those who enjoy vessels.
47:33: Ibn Abi Hatim and Muhammad bin Nasr al-Marwazi narrated in the Book of Prayer on the authority of Abu al-Aaliyah, who said: The companions of the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, believed that no sin would harm anyone who says There is no god but God, just as no action would benefit anyone who does not practice polytheism. Then the verse Obey God and obey the Messenger and do not invalidate your deeds was revealed. They feared that sin would invalidate the deeds.
48:1: Al-Hakim and others narrated on the authority of Al-Miswar bin Makhramah and Marwan bin Al-Hakam that they said: Surat Al-Fath was revealed between Mecca and Medina regarding Al-Hudaybiyyah from beginning to end.
48:2: And the two Sheikhs, Al-Tirmidhi and Al-Hakim narrated on the authority of Anas who said: It was revealed to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, so that God may forgive you your past and future sins. His return from Al-Hudaybiyah. The Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, said: A verse has been revealed to me that is more beloved to me than anything on the earth. Then he recited it to them and they said: Congratulations, O Messenger of God. God has made clear to you what He will do with you, so what will He do with us? It was revealed so that the believing men and women may enter until he reached a great victory.
48:18: And Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Salamah bin Al-Akwa’ who said: While we were saying, when the caller of the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, called out, O people, pledge allegiance, pledge allegiance, the Holy Spirit descended, so we went to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, while he was under a samura tree, and we pledged allegiance to him, and God revealed: God has been pleased with the believers, the verse.
48:24: Muslim, Al-Tirmidhi, and Al-Nisa’i narrated on the authority of Anas that he said: “On the day of Al-Hudaybiyyah, eighty men in arms descended upon the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and his companions from Mount At-Tan’im, intending to surprise the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace. They seized them, and he freed them. Then God revealed: ‘It is He who restrained their hands from you and supported you from them.’” K and Muslim narrated something similar from the hadith of Salamah bin Al-Akwa K and Ahmad and Al-Nasa’i narrated something similar from the hadith of Abdullah bin Mughaffal Al-Muzani K and Ibn Ishaq narrated something similar from the hadith of Ibn Abbas
48:25: Al-Tabarani and Abu Ya’la narrated on the authority of Abu Juma’ah Junayd ibn Sab’ who said: I fought the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, at the beginning of the day as an unbeliever, and I fought with him at the end of the day as a Muslim. We were three men and seven women, and it was revealed about us: And if it were not for believing men and believing women.
48:27: Al-Faryabi, Abd bin Hamid, and Al-Bayhaqi narrated in Al-Dala’il on the authority of Mujahid who said: The Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, saw while he was at Al-Hudaybiyah that he and his companions were entering Mecca in safety, with their heads shaved and their hair cut short. When he slaughtered the sacrificial animals at Al-Hudaybiyah, his companions said: Where was your vision, O Messenger of God? Then the verse was revealed: God has fulfilled the vision of His Messenger, the verse.
49:1: God Almighty said: O you who have believed, do not put yourselves forward before God and His Messenger. Al-Bukhari and others narrated on the authority of Ibn Jurayj on the authority of Ibn Abi Malekah that Abdullah bin Az-Zubayr informed him that a group of Banu Tamim came to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and Abu Bakr said: The command of Al-Qa’qa’ bin Ma’bad. Umar said: Rather, the command of Al-Aqra’ bin Habis. Abu Bakr said: You only wanted to oppose me. Umar said: You only wanted to oppose you. So they argued until their voices rose, and God Almighty revealed concerning that: “O you who have believed, do not put yourselves forward before God and His Messenger until His saying: “And if they had been patient.” K and Ibn al-Mundhir narrated on the authority of al-Hasan that some people slaughtered before the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, on the Day of Sacrifice, so he ordered them to repeat the slaughter, so God revealed: O you who believe, do not put yourselves forward before God and His Messenger. Ibn Abi al-Dunya narrated in the Book of Sacrifices with the wording: A man slaughtered before the prayer, so this was revealed. Al-Tabarani narrated in Al-Awsat on the authority of Aisha that some people used to fast before the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, so God revealed: O you who have believed, do not put yourselves forward before God and His Messenger. K and Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Qatada who said: It was mentioned to us that some people used to say: If only something had been revealed about such and such, then God revealed: Do not put yourselves forward before God and His Messenger. K and he narrated on his authority that they used to speak to him loudly and raise their voices, so God revealed: Do not raise your voices, the verse. And he also narrated on the authority of Muhammad bin Thabit bin Qais bin Shammas, who said: When this verse was revealed, Do not raise your voices above the voice of the Prophet, Thabit bin Qais sat in the road crying. Then Asim bin Adi bin Al-Ajlan passed by him and said: What makes you cry? He said: This verse, I fear that it was revealed about me, and I have a loud voice. So Asim reported that to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, so he called for him and said: Are you not satisfied to live with praise, be killed as a martyr, and enter Paradise? He said: “I am satisfied, and I will never raise my voice above the voice of the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace.” Then God revealed: Those who lower their voices verse.
49:4: The Almighty said: Indeed, those who call you from behind the rooms, the two verses. Al-Tabarani and Abu Ya’la narrated with a good chain of transmission on the authority of Zayd ibn Arqam, who said: Some Arabs came to the room of the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and they began to call out, O Muhammad, O Muhammad. Then God revealed: Indeed, those who call you from behind the rooms, the verse. And Abd al-Razzaq said on the authority of Muammar on the authority of Qatada that a man came to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and said, O Muhammad, my praise is adornment and my cursing is disgraceful. So the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, said, That is God. So the verse was revealed, Indeed, those who call upon you… There are supporting evidence from the hadith of al-Bara’ and others in al-Tirmidhi without the verse being revealed. And Ibn Jarir narrated something similar on the authority of al-Hasan. K and Ahmad narrated with a sound chain of transmission on the authority of Al-Aqra’ bin Habis that he called out to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, from behind the rooms, but he did not answer him. He said, O Muhammad, my praise is adornment and my blame is ugliness. He said, That is God. K and Ibn Jarir and others also narrated on the authority of Al-Aqra’ that he came to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and said: O Muhammad, come out to us. So this was revealed:
49:6: God Almighty said: O you who have believed, if there comes to you a wicked person, Ahmad and others narrated with a good chain of transmission on the authority of Al-Harith ibn Dirar Al-Khuzami, who said: I came to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and he invited me to Islam, so I acknowledged it and entered it. He invited me to zakat, so I acknowledged it. I said: O Messenger of God, I will return to my people and invite them to Islam and the payment of zakat. Whoever responds to me, I will collect his zakat, so send to me by such and such a time so that what I have collected of zakat may come to you. When Al-Harith collected the zakat and the time came, the messenger was delayed and did not come to him. Al-Harith thought that he had become angry, so he called the leaders of his people and said to them: The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, had set a time when he would send his messenger to me to collect what I had of zakat, and the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, did not break it. I do not know why he delayed his messenger except out of anger. So we set out and came to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace. The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, sent Al-Walid ibn Uqbah to collect what he had. When Al-Walid left, he was afraid and returned and said: Al-Harith He prevented me from paying the zakat and wanted to kill me. So the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, sent an expedition to Al-Harith. Al-Harith approached with his companions when he received the expedition and said to them, Where have you been sent? They said, To you. He said, Why? They said, “The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, sent Al-Walid bin Uqbah to you. He claimed that you prevented him from paying the zakat and wanted to kill him.” He said, No, by Him who sent Muhammad with the truth, I have never seen him, nor did he come to me. When the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, entered upon me, he said, You prevented the zakat and wanted to kill My Messenger. He said, No, by Him who sent you with the truth. Then the verse, O you who have believed, if there comes to you a wicked person with information, was revealed, until His saying, And God is Knowing and Wise. The men of its chain of transmission are trustworthy. Al-Tabarani narrated something similar from the hadith of Jabir bin Abdullah, Alqamah bin Najiyah, Umm Salamah, and Ibn Jarir, something similar from the path of Al-Awfi from Ibn Abbas, and from other mursal paths.
49:9: The Almighty said: And indeed, two parties, the verse: The two sheikhs narrated on the authority of Anas that the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, rode a donkey and went to Abdullah bin Ubayy and said: Go away from me, for by God, the stench of your donkey has bothered me. A man from the Ansar said: By God, his donkey smells better than you. A man from his people became angry on behalf of Abdullah, and each of his companions became angry, and there was a clash between them with palm branches, hands, and sandals. Then the verse was revealed about them: And indeed, two parties among the believers should fight, so make peace between them. K and Saeed bin Mansour and Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Abu Malik that two Muslim men quarreled and one group became angry with the other and the other with the other, so they fought with their hands and sandals. God revealed the verse: And two groups shall fight. Ibn Jarir and Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Al-Suddi, who said: There was a man from the Ansar called Imran, and a woman called Umm Zaid loved him. The woman wanted to visit her family, but her husband kept her in his upper room. The woman sent word to her family, and her people came and brought her down to divorce her. The man had gone out and sought help from his family, but his cousins came to prevent the woman from her family, and they pushed each other and fought with sandals, so this verse was revealed about them. If two groups of believers fought, the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, was sent to them and made peace between them, and they obeyed God’s command. K and Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Al-Hasan, who said: There would be a dispute between two tribes, and they would be called to judgment, but they would refuse to answer. Then God revealed: And if two parties among the believers should fight, the verse. And he narrated on the authority of Qatada who said: It was mentioned to us that this verse was revealed about two men from the Ansar who were in dispute about a right between them. One of them said to the other: I will seize him by force because of the large number of his tribe. The other called him to bring him to trial before the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, but he refused. The matter continued until they pushed each other and attacked each other with their hands and sandals, but there was no fighting with swords.
49:11: God Almighty said: And do not insult one another by nicknames, the verse. The four Sunnah scholars narrated on the authority of Abu Jubayr ibn al-Dahhak, who said: A man among us would have two or three names and would be called by one of them, and perhaps he would dislike it. So this verse was revealed: And do not insult one another by nicknames. Al-Tirmidhi said: It is good. Al-Hakim and others also narrated from his hadith that he said: “There were nicknames in the pre-Islamic period, so the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, called a man from them by his nickname. It was said to him: O Messenger of God, he dislikes it. So God revealed: “And do not insult one another by nicknames.” Ahmad’s wording from him: He said: It was revealed about us, Banu Salamah, And do not insult one another by nicknames. The Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, came to Medina and there was not a man among us who did not have two or three names. So if he called one of them by one of those names, they would say: O Messenger of God, he gets angry about this. So it was revealed.
49:12: God Almighty said: And let not some of you backbite others. The verse was narrated by Ibn al-Mundhir on the authority of Ibn Jurayj who said: They claimed that it was revealed about Salman al-Farsi. He ate, then slept, and puffed. A man mentioned his eating and sleeping, so it was revealed.
49:13: God Almighty said, O people, the verse. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Ibn Abi Malekah, who said, “When the day of the conquest came, Bilal ascended to the roof of the Kaaba and called to prayer. Some of the people said, ‘Is this black slave calling to prayer on the roof of the Kaaba?’ Some of them said, ‘If God is displeased with this, He will change it.’ So God revealed, ‘O people, indeed We have created you from male and female.’ The verse was revealed. Ibn Asakir said in his ambiguities, ‘I found in the handwriting of Ibn Bashkuwal that Abu Bakr Ibn Abi Dawud narrated in his interpretation that it was revealed about Abu Hind. The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, ordered the Banu Bayada to marry him to a woman from among them. They said, ‘O Messenger of God, we marry our daughters to our clients.’ So the verse was revealed.”
49:17: The Almighty said: They do you a favor by becoming Muslims. Al-Tabarani narrated with a good chain of transmission on the authority of Abdullah Ibn Abi Awfa that some Arabs said: O Messenger of God, we have become Muslims and we did not fight you, but the Banu So-and-so fought you. So God revealed: They do you a favor by becoming Muslims. The verse Al-Bazzar narrated on the authority of Saeed bin Jubair on the authority of Ibn Abbas something similar. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated something similar on the authority of Al-Hasan, and that was when Mecca was conquered. Ibn Saad narrated on the authority of Muhammad bin Kaab Al-Qurazi, who said: Ten men from Banu Asad came to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, in the year nine, and among them was Tulaiha bin Khuwaylid, and the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, was in the mosque with his companions, so they greeted him.
