Their speaker said: O Messenger of God, we have borne witness that there is no god but God alone, with no partner, and that you are His servant and His Messenger. We came to you, O Messenger of God, and you were not sent to us, and we have surrendered to those behind us. Then God revealed: It is a favor to you that they have submitted. Saeed bin Mansour narrated in his Sunan on the authority of Saeed bin Jubair, who said: A group of Bedouins from Banu Asad came to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and said: We came to you and did not fight you. So God revealed: They do you a favor by converting to Islam.
50:36: <div id='div_ar2' style='text-align:left; direction:ltr; قوةَُْمَْْ ُْمن اَََونَُُْلَْا قَلْْفfont-size:20px;'>36 - من مَُب؟ُْْاُوا في الرض فْث
50:38: ا صلى ا َلَهْوصححه َن اَن ََاس َن َْت رَوَْْخَج اَحا ْالحْخلق ا الرض َوْفَأَه َن خلق اََموات والرض فَاْوَل الرَعاء غَوب اَْمسْاَثلثاء وفَُن من مَافع خلق َوَْوْوخلق ََا واَْمسًاََمعْاَخمَس اََماء خلق َوْواَمْائن واَعمَان وخلق َو حَى مات من ماتًََاَْاَات َََن مَه في َوًَِى ثلثًْوملئَواََم َِلَسَوَمًَََوَََْه اْْخلق آًَََفع َه اََاس وفي اَثاَث. ًََفي اَثا اََوىْثْقاْماَا َا محمْثْاََُوْقاًََاََه وَخََه في آخَََْو اَََاح فْضب اَََي صلىَْصَت َو َْممت قاَوا ثَْلى اَعَش قاَوا ق فيََُْغضَا َْْْا فَزَت وخلََا اََموات والرض وغْا َلَه وَل َْلى ما ََوََوما مََا من ََوب فاصََْاًََ <div id='div_ar2' style='text-align:left; direction:ltr; خلََا اََماوات والرض وما َََُما فيََْوfont-size:20px;'>38 -.وما مََا َْوبََْاًََ
50:39: <div id='div_ar2' style='text-align:left; direction:ltr; َْرَك قَْلى ما ََوَون وََح َحمََفاصfont-size:20px;'>39 - اََْوبَُْلوع اَْمس وق
50:45: And Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Amr bin Qais Al-Mala’i on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: They said: O Messenger of God, if you had frightened us, then it was revealed and he mentioned in the Qur’an whoever fears the threat. Then he narrated on the authority of Omar, without a chain of transmission, like it.
51:19: Ibn Jarir and Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Al-Hasan bin Muhammad bin Al-Hanafiyyah that the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, sent out a raiding party and they captured and took booty. Then some people came after they had finished, so the verse was revealed: And in their wealth there is a right for the needy and the deprived.
51:55: Ibn Mani', Ibn Rahawayh, and Al-Haytham Ibn Kulayb also narrated in their Musnads on the authority of Mujahid on the authority of Ali, who said: When the verse turn away from them, for you are not to be blamed was revealed, there remained not one of us who was not certain of destruction when the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, was commanded to turn away from us. So it was revealed and reminded, for reminding benefits the believers, and our souls were at peace. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Qatada who said: It was mentioned to us that when the verse So turn away from them was revealed, the companions of the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, were distressed and they saw that the revelation had been cut off and that the punishment had come. So God revealed and reminded, for the reminder benefits the believers.
52:30: Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas that when the Quraysh gathered in Dar al-Nadrah regarding the matter of the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, one of them said: Imprison him in chains and then await death for him until he perishes as the poets before him, Zuhayr and al-Nabigha, perished. He is like one of them. So God revealed concerning that: Or do they say, ‘He is a poet; we await for him the vicissitudes of death?’
53:32: Al-Wahidi, Al-Tabarani, Ibn Al-Mundhir Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Thabit Ibn Al-Harith Al-Ansari, who said: The Jews used to say when a young boy of theirs died, he was a friend. This reached the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and he said: The Jews have lied. There is no soul that God creates in its mother’s womb except that He knows whether it will be wretched or blessed. Then God revealed this verse: He knows you best when He created you from the earth.
53:33: Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Ikrimah that the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, went out on a military expedition and a man came wanting to carry something but he did not find anything to carry. He met a friend of his and said, Give me something. He said, I will give you this young camel on the condition that you do not bear my sins. He said to him, Yes. Then God revealed, Have you seen the one who took over? [The Verses] And he narrated on the authority of Darraj Abu al-Samee’ who said: A raiding party went out and a man asked the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, to carry him. He said: I do not find anything to carry you on. So he turned away sadly and passed by a man whose saddle was lying in front of him. So he complained to him. The man said to him: Would you like me to carry you so that you can join the army with your good deeds? He said: Yes. So he rode and dismounted. So have you seen the one who turned away? Until His saying: Then He will reward him with the fullest reward. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Ibn Zayd, who said: A man converted to Islam and was met by someone who was taunting him. He said: Have you abandoned the religion of the elders and led them astray and claimed that they are in Hell? He said: I feared the punishment of God. He said: Give me something and I will bear all the punishment that was upon you. So he gave him something. He said: Give me more. So they argued until he gave him something and wrote a letter and bore witness for him. So this verse was revealed about him: Have you seen he who turned away and gave little and was deprived?
53:34: Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Ikrimah that the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, went out on a military expedition and a man came wanting to carry something but he did not find anything to carry. He met a friend of his and said, Give me something. He said, I will give you this young camel on the condition that you do not bear my sins. He said to him, Yes. Then God revealed, Have you seen the one who took over? [The Verses] And he narrated on the authority of Darraj Abu al-Samee’ who said: A raiding party went out and a man asked the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, to carry him. He said: I do not find anything to carry you on. So he turned away sadly and passed by a man whose saddle was lying in front of him. So he complained to him. The man said to him: Would you like me to carry you so that you can join the army with your good deeds? He said: Yes. So he rode and dismounted. So have you seen the one who turned away? Until His saying: Then He will reward him with the fullest reward. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Ibn Zayd, who said: A man converted to Islam and was met by someone who was taunting him. He said: Have you abandoned the religion of the elders and led them astray and claimed that they are in Hell? He said: I feared the punishment of God. He said: Give me something and I will bear all the punishment that was upon you. So he gave him something. He said: Give me more. So they argued until he gave him something and wrote a letter and bore witness for him. So this verse was revealed about him: Have you seen he who turned away and gave little and was deprived?
53:35: Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Ikrimah that the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, went out on a military expedition and a man came wanting to carry something but he did not find anything to carry. He met a friend of his and said, Give me something. He said, I will give you this young camel on the condition that you do not bear my sins. He said to him, Yes. Then God revealed, Have you seen the one who took over? 53:61: Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: They used to pass by the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, while he was praying, standing tall. Then the verse was revealed: And you are standing tall.
54:1: Al-Bukhari and Muslim and Al-Hakim narrated, and the wording is his, on the authority of Ibn Masoud, who said: I saw the moon split into two halves in Mecca before the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, left. They said: The moon was bewitched, so it descended and the Hour drew near, and the moon split. Al-Tirmidhi narrated on the authority of Anas that he said: The people of Mecca asked the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, about a sign, and the moon split in Mecca twice, so it was revealed: The Hour has drawn near, and the moon has split, until His saying: Continuous magic.
54:45: Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas who said: They said on the day of Badr, We are all victorious. So the verse was revealed: The army will be defeated and they will turn their backs.
54:47: Muslim and Al-Tirmidhi narrated on the authority of Abu Hurairah, who said: The polytheists of Quraysh came to dispute with the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, about predestination, so the verse was revealed: The criminals are in error and madness, until His saying: Indeed, We have created all things with measure.
55:29: 29 - Whoever is in the heavens and the earth asks Him verbally or in a state, for what they need of strength for worship, provision, forgiveness, and other things every day at a time He is concerned with a matter that He reveals in accordance with what He has ordained in eternity of giving life and death, honor and humiliation, enrichment and destruction, answering a supplicant, giving to a supplicant, and other things.
55:46: Ibn Abi Hatim and Abu Sheikh narrated in the Book of Al-Azmah on the authority of Ata’ that Abu Bakr Al-Siddiq mentioned one day the Day of Resurrection, the scales, Paradise, and Hell, and he said, I wish I were one of these green things, and that an animal would come and eat me, and that I had not been created. So this verse was revealed: For whoever fears standing before his Lord are two Paradises. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Ibn Shawdhab, who said, This verse was revealed about Abu Bakr Al-Siddiq.
56:13: Ahmad, Ibn al-Mundhir, and Ibn Abi Hatim narrated with a chain of transmission that includes someone who is unknown, on the authority of Abu Hurayrah, who said: When a group of the first and a few of the last were revealed, it was difficult for the Muslims, so a group of the first and a group of the last were revealed. And Ibn Asakir included in the History of Damascus with a chain of transmission that is questionable on the authority of Urwah ibn Ruwaym on the authority of Habir ibn Abdullah who said: When the verse When the Event Occurs was revealed and a group of the former and a few of the latter were mentioned in it, Umar said: O Messenger of God, a group of the former and a few of us. So he held back the end of the surah for a year, then a group of the former and a group of the latter were revealed. So the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, said: O Umar, come and hear what God has revealed to a group of the former and a group of the latter. And Ibn Abi Hatim included on the authority of Urwah ibn Ruwaym, with no chain of transmission.
56:27: And Saeed bin Mansour included in his Sunan and Al-Bayhaqi in Al-Baath on the authority of Ata and Mujahid, they said: When the people of Taif asked for a valley to protect them and in it was honey, it was done. It was a marvelous valley, and they heard the people saying: There is such and such in Paradise. They said: Oh, would that we had in Paradise a valley like this. So God revealed: And the companions of the right hand, what are the companions of the right hand? Among thornless lote trees. The verses.
56:29: Al-Bayhaqi narrated from another source on the authority of Mujahid who said: They were amazed by the shade of a tree, its tamarisk trees, and its lote trees. Then God revealed: “And the companions of the right hand: What are the companions of the right hand? Among thornless lote trees and banana trees arranged in layers and an extended shade.” Muslim narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: It rained on the people during the time of the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, said: Some of the people are grateful and some are disbelievers. They said: This is a mercy that God has placed. And some of them said: The star of such-and-such has come true. So these verses were revealed: I swear by the positions of the stars, until he said: And you consider your provision to be lies. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Abu Hazra who said: These verses were revealed about a man from the Ansar during the Battle of Tabuk. They camped at Al-Hijr, so the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, ordered them not to carry any of its water. Then he moved on and camped at another place, and they did not have any water with them. They complained about that to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace. He stood up and prayed two rak’ahs, then he supplicated, and God sent a cloud that rained on them until they drew water from it. Then a man from the Ansar said to another of his people who was accused of hypocrisy: Woe to you! When will you see what the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, supplicated for, and God rained rain on us from the sky? He said: We were only rained by such and such a star.
56:39: Ahmad, Ibn al-Mundhir, and Ibn Abi Hatim narrated with a chain of transmission that includes someone who is unknown, on the authority of Abu Hurayrah, who said: When a group of the first and a few of the last were revealed, it was difficult for the Muslims, so a group of the first and a group of the last were revealed. And Ibn Asakir included in the History of Damascus with a chain of transmission that is questionable on the authority of Urwah ibn Ruwaym on the authority of Habir ibn Abdullah who said: When the verse When the Event Occurs was revealed and a group of the former and a few of the latter were mentioned in it, Umar said: O Messenger of God, a group of the former and a few of us. And Ibn Abi Hatim included on the authority of Urwah ibn Ruwaym, with no chain of transmission.
56:40: Ahmad, Ibn al-Mundhir, and Ibn Abi Hatim narrated with a chain of transmission that includes someone who is unknown, on the authority of Abu Hurayrah, who said: When a group of the first and a few of the last were revealed, it was difficult for the Muslims, so a group of the first and a group of the last were revealed. And Ibn Asakir included in the History of Damascus with a chain of transmission that is questionable on the authority of Urwah ibn Ruwaym on the authority of Habir ibn Abdullah who said: When the verse When the Event Occurs was revealed and a group of the former and a few of the latter were mentioned in it, Umar said: O Messenger of God, a group of the former and a few of us. So he held back the end of the surah for a year, then a group of the former and a group of the latter were revealed. And Ibn Abi Hatim included on the authority of Urwah ibn Ruwaym, with no chain of transmission.
57:16: Ibn Abi Shaybah narrated in Al-Musannaf on the authority of Abd Al-Aziz bin Abi Rawad that joking and laughter appeared among the companions of the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, so the verse was revealed: Has not the time come for those who have believed? And Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Muqatil bin Hibban, who said: The companions of the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, had engaged in some joking, so God revealed: Has not the time come for those who have believed that their hearts should become humbly submissive at the remembrance of God? The verse And he narrated on the authority of Al-Suddi on the authority of Al-Qasim, who said: The companions of the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, became bored with a way of life and said: Tell us, O Messenger of God. Then God revealed: We narrate to you the best of stories. Then they became bored with a way of life and said: Tell us, O Messenger of God. Then God revealed: Has not the time come for those who have believed that their hearts should become humbly submissive at the remembrance of God? Ibn al-Mubarak included in al-Zuhd that Sufyan told us on the authority of al-A’mash, who said: When the companions of the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, came to Medina and found what they had found of life after the hardship they had been under, it was as if they had relaxed from some of what they had been doing, so the verse was revealed: Is it not time for those who have believed that their hearts should become humbly submissive?
57:28: And Al-Tabarani narrated in Al-Awsat with a chain of transmission that includes someone who is not known on the authority of Ibn Abbas that forty of the Negus’ companions came to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and they witnessed Uhud with him. There were wounds among them, but none of them were killed. When they saw the need of the believers, they said, O Messenger of God, we are people of journeying, so give us permission to come with our wealth to help the Muslims. So God revealed about them, Those to whom We gave the Scripture before it believe in it. So when it was revealed, they said, O group of Muslims, as for he who believes among us in your Scripture, he will have two rewards, and he who does not believe in your Scripture, he will have a reward like your rewards. So God revealed, O you who have believed, fear God and believe in His Messenger; He will give you a double portion of His mercy. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Muqatil that when the verse Those will be given their reward twice for what they patiently endured was revealed, the believers from the People of the Book fell down before the Companions of the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and said, We have two rewards and you have one. This was difficult for the Companions, so God revealed, O you who have believed, fear God and believe in His Messenger; He will give you a double portion of His mercy. So He made for them two rewards like the rewards of the believers from the People of the Book.
57:29: Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Qatada who said: It has reached us that when the verse, He will give you double portions of His mercy, was revealed, the People of the Book envied the Muslims for it, so God revealed the verse, So that the People of the Book will not know. K and Ibn al-Mundhir narrated on the authority of Mujahid who said: The Jews said: A prophet will soon emerge from among us who will cut off hands and feet. So when he emerged from among the Arabs, they disbelieved. So God revealed the verse: So that the People of the Book will not know. He meant by the virtue of prophethood.
58:1: Al-Hakim narrated and authenticated it on the authority of Aisha, who said: Blessed is He whose hearing encompasses all things. I hear the words of Khawlah bint Tha’labah, and some of them are hidden from me. She is complaining about her husband to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and she is saying: O Messenger of God, my youth has been consumed and my belly has been scattered for him. Then, when I have grown old and my children have ceased to be children, he has become apparent to me. O God, I am complaining to You. So I did not stop until Gabriel came down with these verses: God has heard the words of the one who argues with you about her husband, and he is Aws ibn al-Samit.
58:8: Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Muqatil ibn Hibban, who said: There was a truce between the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and the Jews. Whenever one of his companions passed by them, they would sit and whisper among themselves until the believer would think that they were whispering about killing him or about something he disliked. The Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, forbade them from whispering, but they did not stop. Then God revealed: Have you not seen those who were forbidden from whispering? The verse. Ahmad, Al-Bazzar, and Al-Tabarani narrated with a good chain of transmission on the authority of Abdullah bin Amr that the Jews used to say to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, “Peace be upon you,” and then they would say to themselves, Why does God not punish us for what we say? So this verse was revealed: And when they come to you, they greet you with what God has not greeted you with. There is also a chapter on the authority of Anas and Aisha.
58:10: Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Qatada who said: The hypocrites were whispering among themselves, and that used to enrage the believers and make them feel heavy, so God revealed: It is a whisper from Satan, the verse.
58:11: And he also narrated on his authority that he said: When they saw someone coming to them, they were reluctant to sit with the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace. So the verse, O you who have believed, when you are told to make room in assemblies, was revealed. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Muqatil that it was revealed on a Friday and some people from the people of Badr had come and the place was narrow and no room was made for them, so they stood on their feet. So the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, made a group of them sit in their place, and those people disliked that, so it was revealed.
58:12: And he brought out through the path of Ibn Abi Talha on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said that the Muslims asked many questions of the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, until they became difficult for him, so God wanted to make things easier for His Prophet, so He revealed: When you confer privately with the Messenger, then present your private conversation before your private conversation. The verse was revealed. When it was revealed, many of the people were patient and stopped asking, so God revealed after that: Do you fear? the verse. Al-Tirmidhi narrated, and he authenticated it, and others narrated on the authority of Ali, who said: When the verse, O you who have believed, when you confer privately with the Messenger, present before your private conversation some charity, was revealed, the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, said to me, What do you think of a dinar? I said, They cannot afford it. He said, Then half a dinar. I said, They cannot afford it. He said, Then how much? I said, A barley grain. He said, You are indeed insignificant. Then the verse, Do you find it hard to present before your private conversation some charity? was revealed, and through me God has lightened the burden on this nation. Al-Tirmidhi said, Hassan.
58:13: And he brought out through the path of Ibn Abi Talha on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said that the Muslims asked many questions of the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, until they became difficult for him, so God wanted to make things easier for His Prophet, so He revealed: When you confer privately with the Messenger, then present your private conversation before your private conversation. The verse was revealed. When it was revealed, many of the people were patient and stopped asking, so God revealed after that: Do you fear? the verse. Al-Tirmidhi narrated, and he authenticated it, and others narrated on the authority of Ali, who said: When the verse, O you who have believed, when you confer privately with the Messenger, present before your private conversation some charity, was revealed, the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, said to me, What do you think of a dinar? I said, They cannot afford it. He said, Then half a dinar. I said, They cannot afford it. He said, Then how much? I said, A barley grain. He said, You are indeed insignificant. Then the verse, Do you find it hard to present before your private conversation some charity? was revealed, and through me God has lightened the burden on this nation. Al-Tirmidhi said, Hassan.
58:14: Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Al-Suddi regarding his statement, Have you not seen those who took a people as allies? The verse says, We have been informed that it was revealed about Abdullah bin Nabtal.
58:18: Ahmad and Al-Hakim narrated, and authenticated it, on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, was in the shade of a room, and the shade was about to shrink. He said: A man will come to you and look at you with the eyes of a devil. When he comes to you, do not speak to him. They had not waited long before a blue-eyed man appeared to them. The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, called him and said to him when he saw him: Why do you revile me and your companions? He said: Leave me and I will bring them to you. So he went and called them, and they swore to him that they did not say or do anything. Then God revealed: On the Day when God will resurrect them all, they will swear to him as they swear to you, the verse.
58:22: And Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Ibn Shawdhab who said: This verse was revealed about Abu Ubaidah ibn al-Jarrah when he killed his father on the day of Badr: You will not find a people who believe in God and the Last Day having affection for those who oppose God. The verse was narrated by al-Tabarani and al-Hakim in al-Mustadrak with the wording: The father of Abu Ubaidah ibn al-Jarrah began to confront Abu Ubaidah on the day of Badr, and Abu Ubaidah began to turn away from him. When he increased, Abu Ubaidah attacked him and killed him, so it was revealed. Ibn al-Mundhir narrated on the authority of Ibn Jurayj, who said: I was told that Abu Quhafah cursed the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and Abu Bakr slapped him and he fell down. This was mentioned to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and he said: Did you do that, Abu Bakr? He said: By God, if the sword were near me, I would have struck him with it. Then the verse was revealed: You will not find a people like that.
59:1: Al-Bukhari narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas that he said: Surat Al-Anfal was revealed about Badr and Surat Al-Hashr was revealed about Banu Nadir. Al-Hakim narrated and authenticated it on the authority of Aisha, who said: The battle of Banu Nadir, who were a group of Jews, took place six months after the Battle of Badr. Their homes and palm trees were on the outskirts of Medina, so the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, besieged them until they agreed to evacuate and that they would have whatever goods and money the camels carried except for the weapon. Then God revealed about them: Glorify God, whatever is in the heavens and the earth.
59:5: Al-Bukhari and others narrated on the authority of Ibn Umar that the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, burned the palm trees of Nadir and cut down the valley of Al-Buwayrah, so God revealed: Whatever palm trees you cut down or left behind - the verse. Abu Ya`la narrated with a weak chain of transmission on the authority of Jabir, who said: He gave them permission to cut down palm trees, then he made things difficult for them. They came to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and said: O Messenger of God, is there a sin upon us for what we cut down or left? Then God revealed: Whatever you cut down of palm trees or leave them… K and Ibn Ishaq narrated on the authority of Yazid bin Ruman who said: When the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, descended upon Banu Nadir, they fortified themselves against him in the fortresses. He ordered the palm trees to be cut down and burned. They called out to him: O Muhammad, you used to forbid corruption and criticize it, so what is the matter with the cutting down and burning of the palm trees? Then it was revealed. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Qatada and Mujahid something similar.
59:9: Ibn al-Mundhir narrated on the authority of Yazid al-Asamm that the Ansar said: O Messenger of God, divide the land between us and our emigrant brothers in half. He said: No, but you should provide them with the expenses and share the fruit with them, and the land is your land. They said: We are satisfied. Then God revealed: And those who have settled in the home, the verse: Al-Bukhari narrated on the authority of Abu Hurairah who said: A man came to the Messenger of God (peace and blessings of God be upon him) and said: I have been afflicted with hardship. So he sent to his wives but did not find anything with them. He said: Is there not a man to host me tonight so that God may have mercy on him? A man from the Ansar stood up and said: I am, O Messenger of God. So he went to his family and said to his wife: He is the guest of the Messenger of God (peace and blessings of God be upon him), do not withhold anything from him. She said: By God, I have nothing with me except food for the children. He said: When the children want dinner, put them to sleep and come and extinguish the lamp and we will eat our stomachs tonight. So I did. Then he went to the Messenger of God (peace and blessings of God be upon him) in the morning and said: God has certainly amazed and made you laugh at so-and-so and so-and-so. Then God revealed: And they give preference over themselves, even though they were in need. Musaddad narrated in his Musnad and Ibn al-Mundhir on the authority of Abu al-Mutawakkil al-Naji that a man from the Muslims mentioned something similar, and in it is that the man who hosted Thabit bin Qais bin Shammas, and this verse was revealed about him. Al-Wahidi narrated on the authority of Muharib bin Dithar on the authority of Ibn Umar who said: A man from the companions of the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, was given the head of a sheep as a gift, and he said: My brother so-and-so and his family are more in need of this than us. So he sent it to him. He kept sending it to one another until it was passed around to the people of seven households until it returned to those people, and then the verse was revealed: And they give preference over themselves, even though they were in need.
59:11: And Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Al-Suddi, who said: Some people from the people of Qurayzah converted to Islam, and among them were hypocrites. They used to say to the people of Nadir: If you are expelled, we will go out with you. So this verse was revealed about them: Have you not seen those who were hypocrites saying to their brothers?
60:1: Al-Bukhari and Muslim narrated on the authority of Ali, who said: The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, sent me, Al-Zubayr, and Al-Miqdad bin Al-Aswad. He said: Go until you come to Rawdat Khakh, for there is a woman there with a letter. Take it from her and bring it to me. So we went out until we came to the garden, and there we saw the woman. We said: Bring out the letter. She said: I have no letter with me. We said: Either you bring out the letter or we will throw away the clothes. So she took it out of her tie and we brought it to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace. It was from Hatib bin Abi Balta’ah to some of the polytheists in Mecca, informing them of some of the affairs of the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace. He said: What is this, Hatib? He said: Do not rush me, O Messenger of God. I was attached to the Quraysh and I was not one of them. And those who were with you were from the emigrants who had relatives with whom they protected their families and their wealth in Mecca. So I wanted, since I missed that lineage among them, to take a hand with which they could protect my relatives. I did not do that out of disbelief, nor apostasy from my religion, nor approval of disbelief. So the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, said: He spoke the truth. And in it this Surah was revealed: O you who have believed, do not take My enemies and your enemies as allies, extending to them affection.
60:8: Al-Bukhari narrated on the authority of Asma bint Abi Bakr, who said: My mother came to me wanting to visit the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, so I asked him if I could visit her. He said: Yes. Then God revealed about her: God does not forbid you from those who do not fight you because of religion. Ahmad, Al-Bazzar, and Al-Hakim narrated, and authenticated it, on the authority of Abdullah bin Al-Zubayr, who said: A woman was brought to her daughter, Asma bint Abi Bakr, whom Abu Bakr had divorced during the pre-Islamic period. She was brought to her daughter with gifts, but Asma refused to accept them from her or to let her into her home until she sent to Aisha, “Ask the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, about this.” She told him, and he ordered her to accept her gifts and let her into her home. Then God revealed, God does not forbid you from those who do not fight you because of religion.
60:10: And the two sheikhs narrated on the authority of Al-Miswar and Marwan bin Al-Hakam that when the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, made a treaty with the infidels of Quraysh on the day of Al-Hudaybiyah, believing women came to him, so God revealed: O you who have believed, when believing women come to you as emigrants, until His saying: And do not hold fast to the bonds of disbelieving women. And Al-Tabarani narrated with a sound chain of transmission on the authority of Abdullah bin Abi Ahmad, who said: Umm Kulthum bint Uqbah bin Abi Mu’ayt migrated during the truce, so her brother Ammarah and Al-Walid, the sons of Uqbah, went out until they came to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and spoke to him about Umm Kulthum, asking him to return her to them. Then God broke the covenant between him and the polytheists, especially with regard to women, and prevented them from being returned to the polytheists. Then God revealed the verse of testing. K and Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Yazid bin Abi Habib that he was informed that it was revealed about Umaimah bint Bishr, the wife of Abu Hassan Al-Dahdaha. K and he narrated on the authority of Muqatil that a woman named Sa’idah was married to Saifi bin Al-Rahib, who was a polytheist from the people of Mecca. She came during the time of the truce and they said, Return her to us. So it was revealed. K and Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Al-Zuhri that it was revealed to him while he was at the bottom of Al-Hudaybiyah and he had made peace with them that whoever came to him would be returned to them. So when the women came to him, this verse was revealed. K and Ibn Mani’ narrated on the authority of Al-Kalbi on the authority of Abu Salih on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: Omar bin Al-Hattab converted to Islam, but his wife stayed behind among the polytheists, so God revealed: And do not hold fast to the bonds of disbelieving women.
60:11: And Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Al-Hasan regarding his statement, And if any of your wives escape you verse, he said, It was revealed regarding Umm Al-Hakam bint Abi Sufyan, who apostatized and a Thaqafi man married her, and no other woman from Quraysh apostatized except her.
60:13: And Ibn al-Mundhir narrated on the authority of Ibn Ishaq on the authority of Muhammad on the authority of Ikrimah and Abu Saeed on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: Abdullah bin Omar bin Zaid bin al-Harith used to befriend some Jewish men, so God revealed: O you who have believed, do not take as allies a people with whom God has become angry, the verse.
61:1: Al-Tirmidhi and Al-Hakim narrated and authenticated it on the authority of Abdullah bin Salam, who said: We sat with a group of the companions of the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and we talked, so we said: If we knew which deeds are most beloved to God, we would know them. Then God revealed: “Whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth exalts God, and He is the Exalted in Might, the Wise. O you who have believed, why do you say what you do not do?” So the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, recited it to us until he finished it. And Ibn Jarir narrated something similar on the authority of Ibn Abbas. K and he narrated on the authority of Abu Salih who said: They said: If we knew which deeds are most beloved to God and best, then the verse was revealed: O you who have believed, shall I guide you to a trade? The verse was revealed. They hated jihad, then the verse was revealed: O you who have believed, why do you say what you do not understand? K and Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Ali on the authority of Ibn Abbas something similar to it.
61:2: Al-Tirmidhi and Al-Hakim narrated and authenticated it on the authority of Abdullah bin Salam, who said: We sat with a group of the companions of the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and we talked, so we said: If we knew which deeds are most beloved to God, we would know them. Then God revealed: “Whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth exalts God, and He is the Exalted in Might, the Wise. O you who have believed, why do you say what you do not do?” So the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, recited it to us until he finished it. And Ibn Jarir narrated something similar on the authority of Ibn Abbas. K and he narrated on the authority of Abu Salih who said: They said: If we knew which deeds are most beloved to God and best, then the verse was revealed: O you who have believed, shall I guide you to a trade? The verse was revealed. They hated jihad, then the verse was revealed: O you who have believed, why do you say what you do not understand? K and Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Ali on the authority of Ibn Abbas something similar to it.
61:11: K and he brought out on the authority of Ikrimah on the authority of Ibn Abbas and Ibn Jarir on the authority of Al-Dahhak who said: It was revealed: Why do you say what you do not do about a man who says in battle what he did not do of striking, stabbing, and killing K and Ibn Abi Hatim brought out on the authority of Muqatil that it was revealed about their taking sides on the day of Uhud. K and he narrated on the authority of Saeed bin Jubair who said: When the verse O you who have believed, shall I guide you to a trade that will save you from a painful punishment was revealed, the Muslims said: If we knew what this trade was, we would have given our wealth and families for it. Then the verse You believe in God and His Messenger was revealed.
62:11: The two sheikhs narrated on the authority of Jabir, who said: The Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, was delivering the sermon on Friday when a caravan approached. They went out to it until there were only twelve men left with him. Then God revealed: And if they see a trade or entertainment, they disperse to it and leave you standing. K and Ibn Jarir also narrated on the authority of Jabir who said: When the slave girls were married, they would pass by the bellows and flutes and leave the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, standing on the pulpit and they would rush to her. So it was revealed, and it seems that it was revealed about both matters together. K Then I saw Ibn al-Mundhir narrate it on the authority of Jabir regarding the story of marriage and the arrival of the caravan together through one chain of transmission and that it was revealed regarding the two matters, so praise be to God.
63:5: Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Qatada who said: It was said to Abdullah bin Ubayy, Why don’t you go to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and he will ask forgiveness for you? He started to shake his head, so the verse, And when it is said to them, ‘Come, so that the Messenger of God may ask forgiveness for you,’ was revealed about him. Ibn al-Mundhir narrated something similar on the authority of Ikrimah.
63:6: And he narrated on the authority of Urwah who said: When the verse was revealed: “Ask forgiveness for them or do not ask forgiveness for them. If you ask forgiveness for them seventy times, God will not forgive them,” the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, said: I will add to the seventy. So God revealed: It is the same to them whether you ask forgiveness for them or do not ask forgiveness for them. The verse was also narrated on the authority of Mujahid and Qatadah. K and he brought out through the path of Al-Awfi on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: When the verse of Bara’ah was revealed, the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, said: “I hear that I have been given permission to deal with them. By God, I will seek forgiveness more than seventy times, perhaps God will forgive them.” Then it was revealed.
63:7: Al-Bukhari and others narrated on the authority of Zaid bin Arqam who said: I heard Abdullah bin Ubayy say to his companions: Do not spend on me from the Messenger of God until they disperse, for if we return to Medina, the more honorable will expel therefrom the more humble. I mentioned that to my uncle, and my uncle mentioned that to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace. The Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, called me and I told him. The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, sent for Abdullah bin Ubayy and his companions, and they swore that they did not say anything. He lied to me and believed him, and something happened to me the like of which I had never been afflicted with before. I sat in the house and my uncle said: I only wanted to accuse the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, of lying and of hating you. Then God revealed: When the hypocrites come to you. So the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, sent for me and recited it, then he said: Verily, God has confirmed you. There are many chains of transmission on the authority of Zaid, and in some of them it is said that it was during the Battle of Tabuk and that the Surah was revealed at night.
63:8: Al-Bukhari and others narrated on the authority of Zaid bin Arqam who said: I heard Abdullah bin Ubayy say to his companions: Do not spend on me from the Messenger of God until they disperse, for if we return to Medina, the more honorable will expel therefrom the more humble. I mentioned that to my uncle, and my uncle mentioned that to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace. The Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, called me and I told him. The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, sent for Abdullah bin Ubayy and his companions, and they swore that they did not say anything. He lied to me and believed him, and something happened to me the like of which I had never been afflicted with before. I sat in the house and my uncle said: I only wanted to accuse the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, of lying and of hating you. Then God revealed: When the hypocrites come to you. So the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, sent for me and recited it, then he said: Verily, God has confirmed you. There are many chains of transmission on the authority of Zaid, and in some of them it is said that it was during the Battle of Tabuk and that the Surah was revealed at night.
64:14: Al-Tirmidhi and Al-Hakim narrated and authenticated it on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: This verse was revealed: Indeed, among your wives and your children are enemies to you, so beware of them about a people from the people of Mecca who had become Muslim, but their wives and children refused to let them come to Medina. When they came to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, they saw that the people had understood, so they decided to punish them. Then God revealed: And that you pardon and overlook the verse. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Ata’ bin Yasar who said: The entirety of Surat At-Taghabun was revealed in Mecca except for these verses: O you who have believed, indeed among your wives are your wives. This was revealed about Awf bin Malik Al-Ashja’i. He had a family and children. Whenever he wanted to go on a raid, they would weep for him and stop him, saying: To whom are you leaving us? So he would be moved by fear and stay. This verse and the rest of the verses until the end of the surah were revealed in Medina.
64:16: Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Saeed bin Jubair who said: When the verse Fear God as He should be feared was revealed, the people found work difficult, so they stood until their heels became swollen and their foreheads became ulcerated. Then God revealed relief for the Muslims, Fear God as much as you are able, the verse.
65:1: Al-Hakim narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: Abd Yazid Abu Rukana divorced Umm Rukana, then he married a woman from the Muzaynah tribe. She came to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and said: O Messenger of God, what has happened to me is this blond woman. Then the verse was revealed: O Prophet, when you divorce women, divorce them during their waiting period. Al-Dhahabi said: It is weak and the report is wrong, because Abd Yazid did not live to see Islam. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Qatada on the authority of Anas, who said: The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, divorced Hafsa, so she went to her family, and God revealed: O Prophet, when you divorce women, divorce them during their waiting period. So it was said to him: Take her back, for she is one who fasts and prays. Ibn Jarir narrated it on the authority of Qatada, without a chain of transmission, and Ibn al-Mundhir narrated it on the authority of Ibn Sirin, without a chain of transmission. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Muqatil regarding his statement, O Prophet, when you divorce women verse, he said: We have been informed that it was revealed about Abdullah bin Amr bin Al-Aas, Tufail bin Al-Harith, and Amr bin Saeed bin Al-Aas.
65:2: Al-Hakim narrated on the authority of Jabir who said: This verse was revealed, And whoever fears God - He will make for him a way out about a man from the Ashja’ tribe who was poor, light in wealth, and had many dependents. He came to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and asked him. He said: Fear God and be patient. He did not stay long until his son came with sheep that the enemy had captured. He came to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and told him about them. He said: Eat them. So it was revealed. Al-Dhahabi said: It is a rejected hadith with a witness. K and Ibn Jarir narrated something similar on the authority of Salim bin Abi al-Ja`d K and al-Suddi and the man was named Awf al-Ashja`i K and al-Hakim also narrated from the hadith of Ibn Mas`ud and named him thus. Ibn Mardawayh narrated on the authority of Al-Kalbi on the authority of Abu Salih on the authority of Ibn Abbas who said: Awf bin Malik Al-Ashja’i came and said: O Messenger of God, my son has been captured by the enemy and his mother is distressed, so what do you command me to do? He said: I command you and her to say often, There is no power or strength except with God. The woman said: Yes, what do you command? They began to say it often, but the enemy neglected him, so he drove off their sheep and brought them to his father. Then the verse was revealed: And whoever fears God - He will make for him a way out. Al-Tayyib included it in his history on the authority of Juwaybir on the authority of Al-Dahhak on the authority of Ibn Abbas. Al-Tha’labi included it on the authority of another weak chain of transmission. Ibn Abi Hatim included it on the authority of another chain of transmission without a chain of transmission.
65:4: And Ibn Jarir, Ishaq bin Rahawayh, Al-Hakim and others narrated on the authority of Abi bin Kaab who said: When the verse in Surat Al-Baqarah was revealed about a number of women, they said: There remained a number of women who did not mention the young, the old, and those who are pregnant. So the verse was revealed: And those who have despaired of menstruation. The chain of transmission is authentic. And Muqatil narrated in his interpretation that Khallad bin Amr bin Al-Jamuh asked the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, about the waiting period of a woman who does not menstruate, so it was revealed.
66:1: Al-Hakim and Al-Nisa’i narrated with a sound chain of transmission on the authority of Anas that the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, had a female slave whom he had sexual intercourse with. Hafsa kept at him until he made her forbidden to himself. Then God revealed, O Prophet, why do you forbid what God has made lawful for you?
66:2: And Al-Dhiya’ included in Al-Mukhtara from the hadith of Ibn Umar on the authority of Umar who said: The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, said to Hafsa: Do not tell anyone that the mother of Ibrahim is forbidden to me. So he did not approach her until Aisha told him. Then God revealed: God has imposed on you the expiation of your oaths. K and Al-Tabarani narrated with a weak chain of transmission from the hadith of Abu Hurairah who said: The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, entered Hafsa’s house with his concubine, Maria. She came and found her with him, so she said: O Messenger of God, in my house, not in the houses of your wives. He said: It is forbidden to me to touch her, O Hafsa. Write this down for me. So she went out until she came to Aisha and informed her. Then God revealed: O Prophet, why do the verses forbid? Al-Bazzar narrated with a sound chain of transmission on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: O Prophet, why did you forbid your concubine? Al-Tabarani narrated with a sound chain of transmission on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: “The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, was drinking honey at Sawdah’s house. Then he entered upon Aisha and she said: ‘I smell an odor from you.’ Then he entered upon Hafsa and she said the same thing. Then he said: ‘I think it is from a drink I drank at Sawdah’s house. By God, I will not drink it.’ So this verse was revealed: ‘O Prophet, why do you forbid what God has made lawful for you?’” And it has a corroborating evidence in the two Sahihs. Al-Hafiz Ibn Hajar said: It is possible that the verse was revealed for both reasons together. Ibn Saad narrated on the authority of Abdullah bin Rafi’ who said: I asked Umm Salamah about this verse: O Prophet, why do you forbid what God has made lawful for you? She said: “I had a bowl of white honey, and the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, used to lick some of it and he loved it.” Aisha said to him: Its honey makes a jug of honey. So he forbade it, and this verse was revealed. K and Al-Harith bin Osama narrated in his Musnad on the authority of Aisha who said: When Abu Bakr swore that he would not spend on Mistah, God revealed: God has imposed on you the expiation of your oaths, so he spent on him. The reason for its revelation is very strange. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: This verse was revealed: O Prophet, why do you forbid what God has made lawful for you? Regarding the woman who offered herself to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace. It is also strange and its chain of transmission is weak.
66:5: God Almighty said: Perhaps his Lord, if he divorces you, will forgive you. The reason for its revelation was presented, which is the statement of Omar in Surat Al-Baqarah.
68:2: Ibn al-Mundhir narrated on the authority of Ibn Jurayj that they used to say to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, that he was crazy and then a devil, so the verse was revealed: You are not, by the grace of your Lord, a madman.
68:4: Abu Naim included in Al-Dala’il and Al-Wahidi narrated it on the authority of Aisha, who said: “No one had better character than the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace. No one from his companions or his family called him except that he said: ‘At your service.’ This is what God revealed, and you are indeed of a great moral character.”
68:10: And Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Al-Suddi regarding his statement: And do not obey every worthless swearer, he said: It was revealed concerning Al-Akhns bin Shuraik. And Ibn Al-Mundhir narrated on the authority of Al-Kalbi something similar. And Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Mujahid, he said: It was revealed concerning Al-Aswad bin Abd Yaghuth. K and Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas who said: “The verse was revealed to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace: ‘Do not obey every worthless swearer, slanderer, and backbiter.’ We did not recognize him until after that, ‘Zanim’ was revealed to him, and we recognized him for his hump like the hump of a sheep.”
68:11: And Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Al-Suddi regarding his statement: And do not obey every worthless swearer, he said: It was revealed concerning Al-Akhns bin Shuraik. And Ibn Al-Mundhir narrated on the authority of Al-Kalbi something similar. And Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Mujahid, he said: It was revealed concerning Al-Aswad bin Abd Yaghuth. K and Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas who said: “The verse was revealed to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace: ‘Do not obey every worthless swearer, slanderer, and backbiter.’ We did not recognize him until after that, ‘Zanim’ was revealed to him, and we recognized him for his hump like the hump of a sheep.”
68:13: And Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Al-Suddi regarding his statement: And do not obey every worthless swearer, he said: It was revealed concerning Al-Akhns bin Shuraik. And Ibn Al-Mundhir narrated on the authority of Al-Kalbi something similar. And Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Mujahid, he said: It was revealed concerning Al-Aswad bin Abd Yaghuth. K and Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas who said: “The verse was revealed to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace: ‘Do not obey every worthless swearer, slanderer, and backbiter.’ We did not recognize him until after that, ‘Zanim’ was revealed to him, and we recognized him for his hump like the hump of a sheep.”
68:17: And Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Ibn Jurayj that Abu Jahl said on the day of Badr: Take them by force and tie them with ropes and do not kill any of them. Then the verse was revealed: Indeed, We have tested them as We tested the companions of Paradise. He said about their power over them, just as the companions of Paradise had power over Paradise.
69:12: Ibn Jarir, Ibn Abi Hatim, and Al-Wahidi narrated on the authority of Buraydah that the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, said to Ali ibn Abi Talib, I have been commanded to bring you close and not to keep you away, and to teach you and to understand, and it is your right to understand. He said, So this verse was revealed, and a listening ear understands it. It is not correct.
70:1: Al-Nasa’i and Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas regarding his statement, A questioner asked, he said, ‘He is Al-Nadir bin Al-Harith.’ He said, ‘O God, if this is the truth from You, then rain down upon us stones from the sky.’ Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Al-Suddi regarding his statement, A questioner asked, that it was revealed in Mecca about Al-Nadr bin Al-Harith, and he had said, O God, if this is the truth from You, and his punishment was on the day of Badr.
70:2: And Ibn al-Mundhir narrated on the authority of al-Hasan, who said: It was revealed. A questioner asked about a punishment that would befall. The people said: On whom will the punishment befall? So God revealed it to the disbelievers: There is no repeller for it.
72:1: Al-Bukhari, Al-Tirmidhi and others narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, did not recite to the jinn, nor did he see them. Rather, he set out with a group of his companions, intending to go to the Ukaz market. The devils were prevented from receiving report from heaven, and meteors were sent upon them. So they returned to their people and said: This is nothing but something that has happened. So travel to the east and west of the earth and see what has happened. So they set out. The group who had headed towards Tihamah returned to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, while he was in Nakhlah, and he was leading his companions in the dawn prayer. When they heard the Qur’an, they listened to it and said: This, by God, is what has come between you and the report from heaven. So they returned to their people and said: O our people, we have heard an amazing Qur’an. Then God revealed to His Prophet: Say: It has been revealed to me, but what has been revealed to him is only the word of the jinn. Ibn al-Jawzi included in his book Safwat al-Safwa, on the authority of Sahl ibn Abdullah, who said: “I was in the region of the land of Aad when I saw a city of carved stone, in the middle of which was a palace of stone inhabited by the jinn. I entered and saw an old man of great stature praying towards the Kaaba, and he was wearing a soft woolen cloak. I was not as amazed by the greatness of his creation as I was by the softness of his cloak. I greeted him and he returned my greeting and said: ‘O Sahl, bodies do not create clothes, but rather the smells of sins and the consumption of ill-gotten gains create them. I have been wearing this cloak for seven hundred years. In it I met Jesus and Muhammad, peace and blessings be upon them, and I believed in them. I said to him: ‘Who are you?’ He said: ‘From those about whom it was revealed.’ Say: ‘It was revealed to me that a group of jinn listened.’”
72:6: And Ibn al-Mundhir, Ibn Abi Hatim, and Abu al-Sheikh in al-Azmah narrated on the authority of Kurdam bin Abi al-Sa’ib al-Ansari, who said: I went out with my father to Medina on some errand, and that was the first thing that the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, mentioned. So he gave us shelter at a shepherd’s house. When it was midnight, a wolf came and took a lamb from the sheep. The shepherd jumped up and said: Amir al-Wadi is your neighbor. Then a caller called out, O Sarhan! The lamb came running until it entered the sheep. And God revealed to His Messenger in Mecca, There were men from among mankind who sought refuge with men from among the jinn. The verse Ibn Saad narrated on the authority of Abu Raja’ al-Attardi from Banu Tamim who said: The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, was sent and I was the shepherd of my family and I was responsible for their work. When the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, was sent, we fled and came to a deserted area. When we spent the evening in a similar area, our sheikh said: We seek refuge in the Aziz of this valley from the jinn tonight. So we said that. Then it was said to us: The only way of this man is to testify that there is no god but God and that Muhammad is the Messenger of God. Whoever acknowledges it is safe for his blood and his wealth. So we returned and entered Islam. Abu Raja’ said: I think that this verse was revealed about me and my companions. There were men from among mankind who sought refuge with men from among the jinn, but they increased them in burden. Al-Kharaiti included in his book, The Phones of the Jinn, that Abdullah bin Muhammad al-Balawi told us, that Amara bin Zaid told us, that Abdullah bin al-Ala’ told us, that Muhammad bin Akbar told us, on the authority of Saeed bin Jubair, that a man from Banu Tamim called Rafi’ bin Umair told about the beginning of his conversion to Islam. He said, “I was walking by the sands of ‘Alij one night when sleep overcame me, so I got down from my mount and made it kneel and went to sleep. I had sought refuge before sleeping, saying, ‘I seek refuge in the greatness of this valley from the jinn.’ I saw in my dream a man with a spear in his hand, wanting to put it in the throat of my camel. I woke up startled and looked right and left, but I did not see anything, so I said, ‘This is a dream.’ Then I went back to sleep and saw something similar. I woke up and saw my camel swaying. I turned around and saw a young man like the one I had seen in the dream, with a spear in his hand, and an old man holding his hand, pushing him away. While they were arguing, three wild bulls came out, so the old man said to the young man, ‘Get up and take whichever one you want as a ransom for the camel of my human neighbor.’ So he got up.” The young man took a bull from it and left. Then he turned to the old man and said, “O man, if you descend into a valley and are afraid of its terror, say, ‘I seek refuge in the Lord of Muhammad from the terror of this valley,’ and do not seek refuge with any of the jinn, for its matter has become void.” So I said to him, Who is this Muhammad? He said, An Arab prophet, neither of the East nor of the West, sent on a Monday. I said, Where is his dwelling? He said, Yathrib, the city of palm trees. So I mounted my mount when the morning dawned and renewed my journey until I entered the city. The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, saw me and narrated my story to me before I mentioned anything of it. He invited me to Islam, so I became Muslim. Saeed bin Jubair said, We thought that he was the one about whom God revealed, and that there were men from among mankind who sought refuge with men from among the jinn, but they increased their burden.
72:16: And he narrated on the authority of Muqatil regarding his statement, And if they were to remain on the right path, We would surely provide them with abundant water. He said, It was revealed about the infidels of Quraysh when rain was withheld for seven years.
72:18: Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Abu Salih on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: The jinn said: O Messenger of God, give us permission to witness the prayers with you in your mosque. So God revealed: The mosques belong to God, so do not invoke anyone with God. Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Saeed bin Jubair who said: The jinn said to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace: How can we come to the mosque when we are far from you? Or how can we witness the prayer when we are far from you? So the verse was revealed: The mosques belong to God.
72:22: Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Hadrami that he mentioned to him that a jinn from among their nobles, Dhat Tubba, said: Muhammad only wants God to protect him, and I will protect him. So God revealed: Say: No one can protect me from God, the verse.
73:1: Al-Bazzar and Al-Tabarani narrated with a weak chain of transmission on the authority of Jabir who said: The Quraysh gathered in the House of Assembly and said: Give this man a name that people will reject. They said: He is a soothsayer. They said: He is not a soothsayer. They said: He is mad. They said: He is not mad. They said: He is a magician. They said: He is not a magician. This reached the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, so he wrapped himself in his clothes and covered himself with them. Then Gabriel came to him and said: O you wrapped in clothes, O you wrapped in clothes. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Ibrahim Al-Nakha’i regarding his statement, O you who are wrapped in a blanket, that he said, It was revealed while he was wearing a blanket.
73:20: And Al-Hakim narrated on the authority of Aisha, who said: When the verse, O you enwrapped in a cloak, arise during the night, except for a little, was revealed, they stood for a year until their feet swelled, and then the verse, So recite what is easy for you from it, was revealed. Ibn Jarir narrated something similar on the authority of Ibn Abbas and others.
74:1: The two sheikhs narrated on the authority of Jabir who said: The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, said: I lived in Hira for a month. When I had completed my stay, I went down and went into the valley. Then I was called, but I did not see anyone. I raised my head and saw the angel who had come to me in Hira. So I returned and said: Cover me. Then God revealed: O you wrapped up in a cloak, arise and warn. And Al-Tabarani narrated with a weak chain of transmission on the authority of Ibn Abbas that Al-Walid bin Al-Mughirah made food for the Quraysh, and when they had eaten, he said, What do you say about this man? Some of them said, He is a magician, and some of them said, He is not a magician, and some of them said, He is a soothsayer, and some of them said, He is not a soothsayer, and some of them said, He is a poet, and some of them said, He is not a poet, and some of them said, He is magic that has been passed on. This reached the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and he became sad, covered his head, and wrapped himself in a cloak. Then God revealed, O you wrapped in a cloak, arise and warn, until His Most High’s saying, And for your Lord, be patient.
74:2: And Al-Tabarani narrated with a weak chain of transmission on the authority of Ibn Abbas that Al-Walid bin Al-Mughira made food for the Quraysh, and when they had eaten, he said, What do you say about this man? Some of them said, He is a magician, and some of them said, He is not a magician, and some of them said, He is a soothsayer, and some of them said, He is not a soothsayer, and some of them said, He is a poet, and some of them said, He is not a poet, and some of them said, He is magic that has been passed on. This reached the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and he became sad, covered his head, and wrapped himself in a cloak. Then God revealed, O you wrapped in a cloak, arise and warn, until His Most High’s saying, And for your Lord, be patient.
74:3: And Al-Tabarani narrated with a weak chain of transmission on the authority of Ibn Abbas that Al-Walid bin Al-Mughira made food for the Quraysh, and when they had eaten, he said, What do you say about this man? Some of them said, He is a magician, and some of them said, He is not a magician, and some of them said, He is a soothsayer, and some of them said, He is not a soothsayer, and some of them said, He is a poet, and some of them said, He is not a poet, and some of them said, He is magic that has been passed on. This reached the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and he became sad, covered his head, and wrapped himself in a cloak. Then God revealed, O you wrapped in a cloak, arise and warn, until His Most High’s saying, And for your Lord, be patient.
74:4: And Al-Tabarani narrated with a weak chain of transmission on the authority of Ibn Abbas that Al-Walid bin Al-Mughira made food for the Quraysh, and when they had eaten, he said, What do you say about this man? Some of them said, He is a magician, and some of them said, He is not a magician, and some of them said, He is a soothsayer, and some of them said, He is not a soothsayer, and some of them said, He is a poet, and some of them said, He is not a poet, and some of them said, He is magic that has been passed on. This reached the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and he became sad, covered his head, and wrapped himself in a cloak. Then God revealed, O you wrapped in a cloak, arise and warn, until His Most High’s saying, And for your Lord, be patient.
74:5: And Al-Tabarani narrated with a weak chain of transmission on the authority of Ibn Abbas that Al-Walid bin Al-Mughira made food for the Quraysh, and when they had eaten, he said, What do you say about this man? Some of them said, He is a magician, and some of them said, He is not a magician, and some of them said, He is a soothsayer, and some of them said, He is not a soothsayer, and some of them said, He is a poet, and some of them said, He is not a poet, and some of them said, He is magic that has been passed on. This reached the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and he became sad, covered his head, and wrapped himself in a cloak. Then God revealed, O you wrapped in a cloak, arise and warn, until His Most High’s saying, And for your Lord, be patient.
74:6: And Al-Tabarani narrated with a weak chain of transmission on the authority of Ibn Abbas that Al-Walid bin Al-Mughira made food for the Quraysh, and when they had eaten, he said, What do you say about this man? Some of them said, He is a magician, and some of them said, He is not a magician, and some of them said, He is a soothsayer, and some of them said, He is not a soothsayer, and some of them said, He is a poet, and some of them said, He is not a poet, and some of them said, He is magic that has been passed on. This reached the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and he became sad, covered his head, and wrapped himself in a cloak. Then God revealed, O you wrapped in a cloak, arise and warn, until His Most High’s saying, And for your Lord, be patient.
74:7: And Al-Tabarani narrated with a weak chain of transmission on the authority of Ibn Abbas that Al-Walid bin Al-Mughira made food for the Quraysh, and when they had eaten, he said, What do you say about this man? Some of them said, He is a magician, and some of them said, He is not a magician, and some of them said, He is a soothsayer, and some of them said, He is not a soothsayer, and some of them said, He is a poet, and some of them said, He is not a poet, and some of them said, He is magic that has been passed on. This reached the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and he became sad, covered his head, and wrapped himself in a cloak. Then God revealed, O you wrapped in a cloak, arise and warn, until His Most High’s saying, And for your Lord, be patient.
74:11: And Al-Hakim narrated and authenticated it on the authority of Ibn Abbas that Al-Waid bin Al-Mughirah came to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and recited the Qur’an to him, and it was as if he felt sorry for him. When this reached Abu Jahl, he came to him and said, O uncle, your people want to collect money for you to give it to you, for you came to Muhammad to attack us before him. He said, The Quraysh know that I am one of the wealthiest of them. He said, Then say something about him that will make your people know that you disapprove of him and that you hate him. He said, “What should I say? By God, there is no man among you who knows more about poetry than I do, or its rajaz, or its qasida than I do, or the poetry of the jinn. By God, the one who says this does not resemble anything of this. By God, his speech has sweetness and freshness, and it is luminous at the top and bright at the bottom. It rises and what rises, and it crushes what is beneath it.” He said, Your people will not be pleased with you until you say something about it. He said, Then leave me until I think. When he thought, he said, This is magic that is being transmitted from someone else. Then the verse, Leave me and he whom You created alone, was revealed. Its chain of transmission is authentic according to the criteria of Al-Bukhari. Ibn Jarir narrated it. And Ibn Abi Hatim narrated it from other chains of transmission.
74:30: And Ibn Abi Hatim and Al-Bayhaqi narrated in Al-Baath on the authority of Al-Bara’ that a group of Jews asked a man from the companions of the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, about the keepers of Hell. So he came and informed the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and at that time nineteen verses were revealed to him about them.
74:31: And he narrated on the authority of Ibn Ishaq, who said: Abu Jahl said one day: O people of Quraysh, Muhammad claims that the soldiers of God will torture you in a fire of nineteen, and you are the most numerous of people. So are a hundred men from among you unable to defeat a man from among them? So God revealed: And We have not made the companions of the Fire except angels, the verse. And he narrated something similar on the authority of Qatada, who said: It was mentioned to us, so he mentioned it. K and he narrated on the authority of Al-Suddi who said: When the verses nineteen were revealed to her, a man from Quraysh called Abu Al-Ashd said: O people of Quraysh, do not be frightened by the nineteen. I will defend you with my right shoulder ten and with my left shoulder nine. Then God revealed: And We have not made the companions of the Fire except angels.
74:52: Ibn al-Mundhir narrated on the authority of al-Suddi who said: They said: If Muhammad is truthful, then let there be a scroll under the head of every man among us in which there is innocence and security from the Fire. So the verse was revealed: Rather, every man among them wants to be given scrolls spread out.
75:16: Al-Bukhari narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas, who said: When revelation came down to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, he would move his tongue with it, wanting to memorize it. So God revealed: Do not move your tongue with it to hasten it, the verse.
75:34: Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Al-Awfi on the authority of Ibn Abbas who said: When the verses nineteen were revealed, Abu Jahl said to the Quraysh, may your mothers be bereaved of you, Ibn Abi Kabshah tells you that the keepers of Hell are nineteen and you are the most numerous. Is it possible for every ten of you to strike a man from the keepers of Hell? So God revealed to His Messenger to go to Abu Jahl and say: It is more appropriate for you, then more appropriate, then more appropriate for you, then more appropriate. K and Al-Nisa’i narrated on the authority of Saeed bin Jubair that he asked Ibn Abbas about his saying It is more appropriate for you, so it is more appropriate. Was it something that the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, said on his own initiative, or did God command him to do it? Rather, he said it on his own initiative, then God revealed it.
75:35: Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Al-Awfi on the authority of Ibn Abbas who said: When the verses nineteen were revealed, Abu Jahl said to the Quraysh, may your mothers be bereaved of you, Ibn Abi Kabshah tells you that the keepers of Hell are nineteen and you are the most numerous. Is it possible for every ten of you to strike a man from the keepers of Hell? So God revealed to His Messenger to go to Abu Jahl and say: It is more appropriate for you, then more appropriate, then more appropriate for you, then more appropriate. K and Al-Nisa’i narrated on the authority of Saeed bin Jubair that he asked Ibn Abbas about his saying It is more appropriate for you, so it is more appropriate. Was it something that the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, said on his own initiative, or did God command him to do it? Rather, he said it on his own initiative, then God revealed it.
76:8: And Ibn al-Mundhir narrated on the authority of Ibn Jarir regarding his statement, And a prisoner, that the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, did not take the people of Islam captive, but it was revealed regarding the captives of the polytheists, and they were taking them captive while they were being tortured, so it was revealed regarding them, so the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, would command that they be treated fairly.
76:20: And Ibn al-Mundhir narrated on the authority of Ikrimah who said: Umar ibn al-Khattab entered upon the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, while he was lying on a palm frond mat and it had left marks on his side. Umar wept, so he said to him: What makes you weep? He said: I remembered Kisra and his kingdom, Hormuz and his kingdom, the ruler of Abyssinia and his kingdom, and you, the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, are lying on a palm frond mat. The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, said: Are you not satisfied that they have this world and we have the hereafter? Then God revealed: And if you see, then you will see bliss and a great kingdom.
76:24: And Abd al-Razzaq, Ibn Jarir, and Ibn al-Mundhir narrated on the authority of Qatada that he was informed that Abu Jahl said, If I see Muhammad praying, I will step on his neck. So God revealed, And do not obey among them a sinner or an ingrate.
77:48: Ibn al-Mundhir narrated on the authority of Mujahid regarding his statement, And when it is said to them, ‘Bow down,’ they do not bow down, that it was revealed about Thaqeef.
78:1: Ibn Jarir and Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Al-Hasan that he said: When the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, was sent, they began to ask each other questions, so the verse was revealed: They are asking each other questions about the great report.
79:10: Saeed bin Mansour narrated on the authority of Muhammad bin Kaab who said: When the verse, Will we indeed be returned to the grave was revealed, the infidels of Quraysh said, If we live after death, we will surely lose. So it was revealed and they said, That then is a losing return.
79:12: Saeed bin Mansour narrated on the authority of Muhammad bin Kaab who said: When the verse, Will we indeed be returned to the grave was revealed, the infidels of Quraysh said, If we live after death, we will surely lose. So it was revealed and they said, That then is a losing return.
79:41: And Al-Hakim and Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Aisha, who said: The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, was asked about the Hour until the following was revealed to him: They ask you about the Hour, when is its arrival? What is your position regarding its remembrance? To your Lord is its end. Then he finished. And Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on the authority of Juwaybir on the authority of Al-Dahhak on the authority of Ibn Abbas that the polytheists of Mecca asked the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and they said: When will the Hour come, mocking them. So God revealed: They ask you about the Hour, when is its arrival? To the end of the Surah. K and Al-Tabarani and Ibn Jarir narrated on the authority of Tariq bin Shihab who said: The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, used to mention the Hour often until the verse was revealed: What do you have to do with its mention? To your Lord is its end. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated something similar on the authority of Urwah.
80:1: Al-Tirmidhi and Al-Hakim narrated on the authority of Aisha that she said: He frowned and turned away was revealed about Ibn Umm Maktum, the blind man who came to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and kept saying: O Messenger of God, guide me. And with the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, was a man from the leaders of the polytheists. So the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, kept turning away from him and turning to the other man, saying to him: Do you see anything wrong with what I am saying? He said: No. So the verse: He frowned and turned away was revealed when the blind man came to him. Abu Ya’la narrated something similar on the authority of Anas.
